description:
Town Center: London / Hong Kong is comprised of 4 double-sided player boards, with the all-new London expansion map on one side, and the also all-new Hong Kong expansion map on the other. There's also an A5-sized rulesheet/cover that explains the special rules of each map.

Both maps are something new and challenging (in fact, very challenging), as players will have to manage their budget (London), and their building plans (Hong Kong) very, very carefully.

Expertly designed by Alban Viard and beautifully illustrated by Todd Sanders, these highly thematic maps were developed to showcase just how deep this game can be, while taking advantage of each city's unique characteristics.

This is a simple roll and move game with the objective of touring wartime Germany and being the first to reach the final (100) space.

However, the primary objective of the game was to encourage both patriotism and obedience to wartime rules, specifically the prohibition on listening to foreign broadcasters.

When landing on domestic transmitter spaces, the players must carry out certain actions: sing a ditty, send a message, make a weather report or sing a military song. But if they land on a foreign transmitter space, they must exclaim "I have committed a criminal offense", pay a pfennig into the pot and go back to start. Worst is if they land on London's transmitter, when they must shout "God punish England!" and pay 5 pfennig into the pot as well as start over. The pot is taken by the winner.

The title "Radio-Sende-Spiel" translates in Google Translate to "Radio Broadcast Game".

Confounded is a game for two, played on a 3x9 rectangular grid of squares.  Each player has nine pieces, or "spots," in their own color.  The board is divided into three 3x3 sections, with each player claiming one side and the central territory in dispute.  Three each of a player's "spots" are marked with either the number one, two, or three.  The game begins with the three spots in the row closest to the players, the two spots in the next row inward, and the one spots in the next row.

Players take turns moving one piece at a time and try to claim the central area or prevent their opponent from doing so.  All spots move only forward.  One spots move one square diagonally.  Two spots move two squares directly forward.  Three spots move either two squares forward and one square sideways, or one square sideways and two squares forward.

Capture of pieces is by displacement and there are a number of rules governing it.  To win the game, a player must occupy five squares of the central area, or block his opponent from moving, or reduce his opponent to less than five pieces.

Coloration is played on a 17x17 grid of squares.  Some squares are marked with a circle in one of eight colors; these squares form diagonal lines crossing the board from corner to corner and side to side.  The board's central square is marked with a red diamond. The game includes 154 square tiles: 15 red, 25 orange, 35 yellow, 35 green, 25 blue, 15 violet, and 4 "stopper" tiles, which are half black and half color.  Racks are included for each player.

The tiles are placed face down in a "pool."  Each player draws seven tiles to make his "hand," and will continuously draw again to maintain a hand of seven.  Players begin placing their tiles on the board, attempting to make "sequences" of colors.  The rules provide for a number of these, including a rainbow sequence, a hot spectrum sequence, a cold spectrum sequence, and more.  These sequences incorporate the existing colors of the board and are governed by still more rules.

Different levels of points are scored for each sequence as players complete their turns.  Play continues until no more sequences are possible; the player with the most points wins the game.

Farmageddon: The Frankencrops includes fifteen new crop cards, each with a different ability while in play, for farmers to fight over, spicing up the Farmageddon base game.

The Frankencrops can be combined with the Livestocked and Loaded if desired.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Anti-Social: The Anti-Pawn Placement Game

The Anti-Social, pro social game about playing with your friends, more then they play with you.

In a world of games where it’s easy to win, and victory is all about claiming as much space on a board as possible, In Anti-Social, the point is to do just the opposite. Occupy as little space on the board as you can. Develop negative personality disorders, or force others to play against themselves, but keep yourself off the board.

In this game you do not want to place, or have your pawns placed on the board. Each turn players may play up to two cards (Anti-Social Behavior), and must place one pawns on the board.

Once the board is filled, the game is over. Player with the least amount of pawns on the board wins.

Mice and Mystics: Downwood Tales is a box chock full of adventure and whimsy.  New storybook, a pile of new miniatures, new heroes, new villains, new location tiles, new search deck items.  Downwood Tales takes the adventure outdoors.  Meet frogs, turtles, rabbits, the wise sages - the three blind mice, ride leaves like parachutes out of the trees and much, much, much more.

Downwood Tales gives you multiple playable characters, new villains, new minions, tiles, a ton of new mechanics, equipment, new story arcs and more! New to this big box expansion is the gecko named Jakobe and he's not your run-of-the-mill gecko, he's a smooth operator, a ranger of the forest, a lizard for hire who is leading our heroes through some of the greatest dangers they will ever face! With more fun elements than you can shake your tail at, Downwood Tales is the next exciting installment in the Mice & Mystics world!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A press-your-luck board game for two or more players. Each player is the captain of a freighter on the Galilean Run between Mars and the moons of Jupiter. The object is to get the most supplies to each of the four major moons. The challenge is to balance fuel and cargo. Each ship carries six units of fuel (represented by dice). Each cargo token displaces a fuel die. To move to the next port roll the dice. Rolls of 1 or 2 are displaced as spent. If any fuel dice remain, move up the track. At each port you can deposit or collect cargo tokens but you can only deposit other players' cargo tokens at Mars.

Clan War: Crab Army Expansion includes a 64-page army list filled with Crab personalities and units, rules for items, new spells, a new maneuver, and a lot of great background on the Crab Clan. In addition, it includes Force cards, Tactical cards, Hida Yakamo, and twelve Hida House Guard models (13 metal figures) - only available in this box set! (Box Set - 13 metal miniatures, 64-page army book)
family: Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

description:
Navigate your pawns across an ever-changing maze, while avoiding the Minotaur.

Players can rotate, swap, and shift tiles to create a path, while blocking opponents.  They also move their pawns in an effort to get across, and the Minotaur in an effort to eat opposing pawns.

Tibbar and friends want to surprise their friend Beary wtih his favorite berry pie.
So it's off to the Pie Maker.Will they get there in time?  They better hurry if they are going to get the berries to the pie maker before Beary eats them all. Everyone wins when all 5 of Beary's friends get their berries to the Pie Maker!

Roll the die and perform the action shown on the Action Board to move Tibbar and Friends along the game board.  Act fast though!  Beary's hourglass is always running.  Don't let him catch you!

Work together with the other players to get all five characters to the Pie Maker to win the game!

Features

    Collaboration
    Action- Based Strategy
    Fine Motor Skills

MOOOVE your Money Tokens to the money pool to match the amount on the Goal card and the Money Tokens are yours! Don’t worry if you can’t make a total match, you can cash in your Cow Token and the pool is yours! Have the Money Tokens with the most dollar value at the end of the game and win!

Add Money Tokens to money pools to reach goal amounts. Match the goal exactly and collect all the tokens from that pool. Be careful not to exceed the goal amount or the Money Tokens from that money pool will go to the other players. Players calculate the monetary value of the Money Tokens they’ve collected and the player with highest total wins!

Features

    Monetary Values
    Adding and Subtracting Amounts
    Adaptive Strategy

Vinculum is a fast paced game of fractions!

Race to get the most Fraction Tiles and win! A fraction consists of a Numerator (on the top) and a Denominator (on the bottom). The line that separates the two is called a Vinculum. Learning how to reduce and expand fractions will help players recognize equivalent fractions.

Turn a new Fraction tile face-up and all players start searching for a match. Quickly slap a tile with a matching equivalent fraction to claim it. Claim the most tiles to win the game!

Features:


    Recognizing Equivalent Fractions
    Reducing and Expanding Fractions
    Observational Reflexes

In Brawl, two players fight to the finish. You don't win until your opponent is knocked out.

Each round, both players play a card from their hand face down, then they flip over and execute the card played in the previous round. The five cards (Block, Dodge, Jab, Hook, and Roundhouse) allow the players to use offensive and defensive techniques and combinations to reduce their opponents health to 0. If your health reaches 0, you\'re knocked out and you lose.

In RPG Merchant Tycoon!!, players take the roles of optimistic start up entrepreneurs, looking to set up shops in the bustling villages, towns or cities spread throughout the world of your typical 8-bit RPG game. However, these said villages, towns and cities aren't big enough for the 2 (to 4) of yas'! So it's time to get busy and dirty, trading and searching for loot to fulfill the growing line of adventurers looking for some gear! Expand your shop, spend your Gold stock (or hoard it!) wisely, trade with other rival shops, keep secret Stock all to yourself, call in favors from your happy Clients.....do whatever it takes to clinch market Dominance and be the only show in town and start reeling in the BIG money! Who knows, maybe that Flaming Sword of Justice you just sold will be used by the Hero that will save the world?!

RPGMT!! is a 30-60min 2-4 Player high strategy card game in which the player's goal is to finish the game with the best balance between: The amount of Client's orders they were able to fulfill; the amount of Gold they personally have by the end; and the total quality's worth of items in their Display.

Each turn Players can take up to 2 "Actions" (unless they get extra Actions thanks to fulfilling certain Client orders) in their attempt to fulfill Client orders for certain weaponry or item...ry. These Actions let the Players manipulate their Stock items (or Hand cards) and/or their Display items (cards face up in front of a Player for everyone to see/trade) in specific ways to try and beat the rush to fulfill those orders! Ran out of Actions for your turn, but you're only ONE Action away from fulfilling that order?! No Worries!! There are "Gold Actions" that you can use in a pinch, which let you spend Gold to do certain Actions that DON'T count towards your Action limit for your turn....WOOHOO!! After all the Clients have been fulfilled, the game ends and Players then move on to calculating "Dominance", which will determine who the TRUE Merchant Tycoon is!!

The strategic decisions and stiff competition in RPGMT!! really heat up quick due to the fact that Players start out with only a limited number of Actions per turn AND a limited number of cards they can have, so EVERY decision counts! Everything from in what order you'd like to take your Actions, which Client you should fulfill, and should you or shouldn't you use Gold are crucial to Dominance. You might spend all your Action efforts to build up enough items to fulfill an order for a specific Client, but then the Player before you fulfills them leaving you at a disadvantage with items that you might not need now!! Remember, it's not just playing the current Client market, it's also knowing and playing to the weaknesses of your fellow Players!

Board at Sea is a pirate themed strategy game for 2 players that takes about half an hour.

The board shows 2 ships, side by side, and the rope bridges that connect them. Start by standing your stones on your ship so that your opponent can't see what they are. On each turn you'll move a piece and roll dice to resolve attacks. Your crew moves between ships, the cannons fire across the board, and the treasure tries its best to stay hidden.

The pirates win by stealing one of the treasure chests and bringing it back to their ship, and the merchants win if they can stop the pirates.

Cay Control is a pirate themed strategy game for 2 players that takes about half an hour.

The board shows a sea dotted with islands and pirate bases on either side. Start by placing your ships on your base. On each turn you'll roll the dice and move your ships. You capture enemy ships by landing on them, but you have to bring them back to your base to claim them. Stacks of ships can be captured over and over, and you can even break out with a lucky roll.

The first person to claim all of their opponents ships wins the game.

Divide the Booty is a pirate themed trading game for 2-4 players that takes about an hour.

The board shows a treasure chest with a race track around the outside. Start by placing your stone on the start space and all the booty face-down on the chest. Each player draws three pieces of booty and stands them up so the other players can't see what they are. On each turn you'll trade a piece, bank a piece, and draw a piece. Try to collect the biggest set of one symbol, but watch out for the bombs. Once all the booty has been banked, count up your sets and move around the track.

Shuffle the booty and play again until someone circles the track and wins the game.

Escape the Plank is a pirate themed race game for 2-6 players that takes about half an hour.

The board shows a ship with a U-shaped track with the plank on one end and a rowboat on the other. Start by placing your crew on the start space at the back of the ship. On each turn you'll roll the dice and move the crew along the track. You can move any crew you want, even if it belongs to someone else. If your crew gets pushed off the plank, it's out of the game. If your crew makes it to the rowboat, it's safe.

Once all of the crew is off the ship, whoever has the most crew in the rowboat wins the game.

Pillage / Burn is a pirate themed bluffing game for 2-8 players that takes about half an hour.

The board shows a mystery ship surrounded by a set of pillage/burn spaces for each player. Start by placing all of the loot in the center of the board. On each turn as the scout, you'll roll the die in secret and try to trick the other players. Everyone who got it right collects some loot, and everyone you tricked pays you.

Once all the loot has been collected, the pirate with the most wins the game.

Sea Stories is a pirate themed storytelling game for 2-4 players that takes about an hour.

The board shows a grid with difficulty ratings surrounded by quick descriptions of the story elements. Start by choosing whether each player will be a Fresh Fish or Salty Dog. On each turn you'll roll the dice and tell a story based on what you roll. The number of story elements you use determines your placement on the board. If the other players catch an interaction you forgot, they'll call hornswaggle and prevent you from placing a piece.

The first person to get a three-in-a-row wins the game.

The Blockade is a pirate themed strategy game for 2 players that takes about half an hour.

The board shows a sea with marked starting spaces and pirate island on one side. Start by placing your ships on the marked spaces and setting the difficulty. On each turn you'll move one of your ships. Pirates are more maneuverable, but there are only 3 of them. The navy moves slower, but has a large fleet.

The pirates win if they can get just one treasure back to the island, and the navy wins if they can stop the pirates.

Liaoshen Campaign(遼瀋戰役), 1948 is a 2-4 players operational-level board wargame that simulates the battles between Chinese Communist Party (CCP,中國共產黨) and Kuomintang (KMT,中國國民黨) from Sep, 1948 to Nov, 1948 during the second Chinese Civil War.  It is the first game in the Chinese Civil War Series(CCWS,國共內戰系列).  2 players command the People Liberation Army (PLA), and the other 2 players command the National Government Army (NRA) respectively.

Each turn represents 7 days and each hex is about 10 km across.  There are several kinds of basic combat units, including infantry, artillery, artillery, cavalry, armor, etc.  The scale of the basic unit is from regiment to division size.  There are three phases in the sequence of play, strategic, operational, and supply phases.  During the strategic phase, player use strategic move to deploy your units to the right place quickly.  During the operational phase, players use cards to activate the corps HQ to command its units to move, combat, and advance after combat.  You can also use cards to initiate some events or special tactics during combat.  During supply phase, you produce the resource points from the city/town you occupied and refill your hand cards.

There are four scenarios in this game, including Liaoshen Campaign (7 turns), Battle of Jinzhou (4 turns), Battle of Heishan (3 turns), Huludao evacuation (2 turns).  The victory condition is different from both sides in different scenarios.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Tholia One Expansion Pack
description:
Tholian starships are small starships of characteristic shape, utilized by the Tholian Assembly during the 23rd century. These vessels are armed with plasma torpedo-like weaponry, but their most powerful weapon is the so-called "Tholian web", which is "weaved" by two or more Tholian vessels around an enemy ship, trapping it. The web can then be contracted, destroying the vessel inside.

The Star Trek: Attack Wing – Tholia One Expansion Pack is the competitive prize for "The Tholian Web" episode during Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Skilled Helmsman Resource
description:
Skilled Helmsman Resource (5 SP)
Players receive the 1 Full About Maneuver Template and place it on one of their Ship Cards. For the duration of the battle, the chosen ship (and only that ship) may perform the Full About [FULLABOUT] Maneuver.

This resource is a participation prize for the episode Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, "The Tholian Web."

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Red Alert
description:
Unique Federation Elite Talent (2 SP)
When you spend a [BATTLESTATIONS] Token, you may discard this card instead of returning the [BATTLESTATIONS] Token to the Action Token supply.

This resource is a participation prize for the episode Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, "The Tholian Web."

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Full Alert
description:
Unique Federation Elite Talent (5 SP)
ACTION: Discard this card to place 1 [EVADE], 1 [SCAN], 1 [BATTLESTATIONS] Token and 1 Auxiliary Power Token beside your ship. Your ship cannot perform any free Ations during the same round that you perform this Action (either before or after you perform this Action).

This resource is the competitive prize for the episode Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, "The Arena."

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Chief Engineer Resource
description:
Chief Engineer Resource (5 SP)
Players receive 5 Chief Engineer Tokens and place them beside their Ship Cards. A player may only use Chief Engineer Tokens for his/her ships.

Once per round, during the Combat Phase, a player may spend 1 Chief Engineer Token to gain one of its effects.

This resource is a participation prize for the episode Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, "The Arena."

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Soong Expansion Pack
description:
Designated by Starfleet as the Borg Type 03, this class of Borg starship was used by a rogue faction of Borg led by Lore. This unusual design was asymmetrical and totally unlike the previously encountered Borg cube and Borg scout, causing Captain Jean-Luc Picard to speculate whether it was something they constructed or an alien vessel they captured and assimilated. Whatever its origins, the vessel was still heavily armed and massively armored, capable of great destructive power.

This ship is part of the 6th wave of expansion packs for the Star Trek: Attack Wing game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Enterprise (Refit) Expansion Pack
description:
The USS Enterprise (NCC-1701-A) was a Federation Constitution-class heavy cruiser that was in service with Starfleet in the late 23rd century. This starship became the second Federation ship to bear the name Enterprise.

Externally, the Enterprise-A was virtually identical to the refit USS Enterprise, destroyed several months prior to the launch.

This ship is part of the 6th wave of expansion packs for the Star Trek: Attack Wing game.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – 2nd Division Cruiser Expansion Pack
description:
A group of these warships formed part of the first wave of Dominion ships to pass through the Bajoran wormhole, following their annexation of the Cardassian Union in 2373.

Gul Dukat would command one of these vessels during his year-long reign as leader of Cardassia. Its first notable appearance in battle against the Federation was at the outbreak of the Dominion War in late 2373, where it served as a command ship of the combined Dominion and Cardassian fleet.

This ship is part of the 6th wave of expansion packs for the Star Trek: Attack Wing game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Objective: Be the first reaching Leipzig
 - moving by turns -
each player determines (by die) his/her starting point - you must roll your die until you get a different starting number/position.
Move according die roll into indicated direction (clockwise/contraclockwise) around the board. If the space is already occupied you end on skip one turn. 
After you have finished one round head for Leipzig. Ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid.

The goal of the game is to reach Mehisto at the center of the game board and defeat him. The movement is determined by the spinner. The playing pieces have two sides, the costumed hero and his alter ego (for instance Spider-Man and Peter Parker). While in their secret identity the players are safe from attack and can move into position. To attack Mephisto they have to change into their superhero identities. On the Zoom Spaces the heroes get to collect Power Cards to use in the fight against Mephisto.  If a Superhero succesfully attacks Mephisto, he gets a Talisman Card. The first Player who gets 3 Talisman Cards wins the game.

boardgame: The Horus Heresy: Book Three – Extermination
description:
The third and final part of the Isstvan trilogy, this book details the aftermath of the Dropsite Massacre on Istvaan V, and two other major battles fought between the Loyalist and Traitor Legions as the Horus Heresy swept out from the Isstvan system and into the wider Imperium of Mankind: the Battle of Phall and the Invasion of Paramar.

Also featured are the background and game rules for the units, characters and vehicles of the four Legions covered in this book: the Alpha Legion, Imperial Fists, Raven Guard and Iron Warriors, and a Mechanicum army list, as well as new campaigns and missions.

The book itself is a full colour 285 page hardback, leather bound with metal corner clasps, foil-edged pages and embossed and silvered text.

boardgame: Gute Fahrt durch unser deutsches Vaterland!
description:
Objective: Be the first reaching Berlin (making a round trip - red route) - main game 
 - moving by turns -
Roll the die and move forward. If you end on a marked space, follow the  instructions glued into the lid.

Note:
There are a few different routes (yellow and blue) with slightly different rules. It's remarkable, that at that time a GDR publisher  produced a game with routes through all of Germany.

boardgame: For the Crown (Second Edition): Expansion #2 – The World is Round
description:
The second expansion to For the Crown, The World is Round introduces several new units with the ability to move in a cylindrical fashion – moving off the edge of the board and re-appearing on the other side. Rush your opponent with the vicious Raider, and command your troops with the Khan. Take charge of the battlefield and utilize maneuvers that will make your opponent's head spin with The World is Round.
 Note: For the Crown expansion kit 2: The World is Round is not a "stand alone" game. You must own a copy of For the Crown 2nd edition to use it. This expansion kit cannot be used with the first edition of For the Crown.

Game Data:

Complexity: 3 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 2 on a 9 scale

boardgame: We Didn't Playtest This Either with Blue Deck and Dice Are Fun Expansions
description:
From the box:

Many will enter, some will win. All will laugh.

We are back with more random! And you like random. Or you will, soon!
A full new set of cards that can be played with the original game, or on their own. Be sure to watch out for sharks! And cake. Especially the cake.

Play Summary (from We Didn't Playtest This at All)

Everyone starts with 2 cards. On your turn, draw one card from the deck, and then play one from your hand following the instructions on the card. There are many hazards to avoid, including: bombs, dragons, arrows, laser pointers, arrows, black holes, Rock Paper Scissors battles, number battles, zombies, etc. Luckily items abound that can save you, too: spaceships, science, shields, dinosaurs and a kitten ambush!

This is a standalone game that may be integrated with We Didn't Playtest This at All.
family: Game: We Didn't Playtest This At All

boardgame: The Silver Lion: The Great Battle of Tong Pass
description:
Han dynasty, August AD 211. CaoCao arrived at the front lines at Tong Pass, facing the main army of Ma Chao. His sole purpose is to eliminate the force of Xi Liang and conquer the vast area of western China. Facing a huge force at the border and short of supplies and opportunities for alliance, MaChao must overcome the biggest crisis of his life. How can you make decisions in right way? How can you find the weaknesses in Cao's incredible army? Every step you make will absolutely influence the battle's destiny. Will history be repeated? Everything is in your hands! The duel of fate is today!

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Tong_Pass_(211)

The Silver Lion has three campaign scenarios. They usually take 40-120 minutes.

Time scale: Each Game Turn equals 3 days.
Unit scale: Each step equals 2500 men or cavalry.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
After a nuclear disaster, the contaminated water has created these deadly monsters in the sea. They then sought land where the human civilization was nearly decimated. They then turned on each other in a monster war like no other. Only the fastest and strongest shall win!

Kaiju is a fun party game in which players are the monsters in battle, and they roll a die and have to perform the action as quickly as possible. The slowest person gets a wound which is drawn on by the crayon marker, and the one who gets six wounds is out. The box is also part of the components and is used during the game.

Kaiju comes with two sets of rules, one for the main game and another for a mini game.

Game description from the publisher:

For the third time, Munchkin explores your favorite myths and legends! Munchkin Legends 3: Myth Prints introduces the Gladiator, who gets tougher when his life is at stake! This 56-card set has new monsters to fight, new Treasures to gain, and dozens of new jokes to enjoy! (Or groan at — your choice.)

This game is based on 6-Tage-Rennen by the same author. Everybody has  the same hand of movement cards. Each player plays one card and moves his pawn accordingly. If he lands on the same spot as fellow player he may move the same number again immediatly.

Instead of a bike race however, Auf Gehts! simulates a race of Biathlon. The card play is similiar to 6-Tage-Rennen, but the track features slopes and hill where slightly different rules apply. Every round there is a shooting, which is simulated by a real-time-dexterity element using a small "Marble gun".

First to cross the finish line is the winner.

Game description from the publisher:

Rather than rot the teeth of your trick-or-treaters this year, why not rot their minds with a Munchkin Halloween Pack? Each of the twenty packs contains four Munchkin cards — with the same four cards in each pack — and a quick explanation of the game. No one has to have a bellyache in the morning!

Get Bit! Collectors Edition contains Walk the Plank!, Get Bit!, and Hold Your Breath! — three independent games that collectively tell a larger story.

In Walk the Plank!, players represent the worst pirates in a captain's crew. The captain has rounded you all up because you're all lazy and stupid and simply not worth the rum and loot you get paid. That said, the captain has decided he's willing to keep two of you in his crew. To prove you're worthy, you will fight amongst yourselves, trying to shove other players' pirates off the end of the plank while keeping yours alive!

Get Bit! is a card game in which the player pirates find themselves in the ocean, competing to stay alive as others are being eaten by a shark. The order of the swimmers is determined by simultaneously playing cards face down, then revealing the values. The number on each player's card determines position in line (higher numbers in front, lower numbers in back), however ties don't move. The swimmer at the back loses a limb to the shark and is flung to the front of the line! The process is repeated until only two swimmers remain on the table. When this happens, the swimmer at the front of the line wins the game!

In Hold Your Breath!, the game is played in two rounds. In the first round each player may dive deeper, providing they have the "Swim" and "Air" cards to do so. Players press their luck each turn since it is the pirate who dives the deepest in round one AND resurfaces in round two who is the winner. Players can play "Panic", "Shark" and other nasty cards on the other players, slowing their progress. In the second round each player struggles to reach the surface before they run out of air and therefore decide to take a rest (aka LOSE). Most of the pirates may not make it back to the surface, but the one who makes it back alive AND dove the deepest is the winner.

Included in each copy is a bonus chipboard with a newly designed pirate ship, an additional "Captain" stand-up starting token, and even some hunger tokens to go with the now included Sharkspansion for Get Bit.

Last Saga is a skirmish wargame played with 32mm miniatures.

Different factions fight against each other in a science-fiction universe: The Council, survivors of the human race; Uruhvel, users of powerful biotechnology; Raiders of Saanar, corsairs and pirates eager for loot; Guardians of Tanaor, high and proud of their advanced technology.

Games portray confrontations between two small groups of units controlled by players, filled with action and where tactics are key to victory!

Receive your actions each turn and use them wisely on your units. Examples of actions are: "Fire and movement", "Rapid fire", "Race", etc.

Every situation is resolved by a 2D6 roll, on a fast paced action where you will not lose sight of the game goal: accomplish the mission.

Configure your Combat Force, set the mission, and work for the success!
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Inspired by the Titanic – The World’s Largest Museum Attraction, the board game allows you to re-live the grandeur of the ill-fated ship. But don’t become too immersed in the history, your aim is to buy up all the sea-faring properties and win the game!

Join your opponents and sail through Titanic history while buying up your favorite Titanic rooms and galleries in 1st, 2nd, and 3rd class - but don't run out of cash. Try to avoid paying port taxes and watch out for ice fields ahead.

Features:

Full colour game board with a Titanic theme.

“Purser's Office” and “Ice Field” cards replace traditional Community Chest and Chance cards.

Money is printed with a helm design.

Includes six collectible tokens – anchor, shovel, coal, captain's hat, helm and key.

Traditional houses and hotels are renamed “Cabins” and “Suites”.

Set also includes two dice and an instruction booklet with the rules of the game, plus rules for 60-minute speed play game.
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic

boardgame: Battleground Historical Warfare: Alexander vs. Persia
description:
This is an expansion for the Battleground system, although it can be played as a stand-alone game since it contains both opposing armies and the rules, containing the armies of Alexander the Great and the Persian Empire and explores Alexander's invasion of Persia.  Alexander vs. Persia is compatible with all other Battleground armies; each AvP army can be played against any others in the Battleground system.

To simulate Alexander and his generals leading from the front, the Alexander army features the ability to attach general-specific command cards to a unit and then to replay the card on that unit.  The large and relatively unwieldy Persian army enables its general to sacrifice command actions at the start of the game in order to receive extra points for army building.

There are official solitaire rules for the Battleground series of games downloadable at the company website.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Game: Battleground Historical Warfare (Your Move Games)
History: Wars of Alexander the Great

description:
The Gambler Expansion is an exciting new addition to Spurs: A Tale in the Old West. 10 new Equipment Cards, rules for a 2 player game, and a new 6th character, The Gambler, complete with bullet bag and cowboy miniature!

Everything you ever wanted to make your Spurs Core Game playable for 2 or 6, or anything in between. Will you become the next Legend of the Old West?

Components:

1 black bullet bag
1 player miniature w/ snap-on base in light blue
1 oversized "The Gambler" character card
3 "No Duels" tokens
1 "High Noon" rules card for 2-6 players

9 Equipment Cards:
Camp Gear (x2)
Coffee (x2)
Spyglass (x2)
Henchman (x3; unique)

boardgame: Agents of SMERSH: Swagman's Hope Kickstarter Extras
description:
Promotional extras shipped out to Kickstarter backers of Swagman's Hope. These include three new UN characters, a new henchman, a new deck of Secret Mission cards, and an epic Space Station scenario.


     1 Henchman mat
         The Gambler
    
     3 UN Character mats
         Mosé Calderón
         Tilly Philips
         Grant Massey
    
     3 plastic standees
     21 Secret Mission cards
     Space Station Scenario and tokens
     Instructions

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Family Edition – Card Set
description:
Here\'s the perfect Trivial Pursuit edition for kids and parents alike. This Family Edition Card Set features two boxes of questions - one specifically for adults, one tailored just for kids - to give everyone a chance to win in these categories: People & Places, Entertainment, History, Science & Nature, Sports & Leisure, and Wild Card.
Grown-UPS will test their trivia memory on everything from Thomas Jefferson to the Ayatollah... Cobb to Clemens... Pearl Harbor to Kuwait City;... and Trump Tower to the Ozone Layer. The kids can show what they know from Dinosaurs to Gerbils... Adam and Eve to Cabbage Patch Kids... the Little Dipper to the Little Big Horn... and the Alamo to Oz.
Trivial Pursuit Family Edition... It\'s fun for all - and all for fun!

boardgame: From The Ranks To Field Marshal
description: A game produced in Britain during the First World War for the soldiers at the front.  Each player enlists as either a Private, Sapper, Gunner or Trooper depending on which arm of the armed forces they join, and as they progress around the board they get promoted through the ranks.  Whoever becomes Field Marshall is the winner.  Along the way are various events which may accelerate or hinder your progress.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This is a quiz game where the players have to answer questions about music theory, instruments and history in order to advance and collect the name, birth of date and portrait of specific composers.

Contents:
1 Board
1 Rulebook with the answers to the questions.
320 Question cards.
100 Composer cards
8 Gameplys figures
2 Dice

Scarborough Fair is a resource selling game for 2-4 players.  Players will buy and sell four resources; Parsley, Sage, Rosemary and Thyme.  They will set the prices that they sell each resource at, place Merchants in tents and buy Victory Points to give them the lead.  But the customer is a shrewd shopper so don’t raise your prices too high or you’ll be left holding resources that you can’t sell.  Pick the prime locations, corner a market or undercut your opponents to win.

Scarborough Fair is not a complicated game to learn and is fairly quick to play, but it has enough strategy to keep more seasoned gamers interested.  It's a great game for families and hard core gamers alike.

boardgame: Fish In A Barrel: The Fish-Linking Game
description:
The Fish-Linking game and more fun than a barrel of monkeys. See how many fish you can catch before time runs out! Includes eight fish (three bass, three trout, and two muskies) in a wood-pattern barrel that’s easy to transport to the campsite or even carry along on a fishing trip! Includes stringer, 60 second timer, and rule sheet.

Randomly place all 8 fish on a flat surface...turn over the 60 second timer....grab a fish and link as many together as you can (tail-hook to mouth-hole) before the timer runs out. Earn one point for every fish you link and a bonus point if you link all fish species of the same type back-to-back (example, all bass one behind the other). The player with the most points wins the round and gets to string up their "catch" for show (stringer included).

A press-your luck card game with an underground theme.

Players play as treasure hunters exploring deep underground, facing monsters such as giant earthworms and lava swimmers, and dangers such as narrow bridges and tunnel collapses.

Each turn the active player purchases tool cards with various special abilities. Then they encounter monster and danger cards from the dig deck until either they choose to stop digging or a card knocks them out. They score treasure based on the number of cards they encountered that turn, but only if they weren't knocked out.

Once the dig deck is depleted, whoever has the most treasure wins.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from VFG, a company in Germany.

boardgame: Battle of the Bands: World Tour Edition
description:
The premier Rock and Roll Card Game is back...but now it's taking over the world! Battle of the Bands: World Tour Edition, the game where you are the star of your own music group.

This new edition represents the natural evolution of the original Battle of the Bands game and its expansions.  The game mechanics have been streamlined, tightened, and improved over many years of play. New cards have been added that reflect the changes in music and the industry since the first Battle of the Bands incarnation. Famous cities and venues have been added to truly make this the "World Tour Editon"

With new cool and crazy band members and instruments, reputations that will elevate or cripple your band...you will be armed to the teeth when you hit the stage! Perform Hit Singles, work the Music Biz and ultimately blow your competition off the stage as you fight for Gigs all over the world!

Originally the basic game had a 13+ age level, now it's all ages friendly. BUT if you don't want to play Radio Disney you can add the Sex, Drugs and Rock and Roll expansion for the real band experience! With the Back Stage Pass and Front Row Center cards, you will expand your gaming experience to new heights and depths, just like the the music business. Pop, Rock, Metal, Punk, Funk, R&B, Country (and Western!), Thrash, Swedish Black Metal, Jazz... even the questionable sounds they call "music" today! It's all here for your gaming enjoyment.

A great gateway game for folks who love music but aren't into games...yet.

Sweet Sabotage is a board game about maids, butlers, desserts, and SABOTAGE! Build and serve your customers desserts to please your master, or destroy other player’s progress through sabotage! The cafe inheritance is on the line, make sure YOU win it!

Players start by choosing a maid/butler, and each of them have specific skills. Customers come in with their dessert card order, and the player has to collect ingredients to fill up the dessert card and serve the customer successfully. Be sure to act fast though! Some customers are impatient and will not tolerate slow service!

If you can't find the ingredient you urgently need, or you have to prevent someone from serving their final customer and winning the game, you can draw a Sabotage Card. These cards are powerful, and allows you to steal ingredients from other players, or even wreck their dessert forcing them to start over... but be careful! You can draw a CAUGHT card and you lose points!

The first player to reach a certain amount of favor points (gained from serving desserts to customers) wins the game, and the inheritance of the whole cafe!

Enter the world of the Mame-ista Championship!  Who will be the best Hiyoko dripper?  \"Hiyoko\" means chickpea, but these chickpeas are so cute they almost look like little chicks.

Players take turns in trying to tip out a combination of beans.  But you need to be careful as sometimes 1 bean may tip out...or a LOT!  Only the player who is able to get the right combination of beans over a certain amount of rounds (depending on the number of players) can be declared the Mame-ista winner!

On a turn you tip out some beans, if you get a blend/straight combination you can try one more time to get another combination.  If you are unsuccessful, you remove 1 bean from the ones that fell out, and try one more time.

Quaff is a collectible card game with fast-paced gameplay and a sense of humour.

The object of Quaff is to eliminate all other players whilst simultaneously attempting to remain in the game.  Pick a deck - be that the horde of zombies, the superhero, the spaceship, or the fantasy army - and sparingly play cards to take down your opponents.

At the start of each round, players bid to determine the total cost of the cards they can play this round - however this is linked to the amount of drink consumed.  Cards, ranging from permanents (which remain in play until removed by the effects of another card) to counters (which can be used on an opponent's turn to hinder their strategy), are utilised to decrease a player's life points. Once they reach 0, they are eliminated.

Contrées is a two player abstract game based on the mechanism of majority and surrounding.

Short resume of the rules:
The board is modular, it is made of specific hexagonal territories.
On that board each player plays his pawns on the corner of the hexagons and try to control as much territories as possible.
When a player have 4 pawn around a territory, he can place a possession mark on it and remove the enemy pawns from it. 
It is pretty simple but be careful, like in the game of GO, you can surround the opponent pawns and capture them.
When both player have played all their pawns, the one who control the most territories wins.

Sticks, Stones and Dragonflame is a 2P micro-game in which each player attempts to destroy the castle of their sworn enemy. Castles are formed by 6 cards or locations, numbered 1 to 6.

Every turn, players secretly roll a die and assault the matching location of their opponent's castle. However, players are allowed (and encouraged) to lie about their die result. The consequences of getting caught are dire, but getting away with the lie will bring victory much more swiftly. In addition, players have the option to manipulate luck by employing the services of powerful reinforcements.

Whenever a location is destroyed, the card is flipped and can't be used or assaulted again. Once the Heart of the Castle is destroyed, the owner of the castle loses the game and their opponent is declared the winner!

In Grad Wars all players are students in different Universities. Their aim is to get their degree before everyone else does by passing 20 lessons first. They do this either by studying hard, or by visiting the other players in their classroom and harassing them.

Players can pass up to 5 lessons in every turn. They get their grade by rolling a 10-sided die for every lesson and then modifying the die with cards thrown by themselves or other players.

Cards can be adding or subtracting from a grade, and they can be special cards. But basically they describe student life as it is, or rather as we think it is. Players drink tons of coffee, they get shut in in bunkers to study focused, and they give exams using a Divine Pen. They also invade other players’ homes to sabotage their alarm clock, and they cause a Zombie Apocalypse inside other players’ classrooms. And they start crying in front of professors to get a passing grade, they cheat like there is no tomorrow, and they go to parties that cause year-long hangovers. Grad Wars is a game for the students who want to get their degree first no matter what the cost, or the means.

Στο παιχνίδι αυτό ο παίχτης είναι φοιτητές σε διαφορετικές σχολές. Σκοπός του παιχνιδιού είναι να πάρει πτυχίο πριν από τους υπόλοιπους, συμπληρώνοντας 20 περασμένα μαθήματα. Αυτό γίνεται είτε με το να κάτσει να διαβάσει, ή με το να πηγαίνει στη σχολή του άλλου και να τον παρενοχλεί. Σε κάθε γύρο μπορεί να δώσει μέχρι 5 μαθήματα, και οι γύροι συνεχίζονται μέχρι να βγει κάποιος νικητής.

Οι κάρτες περιγράφουν τη φοιτητική ζωή έτσι όπως είναι, ή μάλλον έτσι όπως πιστεύουμε ότι είναι. Ο παίχτης πίνει τόνους καφέ, κλείνεται σε πυρηνικά καταφύγια για να διαβάσει πιο συγκεντρωμένος, γράφει εξετάσεις με το Στυλό των Θεών. Μπαίνει στο σπίτι του άλλου και του σαμποτάρει το ξυπνητήρι, κλείνει γρίπη σε ένα βαζάκι και το βάζει κάτω από μύτες, στέλνει Ζόμπι Συμφοιτητές στο αμφιθέατρο των συμπαικτών του. Κλαίγεται στους καθηγητές για να περάσει, αντιγράφει μέχρι τελικής πτώσης, πηγαίνει σε πάρτι που το χανγκόβερ κρατάει ένα χρόνο. Ο Πτυχιούχος είναι το παιχνίδι του Φυτού, του φοιτητή που πρέπει να πάρει πτυχίο πρώτος με καθε μέσο.

Each of the participants of the game becomes one of the heroes of the Erenthi world, wandering around the vast lands on their own or with their allies in search of magical stones. The task of the player involves getting the treasure or thwart the other participants' plans. An important element of the game is defining an ally and an enemy, owing to how easier it is to win and eliminate the competition. Each round ends by dealing the award cards and establishing the wounds incurred during the round. The player who has gained the most stones wins.

In the basic version of the game, in addition to good quality cards, you will find a magic code. On the publisher's website, you can activate it and receive a poster board and a map of the land delivered to your home. The board is not necessary for the game, but it is an accessory that helps to organize the place during the game. On the other hand, if you want to broaden your knowledge about the "flaming heart", you can buy a collector's version of the game on the publisher's website.

From the publisher's website:

Welcome to Top Billing! In this game, you and your friends become competing movie producers working on a film. Your objective is to duke it out with the others to put your unique imprint on the final film by gathering Actors, Sets, and Props. By strategically putting them together to earn you the most points, you can earn the title of executive producer and Top Billing!

Welcome to DeckQuest, a light RPG-simulating card game!

This game has been designed to be a simple yet fulfilling light RPG adventure in card format. This game requires no dice, no character sheets, no miniatures, and no complicated math.

DeckQuest is easy to set up: simply select your character (one of four) and the relevant stat cards.  Select your adventure--dungeon or outdoors--and create your adventure deck.  The adventure deck consists of path cards, monsters, events, and locations.  Your goal?  Defeat the dragon for fame and glory!

DeckQuest has very little text and uses simple yet expandable rules, making it a great "gateway" game for kids or non-gamers, but enough depth to make it a great filler game for more advanced players.  It requires no dice or any supplies--just the cards!

Game of observation and reaction for young children. One player throws a pair of six-sided dice to establish the target color combination. Players then search amongst a pile of butterfly figures for the butterfly with the matching color combination. The player who finds (i.e., "catches") the correct butterfly receives a scoring counter. The first player to get five counters wins the game.

Augmented to be more than human, you have but one task: save the last of humanity!

You are a Psyborg.  You have been augmented to be more than human with robotic limbs and powers of telekinesis.  You have but one task: save the last of humanity! But it isn't going the way you expected... Other Psyborgs are out there, trying to kill you while they steal the glory of saving humanity!

Your Job:
• Kill other Psyborgs!  
• Find, steal and protect humans!  
• Make sure you are the last Psyborg standing with a human under your protection!

The Rules:

• Shuffle all the cards, minus the rules cards, together
• Deal everyone two cards
• Determine who goes first
• At the start of turn, draw a card
• After drawing a card players can choose to play a card from their hand or pass.
• If there are no more cards to draw, shuffle the discard pile into the draw pile.
• The winner is the last player left alive with a human in their hand.

Each card has different effects - Shields block all cards used against you, Humans are to be protected and discarding one means you are out of the game, EMP Pulses forces a player to discard a card of their choice, Scans reveal Humans, etc...

If you don't have a Human card in hand, you want to find and take one from another player.  If you do have a Human, you want to protect it from other players.  Attack other players, but make sure you have a Human by the end of the game, or everyone loses.

Location for high-quality PnP files is located here.
The Game Crafter page of the game is located here.

This game won first place for the 24 hour contest - May 2014.

~ Introduction ~
Ever wondered if you could survive the Apocalypse? Test out your survival strategies in the most realistic Post-Apocalyptic card game ever created, Survivalistic! Set in a modern day Post-Apocalyptic era, Survivalistic lets a player embark on an epic journey with fellow survivors. While on this journey, survivors will unite with one another to form clans. A player's clan must outlast and outwit all opposing clan members in order to win. Survivalistic is sure to thrill any gamer on any level. Please feel free to check out the brand new card game Survivalistic!

~ Family Friendly ~
Don't be mistaken by the theme, this game makes for a great family game! No violence, no explicit images, and no brutality. This game focuses on survival strategy by using moral methods instead. These methods include: farming, cultivating, pillaging, adapting to new environments, purifying water, communication skills, gathering resources, resource management, hunting, and scavenging. The youngsters will find the game very easy to learn and enjoy playing time and time again!

~ Player Interaction ~
The player interaction in Survivalistic makes it a great social game. Just the fact that you have so many opportunities to get back at your opponent makes it incredibly fun. Players may find themselves losing at one point and then winning the next! Because you are often stealing and battling other players for resources there is hardly a dull moment.

~ Game Play ~
The deck symbolizes the various paths in which one can take through the apocalyptic world. While on this path your prime objective will be to build a team of survivors. This team of survivors will be called your clan. You can find water, food, equipment, and fellow survivors along the way to enhance your clan. Your clan members will display many skills while on your journey and these events are represented through clan member abilities. There are many strategies to this game through all the different types of cards and there are countless number of ways you can arrange your clan. In order for your clan to reign supreme they must be nourished with food and water. Nourished clan members may battle opposing clans for valuable resources. In the end, the first clan to have three clan members fully nourished at the same time wins the game. These elements make up the basis of the game. You can use any combination of cards but one thing must happen, you must survive!

Word Throwing Game is a word dice game where players take turns and roll the dice once. They form words of four or more letters. Blank sides are wild.

Scoring:

Each letter used scores 5 points. 
Players score a bonus of 25 points for spelling their first name.
Players score a bonus of 50 points for using all 12 dice.

The first player to reach 500 points is the winner.

A word dice game with 12 letter dice.

Players roll the dice and make words of three or more letters. Blank sides are wild.

Scoring:


     1 point for each letter used
     35 bonus points for spelling Throw or Word


If a player uses all 12 dice, he gets a free roll.

The winner is the first person to reach 200 points.

Challenge is a word dice game similar to Poker Challenge. Players roll the letter dice and try to form words to score points. However, it is different because of its board with red and blue boxes which can double or triple letters and words.

It is for two to ten players. Everyone plays in turn for two minutes.

A word dice game for 2 to 4 players. Players take turns to roll the dice and race against an hourglass to spell words for points. Similar to Challenge, it is also played on a board with double and triple spaces. Unlike Challenge, it is limited to four players.

A word dice game where players roll the dice and make words to score points. The player with the most points after 10 rounds is the winner.

This 2003 edition by Endless Games includes 22 (14mm, white) letter cubes, rather than the 15 in the original version. The scoring in this edition is also different. Rather than squaring the letter count for each word, a point value is assigned to each letter based upon the length of the word it is part of, with letters used in longer words worth more. Crossed words score the shared letter twice, possibly with two different values.

Scoring:


    3 letter words – 1 point per letter
    4 letter words – 2 points per letter 
    5 letter words and up – 3 points per letter 
    5 point bonus for using all 22 dice
     Subtract a point for each unused die

A set of 6 additional dice to add to the monster pool. It contains the following:

Vampire 
Dark Knight
Monstrous Crab
Will-O-Wisp
Trap
Unguarded Treasure #2

A set consisting of 5 familiars, which are added to the monster pool. If a player draws a familiar, he has the option to roll it once per combat and gain the benefit of their abilities.

The set includes the following five familiars:

Faerie
Rat
Serpent
Wolf
Baby Dragon

This expansion consists of 6 new dice for the red "one-use" bag as well as an additional Key dice for balancing reasons. These dice represent potions, which - when drawn - can be used once and are then discarded.

The six red dice are:

Boomerang
Toad Potion
Poison Potion
Luck Potion
Strength Potion
Invisibility Potion

This pack adds components for a fifth player to the base game. It consists of 20 dice:

3 Hero Level dice
5 Equipment dice
2 Artifact dice
4 One-Use dice
1 Experience Tracker die
5 Monster dice
1 Thief die

The Monster, Equipment, and Artifact dice are all randomly selected from the base Dungeon Dice game.  The One-Use dice are one of each of the 4 basic types.  The Thief die was originally only available in this set, but was later sold separately.

Flynn, the pirate owl is a real mischief-maker. Very secretly he has pinched anything and everything that he could fi nd on deck and has stashed it away in the look-out – the crow‘s nest: the gold drinking cups of the crew, the captain‘s sword, the treasure chest, the jewellery for the lady pirates and a few mice – just in case. You can never know how long you‘re going to be left sitting high and dry. Cheekily, he‘s throned high up in the crow‘s nest and is smirking joyfully over so many riches. It‘s no surprise that the ship‘s crew is fuming and want their belongings back!

Find the objects that Flynn has stashed away in the crow‘s nest. A die shows you what you have to look for. Like in the trick with the table cloth you have to pull a cord stick to a card and hope that the Flynn stays in his nest and that the nest is not toppling over.

Have I dressed up as wolf or fox, or as stork or crow? Sixteen cards are on the table, showing children dressed up in animal constumes. In addition, there are sixteen cards showing the corresponding animals. They are in a face-down pile.

In variant for young children, each child draws a card. The first player to find the correct costume, gains the card.

Older children play a question/answer game. By asking questions like "Can you fly?" or "Are you a pet?," they attempt to guess what card the other player is holding.

Battle of Byeokjegwan is an issue game of War Drum Games' wargame mook for beginners "Battle on the Table" #1.

The Battle of Byeokjegwan is a battle between Japan and China, fought in Korean peninsula on 27 January 1593.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Byeokjegwan

Including one mapsheet, six Chinese units, nine Japanese units, two markers and two dice. Each unit represents one commander and his troop. Rules, tutorial and historical notes are included in "Battle on the Table".

Chinese victory condition is decline of Japanese morale or killing Kobayakawa Takakage or Ukita Hideie. Japanese victory condition is rise of Japanese morale or killing Li Rusong.

Singapore is the first game of The Second World War Series (TSWW) from Diffraction Entertainment for the Pacific Theater of War.

It covers the campaigns in Malaya, Singapore, Burma and the Dutch East Indies (except Biak/New Guinea) for the entire Second World War.

The game includes 22  18″ x 26″ Map segments (plus a 1/8 map for northwestern Burma), 10 countersheets (2800 counters) plus the usual Rules, Charts, Orders of Battle for 23 scenarios and campaigns, and a pair of D10 Dice.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: The Second World War (Diffraction Entertainment)

description:
You're a mad scientist with the goal of evolving cute animals into minions in order to destroy the enemy's animals. Each creature can evolve into three different evolution trees, each with their own unique skills.

Evilution is a stand-alone card game. Each player chooses four animals to start the game with and performs actions each turn to further their position. Once you kill all the opposing creatures, you win the game.

In order to evolve your animals, you'll need to purchase evolution trees. You can earn money through sponsorships as well as completing objectives. You can also use the money to hire lab assistants and purchase equipment.

Also, every turn you'll either navigate random events or discover potions with good and bad effects. Some of your creatures may die, but that's a sacrifice you must be willing to make.

Do you have what it takes to be the best Mad Scientist? Only time will tell.

The mini-expansion includes 2 new triangle tiles, 3 new skills to be incorporated into the base game. 4 tiles (2 triangle and 2 diamond) are also included to correct misprint and miscut tiles in the base game.

The New Skills Include:


    The Squirrel Hunter: lets you hunt next to an animal trap even if there are no animals present.
    The Expert Miner: makes building a mine easier.
    The Well Builder: which lets you find a water source in the middle of the wasteland.


The New Triangle Tiles Include:


    Treasure Hill: adds a fun wrinkle to the buried treasure mechanic.
    Workshop: lets you switch out your earned skills with new ones.

Script-o-Gram is a word dice game with 13 cubes where players make words to score points.

It is similar to Spill and Spell and Ad-Lib Crossword Cubes, but has several differences:

Crossword play is forbidden, dice are not touched once cast, play is reminiscent of Boggle.

Scoring: per letter value, variations include one word scored or multiple words scored.

Letter Dice is a word dice game similar to Spill and Spell and Ad-Lib Crossword Cubes, where players make words to score points.

It scores differently than those games, with objectives like longest word and most words:

Throw the dice and race against your opponents (and the clock) to turn the jumbled-up letters into words. Can you make the longest word? Can you make the greatest number of words? Can you score the most points? Lots of different rapid-fire challenges for word game fans of all ages.

It is for 2 or more players.

Letter Dice by HABA is a word dice game similar to Letter Dice by Fun and Games and Ad-Lib Crossword Cubes, where players make words to score points.

HABA's version is for 1-6 players. It has several play modes:

1. Crosswords: One player rolls all 13 dice and forms words in a crossword puzzle shape. Players agree on the length of the round (between 2-5 minutes) and the number of rounds before the start of the game. Letters used score their point value. Letters used in two words score for both words. Letters not used are subtracted from the score.

When a player finishes making words, players can try to make a funny sentence with the words.

2. Letter Yatzi: The first player rolls all 13 dice and tries to form the longest word. Letters which don't fit are rolled again. At least one letter must be put aside each time. Play continues until all letters are used or set aside. Points are scored for the letters used. Unused letters are not counted.

If a player uses all 13 letters, the score for the word is doubled.

3. Vocabulary: All players together make one word, with all 13 dice if possible. One player is chosen as the secretary. Players try to make words with the letters of the first word, and the secretary keeps track of them. The winner is the player who can find the last intelligible word.

Fire up your engines and full burn out to the edge of the 'Verse. Firefly: The Game – Blue Sun expands the game with a new 10" x 20" map extension that adds new systems to explore and exploit in an area of space known as the Rim.

Rim Space is not a place for the faint-hearted, and a ship pushing past Border Space out towards the raggedy edge of space had best be ready to deal with what's out there. Navigate this frontier space with a new Nav Deck. A new Supply Planet offers new Gear to purchase and new Crew to hire. Two new Job Contacts offer new opportunities and unique game-changing challenges while the game's other Contacts have new Jobs to claim a piece of the Rim for themselves. Two new Leaders present new strategies for players to execute as they focus on three new Story Cards. Two additional Reaver Cutters and new rules for these dangerous savages put the 'Verse on high alert. Reaver activity is on the rise and your Crew is on the menu!

description: Texarkana Trivia is a knock-off of Trivial Pursuit, focused on local trivia about the Texas-Arkansas-Louisiana (Tex-Ark-Ana) tri-state area.  Two to four players roll-and-move around a board decorated with local businesses, answer questions about local history and miscellanea, and collect four colored pegs for their pawn.  The first player to bring a pawn with all four pegs to the center wins!

The Outlaws is a euro-style card game where you get to lead a posse of bandits in the American Wild West. The meanest cowboys and most daring heists are all up for grabs in this fight to be the baddest outlaw in town.

You start by hiring characters to build your posse. Send your gang on jobs by matching their stats to what the job requires and roll some dice to find out if they brought back some loot, earned you some reputation, or got shot in the process. The characters and jobs all come from a communal field, so you'll have to plan ahead and act quickly to get the good stuff. 
 The player with the most reputation will win the game, and rep only comes from doing jobs, so you won't get ahead by playing it safe. You'll have to take chances, and your posse will probably die, but that's just the nature of the game for The Outlaws.

description:
Mariáš (the final sound is pronounced as "sh") is the most popular card game in the Czech Republic and Slovakia, often seen being played in pubs. This trick-taking game is the basis for Ulti, the national card game of Hungary.

It is played with a 32-card deck, similar to the William Tell deck but with specific Bohemian art. There are four suits: hearts, bells, acorns, and leaves, each with eight cards: ace, king, over-knave, under-knave, 10, 9, 8, and 7.

The most common setup is with three players, with one player playing alone against a partnership of the other two for each hand. The lone player seeks to win more card points than the other two players together. Points are received for winning aces and tens in tricks, winning the last trick, and for the combination of king and over-knave of a suit. There are also several bonuses and penalties that affect the final score.

(Description borrowed from Mariáš page on pagat.com)

Learn key facts about the 50 states in the United States of America playing this fun, easy-to-learn card game!

State Master is simple to learn, easy to set up, requires little space, can be played in as little as 10 minutes, and keeps all players engaged throughout as everyone plays in every round (i.e., no waiting your turn!).

You and your kids will learn each state's:


     Abbreviation
     Capital
     Largest City
     Year of Statehood
     Nickname
     Population Rank
     Location on the Map


A BETTER WAY OF LEARNING FACTS
While flash cards are great for committing facts to short-term memory, they don't engage kids for long. Games provide a good alternative, but in most "educational" games, the learning comes slowly as players must wait their turn to get a question and rarely get the same question twice in the same game.

In State Master, all players participate in every turn, so everyone is learning and testing their knowledge constantly. Player who get the wrong answer take a quick moment to study all six facts on the state card and then try to get the same card the next turn, immediately putting their new knowledge to use.

State Master is fun for all ages and its a game in which younger players can often beat older players as they tend to be better at retaining facts.

				
				
					================ HOW TO PLAY
				
				
					================NUMBER OF PLAYERS
2 to 6

OBJECTIVE
Finish the game with the most cards.

COMPONENTS
50 State Cards
6 Colored Dice
1 Category Die
1 Map Card

SETUP
Shuffle State Cards.
Place 5 State Cards on the table with the flag side up for each player in the game: 10 cards if there are 2 players, 15 cards if there are 3 players, etc.
Place the Blank Map face up on the table.
Place the Filled Map face down on the table.
Each player chooses a colored die.

ROUND PLAY
Each player rolls his/her die to decide who will be Player 1 for Round 1.
Player 1 puts his/her die on top of one of the State Cards.
Moving clockwise, each player puts his/her die on of a different State Card.
Player 1 then rolls the Category Die.
Each player, starting with Player 1 and moving clockwise, must then say the correct answer for his/her chosen state for the category shown. For example, if the die says "CAPITAL," a player with his/her die on New York would say "Albany."
If a player correctly answers the category question, that player tries to locate his/her state on the Blank Map.

ENDING A ROUND
Players who answer correctly and are able to locate their states win their cards. All others replace them face down on the table (but not before studying the card a little so they can get the right answer the next round).

WINNING THE GAME
Rounds continue until fewer State Cards remain face down than there are players in the game. The player with the most State Cards at end of the game is the winner.

Frederick Adler is a brilliant strategist and has controlled the Empire's intelligence service , the Hand of the Emperor, since the reign of the last Emperor. Despite being a cold and remorseless monster of a man, his loyalty to the throne is fanatical and unwavering. He wholeheartedly supports the Empress with his extensive network of covert agents.

Dark Empire Prowler — Lv. 45

At 4 | Dm 4 | Df 9 | Ar 2 | LP 13 | Re 10 | Mo 8/12 | AP 3/4

Imperial Resources: Imperial Agents (male and female) may spend an additional 5 levels on upgrades. This doesn't actually increase their cost.

o — Order of Assassination (Subterfuge, Effect, Reactive)
When a friendly Dark unit within Adler's Control Zone makes an attack they roll an additional die keeping the highest result.

o — War Sacrifice (Subterfuge, Effect, Reactive)
A friendly unit within Adler's Control Zone gets +X Attack until the end of the turn. X equals the amount of LP that unit sacrifices for this ability, up to 3 LP.

o — Disposable Agents (Special Effect, Reactive)
This ability may only be used once at the end of the Maintenance Phase. A friendly unit within Adler's Control Zone gets Shield Lv. 1 and Berserker Lv. 1.

ooo — Kortana (Ki, Attack)

+ 2 Damage / -2 Armor. Adler has Critical Mastery for this attack.

description:
Two players confront one another to construct the least high tower with 3D polyominoes.

Both players have a gameboard 3x3. In the center of the table, there are 29 3D polyominoes. Initially, each player chooses a polyomino. When both players have chosen a polyomino, they exchange. Everyone must now place the piece on his board. A polyomino must not exceed the gameboard. Then everyone chooses a new polyomino.

When each player has placed 14 polyominoes, the game is over. The player whose tower is less high wins.

Die verrückte Reise durch Rheinland-Pfalz has two modes of play. In the first mode, players assume the roles of reporters who are trying to get an exclusive scoop of UFO sightings in  Rheinland-Pfalz, a federal state of Germany.

Each player is assigned a color and a transmission tower. Their goal is to transport photographs with a hot-air balloon  at locations in the state to their towers (transmitting the scoop back to their news agencies). Each player starts with one photo card, and take turns to roll a die, moving their card by the die's result. They draw new photo cards on a "6". The reporters can deploy Stop Stones to stop other reporters from moving through. The first reporter to transmit back three photographs wins the game.

The second mode of the game is called Top Secret, which is played on the other side of the game board. The game takes place in the news agency and players are racing against time to dig out what the others know. Each player thinks up a secret word at the start. On their turn, they roll a die, move their marker by the result, and can ask another player for one letter of their word. Players get points for guessing another player's secret word. The game ends when one player's marker reaches the end point and the winner is the player who has the highest score.

A high speed death race against the clock. Drivers have a limited time to out wit, out manoeuvre and out pace the competition. Their fission powered Skimmers speed along future highways ramming each other and firing dangerous energy weapons to try and secure a win in the controversial Light-Speed Cup. When the race starts the clock starts and players have until the time runs out to wipe their opponents off of the track. They can aggressively pursue the other racers and try to destroy them, they can speed ahead and try to force the other vehicle off of the end of track or they can duck and dodge allowing the other Skimmers to tear each other apart before speeding home to victory. Which ever player’s Skimmer is in the best condition when the clock reaches zero wins the Cup. This game is a thrill packed adrenaline ride where mayhem reigns and there is never a dull moment!The LSC moto is Action Not Protraction!

This games puts 2-6 players in control of one Skimmer (futuristic race car) each. They race each other along an never ending  treadmil like race track that is made out of 4 rolling tiles. When the race starts an electronic timer is started, it has between 10 and 15 minutes on it per player, and when the timer runs out and the buzzer souds the game is over. Durring the game players have to ram and shoot at each other to try and destroy the enemy skimmers before the timer runs out. Each skimmer has different strengths and weaknesses and players get to choose their own drivers which also have unique talents. There is another way of destroy other skimmers and that is to get so far ahead of them that when the track is rolled they fall off of the back of it. In this way the lightly amoiured fast skimmers can win the game aswell.

A player press the locomotive's chimney, and the train rolls along the track. When the train comes to the the first station, the player pushes the chimney and thus stops the train.

The player takes any passenger from the station who he thinks has the same color as his station. He shows the other players what color the passenger has under their "feet".

If the color matches the station, the player places the passenger at his station and then starts the train by pressing the chimney.

If the color does not match, the player puts the passenger back on the train, after showing the other players what color it is, and starts the train.

The train moves to the next station and the next player will have a turn.

The winner is the player who has collected the three passengers in the same color as his station.

A microgame of tricks and treasure.

The goal is to find the map leading to buried treasure.  And someone on this pirate ship has the map!  To win, find that map and hold on to it, surviving the round - or be the last pirate alive with the lowest ranking card.

Rules:

• Each player is dealt two cards.

• Players can choose to use one or two cards against their opponents, discard and draw a new card, or bluff.

• A player can bluff as long as they have at least one card of the card type needed to perform an action in their 
hand.

Example: Danielle has one “Barrel o’ Grog” card and one “Cannon” card in her hand. She decides that she wants to use the 
special ability that requires two “Barrel o’ Grog” cards. This special requires that every player with a “Barrel o’ Grog” 
card must place the grog card face up in front of them.  That card is out of play for the rest of the game.  Those players now may only have one card in their hand.

• If a player does not believe that another player has the cards to do the action they just performed, they may call 
the player’s bluff.  If the first player was lying, they have to reveal the card that was the lie, and they discard their remaining card and draw a new one.

Example: John doesn’t believe Danielle has two “Barrel o’ Grog” cards. He calls her bluff. As Danielle did not have 
two “Barrel o’ Grog” cards, she places the “Cannon” card in front of her, as it was the lie - and that card is now 
out of play. She then discards her “Barrel o’ Grog” and draws a new card.  She is now down to having only one card in her hand.

• When the deck runs out of cards, the discard pile may be shuffled back in to form the draw pile.

• When there are no more cards to be drawn and no discard pile, the players that are still alive reveal their hands and what is stored in their “Smuggler's Chambers”.  The player with the lowest numbered card wins.
