boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Brother Bastian
description:
Brother Bastian was a member of a severe sect devoted to Korash, the god of justice and light. When he learned of an orc masquerading as a paladin of his god, he was furious. But upon meeting her, he realized that this orc was no imposter and that there was more to Korash than he had been taught.


    The Good: Brother Bastian knows many powerful prayers and knows exactly when to use each one.



    The Bad: Unfortunately, because he knows so many prayers, you never know what he’s planning next.


The Red Dragon Inn: Allies - Brother Bastian expands the party at the Red Dragon Inn with a brand new character! Bastian brings a powerful prayer cards with him, giving him more flexibility and secrets than you’d expect from a humble servant of Korash. Making sacrifices throughout the game so you can use your prayers at the perfect moment will lead you to victory!

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies is a series of expansions for any standalone The Red Dragon Inn game. Each Allies set includes a single Character Deck, plus components to add a brand new character to your game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Challenge and Persuade is a game of argument!

In this game, players develop arguments and challenge each others’ ideas. The game cards provide easy-to-read facts for any level player to construct an argument around topics at the nexus of energy, water, and climate. An adjective card (e.g. Lasting, Sustainable, Lucrative) is drawn each round that the players must use in conjunction with their fact cards to construct an argument. A Judge decides the winning argument using his own criteria. The player with the winning argument wins the round, and the first player to win three rounds wins the game!

Challenge and Persuade is a fun and social card game that challenges players to think about the choices we have in balancing our need for water and energy with the challenge of minimizing climate change.

Smart!
Through play, this game teaches fundamental knowledge about our environment, challenges players’ understanding of facts, and guides players to construct solid and convincing arguments. Challenge and Persuade teaches concepts and strengthens communication strategies required to engage in scientific discussions concerning our world today. Developed with a team of experienced scientists, gamers, and educators, Challenge and Persuade is aligned to national science standards and may be used in a variety of science, government and policy courses.

Fun!
This game, developed with teenagers and adults in mind, is engaging and stimulating for a wide range of ages. The game can quickly turn into a lively discussion involving current topics on water, energy, and climate.

Social!
Challenge and Persuade is an intensely interactive game in which players either act as Presenter or Judge. The presenters draw fact cards and construct convincing arguments to present to the judge. While the judge decides which argument is strongest, any player may challenge an argument!

As you travel through the game, you'll learn about THE NEW SECRET CITY. The object of the game is to keep on playing until you can draw your very own completed version of THE NEW SECRET CITY ADVENTURES mural! Are you ready! Here we go!"

Space Frontier is a game of space exploration for 2-4 players. Players compete to spread their influence across a region of uncharted space.  The game takes roughly an hour to an hour and a half to play with 2 players with each additional player adding about a half hour.

Each player controls a command ship and its supporting fleets. The command ship is a mobile factory, research lab, and ship carrier. Players will use it to construct the fleets that help spread their influence far and wide.

The modular space map is built from randomized tiles each game as players explore. The random map generation ensures no two games of Space Frontier are exactly the same.

Players gain influence through missions, research, trade, or conquest. Players can be as cooperative or antagonistic with each other as they wish. Thus, games can be played and won where military might reigns supreme, or without a shot being fired.

The game has 2 decks of cards: Fast Buck Cards (FB) and Market Value Cards (MV). Each player is dealt 7 FB cards, and a FB card is dealt face down in the middle of the table. This is the bonus card.

Players may draw 1 FB card per turn from the deck or the discard pile. They then check their FB cards for 3 of a kind and place them face down on the table. The player then draws a MV card, and follows what it says. It can either affect your hand's value or have you draw another FB or MV card.

When a player has no more FB cards in hand, that player gets a $150,000 bonus and the bonus card is revealed. If a player has a card that matches the bonus in hand, that player receives a $250,000 bonus. The other cards on the table are then scored. Play continues until someone reaches $1,000,000.

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Adventures Under Turtleopolis
description:
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Adventures Under Turtleopolis, is graphically based on the late 80's cartoon that originated from the comic book by Kevin Eastman and Peter Laird.

2 to 4 players play as one of the four Turtles competing around the board while trying to avoid traps and bad luck spaces. The object of the game is to reach the end of each game board, which supposedly represent a different mission:

Game 1: Rescue April
Game 2: Take Over The Technodrome
Game 3: Battle to Stop the Ooze
Game 4: Race for the Pizza Parlor

These four boards came on the back of the Ralston Cereal Boxes and as such are in conjunction a very simplistic version of similar games like "Snakes and Ladders". Just throw the dice and let luck decide in which space you´ll end up. Some spaces have rewards while others punishments. In reality is what you can expect from a board game that came on a cereal box, but it has a lot of charm and richness of details in the drawings and graphic design.

There was another Ralston´s TMNT cereal box board game called "Adventures In Turtleopolis" that came out during the same time, but it didn´t have art as nice as this nor as much TMNT thematic enemies and elements such as the Technodrome or the sewers.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Objective: collect as many animals cards (pairs) as possible, bringing them back home & gaining bonus chips.
 - acting by turns -

Roll the die and move forward on a circle; flip appropiate animal card; finding a matching pair you may bring them gome gaining a bonus chip; a dragon will let you fly, a rat will turn over all already flipped cards.

Game ends after all animals are brought home.

In Penguin Smash, players are a group of penguins that can not take the cold. To stay warm in the south pole, they figure out that they can just smash each other to keep body temperature high! (What a genius penguin!)

Penguin Smash is a dexterity game in which players will put one hand on the table and take turns flipping one card from his/her own deck of penguin cards by the other hand. If the total number of the revealed penguin cards matches the corresponding penguin player's status card, everyone must race to SMASH that player's hand on the table. The first player to smash the penguin player's hand scores all the corresponding penguin cards. The player who manages to SMASH the most penguin will be the master of penguin smash and win the game.

Be alert! Some special cards will put your penguin into sleep or sent it to dinner, and therefore making the SMASH not easy as you may imagine.

Wiz-War: Bestial Forces, the second expansion for Wiz-War, introduces three new schools of magic — Mythology, Totem, and Draconic — and six creatures storming the dungeon.

Mythology school introduces summons for creatures and powerful new items known as relics, while Draconic school focuses mostly on spells altering creature or wizard attacks. Finally, the Totem school allows different effects to be triggered by placing totems on the board: increasing the energy of spells, slowing your opponents, blocking damage, or increasing your line of sight are all possible boosts due to totem deployment.

description: What the Food?! Add on – bonus player cards mixes the targeting cards “awesome sauce” and “frosting” into the mix and includes all of the action cards needed to add a 9th and 10th player to your games, pumping up the size of your tabletop food fight!

What the Food?! add-on “O Canada” bonus cards (the unique combo of Maple syrup and Poutine) are fun Kickstarter extras that came up during our campaign. Someone asked in the comments if we had maple syrup,  and I was shocked that we didn’t  - yet. So we added this food and topping in honor of our Canadian artist and team members on the project.

A game for 2-players.

Materials

4 pawns, 2 black and 2 white.

49 Othello like tiles, black on one side and white on the other.

Definitions

Claim - An orthogonally adjacent group of friendly tiles.
Adjacent – adjacent from this point on will be considered orthogonally adjacent.

Goal

Win by capturing all of your opponent’s pawns or by having the largest claim.

Setup

Lay out the 49 tiles into an 7x7 black and white checkerboard pattern.

Then alternately starting with black, place a pawns onto an empty friendly color tile.

Rules

On a turn you must take one of the following actions.

1) Select an empty enemy colored tile that is in a straight line (horizontally or diagonally) with one of your friendly pawns, then flip this tile over and move the friendly pawn placing it on top of the tile just flipped or

2) Capture an enemy pawn by approach, this is done by moving a friendly pawn to an empty enemy tile (this tile must also be flipped over before completing the capture) that is adjacent to an enemy pawn, provided that the enemy pawn is on the continuation of the capturing pawns's movement line (which could be horizontally or diagonally) or

3) Move a friendly pawn freely within a claim that is occupied by that pawn.

The game ends when one of the following occurs.

1. When all enemy pawns are captured or

2. When players are unable of following either rules #1 or #2 after 2 consecutive turns.

Finally add up the tiles in your claims, the player with the largest claim or the player that managed to capture all the enemy pawns is the winner. If by chance there is a tie, look at each player’s second largest claim and so forth.

Restrictions

Unless performing a capture, you can't flip a tile of an opponent's claim if that claim is occupied by an opponent's piece.

O que seria da vida sem alguém ao nosso lado? Amizades que podem virar paixão e por que não amor?
 Em marry me?! Os jogadores são pessoas em busca de sua cara metade, com gostos variados, que ao longo dos anos passaram por vários relacionamentos, terão recaídas com os “ex”, para que no final possam dizer em alto e bom som “–Marry me?” (-casa comigo?) ou não 
(a vida não é perfeita).
Do que você gosta? 
 Pessoas mais velhas? Pessoas jovens? Loiras(o)? Morenas(o)? , independente do seu gosto pessoal, no fim o que vai realmente importar é ser feliz.

English translation:
What would life be without someone on our side? Friendships that can turn to passion and, why not, love?

In marry me?! Players are people searching for their soul mate, with varying tastes. Over the years, they may have gone through several relationships, and may relapse with an "ex", so in the end they can say loud and clear "Marry me?" (House me?) or not 
(Life is not perfect).

What do you like?

Regardless of your personal taste in the end what will really matter in this game is to be happy.

The music edition of Konito?. Complete the fill-in-the-blank statements on the game cards in order to move forward on the modular scoring board. (The length of the game is determined by how many sections are used to create the board.) There is a time limit to complete each card.

The object is to be the first to complete famous song titles and lyrics, or try to make your teammates find the right answer by any possible means.

Kłuszyn 1610 covers the battle between Polish and Russo-Swedish forces during the Polish-Russian war 1609-1618. It was one of the most glorious victories of polish heavy cavalry Husaria, where 5600 polish Husars defeated 30 000 soldiers of Sweden and Russia.

This is a typical hex and counter wargame and first of the planned series of wargames taking place in XVI-XVII century.

A science fiction game published in White Dwarf (Issue 21 - Oct 1980), and was later released together with The Barbarian (a game published in a previous issue a year earlier) as one of their pocket games in Survival / The Barbarian.

Survival is a science fiction game depicting the struggle of Jardine, a crashed Scout, to reach safety. Along the trek he will meet and fight various vicious animals. While Survival is an excellent solitaire game, there are scenarios for up to six players.

Truth is a strategic Pattern making game of Divine proportions; featuring 270 graphic Scripture cards, with a Bible verse on each card!

Become Champion of Truth by receiving Blessings, Rewards, and Victory Points, from creating various types of Patterns.

Draw and Play cards each turn. Thwart your peers from making patterns, while building your own. Making certain patterns give various special bonuses to your efforts.

There are 3 ways to win the game:
1. Collect 10 Victory Points
2. Achieve the Creation Pattern (6 Scriptures)
3. Achieve the Atonement Pattern (10 Spirits)

The first to meet one of the above Victory Conditions is proclaimed Champion of Truth!

Le Truc le + ("What's the most?") is "the most" fast and fun party game.

Each round, players draw a "most" card to determine what they will be voting for: "The most hilarious", "The most free", "The most incredible", "The most sexy", etc. Simultaneously, they then use their Vote cards to vote for which of the five Thing cards they believe is "the most" for that category. Each player who voted with the majority of players discards a "solo" point. The winning Thing card (or cards) is discarded and replaced by a new one, then a new round begins.

The first player to get rid of all ten of her solo points wins!

description:
Immunity is a board game in which your immune system will fight against the pathogens agents that attempt to infect your body. Become the healthiest player of the table, and live all your life free of harmful diseases! Take the control of virus, bacteria and parasites, and strike the vital organs of your rivals! The game emulates in a proper way some aspects of the immunity, like the inflammation, adverse effects of medicaments, the disease transmission, the spread of tumors, and many others.

In this card game, each player receives an organism board which shows the health of your five vital systems (nervous, cardiorespiratory, digestive, hepatic, and renal), as well as your age throughout the life.  There are also two environment squares that will be occupied with the pathogens before they invade your organism. In addition, the board shows different human cells (red blood cells, immune cells, platelet, etc) that can be bought with oxygen. These cells are responsible for eliminating pathogens.

There are 3 types of deck cards in the game. Pathogen cards can be placed on the environment squares of the rivals, in order to infect and harm their organs. Medic cards can be used to prevent damage, transplant organs, eradicate microorganisms, etc. There is a common deck for all the players, the environmental deck, which represents hazardous effects that can affect the health of a person, like occupational accidents, blood donation and school fights.

Here comes the CAT FASHION SHOW! Dress your cats up and have them show off your fascinating costume design on the catwalk stage! Whoever earns the greatest fame after three fashion show seasons will be crowned the Master of Catwalk. But be alert! Don't let those copycats get all the fame out of cloning your masterpiece!

In Copy Catwalk, players earn fame points by carefully playing their cat cards (number 0-10 plus three Copycat). Cat cards with higher number (more gorgeous costumes) win you some bonus, but only if no one play the same number as yours. Playing Copycat cards grants you no fame points, but they give you chances to copy other players scored costumes in the end of every season.

Manage your cat cards carefully, predict your competitors' next move wisely, wish for the best luck using your Copycat, and you shall become the Master of Copy Catwalk!

Explore The World is a board game journey that takes players all around the man-made world vs. the natural world.

The goal is to be the first player to make a round trip back to his/her start square.  Players advance around the board by correctly answering from the Question Cards or by following the directions on the Travel Cards.

The type of card used on a turn is determined by the flick of a spinner.  Play continues clockwise around the table, with each player taking only one turn at a time.

A stand-alone game that integrates with Rock Science or Rock Science: Festival.

320 new questions to Rock Science. Of Bandit Rock (Swedish Radio Station). In Swedish. Boost your original game or play instantly with the included special rules.

Challenge your mates and see who is the most rock for real! Three difficulty levels and smart betting allows the beginner to  give the uber nerd a fair fight. Perfect for pre-party or a hard-packed evening.

Meet the heaviest rock stars, remember the songs and hear the wildest stories.


     320 new questions about the myths, rock stars, songs and albums. 
     Play directly with the supplied Booster Pack Rules. 
     Bandits host reveals juicy details about rock stars ... and themselves!

Rive is a multi-faction strategic game where the contents of your customizable deck (units, actions & battlefield manipulation cards) interact with a hex-based game board.

Designed for 2-4 players, and taking about an hour to play, Rive blends together familiar game mechanics to create a combat focused, highly replayable, deeply strategic and unique gameplay experience.

Attempt to destroy your opponent’s fortress before they destroy yours by playing a variety of different units, actions and battlefield manipulation cards from your hand. Each unit comes with a corresponding hex tile that is placed strategically on the battlefield. Utilize special powers unique to your faction and to your units to give you an edge on the battlefield all while taking advantage of dynamic game board features to block or trap other units.

description:
A simplified reimplementation of Ali Baba. Gameplay is similar, but the the push-your-luck aspect is missing, and there's only six dials (rather than 9), and twelve treasure cards (rather than 29).

Players turn the dials, trying to match the pattern for the round. When they succeed, they take one treasure card, which at the same time will show the pattern for the next round. Once all treasure cards have been taken, the game ends, and the player with the most points wins.


Unlock the treasure chest.

The missing treasure chest form Ali Baba has been found! You have to turn the locks into the right position. But only the one who turns the last lock in the right way, may open the chest.

On a turn a player may turn one lock 1 or 2 images to the left or to the right. When the desired image becomes visible, the lock stays open and the player may attempt to open another lock. If another image shows than the desired image, the next player is on turn.

You always have to try to get at least one lock right. You are allowed to stop at any time after that. Locks who show the desired image are not allowed to be turned anymore.

The aim of the game is to turn the locks so each lock shows the same picture. The person who manages to turn the last (6th) lock may open the treasure chest and get a treasure out. That treasure is the next image that has to be shown on all locks. The player left from the one who found the treasure starts the next treasure search.

When all treasures have been gotten out, all points on the treasure cards are counted. The player with the most points wins. In case of a tie, the player with the most treasures wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves
Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Cards

There are twelve icons, each with a different point value. Each six-sided card has either two different sets of three identical icons (ie: 3 Fire icons and 3 Beaker icons), or has one set of identical icons (ie: 6 Beaker icons), known as a hextuple. (Hextuple? We made up this word. Think of a "double" in dominoes.) The value of each card is the sum of the values of each icon and is displayed in the center of each card.

Hextuples

A hextuple is a card where all the icons are the same. There is a hextuple for every icon. Every round of the game is started by placing a hextuple in the center of the playing surface. You can play just one round, or you can play multiple rounds, starting with a different hextuple each time. Choose it before distributing cards to the players.

If you play a hextuple during your turn, you can play another card during your turn to match it.

Scoring

During your turn, you play one card from your inventory by matching icons with one or more cards already played. Your score is the value of your card plus the value of the cards you matched.

Replenish

You must always have ten cards in your inventory. You can keep your inventory in your hand or just place them in front of you on the playing surface. After you play a card, you replenish your inventory by drawing a card from the pile. If you play a hextuple and are able to match it with another card from your inventory, you draw two cards.

Jokers

Jokers are wild. They can match any icon and each of the three Joker hextuples can be used to match six cards at once. They have no point value when played, but make sure you play them, because their value switches to 100 points and count against your score if left in your inventory.

Ending Rounds

Keep playing until all the cards in the pile are drawn. Then play until someone has played all their cards or until no other play can be made. Each player then adds up the card values left in their inventory and subtracts the sum from their score.

Ending Play

If you play multiple rounds, you can add the scores from each round to determine the winner. Or, you can treat each round separately and award a point to the player with the highest score for each round, and the winner is the player who won the most rounds.

boardgame: Empire: The Game of New York
description:
Empire is a battle game, similar to Risk in game play, set on a map of New York City. The map has NYC (and neighboring Jersey) separated into 6 boroughs, distinguished by color palette: Manhattan in red, The Bronx in orange, Staten Island in yellow, Queens in green, Brooklyn in blue, and Jersey in purple. Each borough contains neighborhoods. These are actual neighborhoods, but the size and boundary are exaggerated in this map for game play. Neighborhoods attached by bridges, tram or subway are also considered adjacent.

The objective is to occupy all neighborhoods on the map with your player cubes by systematically booting out neighboring players by rolling higher dice values than the other players.

Grave Digger is a push-your-luck game where each player acts as one of four Grave Diggers in a race to beat a curse that causes the dead to rise.

Players take turns rolling 3 dice at a time, claiming spots on a 3x3 grid of playing cards by placing the dice on matching cards. Face cards have a value of 10, and Aces can be 1 or 11. A dice roll of double 6 can be placed anywhere.

A player can stop rolling any time, and collect any claimed cards, or must stop when no dice can be placed, losing any claimed cards.

The game ends when a player has collected 10 cards. Each other player gets one more turn. The player who has collected the most cards wins the game.

(from the coverback)
English (google translation)
Live a thrilling final as never Newteam SC between  and FC Muppet!
Now you can enjoy the strength of football in this board game as simple as exciting.
Deploy your strategy, use your cards and you will get amazing goals.
When all your dreams are at stake and all your team depends on you, your passion for the game will take you to victory.
Who will win? Does the tactical skill of Oliver Atom or aggression of Mark Lenders?
Fight to the last breath!
(Based in Captain Tsubasa cartoons)



No official:This game is a faithful simulation of a soccer match cartoon of the Captain Tsubasa serie.

The objective of the game is that when you finish the game, your team has scored more goals than the opposite.
Before the game start there is the choosing to play 45 minutes or 90.

The game simulates a soccer match through a square board by both player's chips from each team as the ball moves. In turn, the player controls the team through a deck of team cards where describe the skills of each player and 3 cards of injuries on opposing players (21 total cards each player).

Player's chips can injure and even have warnings by yellow and red cards.

An interesting engine ball movement from a 6-sided die on the inside of the lid of the box with directions drawn on a grid 3x3 solves the movement of the ball where a tie in points between the  player's chips.

Este juego es una fiel simulación de un partido de fútbol de serie de dibujos animados de Campeones.

El objetivo del juego es que cuando acabe el partido, tu equipo haya marcado más goles que el contrario.
Previamente al juego hay que escoger jugar a 45 minutos o a 90.

El juego simula un partido de fútbol, mediante un tablero cuadriculado por el que se mueven tanto las fichas de jugadores de cada equipo como el balón. A su vez, el jugador que controla el equipo juega con un mazo de cartas de equipo donde se describen las habilidades de cada jugador así como 3 cartas de lesiones sobre jugadores del equipo contrario (21 cartas en total cada jugador).

Los jugadores se pueden lesionar e incluso ser amonestados por tarjetas amarillas y rojas.

Un interesante motor de movimiento del balón a partir de un dado de 6 caras sobre la cara interior de la tapa de la caja con direcciones dibujadas en una cuadrícula 3x3 resuelve el movimiento de el esférico en los casos que haya empate de puntos entre las fichas de jugadores.

description:
A children's game in which the two players each flip pennies, or some other coin, at the same time.

The players take turns calling "odd" or "even", then both players reveal their coin simultaneously.

Heads/Tails or Tails/Heads is odd

Heads/Heads or Tails/Tails is even.

This isn't much of a game, and there are ways to cheat!! But if done fairly it is a perfect example of an "Dice Rolling". The coins are randomizers. d2 or d6 doesn't matter!

So the game would play just the same if the two players rolled d6 dice and noted if the sum of the dice was "Odd or Even." Since this is a gambling game too, the players would have to each bet a stake of 1 penny.

Objective: Be the first reaching the top of a palm tree with all 3 of your apes
 - moving by turns -

A palm tree is built up (trunk acts as a board).

Each player tries to climb up to the top (palm branches) with his 3 apes by rolling 2 dice. Obtaining a double a chimpanzee is moved instead - kicking down all apes he's passing on the trunk (up or down).

Note: translation of the title 'I'm deloused by an ape' with the meaning: getting surprised

Objective: Be the first reaching space 80 precisely
 - moving by turns -
Simple 'Roll & Move' game; use two dice until space 50 is passed. Ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid.

Note:
Due to breeches (seen on the board) game should had been published later than 1936, likely also later than 1938 (Klee take over) although there is no 'Christian Herbart clover' logo. Theme is glorified by Nazi propaganda.

Objective: be the first reaching space 100; learn more about chocolate 
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll & Move' game, describing the process of getting raw materials for chocolate production; ending on marked space see instructions; some spaces require usage of a letter die.

Note:
Game was delivered with 250 gr. chocolates inside.

Taggle d'amour is an expansion to Taggle that may be played standalone.

Taggle d'amour adds 35 'Thoughts' and 20 'Retort' Love-themed cards to the classic Taggle game.

As in Taggle, each player draws a 'Retort' card. Each turn, a new player reads out loud one of the 'Thoughts' lines and the other players, one after another, must use one of their 'Retort' lines to snap back the silliest or funniest retort. The player who read the "Thoughts" card then gives a "Taggle" counter to the player who provided the lamest retort.

Once a player has accumulated 5 "Taggle" counters, he is declared loser.

Objective: Be the first reaching space 69; learn traffic rules/signs
 - moving by turns -

Each player moves forward according die roll. Ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid.

Objective: Learn numbers between 1 and 24
 - acting by turns -

Flip 24 tiles upside down (symbols or numbers) - other corresponding 24 matching tiles stay visible.

Randomly draw one one of the flipped tiles and find within a given time the matching one; the 'puzzle' concept let you prove if right or wrong.

Game ends after all tiles are merged. Player with most matching pairs is the winner.

ZnajZnak: Monte Cassino is an educational game for two players thanks to which memorizing facts about one of the most important battles of World War II comes incredibly easy. You learn while having a great time playing it. Players get to know selected soldiers, unit badges, weapons used in the battle and most important dates. The 133 symbols placed on the game cards were carefully selected to show the Battle of Monte Cassino in a wide international perspective emphasizing the heroic efforts of Polish as well as the allied forces. The manual and game cards are bilingual Polish-English.

ZnajZnak: Monte Cassino includes 10 cards on each of which 12 different symbols have been placed. Every two cards have a common symbol. Your job is to find and name it as fast as you can. If you don’t recognize your symbol flip your card and read the information on the other side. Then place a new card on the table and go on with the game. If you manage to name your symbol you should give your card to the other player. The first player to get rid of all his cards wins.

ZnajZnak: Monte Cassino is available in 13 collector’s versions labeled with letters from A to N. Every box is a complete game set. You can mix card sets because they always match.

If you don’t recognize your symbol flip your card and read the information on the other side. Then place a new card on the table and go on with the game. If you manage to name your symbol you should give your card to the other player. The first player to get rid of all his cards wins.

ZnajZnak: Monte Cassino is available in 13 collector’s versions labeled with letters from A to N. Every box is a complete game set. You can mix card sets because they always match.

ZnajZnak: Monte Cassino is an educational game for two players thanks to which memorizing facts about one of the most important battles of World War II comes incredibly easy. You learn while having a great time playing it. Players get to know selected soldiers, unit badges, weapons used in the battle and most important dates. The 133 symbols placed on the game cards were carefully selected to show the Battle of Monte Cassino in a wide international perspective emphasizing the heroic efforts of Polish as well as the allied forces. The manual and game cards are bilingual Polish-English.

The players travel the Sol-System (between Earth, Luna, Venus and Mars) and try to accquire Property Cards with allow them to build space stations, rockets, landing crafts or atom reactors. The players have to pay a fee, if they land on a space that belongs to another player, even more it that player has already build something on this space or if the player has several properties on this planet. The last payer who has any money left or the player who has the most money after an agreed upon playing time, wins the game.

Objective: Be the first reaching space 60 (Nimes)
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll & Move' game; starting from Marseille each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a black marked space follow instructions.

My Happy Farm is a game in which players strive to become the best farmer.

All players have their own farm with four nice animals. They feed those cuties. Animals which are fed well fill their stomachs with tasty food, grow and become longer and longer, even reaching unnatural lengths. The longer your animal, the better a farmer you are. The owner of the longest animal receives bonus points. Animals which aren't fed even one time lose their weight, take offense, and leave their bad master. Moreover, they will take some victory points from the bad master to punish him, so players have to be good and careful farmers and feed their animals well to create Their Own Happy Farm.

This is a new edition with new art and the box is already contains the expansion Gazda.

boardgame: Wilki i Owce (zawiera dodatek Grube Ryby)
description:
Each player tries to make the largest pens with his own sheep inside by placing his own tiles. You can easily close small pens, or you can be ambitious and try to build large farms... but if you cannot enclose the fields, your sheep are likely to be eaten by the big bad wolves roaming in the nearby forests. Each player's animal is secret at the beginning of the game, but the soonest you reveal it, the more points you get.

This edition includes the expansion.

From the rules:

Asteroil is the game of space mining. Into the vast reaches of the solar system two players maneuver the space fleets of competing corporations as they vie for the immeasurable wealth contained in the asteroids. Repulse cruisers hyperspace in and out to gain position while the space refineries close in fast. The company that meets its quota first gets the jump on the market to control the world's supply of platinum, titanium and gold.

Description:

Asteroil is an abstract strategy game played on a 12x12 square grid. Each player controls a space refinery and four repulse cruisers. A turn consists of moving a piece one space in any direction, jumping a cruiser in or out of hyperspace (off the board; jumping in allows the cruiser to go to any space), or firing a cruiser's repulsion beam at an asteroid. The beam causes the asteroid to move away from the cruiser; ideally the player's space refinery is on the other side of the asteroid, allowing it to be captured. The player who manages to capture three of the six asteroids wins the game.

From the rules:

Pyramad is the game of pyramid power. The cosmic forces of intuition and chance meet head on with the powers of observation and logic in this multilevel game for two players. Like the Pharaohs of ancient Egypt, each players seeks to build and possess a pyramid by stacking cards drawn from the special 92-card deck. Until the last card is played, you will be hanging on the edge of your throne.

Description:

Pyramad is played on a 6x6 square grid using 92 square cards. Each card shows a pattern of four squares colored red, blue, or white; red and blue are the players' colors and white is neutral.

On a turn a player either draws a card or plays a card (playing is required if a player has five cards in hand). A card can be played on an empty space or on the intersection of four cards, but the latter play can only be made if the intersection has fewer than three squares of the opponent's color.

The object is to either place the single card on the sixth level of the pyramid or make it impossible for your opponent to play.

From the rules:

Alphabid is the game of crosswords. Using identical sets of color-coded letters, two players match vocabularies by drawing, bidding and playing, crossword style, as many letters per word as possible. As the grid fills up, the players may begin to risk their turns in an effort to make longer words than the opponent. The final outcome remains undetermined until a player has drawn the last letter and the opponent cannot catch up.

Description:

Alphabid is played on an 18x18 square grid. As indicated by the description in the rules, each player has an identical set of tiles. However, each starts off with a random selection of eight of these. After the first turn, a player must start their turn by "bidding" the number of tiles they think they can play; this entitles them to draw that many tiles, after which they can raise their bid in order to draw more tiles. After they have bid as high as they want (or they have eight tiles, which is the maximum) they must play at least as many letters as they bid. If a player plays eight tiles on a turn they draw eight more without having to bid.

Play follows standard Scrabble rules: words are played left-to-right or top-to-bottom and all orthogonally adjacent letters must form words.

Plays do not score points. Instead, the object is to draw all of one's tiles. If both players manage to do this on the same turn, the player who has the fewest tiles left wins.

The players may start from any space and use any route they want. They have to pass all 10 numbered spaces to win the game. The movement is determined by a die. If a player doesn't obey a traffic rule, he gets a penality point. Before passing a traffic light, the player has to roll the color die. Only on a roll of "green" he may pass the crossroads. If he rolls "red" he has to wait a turn and try again. The first player who finishes the game with the least penality points, wins the game.

Great Battles of Small Units

The system is representing military actions of small units, like a regiment or a brigade. Very frequently such  actions  in which relatively small  units  fought  on  both sides were important because of operational or moral context. Thanks to versatile rules it is possible to depict any military action from XX century.

Primosole Bridge is a wargame that uses the Great Battles of Small Units ruleset. Battle for Primosole Bridge was divided into two scenarios. In first, British paratroopers have to defend the bridge from the counterattacking German units. At the very beginning, staying true to problems that landing Allied units faced, players have to resolve what forces are available to Aliied side. In game there are units, that historically didn`t reach the battlefield. It is possible therefore to examine the possible outcome if air drop would have been more successful. In second scenario the British are a stronger side. Yet it doesn`t mean easy victory for them. First, they have not only to occupy the bridge but also the road leading to Catania. Germans occupy favorable terrain and their defense line is well prepared. Germans can also attempt to destroy the bridge and stop the attackers.

Game has tried-and-true hex and counter mechanic but with an added twist: random element is handled in this game by a deck of regular cards (which players have to supply themselves) - like combat and random events.

Game also has also ruleset that is relatively easy to grasp. It can therefore serve as an introduction to the board wargaming hobby
family: Series: Great battles of Small Units (Strategemata)

boardgame: Natur in Gefahr: Unsere bedrohte Tierwelt
description:
The players have to get from the starting space on the main track around the board till they reach the starting space again. At some points they have to leave the main track and use the side trails. If a player reaches a numbered space he has to answer a question. If he gives a wrong answer, he has to go back to the last red space and use the next available side trail. If he gives a wrong answer on a side trail, he has to wait a turn before he may move again. When a player gets on an occupied space, he kicks the other player back to the last red space. The first player who is back at the starting space wins the game.

This game was published in the early 1970s.

The goal of the game is to win the motorcycle race.  At the x and o spaces the players advance to the next x or o space and may change the track while doing so. On spaces marked with a letter, the players have to follow the special instructions in the game rules.

There are two different races to choose from:
Speedway: Team race with 4 runs over 4 laps each.
Sandbahnrennen: single race with 1 run over 8 laps.

Objective: be the first reaching space 60; increase patriotism - learn more about German history/cities
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll & Move' game; buy in first; starting in Berlin each player moves forward according die roll. Ending on a marked space follow instructions.

Note:
Likely published early 1930s

Maleficium is a competitive fantasy game for 2-6 players, with no player elimination. Players assume the role of one of twelve Magi, each with a unique set of abilities and who practice one of six specialised schools of magic. Players may develop their magi's powers throughout the game.

Spell-casting is done through the combination of a spell vocabulary to create different effects. For balance, effects are weighted, and action points are used.

A distribution of negative points, rather than the pursuit of positive points, is intended to encourage closer games. There is a set-collection sub-game for players who get loaded up on negative points early where they can access special abilities.  There is also a trading mechanic and simultaneous monster combat.

Owocowy Wyścig (which means "Fruit Race") is a part of Egmont Polska game series: "Dobra gra w dobrej cenie".

In the game the players try to gather as many fruits as they can. Each player is given a tile with a basket depictured which is a pawn. The pawns are moved on a triangle shaped board which contains of 45 hex tiles with fruits on them.

On a single turn a player takes his pawn and moves it by a number of hexes indicated on the tile he starts from. The player can turn freely but cannot move through the same hex twice. After the movement the player takes the tile he started from and put it in his/her personal supply. There is one restriction in taking tiles. A player cannot take a tile if this would cause the board to split up. In this case the player moves without taking a tile.

If in 4 consecutive turns none of the players takes a tile, the game ends. Then the players compare their fruits. Each fruit is worth one point, but a player will take the points only if he is one of the two players (one in a 2-player game) that has the biggest amount of this particular fruit. Other players do not score points for this kind of fruit. The player who scored the most points is the winner.
family: Series: Dobra gra w dobrej cenie (Egmont Polska)

description:
Notorious, immoral, murderous beings, with no interest beyond extending their own sovereignty by the exploitation of others.


    Yes, that’s you; an Arklord.


Your sole objective is to capitalize upon indigenous species by enthralling the strongest to become your enslaved warriors. To unleash havoc upon their planet, and subdue all that oppose you without mercy.


    My, what a despicable being you are!


No one can stop you. The natural order beckons at your every command.
But hold!  This cannot be!  Some insignificant others dare to interfere with your indomitable conquests! None are fearless against your supreme power!
Unless . . . yes, it has begun.
Now we shall see who is the greatest amongst opposing Arklords!


    Uh oh, company is coming.


MENAGERIE: War of the Arklords is a quick playing “4X” card game (explore, expand, exploit, and exterminate) depicting the conflict between 2 to 4 Arklords (players) who gather and subjugate creatures, amassing powerful armies to reign over the lands to directly or indirectly defeat the opposing Arklords.

The play area is created from a random pattern of concealed region cards with the size and shape of the area dependent upon the number of players.  Each turn, players will spend action points, selecting from a variety of tasks – move and explore the land, gather creatures, initiate combat, draw cards, or call upon an overwhelming force of obliteration.

Objective: be the first reaching the moon.
 - moving by turns -
Starting from earth each player chooses a different route (green, red, blue, black) and moves forward according die roll; ending on a marked space (star) follow instructions.

This gorgeous game reeks of mid-20th Century science fiction imagery and optimism. While undated, the rules begin: "According to the latest scientific research, it will be possible at the latest in about 50 years, to voyage to the Moon, to other planets and even to stars much further away."

Underneath the beautiful artwork, it is a roll-and-move game, but with one twist. Up to four players try to move their rocket ships from Earth around the board and home. But another player controls the fearsome Robot, who starts somewhere on a separate network of lines between stars.

The Robot network and player track intersect at various points, and if the Robot can land exactly on top of a rocket, that player is out of the game. If all rocket ships are killed, the Robot player wins.

—user summary

Microvores is a 2-4 player game in which players assume the role of a microorganism, and must choose to seek out food, avoid toxins, attack other players, evolve, mutate, or subdivide during their turn.  The winner is the player who had the last microbe alive, or is the first player to begin their turn with 5 microbes.

In addition to the beginning and ending administrative phases of each player's turn, the 3 key phases are the Metabolism Phase, the Drift Phase, and the Action Phase.  During the Metabolism Phase, the player must choose to pay food or take damage to fuel their turn's activity.  The Drift Phase involves randomly determining where to place food, toxins, and special particles on the game board available for all players to consume.  The Action Phase allows players to choose one action: Hunting & Gathering (includes moving, eating, and attacking), Evolving, Mutating, OR Subdividing.

Evolving costs a player 3 food, but allows them to draw an evolution card at random which grants their microbe(s) abilities, possibly to move further, attack harder, and/or have some other unique ability.  Mutating costs a player 4 food (and one evolution - if any), but it increases the total number of evolution cards a player can have in play, the base movement, and the base damage of the player's microbe(s).  Mutation also allows some damage to be healed.  Subdividing costs a player 5 food per subdivision, but each subdivision creates a new microbe on the board for the player, allowing them alternate start locations each turn, an extra life should a microbe be destroyed, and moves the player closer to a win condition.

User summary

Each player tries to conquer dungeon cards using heroes and items selected from their hands.

Each turn, a player draws a card and then performs one of the following actions: (1) Conquer a dungeon card, (2) develop a conquered card, (3) attack a dungeon card owned by another player, (4) draw an extra card.

Conquering a dungeon card involves matching the value of the card with the "Bravery" values of adventure cards played from your hand or playing a lower value to which the value of one die roll is added.

Each dungeon card shows a star-value, and by developing a dungeon card you can double this value. The player who is first to collect a 10 star value in dungeon cards wins the game.

In this new version of Meikyu Kingdom Card Game: Make You Conquest, a one on the die as "always lose," it has a few new rules for reusing characters, and for luring other players' characters away. Also, at least a few of the cards are different.

Designer's summary
迷宮を探索して魔物の部屋を手に入れて、自国の土地にするカードゲームです。
Search through the labyrinth and take control over the demon lairs in order to expand your own domain.
publishers: Adventure Planning Service (冒険企画局 / Bouken)

description:
User summary
ビバニク (Viva Niku) in English means "Viva Meat". Viva Niku is a card game with a theme of an eating race in a grilled-meat party.

In a grilled-meat party in Japan, they sometimes have a yakiniku bugyou (grilled-meat governor) who annoys everyone else by intervening in everything about grilling meat, such as when this meat should be turned over or lifted from the plate, who should eat that meat. Sometimes the Governor gets furious at you if you have slightly overgrilled meat.

To reflect the situation, this game favors the Governor.

Each card has a big effect and a small effect such as "Gain 400 kcal/Gain 100 kcal". Each round, the opening player plays a card, saying "big" or "small". Then in clockwise, the other players must play the same card and declare "big" or "small" if they have, or pass if they don't. Small effects are granted to all players who declared "small", but the big effect only goes to one of the players who declared "big", and if the Governor is involved, it's the Governor that grabs the big effect.

Everyone else tends to be worried about the Governor's hand. Be cautious until you manage to take over the Goverment's position.

The game ends when a player gains total of 2500 kcal, and the player with the most calories wins the game.

The first edition of Viva Niku is labeled for 3-5 players, while the second edition is for 4-7 players.

Publisher's summary
Tonight, it's "The Day of Meat" for a family. Now, it is good to eat meat! Steal! The desire to follow! 
However, I must suggesting a happy "mother of grilled meat magistrate" because noisy when lust for ...

Of all the people who eat a lot of meat, the player who took the most calories is the winner. Not just lucky, the aim of the game is to win by psychological methods. It is more pleasant with more players in many cases. And ending within 30 minutes with many players, please enjoy the chance to play again.

The game is based on irregular trick-taking systems such as Witch's Brew. The designer added card-drafting and stronger elements of competition between players. However, the rules are still simple.

Publisher's summary
焼肉をします。一番たくさん食べたプレイヤーが勝ちます。
焼肉奉行に振り回されながらもトング片手に肉を食べる、癖のあるバッティング・ドラフトゲームです。
publishers: Alhara Systems (アルハラシステムズ)

description:
Drakerys is a fantasy world where Dwarves, Elves, Orcs and Humans reign over ancient kingdoms. Elemental magic created the world and went into slumber for centuries, leaving myths about dreadful and marvelous creatures ruling over the living. Everybody knows fairies and trolls are just stuff of legends, right? Wrong! A few weeks ago, an adventurer awoke a powerful dragon and triggered a chain of events leading to the destruction of an empire. Magic is returning in a world shaken by sudden chaos. Armies are assembling to repel the awakening creatures and seize power!

In Drakerys, each player chooses a side, gathers miniature troops and takes the lead against other players in story-driven skirmish battles. The terrain setting as well as the victory conditions are laid out by a battle scenario all players agreed upon. The fate of warriors is determined through combat rules and some rolls of dice. As a war leader, you’ll need tactical insight - and a bit of luck - to win!

The Starter Box contains everything you'll need to discover miniature games and enjoy Drakerys. Two armies are featured with fully assembled miniatures: the Human Paladins of Irosia and the wild Orcs of Ashral.

As the story begins, they are pitted against each other in a campaign to conquer a mysterious archipelago filled with danger, secrets about the magical events to come, not to mention countless treasures! The winning side will take an important advantage in the beginning war.

The old world is about to crumble. It’s a time of heroes. Will you be the king of tomorrow? Find out with Drakerys!

The game uses classic fantasy army building with an fresh twist: players use a special Action Wheel to determine whose turn is it to play and what units can do. The same player can play several times in a row or execute glorious feats at the price of increased risks, or use a more cautions approach to moving his army. This unforeseen game mechanic sets a new vision in fantasy gaming strategies.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Blankety Blank / The Price Is Right: Gameshow Duo
description:
A combination edition published by Marks & Spencers, to sell exclusively in their stores.

Blankety Blank is a board game based on the UK BBC TV quiz show of the same name first introduced by Terry Wogan in 1977, itself based on the 1970's version of the US Game Show Match Game.

The game simply involves guessing the missing "blank" in a sentence. The sentence itself often gives an innocuous clue to the blank. Players then compare answers and score points for matching answers.

One player acts as quizmaster and the other players take turns to be a "contestant" and a "panelist." The quizmaster decides who the first contestant is going to be the first question is read out and the contestant and panelists write their answers on the sheet provided.

When everyone has recorded their answer, the contestant reads out his answer first, followed by each panelist in turn. Each time that the answer of the contestant matches that of the panelist the contestant gets a point.

The two contestants with the highest number of points then go on to play the "Super Match Game." In this game there are three possible answers to each question--the answers are worth 50 points, 100 points or 150 points. When a player matches any of these answers, they record the number of points won. The player with the most points at the end of the Super Match is the winner and goes on to the Head to Head game, the last round.

In The Price Is Right, one player acts as host to set up the puzzles and verify correct answers. The other players make bids on prizes. The player who bids closest without going over plays a pricing game against the house for more prizes. (The television series rotates over seventy different games. Some home game editions contains as many as forty-five games; some as few as six.)

The finale is called the Showcase Round. The two players involved may be the players with the highest scores or it may be the players who won their respective Showcase Showdowns. (It depends on the edition of the game.) Each player has three prizes. Each guesses what the total value of one's own three prizes is. The person who guesses closer without going over is the winner.
family: TV Shows: The Price is Right

description:
From the introduction:  "Scutarii is intended mainly for recreating historical warfare prior to the introduction of gunpowder firearms and cannons.  The scale of the rules is designed for fighting the larger battles of antiquity, where armies would number 25,000 men or more, on an average sized table.

Missile and melee combat has been designed to reflect the fatigue and decline in fighting ability during the course of the battle.  While it is possible to defeat an opposing force with missile fire it will take quite a while compared to engaging in melee.  As troops take casualties, their morale will decline with one side usually routing from the field while the victor will usually only have a few losses.

As for battlefield tactics, one thing you will find is that using multiple lines and a reserve, though creating a narrower front, will have an advantage over an army arrayed in a single battle line with no reserve.  Attacking with fresh troops against fatigued enemies will usually result in victory for the army with the fresh troops."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Spaß am Erfolg: Karriere, Das Millionenspiel, Risiko
description:
Buchclub version: German compilation of Risk, Careers, Das Millionenspiel, all based on editions by Schmidt.

In Risk, players have army units that they place onto a map of the world. On a player's turn, they use their units to attack other players' positions, hopefully with superior numbers. Combat is a simple dice rolling affair that stresses attrition, and reinforcements are given to players who collect sets of cards.

Careers is a game where the players set their own victory conditions. A player may choose to pursue Fame, Happiness, Money, or a combination of all three. The limitation being that the total number of "points" earned in the 3 categories must total 60. eg. 60 Happiness, 0 Fame, 0 Money; 20 of each; or any other combination. The players endeavor to fulfill their goal by going through any number of different "occupation paths". All paths have some prerequisite for entry, and benefits accrue from going through any of the paths more than once. The different occupations are designed to be suited to different strategies, eg. Hollywood is good for fame points, while "Going to Sea" is good for happiness. In the end it is the player (or team of players) who gets to their pre-set goal first who will be the winner, and achieve everything they ever wanted in life.

This expansion consists of an additional punchboard, that provides the player with more (and different) terrain choices.

Basic game is needed to play with this expansion.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
This is one of the Ravensburger "Think" games, a brainteaser/mental exercise game-series. These games are good for mental training, e.g. in creativity, concentration, imagination, logic and so on.
the games expand your potential through brain-friendly training.

Think: Mindpack contains three games:

    Smile is trump (2 Players)
    Resources (2 Players)
    Six hats (2-6 Players)


Smile is trump is a Stratego-like game. Each player receives 12 pieces. Each piece has two different sides: 4x Smiling/Neutral, 4x Frowing/Neutral, 3x Smiling/Frowning, 1x Neutral/Neutral. The players move the pieces on the board; when a piece moves into another piece's space, the pieces are flipped and compared. The weakest piece is removed: Smiling defeats all others and Neutral defeats Frowning. Play continues until only three pieces remain. The player that has the most pieces left, wins the game.

Resources: Players have 24 two-color pieces, which they use to make combinations. The player that makes the most combinations, wins the game.

Six hats: Cards are drawn indicating a riddle. Players have to determine the correct hat color. The player that solves the riddle first gets to keep the card. After a pre-determined amount of cards, the player with the most cards wins.

Mückenstich is a trick-taking card game with four suits, with each suit having cards numbered 1-12 (with two 12s) and three mosquitoes; a mosquito has no number but is the highest card in a suit.

Each round, players start with a number of cards in hand, then discard two of those cards face down. For the most part, Mückenstich follows traditional trick-taking rules. The active player lays down a card, then each other player follows by playing a single card, following suit, if possible. Whenever a mosquito is played, that player declares one color to be trump. (The game starts with no trump color.) A mosquito or 12 played later in the round is considered higher than one played earlier. Once all players have laid down a card, the highest trump card (if any) wins the trick; with no trump, the highest card in the suit led for the round wins the trick. Players keep track of tricks won, and the winner of a trick leads to the next trick.

Once all tricks have been played, the single player with the fewest tricks names one suit to count double during scoring, then the single player with the most mosquitoes names one of the three remaining suits to be worthless. Then players tally their points: The first mosquito in a suit is worth -5 points, while all other mosquitos are worth 0. Suited cards are worth 0-2 depending on whether they are worthless, scored as normal, or doubled.

After a number of rounds equal to the number of players (or twice as many rounds with three players), the player with the highest total score wins.


In Danger, players draw numbered and colored cards, trying to collect as many points as possible in each color each round without exceeding the limit of 15 points.

On a turn, a player draws the top card from the deck and places it in front of her in a row for cards of that color; some cards are multicolor, and the player chooses in which row to play these cards. After laying a second card in the round, a player has the option of securing a row at the end of each turn. To secure a row, the player must not have the lowest total in that color when compared to the other players; the player secures a row by turning the last card in that row 90º. A player can secure only one row each turn.

If a player exceeds a total of 15 points in a row, that row is now blocked and worth 0 points; the player turns those cards face-down.

If a player draws a colored card for a row either secured or blocked, she must discard the card. The next player in turn order has the option of taking this discarded card instead of drawing randomly from the deck.

Once the fourth row of any player is secured or blocked, the round ends immediately. Whoever has the fewest points in a color (with blocked rows worth 0 points) scores nothing for this color; all other players score points equal to the numbers showing in this color in their row. Players then empty their rows and continue playing with the cards remaining in the deck. After five complete rounds, the player with the highest total score wins.


Zwickern is a public-domain card game of the fishing variety, in which the game starts with a pool of cards on the table and players try to claim these cards for points by using the cards in their hands. Most cards have a single number in the corner to show their value when being played, while some cards have two numbers (e.g., 2/12) and cards like these can be either value when played or when lying on the table; each card also has a victory point value.

On a turn, a player plays one card from her hand and either:

    Buys cards from the display (using an 8, for example, to buy a 5 and 3, or an 8, or all three cards)
    Builds a pile of cards (by adding or subtracting the card played with a card or pile already on the table, or vice versa)
    Plays to the table (making the card available for piles or buys on future turns)


After players play out the few cards in their hand, they receive a few more cards. After a certain number of rounds, the game ends and players sum the points of the cards they've collected, earning additional points for collecting the most cards and for buying all cards on display during a turn. The player with the most points wins.

Includes rules to play three games: Wali, Yoté, Le Furet.

Wali is a traditional game played on a 5x6 rectangular board. Each player has 12 game piece in his own color.

At the beginin in each turn, each player places a stone from the reserve to any empty space on the board which is not adjacent (orthogonal) to a friendly stone. If a player cannot drop more stones, he must pass. This continues until all stones are placed on the board, or no more stones can be placed.

After that, in each turn, each player moves a stone to an empty adjacent (orthogonal) cell. If he is able to make 3 in a rows (not more or less) he may capture one enemy stone.

The player who capture all of his opponents stones wins the game.

Yote is a traditional African capturing game in the Checkers family. It's played on a 5x6 board with 12 pieces per player and no standard setup, instead the players take turns placing their stones. Captures are made by jumping. In addition one can have the ability to jump multiple pieces. (A player that has just captured a opponents piece can capture another piece for each piece jumped.)

boardgame: Family Game Night Book and Game Set: Scrabble, Clue, Sorry, Yahtzee
description:
In Scrabble, players use their seven drawn letter-tiles to form words on the gameboard. Each word laid out earns points based on the commonality of the letters used, with certain board spaces giving bonuses. But a word can only be played if it uses at least one already-played tile or adds to an already-played word. This leads to slightly tactical play, as potential words are rejected because they would give an opponent too much access to the better bonus spaces.

In Clue, players move from room to room in a mansion to solve the mystery of: who done it, with what, and where? Players are dealt character, weapon, and location cards after the top card from each card type is secretly placed in the confidential file in the middle of the board. Players must move to a room and then make an accusation against a character saying they did it in that room with a specific weapon. The player to the left must show one of any cards accused to the accuser if in that player's hand. Through deductive reasoning each player must figure out which character, weapon, and location are in the secret file. To do this, each player must uncover what cards are in other players hands by making more and more accusations. Once a player knows what cards the other players are holding they will know what cards are in the secret file. A great game for those who enjoy reasoning and thinking things out.

In Sorry!, players race their four game pieces from Start around the board to Home. By turning over a card from the draw deck and following its instructions, players move their pieces around the game board, switch places with players, and knock opponents' pieces off the track and back to their Start position.

Yahtzee is a classic dice game played with 5 dice. Each player's turn consists of rolling the dice up to 3 times in hope of making 1 of 13 categories. Examples of categories are 3 of a kind, 4 of a kind, straight, full house, etc. Each player tries to fill in a score for each category, but this is not always possible. When all players have entered a score or a zero for all 13 categories, the game ends and total scores are compared.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
Monster Breeder is a fast paced worker placement game with a bluffing element. This game is tiny (in its simplest form the game consists of 2 cards and a dollar's worth of pocket change).

In this game, each player takes on the role of one of over 30 different bosses. Each boss has its own unique combination of monsters that it can summon and/or spells that it can cast. In this game, all of the monsters are represented by coins. Pennies are the smallest monsters, nickels are bigger, dimes are bigger still, and quarters are the most powerful monsters that can be summoned.

To begin the game, each player selects a boss card to use and takes 3 pennies as starting monsters. The game consists of 5 rounds. On each round, players will simultaneously place their coins. They can create new monster and/or cast spells by placing coins on their boss card. They can attack other players by placing coins face-up next to their boss card or defend by placing coins tails-up next to boss card. At the end of the 5th round, each player adds up the points for his/her monsters (pennies are worth 1 point each, bigger monsters are worth more). The player with the most points wins.

There are over 5 different modes of play. Some modes (free expansion rules) will accommodate 3, or 4 players. Free Print and Play version available!

Kamara is code-matching strategy game for two to eight players. You play the role of a spirit, or diwa, attempting to influence someone within the chamber of their mind. Each diwa has a different color that evokes a different emotion.

The object of the game is to alter the game tiles to match the yellow kamara card you possess. Each time you do so, you gain that card. The first player to match three yellow kamara cards wins.

A memory bluffing deck-building game. You are a detective using your memory to solve a cold case. Other detectives are observing you closely and are possibly tampering with evidence. Can you present the best case amongst your peers and catch the serial killer?

Das neue Kriegsspiel is a wargame based on chess-like mechanics.

It tries to be a simpler version of Johann C. L. Hellwig's
"Versuch eines aufs Schachspiel gebaueten taktischen Spiels von zwey und mehreren Personen zu spielen".

The main goal of the game is to conquer the enemy fortress, with Infantry, Cavalry and Artillery. The movement of the pieces and combat is very similar to chess. The game is played on a square gridded board with 600 squares, which depict terrain like mountains, towns, water and swamps.

boardgame: I Sumeri di Ur, Uruk, Nippur e Lagash
description:
This game is for pupils aged 8-9 years, studing the Mesopotamic Sumerian Civilization.

Farm your land, harvest your grain, build your city, commerce with your neighbours... it gives a taste of how the Mesopotamian agricolture and first cities worked.

Each player is the king of one of the Sumerian cities of the title;  in every seasonal turn, the king must choose which building to build in its city, each building is represented by the tiles taken at the start of each turn. The king must carefully plan how to increase its city, in order to get enough food for each building; in case of famine, the building without enough food is destroyed! Building some particular buildings permit to commerce with the other cities during the game. The king building all the compulsory buildings, like royal palace or ziqqurat and some other optional buildings, wins the game.

Four maps, 2 pages rules, 110 small tokens are ready to print and play!


Il gioco è dedicato agli alunni della scuola elementare che stanno studiano la civiltà dei Sumeri.

Coltiva i tuoi campi, raccogli i frutti del tuo lavoro, ingrandisci la tua città!

Intende simulare i rapporti tra le città della Mezzaluna Fertile e la necessità di sviluppare l\'agricoltura per la sopravvivenza.

Ogni giocatore è il re di una delle città sumere del titolo e deve ingrandire la propria città costruendo gli edifici obbligatori, come la ziqqurat e alcuni di quelli non obbligatori, facendo attenzione ad avere abbastanza cibo per tutti!

Quattro carte, un regolamento di due pagine, 110 piccole carte da ritagliare completano il gioco.

Game covers "Operation Chromite" - landing of the UN forces at Inchon and battle of Seul in the September 1950. It's a part of B-35 wargames system.

Inchon 1950 was published in the 27 issue of "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine. There are two scenarios:
- Desant pod Inchon (The landing at Incheon)
- Seul 1950

Additional scenario for this game was publish in the 28 issue of "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine:
- Ucieczka z Korei (Escape from Korea)

 - moving by turns -
Each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a coloured space follow instructions like: skip turns/roll a special number to leave that space/ jump...

Street Proz was a street football collectible card game. Each player had a deck of at least 30 player cards, each with an Offensive, Defensive, and Wild rating. Dice are rolled to determine which player from each deck is matched up, and yardage is won or lost based on these ratings.

There was also a online fantasy football league tie in at the now defunct StreetProz.com

boardgame: Town Center: Lower Manhattan / Paris La Cité – St. Louis
description:
Town Center: Lower Manhattan / Paris La Cité – St. Louis comprises 4 double-sided player boards, with the Lower Manhattan expansion map (previously published separately as part of Town Center: Expansion n°2) on one side, and the Paris La Cité –  St. Louis expansion map (previously published separately as part of Town Center: Expansion n°1) on the other. There is also an A5-sized rulesheet that explains the special rules of each map.

The Lower Manhattan map simulates the construction of the densest part of New York City, and the limited space is a challenge for even the most experienced players.

Paris La Cité – St. Louis with its bridges and islands on the river Seine is another challenging map, where players have to carefully consider the connections between the various areas, especially in the construction phase.

Two frigates hunt 15 pirate ships. To win the game, the pirates have to get 7 ships into the bay. The frigates win if they sink 9 pirate ships. The frigates can move in all directions, the pirates only forward. A frigate sinks a pirate ship by jumping over it on a free space.

This is a variant of Fox and Geese.

Objective: being Karl May - reach (=escape) with one of your 4 trappers one corner; being an Indian tribe - catch the very last trapper and escape successfully with one of your Indians (reaching the centre or one corner) OR catch the last of the Indian tribe who caught the last trapper and escape....
 - moving by turns -

Determine who becomes the trapper (highest number).

Trappers start in the centre, each Indian tribe in a different corner (depending on no. of participants). Moving by die, a '6' let you move again. All Indians try to catch trappers; trappers try to escape, but may catch Indians, too. An enemy is caught if your pawn's move ends exactly on opponent's position. After all trappers are caught Indians will fight for the loot.

Note:
Wooden pawns - as if they were manufactured in the Erz mountains

Objective:
Be the first reaching space 130
 - moving by turns -
each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a red marked space follow instructions.

A deceptively simple racing game with the possibility of high levels of meanness and co-operation during each brief race. Players sequentially play one of their cards to move. The opening hand is 4x1, 4x2, 4x3, 3x3, 2x5, 1x7, which is enough to get you only halfway around the circuit. If the space you move to is occupied by other cyclists, you gain a jump move of the card you played multiplied by the number of other cyclists in the space. You only get a bonus move from the first space you move to.

And that’s it! There are 2 sprint lines at one and two thirds distances, and 2 special spaces (miss your next turn, exchange your hand for a set from the replacement deck, which can re-energise a cyclist who’s burnt his best cards) but the beauty of the game is in the movement mechanism. Because of the necessity to get bonus moves every turn you have to stay with the pack while using mid-value cards. Usually there’s a few turns of this, then there’s a slight increase in speed and suddenly someone is playing their 7 card to try to get back to the pack.

boardgame: Das Kriegsspiel, oder das Schachspiel im Großen.
description:
A wargame based on chess-like mechanics.

The goal is to destroy or conquer the enemy Fortress or to capture the enemy King.

The playboard is build out of 460 cubes of different colors.
Whereby:
White represents open ground
Red, walls
Green, woods
Blue, water
Brown, mountains
Black, trenches

Every player has:
1 King
1 General
2 Cavalry Captains
2 Captains
10 Cavalry
20 Infantry
4 Bombardiers
4 Gunners
4 Mortars
2 Conveyances

And several markers:
8 Bridges
7 Scaling Ladders
6 Gabions 
4 Bombs

Water Walker is a game to compete who has walk on the water longest.

Players place 'water' panels closed.
'Water' panel is consist of 'step' and 'dobom'. 'Dobom' means kersplash.
Open the panel, turn player gets a number of "steps" more. Others disrupt by 'dobom' or mislead a turn player by panel array.

"Bay of Bengal"  simulates the World War II carrier struggle for control of the northern Indian Ocean.  It includes the historical Japanese Indian Ocean Raid, but also examines other hypothetical options, in the period from April to November 1942. One player commands the Allied (largely Commonwealth) forces and the other the Japanese. Victory is obtained by destroying the opposing fleets, bombarding ports or airfields, interfering with merchant shipping, and possibly even supporting amphibious invasions or decoy operations.

Based on the system of the prize-winning  Solomon Sea, Bay of Bengal is a double blind 2-player game, which means each player moves his task forces, air strikes, and search planes on his own map, and tells the other player about the hexes he searches. Combat is resolved by a series of dice rolls. Each hex represents a diameter of 100 nautical miles, with 3 hours per turn. Each counter represents 6 to 10 planes, individual carries, or groups of smaller ships. Historical chrome includes rules such as radar equipped planes for night search and strikes, rescuing of survivors, leaking battleship boilers, armoured flight decks, and the secret British naval base. Eight historical and alternate-history scenarios are included.

7th Voyage is our tabletop miniatures game set in the world of classic stop motion mythic fantasy films of the 60s and 70s. Whether you want to quest for the Golden Fleece or raid the nests of the fabled Rocs, 7th Voyage enables you to play out your favourite on-screen action as the story unfolds.
