simple 'Roll&Move' game; move forward according die roll; ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid. 
note:
likely published during 1920s; story is based on 'Ten Little Injuns' from Septimus Winner.

Elemensus is a tile laying word game where the tiles are elements from the Periodic Table. The players form words using these tiles by placing them on a grid board made to represent the Orion Nebula. Players may start their words on specially marked spaces and build upon words from there.

Words are scored based on the elements used in the word with the scores being that element's atomic weight. Many elements are difficult or impossible to form words with so every element has "Dark Matter" reverse side with a letter and a point penalty for using this side in a word.

Bonus points can be earned by forming element names with only the element side of the tiles. Some variants are included in the game rules.

Ticket to Ride: 10th Anniversary contains material from the Ticket to Ride base game and the USA 1910 expansion:


    30 Classic Destination Tickets
    35 Destination Tickets from USA 1910
    4 Destination Tickets from Mystery Train
    1 Longest Route/Globetrotter Bonus card


In Ticket to Ride, players collect cards of various types of train cars they then use to claim railway routes in North America. The longer the routes, the more points they earn. Additional points come to those who fulfill Destination Tickets – goal cards that connect distant cities; and to the player who builds the longest continuous route.

Part of Ticket to Ride series.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Rotational Spacecraft
 1st Expansion Race Pack for Starbattles 
 24 New Control Sheet Diagrams
 6 Page Booklet 
 This set introduces the 1st new race for the Starbattle game system.

This race (referred to as ROTEX)uses Rotational Type Spacecraft (Or, Flying Saucers)which bring new effects and special rules to the game.
 Booklet includes all instances where the ROTEX ships differ from existing ships.

Specific new rules are the barriers created by their unique drive system and the fact that every ship has VTOL capability.

Included are construction notes and charts showing tech levels and build information for the 24 ship and craft types available to a ROTEX player so they can be integrated in one off or campaign games

Also included is an encounter chart for determining random opposing forces. This chart includes fleet types form the original game and it's supplement as well as all three expansion sets.

The Control Sheet Diagrams like previous sets are meant for photocopying and only have the shields, armor and engines established. This leaves the player to determine the internal makeup and armament of each ship allowing for a very customizable game experience.

A quick competitive card game for 3-6 players, where you and your opponents use the elements of nature to shape the world to your will.

Clash is a fast paced card game in which each turn you make tactical decisions to empower your elements or weaken those of your opponents. Every card played directly affects your score and can directly or indirectly affect other players.

Players play a card from their hand and then pass their hand to the player on their right. The player on their left takes a turn. When a player receives a hand of cards, they draw a card.

When the last card is drawn from the deck, one more round is played, and then the game is scored. Each card played is equal to its score card in the middle of the table.

boardgame: RC4 La Route de la Mort: Indochine 1950
description:
RC4, La Route de la Mort: Indochine 1950 is an Operational level Solitaire-Only board-wargame simulating the September/October 1950 combats along the RC4 road during the Indochina War (1946-1954). In this game, the player is the French commander that must prevent Vietminh progression while evacuating the area.

The map displays the Tonkin highlands, from Cao Bang to Lang Son, near the Chinese border. The scale is approx. 1 inch to 1 kilometer. A turn represent 5 days, and the game lasts 10 turns.

description:
Be sure to see "Community Wiki" towards the bottom (after the “Files” section) for quick download file links and useful information.

FLAC (Fast Large Air Combats) is a free set of rules designed for a fast paced, action packed, hex-based, WW2 air combat game using  any scale of miniatures (1:200, 1:144 or 1:100 scale work best) where a single player can control a multiple number of aircraft. The only limitation is the size of the game mat and the number of aircraft models you have. For beginners, we recommend no more than 2-3 aircraft per player, and no more than six to eight players per game.

By design, FLAC is fairly abstract, but that is only because it is designed to capture the flavor of air combat rather than overwhelm the players with lots of detailed characteristics of air combat. For example, there are only six altitude levels in FLAC, which is a fraction of what it should be, but trying to keep track of a vast array of altitudes can be tedious. Also, it would not make for fast, fun game play because aircraft at very different altitudes do not interact well. Overall, FLAC is designed to capture the flavor of air combat in a simple, fast, and enjoyable way.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Crisis on the Right: Plancenoit 1815 is a two player wargame at the division/brigade level simulating the struggle that took place near the village of Plancenoit as part of larger Battle of Waterloo. The game puts the Prussian IV Corps against Napoleons right flank and rear units.

The game is played over 12 turns in which players rally troops, place reinforcements, bombard their opponent, move troops, and conduct combat.

Victory is determined by control of key areas and number of troops activated. Napoleon wanted to save as many men as possible for his main fight against the Allied forces so the Prussian player earns victory points based on how many of Napoleon's troops are activated.

The game is for two competing players seated opposite each other. It uses 25 double sided tiles and a dividing marker. The objective is to remove all your opponent’s tiles from the board.

The tiles have 5 separate actions printed on them and a black side and a white side. Players use matching pairs of tiles (one black and one white) to perform these actions to win the game.

Tiles are placed in a line and players travel left to right utilising their own tiles and then their opponents’. During play tiles are flipped over when one player uses them making them available for the competing player to use.

Каркассон: Королевский Подарок (Carcassonne: Royal Gift) is a Carcassonne collection containing the base game and 4 expansions: Inns and Cathedrals, Traders and Builders, Abbey and Mayor and King and Scout (including the tiles for Carcassonne : Hunters and Gatherers).

Каркассон: Предместья и обитатели (Carcassonne: Suburbans and  Inhabitants) is a compilation or four expansions for Carcassonne game: Inns and Cathedrals, Traders and Builders, Abbey and Mayor and King and Scout (including the tiles for Carcassonne : Hunters and Gatherers)

These are the same expansions as those included in Russian Big Box Carcassonne: Royal Gift.

Каркассон: Дворяне и Башни (Carcassonne: Nobles and Towers) is a compilation for Carcassonne, including two large expansions : The Tower and The Princess and the Dragon, and four small expansions : The Count of Carcassonne, The River II, The Cult (6 tiles) and The Corn Circles.

Modern Times: Wheels of Industrialization is a Eurogame styled boardgame, bordering with a puzzle. IT uses resource management combined with the board rotation mechanic.

Player’s goal is to build from one to three factories using resources available or produced in ever changing environment. But be warned, each production comes with the price. And there are also taxes to be paid to the government…

Game is made for Board Game Geek Solitaire P'n'P contest 2014
http://www.boardgamegeek.com/thread/1179655/2014-solitaire-print-and-play-contest

boardgame: Alfred the Great: The Great Heathen Army 871AD
description:
"Alfred the Great: The Great Heathen Army 871 AD" is a two player hex, dice and counter wargame with complexity on the lower end of the  scale that has the Vikings invading Wessex in 871AD. Players will have to consider when and where to fight, re-supply and other challenging factors to win the game.

Players have Warriors, Earls/Jarls and Kings to do battle with and will have to make strategic and tactical choices to win the game. 
A turn chart of 15 moves commences in January 871AD and goes through to May 871AD.

description:
Erop of eronder is a cardgame for two players published by Plantyn. Aim of the game is to build the highest tower, using different cards. The game is developed as an educational game and has three versions in its rules. Version 1; 5-6 years, Version 2; 7-10 years and version 3; 10+ years.
The rules are in Dutch.

A party game in which players race to create a chain of destinations by consulting twelve timetables (or 'Key Cards') scattered around the game space. Each Key Card lists twenty departure points with the departure time for the associated destination. Players log the times (to show that they really have seen the key card) and seek the next destination. The winner is the first to turn in a Journey Card listing twelve destinations and their associated departure times.

A racing game with The Smurfs.

Every player has two Smurfs. Everyone also has a stopper (Gargemel). In his first turn, the player moves the runner (with a die roll). In the second turn, the stopper must be moved.
If the stopper comes on a place where a smurf is, that smurf has to start again.

Winning the game: be the first to get your two smurfs on the finish.

A Lawless Town is trick-taking game set in the Wild West where players must swing deals and spend their cash to pay one another off in order to come out ahead.

The game is played over the course of three round in which each player will be dealt a hand of cards which are then then use to win tricks. Unlike other trick-taking games, in A Lawless Town each trick has at least two winners.

Next players will bribe and haggle with one another in order to convince the trick winners to distribute their awards in ways that are favorable to the briber's plan.

Part of the Bagged Game Originals line, Symmetric is a symmetrically balanced abstract strategy game for two players. A player wins by having one Counter in each of their opponent's three starting spaces at the center of the board.

Thy Realm is a strategic skirmish miniature game that uses cards for the figures.

Thy Realm Card/Miniature game will take you into a fantasy realm that you control by picking your own realm and heroes. Your figures are the card therefore, there will be no need to assemble or paint just go out of the box.

Fight with your realm's heroes against other realms in strategic battles. Using cards that are your heroes miniature, you can move across the landscape of the realm. Each hero has its own abilities and or feats. There are five realms to choose from; Elvin, Dwarven, Human, Orc, and Dark.

In the Elven realm, you have graceful yet dangerous opponents with ranged attacks, fast movement and daring.

In the Dwarven realm, your army is solidly built with determined fighters that can withstand almost any assault.

The Human realm is a versatile army that can adapt to many situations.

The Orcs are heavy hitting and numerous.

The Dark Realm relies on fear and terror.

街コロシャープ (which translates as "Machi Koro Sharp") expands the Machi Koro base game with thirteen new establishment cards, including a winery, a moving company and a general store. The designer notes that the new cards somewhat increase the difficulty of the rules, and this expansion can be combined with Machi Koro: Harbor Expansion.

街コロシャープ contains 72 cards: six copies each of the ten red, blue, and green establishments and four copies each of the three purple major establishments.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Magna Carta is a casual two-player card game with a medieval theme sporting easy to learn and play rules.

The players take turns drawing cards from a common draw pile, deploying troop cards, supporters cards and resources cards from their hand to the table in front of them and then attacking the opponent cards with the cards on the table. 
Each attack determines the number of cards the attacker draws from the draw pile as victory spoils. 
When the draw pile is exhausted, the game ends and the player with the most victory spoils wins.

The game includes 90 cards with various units like Infantry, Cavaly e t c, support cards like Fortress, Blacksmith e t c. Resources are the same cards but played face down.
Several special units and supporters exist with special abilities which affects game play.
Each troop/supporter card has deployment requirements, meaning that you need to deploy them in the right order.

Objective: Be the first finishing a round trip though the city (space 1 - 100); learn more about traffic rules.
 - moving by turns -
each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a red marked space tell gamemaster/other players what to do if you see this traffic sign. 
note:
published during 1930's (increasing traffic); large box shows Nazi emblem Hakenkreuz; cute pewter pawns.

Objective: Starting in Berlin be the first finishing a round trip through Germany. Learn more about the big cities of Germany.
 - moving by turns -
each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a white space (city) follow instructions.

note:
likely published during 1920s

From the Box: A succession of noises and mimes is created and added to at the game's pace...Laughs guaranteed for this original memory game!

Players draw from 32 cards with icons of noises and mimes and turn the cards over. The active player has to do all the noises and mimes of the already drawn cards in the right order. When a player has got it wrong, he takes a turned over card as a penalty card.
The winner is the player who has the least penalty cards.

The War of Ashes is not a war of front lines and set piece battles. Small groups encounter each other in every manner, and there can be neither truce nor quarter. Scouts and foraging parties, messengers, guards, and lost soldiers meet. They fight over a cache of food, a lost animal, a footbridge, a message, or just to get back home. These desperate skirmishes take place amongst camps and baggage, in ravines, at the edge of swamps, or on the decks of small ships. There is no warning and no time to call for help. These small battles that will never be named or recorded are decided by the choices and skill of a handful of fighters, and in turn these skirmishes decide the course of the war.

Shieldbash is the skirmish wargame for the War of Ashes setting.  The fights are very flavorful, with ships that move beneath you as you fight on their decks, cavalry, darkness, archers, musicians, berserkers, carnivorous plants, and lumbering trolls.

Two new races of Agaptus are introduced in Shieldbash: the lizard-like Ki-Kak seeking vengeance on the Vidaar, and the True Atronians, does from the Elvorix, Vidaar, and Jaarl people who fight to unify their peoples against the Kuld menace.

A blind activation system leaves the commander unsure of how far he can push his fighters before the momentum of battle changes. Shieldbash concentrates on a commander's ability to use his fighters to best effect. Designed for ease of play, commanders can fight several skirmishes in an evening, following the tale of their warbands as they grow in power and renown. Gather your fighters, plan your attack, and fight for your people. Lead with your sword, keeping an eye open for the errant Shieldbash!

The goal of this game is to acquire the color jewels that will score in the present round, as well as in future rounds until all jewels have been dispersed and the game ends after the final round. However, you don't want any of the white colored jewels, as they are worthless and will never score.

(Setup) From Box Top:
Put all of the jewels into a global stash to one side of the playing area. Give each player a Persian Rug. Choose one player to be the first Sultan. Give that player the Sultan pawn, the Sultan cards, and the dice.

(Play) Summarized From the Box Top:
The Sultan, using the dice, adds 10 jewels of the rolled color into a single pile for that round. The Sultan then divides the pile in any manner they choose among each of the players, so long as each player pile contains at least one item. Finally the sultan secretly determines which color will score at the end of the given round using the Sultan cards. The position of the Sultan then passes to the next player, and play continues until all jewels have been dispersed and final scoring occurs.

Chaos Ninjas is a light card game that combines both strategy and take-that elements.
In Chaos Ninjas you play the role of a Warlord who is hiring Ninjas.
You are trying to be the first player to have six unique Defender Ninjas in front of your play area.
On your turn you only perform one action, either take a card or play a card.
Very easy to learn and easy to play.

A tactical game for two.
Your goal is to capture the enemies knight, represented by a spherical peg. You start the game with the Knight in the front row, pawns are beside and behind him. Movement is one field in any direction by any playing piece. Capturing is done by jumping over enemy pieces which is in case a must. Pawns are only allowed to move in your own territory and the neutral area of the board, knights can move anywhere. Pawn are aloud to jump over pieces that are placed on the corner spaces of the enemy territory when they land in the neutral area.

The name "Sboura" comes from the Greek word for "a spinning top" and included in the game is a numbered spiiner that's used to determine which cards are picked from a deck of destination cards.

Players will enjoy hours of educational fun testing their knowledge and counting skills by answering the many questions asked on the game cards while collecting, paying out and trading Sboura points for each card.

There is, of course, a hitch! Within the card desk, there are 16 cards that contain NO questions. These cards have some strange characters that will give instructions that MUST BE FOLLOWED, some will help you, others will definitely not.

It is basically a trivia game. At the start of the game you start with 544 dollars (yes, 544) you then put 100 dollars in the pot. The first player rolls the dreidel (yup, it's a dreidel with numbers on it). The spinner then gets asked 3 questions, each one harder than the last. For each one they get right, they get money out of the pot corresponding to the amount shown on the card for the question. If they get it wrong, they pay the amount shown on the card to the pot. When the pot is empty, the game is over. The most dollars at the end of the game wins.

A traditional Finnish card game, based on Russian Durak. The goal of the game is to shed cards. On each turn, the active player ("attacker") plays any number of cards in one suit to next player ("defender"). The defender may beat them by playing cards from her hand, either higher cards in the same suit or any cards in the trump suit.

Queen of Spades (aka Musta Maija or Black Mary) cannot be beaten or used to beat anything. As long as there's talon left, players fill their cards to five cards after each turn. Once the talon runs out, the players who get rid of their cards exit the game. The player who is left with the Queen of Spades is the loser.

PrinciPals is a 2 player cardgame where you try to be the first principal to get 3 full classes of students. Or be the one with the most classes.

It's a microgame which means few components but still meaningfull decisions to be made.

Do you use the student card for it's action or do you play it in your class for its number of students...
Supplement for Confrontation: the Age of the Rag'narok that contains additional rules for playing with the Temple (Griffin) Army and its associated artifacts, miracles and spells.

The players start as pedestrians. Later they use a bike and a tram. All blue spaces have to be visited. On several spaces the Players have to explain different traffic situations. If the do this rigth they get a point (green marker) awarded. If not they get a red penality marker (negative points). The traffic lights are placed in the center of the game board. At the roll of a 6, the player may adjust the traffic lights or the arms of the traffic cop on the game board to close the road for opposing players.
The winner is not the player who ist the first to get to the finish space, but the player who gets the most points.

Each card depicts six questions, one from each of six categories. On a players turn, he/she chooses one question or rolls the category die and answers the question. Depending on the game play variant, the turn passes to the player on the left or the other players judge the given answer.

boardgame: Flames of War: Tour of Duty
description:
The Vietnam War (or the American War to the Vietnamese) defined a generation, whether it was through service in Vietnam or opposition to the war. The war in Vietnam, fought nearly half a century ago, remains one of the longest and bloodiest wars since the Second World War. Despite its popular depiction as a guerrilla war of endless patrols and small ambushes, both sides were constantly looking for decisive victories in large-scale battles.

The Free World had all of the advantages of mobility and firepower. Whole battalions flew into battle in helicopters, while the air cavalry rarely even set foot on the ground. Armoured cavalry and mechanised infantry used their M113 ‘tracks’ both for mobility and heavy firepower. Of course, the majority of soldiers were still ‘grunts’ fighting on foot, but even they could call in massive firepower at will.

The Nationalists had the advantages of the home team. They fought as guerrillas, appearing as if from nowhere to ambush the Free World forces hunting them and used local knowledge to outwit their foes. Later, as the war progressed, they switched to conventional tactics using armoured forces in open battle.

This book allows you to recreate these battles on the tabletop using the popular Flames Of War rules.

The traditional Card Game played with the 16 card deck by 4 players. The game is very popular in some Polish regions especially in Kaszuby in Pomerania.

The game is called sometimes the Kaszubian Bridge. Game is played in the sequence (e.g. 16) of rounds. Each round starts from the bidding than, depends on the type of agreed contract, sequence of tricks is played. Round can be played in schema 2 vs 2 or 1 vs 3 depend on the agreed contract. Similarly different card hierarchies are possible.
 Interesting twist is that the personalities of players in 2 vs 2 teams are revealed during the play (the owners of 2 black Queens and in some cases one of Jacks plays together).

Objective: Be the first reaching target space 
 - moving by turns -
Story: A lonesome dog (Red Rover), who lives in the forest tries to catch children being on a race through the woods because he's seeking company.

Each player moves forward according die roll; simultaneously red rover is moved, too, starting from his 'lonesome lodge'. There are some spaces players are safe or must follow instructions; as soon as Red Rover ends on the same space a payer stands or has moved to he/she's caught for one round. Next turn player is going to escape joining the party again on the very last position.

Players choose 1 of 5 characters from the popular PBS tv show, "Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood" to collect their friends in a trolley for a party at the clock factory. Along the way, players get to use their imagination to act out scenes or emotions from the tv show.

This is a game with custom rubber dice made for small rubber figures called Squinkies. Each player starts the game with a number of Squinkies.

The game is played on a six square grid with symbols in each of the grids and a 'Pit' where spare fighters are kept (a downloadable PDF).

Players take turns rolling a die. The die roll determines whether you remove Squinkies from play, regenerate previously defeated Squinkies, lose a turn or reroll a die.

The last player that has Squinkies remaining wins the game.

18SA is an 18xx game which is set in South America.

The players are seeking to make the most money by buying and selling stock certificates of eleven railroad companies. The player with the most stock of a particular company in his possession acts as the president of this company and is responsible for track laying, the placement of railheads (which are necessary for train runs), route selection and train acquisition. Running trains earns money and this money is either divided by the shareholders or kept in the company's treasure for further investments (like buying better trains or constructing track). This decision influences the value of the company's shares - and not the player with the most stock or the most beautiful constructed track wins the game, but the player with the most riches in terms of cash and shares.

The game differs from the usual 18xx games a little, as it uses different tokens for either passenger trains or freight trains. The companies also face a lot of building costs - mountains, Amazonas swamps, and crossing a border into another country costs extra.

You're a mad scientist bent on world domination. Experiments of battle to determine the strongest Destroying robot is repeated endlessly.Pilots of the most skillful robot approaches to conquer the world.

Robotorino is trick taking game to play by a special effect  cards of four and numbers cards of four. It is important to guess that you are thinking of each other.

By it is order to know who is dealt a card, it deal cards face up.

Each player is set to face down robot card and effects card.

The trick battle in the order in which it was decided in advance.

boardgame: World of Tanks: Rush – M2 Light Tank
description: World of Tanks: Rush — M2 Light Tank, a promotional card for the World of Tanks: Rush base game available at conventions and through hobby stores, features a light Amercian tank that costs 1, gives 1 resource, and has a Sabotage ability.

boardgame: World of Tanks: Rush – Второй фронт
description: Second Front — the first expansion for World of Tanks: Rush, a deck-building strategy card game in which your mission is to assemble a powerful tank squadron — adds the nation of Great Britain, twelve achievements, a fourth special base for every player, a new play zone, and two new card abilities. Some minor changes for the basic mechanisms of the game are proposed. The game with the expansion can be played with up to six players instead of the five-player limit of the base game.

Players take turns appeasing the local dragon by offering sacrifices for it to eat in RingWyrm, a trick-taking card game with room for negotiation. Players attempt to predict how may "tricks" they are going to win by placing bids. However, players can mess with other player's bids, changing them by playing certain cards. This ability to "prick" a players bid adds a social element to the game. Players might beg or attempt to broker deals with other players to prevent their bids from getting tampered. When it comes to RingWyrm, it isn't enough to be good at trick-taking. You also have to be a charismatic.

"Street life. The Homeless Survival Game - uses a portion of the proceeds from sales of Street Life to help the needy and Homeless".
The players try to accumulate money while going around the Monopoly type board. 
This is typical spin and move game.

Aguirre, designed by the writer Ann Journey, is a thematic Euro-style historical game about the last and most infamous Spanish expedition to find El Dorado. During the course of this expedition, Don Lope de Aguirre (later known as The Madman and The Tyrant) assassinated the conquistador leading the expedition and the nobleman he propped up in his stead before taking over the expedition personally. Recognizing that El Dorado was a myth and that the only gold to be found was back in Peru, Aguirre declared himself Prince of Peru and changed the purpose of his expedition to overthrow the Pizzaros and take over the country for himself. Sailing down the Amazon and Orinoco rivers, Aguirre's expedition faced all manner of challenges in the form of disease, privation, navigation, lack of morale, and attacks from the natives, against whom Aguirre committed barbaric atrocities (there are portions of South America where Aguirre's name is still used as a curse today). Members of Aguirre's expedition discovered to their dismay that the most dangerous of all the challenges, however, was Aguirre's growing paranoia. One by one, Aguirre executed his own men when he grew suspicious of them, sometimes with cause, more often without. Ultimately, Aguirre managed to capture a Spanish fort before being besieged by government forces, when he was executed by one of his own men, one of the few survivors of his ill-fated expedition.

In the game, players will take on the role of one of the unfortunate members of Aguirre's expedition. Their goal? To accumulate enough influence to overthrow Aguirre before they are executed in turn. In the end, only one player will survive Aguirre’s paranoia. Only one player will be able to accumulate enough influence to overthrow him. Only one player will emerge from the depths of hell on earth to tell the tale of The Tyrant Aguirre!

The principal game mechanics are role/action selection and set collection. Players will earn Influence (victory) Points through a variety of methods, including influencing prominent members of the expedition (the role/action selection part of the game), purchasing Supply Cards (the set collection part of the game), and overcoming thematic challenges. One of these challenges is the "paranoia phase," in which one of the roles/actions is removed from the game every turn, resulting in a diminishing array of role/actions for players to choose from as the game progresses. The game challenges players to use reactive and adaptive strategy, as players must frequently shift and adjust what they want to do in the game according to the changing conditions of the game.

Aguirre is a fast-paced game for 2-6 players and lasts 30-90 minutes depending on the number of players.

The board game Mission: Farm is an original strategy game for everyone, inspired from... the modern rural life!
Welcome to the remote village of Farmaria! Are you ready to live the ultimate rural experience? Make the best investments, expand your farm, increase your income and your fame and be the greatest farmer of all ages! But beware for... environmental conditions and opposing farmers are coveting your fortune! Can you cope with them?

A modern strategy game with the element of intense competition that develops the players' perception of commercial transactions and good management of opportunities.

Ένα σύγχρονο παιχνίδι στρατηγικής με έντονο το στοιχείο του ανταγωνισμού που αναπτύσσει με ευχάριστο τρόπο την αντίληψη των παικτών σε θέματα εμπορικών συναλλαγών και καλής διαχείρισης ευκαιριών.

Καλωσόρισες στο μακρινό χωριό Φαρμαριά! Είσαι έτοιμος να ζήσεις την απόλυτη αγροτική εμπειρία; Μηλιές, πορτοκαλιές, τρακτέρ, κότες και πρόβατα συνυπάρχουν αρμονικά στο χωράφι σου. Αλλά πόσο εύκολο είναι να φτιάξεις και να μεγαλώσεις τη φάρμα σου όταν έρθει κακοκαιρία ή κάποιος ζωοκλέφτης κλέψει τα ζώα σου; Πρέπει με κάθε τρόπο να διαφυλάξεις τη σοδειά σου και φυσικά την τσέπη σου! Η λύση είναι μία: 
αξιοποίησε σωστά και στρατηγικά τις ευκαιρίες σου, κάνε τις καλύτερες επενδύσεις 
και εκμεταλλεύσου τις αδυναμίες των αντιπάλων σου!
Ένα πρωτότυπο παιχνίδι στρατηγικής για όλη την παρέα μέσα από τον κόσμο της 
σύγχρονης αγροτικής ζωής! Επιστροφή στη φύση λοιπόν!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From publisher web:

The'Airstrike' Supplement is a Web-exclusive for 'Last Night on Earth' and features a new scenario and ten new game cards. All pieces are printed on the same high-quality card stock as the boardgame. The cards are exclusive to this supplement and will not be available anywhere else. They feature all new artwork.

Though radio communications have been spotty, the Heroes have learned that the military has begun to mobilize and have declared the entire area around Woodinvale and Timber Peak to be a ‘Stage 4’ quarantine zone... and that doesn’t sound good. By the number of jets flying overhead, it is clear that they are planning an airstrike to level the entire area.  The only chance for survival is to find enough flares to let them know there are still survivors!
family: Series: Last Night on Earth Game Engine (Flying Frog Production)

boardgame: Conquest of Planet Earth: The Hora'Goth Game Supplement
description:
The Hora'Goth are truly masters of diplomacy and deception! Vicious and devious insect creatures, they disguise themselves inside large, grotesque humanoid bodies; unassuming and non-threatening. That is until their refined, senatorial visage splits open to reveal the hideous creatures contained within!

This expansion is available through Flying Frog's web store.

It includes:

Cards:

     2 Space Stuff (Venomous Shroud, Vox Broadcaster)
     1 Location card (World Organization HQ)
     2 Resistance cards (2×Scientist's Daughter)
     5 Event cards (2×A Taste of Victory, 2×Diplomatic Delegation, Face-Off)


The Hora'Goth faction has 2 strength, 4 intelligence. Its powers are:

     Remains in Play cards can be played face down, only revealing them when you want to use them
     Use tokens to cancel events or to auto-win the first battle.


Note that although released in 2014, the main card bears a 2010 copyright date.

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – The Forgotten Island Game Supplement
description: This 15-card expansion for Dark Gothic provides new Minions, Events, Allies and Gear cards. It is best suited for competitive play with a larger group. (Eight of the fifteen cards introduce a set-collection mechanism and have an effect that is much stronger in the competitive than the cooperative version of the game. Three other cards have their power-level keyed to the number of players.)
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

description:
Join the fight and defend the world against Dr. Wily and the evil robot masters!  Use this tin to begin your training in the Universal Fighting System and play your  friends to become the ultimate universal fighter!

The combat system employs a "progressive difficulty" mechanism, which means the more you do in a turn, the harder your later actions become.

UFS is modular and works with other games in the system, so you can have those epic Mega Man or Proto Man vs. Street Fighter or Soul Calibur or Penny Arcade battles.

Compatible with:


     Universal Fighting System: Street Fighter
     Universal Fighting System: Soul Calibur III
     Universal Fighting System: Soul Calibur IV
     Universal Fighting System: Darkstalkers
     Universal Fighting System: SNK Playmore
     Universal Fighting System: Penny Arcade

English description:

In Broad City Affairs each player plays the role of a Don of a gangster family in a turbulent American city of the 1930s. Each Don will work to make his family the most powerful crime family of the Broad City. Players will set up their smuggling rings, open brothels, control the casinos and gambling in the city, traffic with unlawful alcohol shipments, control the unions and bribe to politicians... But they must be smart enough to don't arouse too much suspicions in the incorruptible side of the law. Their guys can arrested in police raids and the district attorney might his eyes over their affairs.

The mechanism of the game is based on area control and workers placement, allowing a higher level of interaction with the other players when each player defends his business and try to hinder his opponents.

A game takes about 90 minutes and offers a lot of options to seize the victory. Will you bribe to politicians to control the crime underworld? Will you become into the new "Capo di tutti capi" by controlling the streets and the monopolizing the smuggling rings? or will you do it controlling the unions? All plays are different: 23 historic events (start of the World War II , end of the Prohibition, Capone arrest...) can modify each play and offer new targets, troubles and options. Nobody said that the thirties were easy years.


Spanish description:

En Broad City Affairs cada jugador representa un Don de una familia mafiosa en una turbulenta ciudad estadounidense en la década de los 30. Cada Don trabajará para llevar a su familia a ser la más influyente en la ciudad y así ganar la partida. Para ello deberá establecer redes de contrabando, abrir negocios de alterne, controlar las casas de juegos, traficar con el alcohol ilegal, controlar los sindicatos, sobornar a políticos… todo aquello que solían hacer los mafiosos por aquél entonces. Pero deberá ser suficientemente astuto como para no despertar demasiadas sospechas en los “incorruptibles” del lado de la ley, evitando que sus hombres caigan en redadas de la policía o que los fiscales pongan la lupa sobre sus asuntos. Por si fuera poco, las otras familias de la mafia lucharán por el control del crimen en la ciudad y no estarán demasiado dispuestas a compartir el pastel.
La mecánica de Broad City Affairs se basa en el control de zonas y la colocación de trabajadores obligando a un alto nivel de interacción entre los jugadores y permitiendo que cada jugador defienda sus negocios y estorbe a sus oponentes.

Las partidas duran aproximadamente 90 minutos y presentan muchas opciones para alzarse con el triunfo. ¿Te harás con el control del mundo del crimen por medio de los sobornos a los políticos? ¿Será el control de las calles y el monopolio de las redes de contrabando los que te convertirán en el nuevo Capo di tutti capi? ¿O tal vez lo hará tu mano de hierro sobre los sindicatos? Además, ninguna partida es igual que la anterior: 23 eventos históricos (el comienzo de la Segunda Guerra Mundial, el fin de la Ley Seca, la condena de Al Capone…) podrán modificar cada partida, ofreciendo nuevos retos diferentes en cada una de ellas. Nadie dijo que los años 30 fuesen unos años sencillos.

It is a fast-paced game of 7-on-7 football, based on a fictional sport, where field position translates to points.  The goal of this game is to provide a football-like game experience that enables sustainable league and career play.  This is accomplished through smaller rosters and streamlined statistics.

boardgame: Pacific War: War at Sea in the Pacific 1941-1944
description:
Description from the publisher:

Pacific War is a strategic level simulation game of the naval operations in the Pacific from 1941 to the first half of 1944. Players become commanders of the Japanese Imperial Navy and the Allied navy, which is centered around the US navy, and have to plan a strategy and execute it in order to win the war.

Even though the rules are simple and cover only 4 pages, a unique gaming system is adopted. Similarly to the actual warfare in order to maximize the efficiency of your aircraft carrier task forces will require experience and a significant amount of trial and error. The designers notes and detailed example inside the provided play book, are designed to help the players get a grasp of the game.

Finally the long awaited game about the Pacific War campaign, which can be enjoyed by novice and veteran players alike, has arrived!!!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Games and exercises for brain-fair training. Train playfully your pictorial imagination.

Erweitern Sie Ihr Potential durch Gehirn-gerechtes Training.

There is a booklet from Vera F. Birkenbihl for training. Your partner on the way to a brain-right using your brain-potential. Also there are games to play. 1. Difference (for concentration) :  2. Play with a correspodence in cipher. 3. Undercover mit 36 cards like a memory game.The cards are open. 6 wooden small plates are to cover 6 cards. Each plate with another colour. You play with a dice with the six colours of the plates and have to say what picture is under the plate.

Difference: is a placement game with 4 laying boards and 36 picture cards for 2-4 players. You have to lay down pictures on a board (with the same pictures). The pictures only have small differences. The first player has his cards stored calls stop. With the control film is controlled. For each correct card there is a point. Four rounds, so everyone plays with each panel. The points are added up. The player with the most points wins.

Undercover: is a memory game with 36 cards, 6 wooden discs and a cube. The aim of the game is to remember which motifs of ancient cultures are covered under the individual colored wooden discs, then color cubes with the correct predictions to collect after roll a most picture cards. The wooden plate is then placed on another subject. The cards are open. 6 wooden small plates are to cover 6 cards. Each plate with another colour. You play with a dice with the six colours of the plates and have to say what picture is under the plate.

Another game of the Ravensburger Think Series, a brainteaser/mental exercise game-series. These games are good for mental training, e.g. in creativity, concentration, imagination, logic and so on.

Designer of GEHIRNPOTENTIAL is Vera F. Birkenbiehl.
Designer of DIFFERENCE is Elisabeth Richter.
Designer of UNDERCOVER is Heinz Meister.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Decks: Khazad-dûm
description:
Nightmare Decks for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game allow players to revisit their favorite scenarios cast in the shadows of the game's challenging Nightmare Mode. Featuring deadlier enemies, darker locations, and more terrifying surprises, each Nightmare Deck introduces new, alternate encounter cards and instructions for using them to reshape a scenario's encounter deck. The result? A nightmarish journey through territory players thought they knew, but with new twists and turns that will have them on the edges of their seats!

Khazad-dûm Nightmare Decks is a single set of three twenty-card Nightmare Decks, one for each of the three scenarios from Khazad-dûm, the first deluxe expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.

description:
The game is a dexterity variant of Katamino.
It consists of 14 different pieces of angled blocks and can be played in several modes :

Beginner mode (from 2 years)
Child has to put the right pieces in place on a 2D model sheet, respecting color and shape. 
The model represents an animal.

"Duel" mode
The two players simultaneously build a simple 3D pre-defined stack, trying to be faster than the opponent

Multiplayers mode
Each player places one piece over the common stack on his turn, respecting a 3D pre-defined model. 
Player who collapse the stack looses the round. 
This mode can be played in two difficulty levels.

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Auckland, in New Zealand.

In Tavern Brawl, each player chooses 5 units which make up their very own adventuring party. The Tavern (game board) is created using modular tiles that can be arranged in many ways. Every turn players may activate up to three of their Units. When activated, a Unit can move, punch, or use its unique power. Each unit has a Life value and when it receives wounds equal to it, it's defeated, and removed from play. The first player to defeat the others Units wins the game!

- Easy to learn, Hard to Master
- Great for new Gamers and skilled ones
- No downtime as every turn is crucial for both players
- Average play time under 20 minutes!
- Immense replay value with hundreds of possible team combinations

Set Dice has three dice games in one box, and in all three games, the more sets you make, the higher your score. What's a set, you ask? To make a set, you need three dice on which the shape of the symbols on them are all the same or all different, on which the number of symbols are all the same or all different, and on which the color of the symbols are all the same or all different. The three games in this collection are:


    Set Cubed: Players score points Scrabble-style by creating sets with their dice and with dice already on the game board.



    Scramble Set: Drop all 42 dice from the bag onto the table, then start calling out sets as soon as you see them. When no more sets remain, tally the number of sets for each player, then start another round. Whoever has the high score after five rounds wins.



    Crossword Set: Each player takes ten dice, then rolls them at the same time to start play, with players trying to combine their dice into a crossword-style grid made of sets. Whoever uses all of her dice first wins.

The momonga (Japanese for flying squirl) siblings are leaving the nest, jumping from tree to tree and collecting food. Who will be the first to establish their own nest and win the game?

Players take turn swooping the velt momonga from the tree branch to the landing tree in the center. Your momonga lands successfully if it sticks to the landing tree, which is divided into 3 parts by 2 lines. Depending on where your momonga lands, you either move 1, 2 or 3 spaces on the circle tile surrounding the landing tree. If you land on a tile with a paw print, you collect a food by taking one of the outer circle tokens. If you land on a tile where there is another momonga already, you can steal a food from that player.
There are 5 different kinds of foods and the aim is to collect 4 different types. The place where you gathered the last of the required food tokens will be your nest. Next you have to go around the forest one more time to inform your friends of your new home. The first player to complete the circle and land exactly on their own nest, wins the game.

The game also includes leaf tokens. You can exchange a food token for a leaf token. A leaf token can be played for one of the following two actions:
1) You momonga is allowed to try one more jump.
2) When another player's momonga is making it's jump, you can blow from behind the landing tree to hinder them.

The rulebook contains a few optional rules to change the base game and some other game variants.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from WWE, Superstars of the ring including John Cena, Randy Orton, The Rock...

OBJECT:
Have the highest total score after five rounds of play.

GAME OVERVIEW:
In BID SIX, each round of play begins with one player rolling a set of six white dice and arranging them in a row in the middle of the table.  All players then use their own deck of 30 cards (28 bidding cards and 2 blocking cards) to win as many of the six dice as possible by placing one card next to each die.

The highest bidding card for each die wins that die and its points, unless someone plays a blocking card for that die.  A blocking card blocks ALL players from winning the die, including the person who played the blocking card. Used cards must be discarded at the end of each round.

If a player wins a “TRIPLE” (a group of any 3 dice) in a single round of play, they get to roll 1, 2, or 3 of the black dice for additional bonus points, depending on the numbers on the three dice in their TRIPLE.  Roll 1 bonus die for a BASIC TRIPLE (any three numbers), 2 dice for a STRAIGHT TRIPLE (numbers in sequence), and 3 dice for a THREE-OF-A-KIND TRIPLE (all the same number).

The player or team with the highest score after all five rounds of play (when all cards have been used) wins the game!

Scrapyard Empire is a strategic hand management card game that is centered around collecting parts and small machines in order to complete the greatest inventions ever known to man. Players can dig through the scrapyard, steal from opponents, trade, or draw at random from the decks to achieve their goal.

In this 1-4 player game, players must rely on strategy, memory, and a little bit of luck to beat opponents, or the deck, by creating their inventions first. The solitaire version runs a speedy 10-15 minutes, while a group game runs between 30-60 minutes.

In order to win a player must be the first to build 2-3 inventions, which are each constructed from 3 required small machines. These small machines have special abilities which can be activated to give players a 1 time use advantage, such as being able to steal back a stolen part or small machine. In group games, each player also has a character given to them at random. These characters all have 1 unique special ability that helps give them an edge in the game.

Beowulf: A Board Game is a dynamic tabletop strategy game for 3-6 players, adapted from one of the oldest and most celebrated works of European literature.

The game uses the Anglo-Saxon epic poem as a point of entry into a vast interpretation of the Viking Age. As the legend of Beowulf's life and death unfolds on the game's board, players will trade cards, scheme and raid one another to grow their own kingdom's influence and power. After the death of Beowulf at the fangs of a dragon, the game moves forward in time, as players scramble to preserve the brave legacies of their kingdoms in the face of a quickly changing religious landscape and the relentless tides of history.

The game uses two decks, the WYRD Deck and the DAED Deck. At the beginning of the game each player is dealt a handful of DAED cards - these represent powers, abilities and resources of your individual kingdom. On each turn, a player draws a card from the WYRD Deck, which is composed of monsters, events, hardships, and instances drawn from the source text and the greater Viking Age. The player must either face the consequences, or reap the benefits of these cards, and can use their DAED cards to repel or augment the effect of the WYRD Deck. In addition, players can attempt to raid one another for their DAED cards. Successfully defeating a Monster drawn from the WYRD Deck nets you a Trophy - in the form of some grisly piece of the felled creature - which acts as a counter for your Kingdom's greatness.

All the while, each draw of the WYRD Deck moves Beowulf further along his life path on the game board, with each stop dramatically affecting the stakes of the WYRD Deck. After Beowulf's death, the winner is determined by the player with the most Trophies amassed.

Doggerland Miniature Skirmish Game is a fast-play, skirmish-level fantasy miniature system that has been co-written by S.E. Lantry and Andrea Sfiligoi.

As with Songs of Blades and Glory, it uses an action-point system that requires no book-keeping. The rules are designed to be simple, fast and fun so that a mini campaign of several games can be played in a single evening.

The Basic Game follows closely to the SoBG model, i.e., the game includes statistics for 180+ monsters and heroes, and details to be able to create your own. Also there are campaign rules where your warband grows more powerful after every battle, and six basic scenarios.

Advanced Rules include the use of an expandable and customizable deck of cards used fro initiative, environmental effects, defence and building a result into historic proportions.

The game offers co-operative and competitive play Adventures, and can be played in 15mm and 28mm.

Funding for this Kickstarter project was canceled by the project creator on May 30 2014.

description: Interstellar Crisis is a skirmish miniatures space combat wargame set in the year 5318. It utilizes a unique movement deck and vector movement to simulate how ships would really move in space.

Materials

Huaca is played on the spaces of a 9x9 Sudoku like board that is divided into nine 3x3 sub-boards.

You will also need a supply of black and white stones.

Definitions

Adjacent – means orthogonally adjacent.
Pair - two friendly stones.
Group- Two or more adjacent friendly stones.

Play

Board starts out empty.

Black starts, swap rules apply, then turns alternate.

On a turn take one of the following actions.

1. Drop a pair of stones onto two adjacent empty spaces that are not adjacent to a group or
2. Drop one friendly stone onto an empty space that is either adjacent to an enemy or friendly stone already on the board

The game ends when a player is unable to take his turn.

Goal

A 3x3 board is won by a majority of grouped friendly pieces. The game is won by a majority of won 3x3 boards.

User summary:

A space-ship themed cooperative game. Players are crew on a derelict spaceship that has wandered in in a dangerous space zone. Game is played on the board that represent the space ship, with various stations such as the bridge, engine room, resting room and so on. On their turn, players roll a die to see what threat happens (ship breaks down, asteroids or space slime attacks), then may do one movement and one action; action depending on what station the player is – an action in the sensor room will increase the sensor system, an action on the bridge will navigate the ship.

All character have different stats in various actions, repairing, and endurance points, that may be used to strain the character for extra actions.

The goal of the game is to increase the sensor system enough so that the players manage to navigate out of the dangerous zone. Game is lost if any player dies, if the spaceship collides with an asteroid or if too many space jellyfish cling to the hull.

description:
User summary:

A push-your-luck game with a deck building mechanism.

Players are criminals in a prison and try to dig their way out. All players start with a deck consisting of cards with numbers 0–3 (mostly 0), representing how far they've dug. Each round, players simultaneously reveal the first 3 cards of their deck, and then all players get in turn to choose to either go on digging or give up for the day. Those who give up for the day get a couple of benefits such as getting new cards for their deck and/or removing one of the cards they've revealed. Those who continue must turn over one of four guard cards, risking getting caught. If they get caught, they don't get any benefits for this round. If not caught, they get to reveal another 3 cards; rinse and repeat.

Once all have chosen to give up for the day, players put all revealed cards in any order they choose in their discard pile, and then place their discard pile face down beneath their deck, hence "programming" the deck.

The first player to be able to reveal 20 dig points in one and the same round wins the game.

User summary:

In Pecunia, players are competing to gain as many points as possible in a world of worship, starvation and struggle.

Players start with a hand of 5 cards depicting characters. Each turn, a player plays one of his characters onto an event (worship, starvation, sabbath or war), or decides to take all the individuals on one specific event, to his population. Taking the individuals on an event will quicken the possibility of that event ocurring.

Each time an event happens, players will score points for different individuals in his population, or lose individuals, all depending on what event triggered and what his population looked like.

The player with the most points after a certain amount of rounds wins the game.

description:
User summary

A rather quick card game with a trick-taking mechanism, in which players try to play the lowest of a certain colour in order to score the card they played.

Players have a set of monsters numbered 1–10 on their hands. Each round, the starting player chooses 4 of 8 monster hunters that will hunt down monsters played each trick; these 4 cards are shuffled into a face down pile. Each trick, players play one card each of any colour and number, and then the top monster hunter card is revealed, and any cards that matches the conditions of the monster hunter are discarded. Of the remaining monsters, the lowest number of each colour are score, the rest discarded. Players can also discard cards from their hand to gain their special effects.

After three tricks, the monster hunter cards are re-gathered, re-chosen and the players play three rounds like this; the most points in their score pile wins.

User summary:

A card drafting game in which players try to collect poker hands. Players start with 6 special cards, each with a priority number and a special effect. Each turn the players select one of these cards, and the lowest priority gets to pick one of the five face-up trump cards on the table to add to his collection. The special cards are equipped with a special effect that also happens.

The player with the best poker hand after 6 rounds wins.

シュレーディンガーの猫 (Schrödinger's Cat) is a three-player card game in which the players must manage their hands so that it corresponds with the final outcome for the cat. Players start with eight cards in hand, with the cards being black or white with values 1-4. Playing a black card increases the chances of the cat being dead — and a white card for it being alive — but anything above 100% gets lowered by 100.

After six rounds, players win depending on the outcome of the cat and the two cards each player still has in hand.

This edition of Carcassonne: Wheel of Fortune includes the Bridges, Castles and Bazaars expansion.

Carcassonne: Wheel of Fortune is both a full replacement for the base game of Carcassonne and an expansion to the original base game. It includes 72 tiles, consisting of 63 tiles released in the original base game, 6 from Inns & Cathedrals, 1 from Traders & Builders and 2 from King & Scout. It also includes a special replacement start tile which depicts the Wheel of Fortune, a new mechanic unique to this edition of the game. The new start tile is the size of 16 tiles, arranged in a 4×4 square and is built using some of the tiles removed from the base game.

Nineteen tiles from the game have numbers printed on them allowing players to randomly spin the Wheel of Fate (between 1 and 3 spaces around the wheel). Otherwise, a player may choose to place a follower on the wheel if they decline placing a follower that turn. The results of the Wheel vary depending on which of the sixths of the wheel the pig lands on. Results vary from forcing all players to remove a follower from the board to rewarding the player who moves the pig with three bonus points. There are six outcomes in all.

Play of this game does not require ownership of any Carcassonne base game. All expansions are compatible with this game. This game can also be played with the base game as an expansion, using its wagon wheel watermark to differentiate it from the base game.

Bridges, Castles and Bazaars is the eighth expansion for Carcassonne. It allows players three new options while playing:

Bridges may be used to continue a road over a field tile, thereby allowing an incomplete road to technically run into a field segment. They are otherwise treated and scored just like roads.

Castle tokens are placed atop 2-segment cities and allow players a chance to cash-in on the next completed adjacent feature.

Bazaars are a new type of tile that allows players to auction off tiles in exchange for points.

Swing is a crazy party card game for 3-8 mature players, published in 2014 by the Polish company Let's Play.

In the game, described by the publisher as "mental Kamasutra", the players' aim is to collect 30 Pleasure Points. They earn them by using Sex Positions cards (Actions, Counter-actions, Events and Special cards) to initiate a "virtual" intercourse with another player's character (they can be of different sexes and orientation). Other players can interrupt and prevent earning points by playing Counter-action cards, often with a humorous effect.

For adults only!

boardgame: Monopoly: Geelong Football Club 2007 Premiership Charity Edition
description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Geelong Cats, an Australian Footballcub;limited Edition to raise money for McGuinness McDermott Foundation (now Little Heroes Foundation).

The match box was dropped and all the matches are mixed up. The goal of the game is to find as many matching pairs as possible. The game plays similar to the classic memory game Concentration, but there are a few twists.

Half of the cards will be face down and the rest will be face up. The start player will lay down the cards in a 3x3 grid. Each player should look at the cards as they are being put down, as it is important to remember the cards & placement. Just like in the classic memory game, the players try to find a matching pair. Whoever says "match!" gets a try. When the cards match, that player takes the cards, which will score at the end of the game. If 2 different cards are picked, these go to the discard pile and the player receives one of the yellow burning matches. If no one else sees another match, the remaining cards are reshuffled and the next player places them in the grid.

The game ends when all cards have been claimed, or if the 5 burning matches are in play. The player with the most cards wins.

The goal is to get to "De Admiraal", the municipality building with the least amount of dimes.

Everybody receives a start fund of 5 dimes to play with. If the bank is empty, it means everybody is out of luck until somebody needs to pay taxes again.

The unfortunate one who is broke can beg for money with the "rich" people. If that doesn't happen he or she needs to return a minimum of 15 blocks to a white space.

The game is influenced by a dice, chance cards challenges, tact, charm and experience.

No Shame Charades is the unclassy twist to the classic party game. Perfect for social adults with an unfiltered sense of humor.

The game comes with a 2 minute sand timer, 20 Game Changer cards and 480 phrase cards, each with it's own hilarious definition to help the one person that doesn't know what a "Rusty Trombone" is.
  
Phrases derive from 4 main topic areas: 
- today's pop culture(ish) (ex. "Anchorman", tiger blood, bath salts)
- dark matter (crack baby, kidnap)
- adult stuff (dry hump, walk of shame, morningwood)   
- general funny (brain fart, "sh*t hit the fan", explosive diarrhea)

There are 4 types of Game Changer cards - lose a turn, all play, charade with a beverage in one hand, and reverse the timer.

Each team takes 2 minute turns to act out as many phrase cards possible.  For every phrase correctly guessed, your team earns 1 point. If a card is passed, you lose a point.  At the end of each round, the other teams can earn a point by taking 1 correct guess at a card that was passed during the round.  Watch out for Game Changers - these cards will make you wish you\'ve never been born.  First team to 21 wins!

We stand staring at the chalk outline on the wet sidewalk.
    “Five bucks says it’s Tanos,” Robertson says.
I toss my cigarette to the ground, grind it down the the toe of my shoe.
    “Five bucks it is.”

Charm City Blues is my love letter to the crime noir genre. As members of the elite Vice Squad, you'll be traveling around Charm City in an attempt to build a case and catch your Suspect before the end of the Plot. Charm City Blues is completely cooperative with each player having a personal Vice that effects the way you play the game.

Charm City Blues is played on a 5x4 grid of cards laid out in numerical order to create Charm City. At the start of the game players will choose a Difficulty and Suspect and deal 12 cards from the Plot deck. Then, roll the d20 Clue die 4 times to spawn Clues on the Location cards with the corresponding number. The goal of the game is to build a case and catch the Suspect by filling up the Difficulty and Suspect cards with clues before the Plot deck runs out.

Each round of Charm City Blues is divided into two phases, the Plot phase and the Investigation phase. During the Plot phase the player with the Commissioner token flips the top card of the Plot deck. This card gives a narrative cue which the Commissioner will expand on in telling a few lines of story about the game so far. Then, each player will have a chance to gain clues during their Investigation.

Each player has four abilities: Fight, Arrest, Research, and Interrogate. During your investigations you will test these abilities by rolling the number of d6 listed next to your Ability and trying to match or beat the number required on the Location card. If you succeed, you move the Clue up to the Difficulty or Suspect card and gain some cold hard cash. If you fail, you lose Hit Points or gain Suspicion Points. Lose too many Hit Points or gain too may Suspicion Points and the game is over.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Between The Shadows
description:
“You will never find a more wretched hive of scum and villainy.”
   –Obi-Wan Kenobi, Star Wars: A New Hope

Escape watchful eyes in Between the Shadows, the third deluxe expansion for Star Wars: The Card Game! This expansion features twenty-six new objective sets (two copies each of twelve objective sets, and one copy each of two “Limit 1 per objective deck” sets), focusing on the Jedi and Scum and Villainy affiliations. The Scum and Villainy use the shadows to pursue illicit operations and illegal dealings, while the Jedi stay hidden to escape the persecution of the Empire. Both affiliations have profited from the shadows, and in this expansion, you’ll gain access to their new strengths.

This expansion highlights the Jedi and Scum and Villainy affiliation, allowing you to play iconic characters and starships from the Star Wars universe, including Obi-Wan Kenobi, Luke Skywalker, Boba Fett, and Virago. Whether you train in the Force on the planet Dagobah, or oversee the rise of the Black Sun crime syndicate, you’ll find plenty of cards to expand your decks in Between the Shadows!

Contains objective sets 127-140.

description:
QUICK JOUST

Quick Joust is a fast 1 to 6-player Jousting card game where your knight faces off against another knight, trying to either unhorse him and win the match outright or win by breaking three lances.

The cards represent the Knights, list, the shields and the movement of the lance!  You setup the game by creating the list and as you race down the list you must react to your opponent shield movement and readjust your lance tip with a HARD or FINE PULL to compensate for the movement.  As you reach the point of contact your final lance tip location will determine whether you unhorse your opponent, break your lance, or miss all together!

At the end of the run you can earn points which will add up to decide the Tournament Champion!

1 point for shield contact!
5 points for a broken lance!
10 points for unhorsing your opponent!

Play time: 20 Minutes plus  "Will be much longer if played with 2 or more players!"

Total card count: 92 cards - which includes the Knight Status cards

[2] Playing tokens.

description:
A science vessel lands un an unexplored planet and a group of scientists sets out from the ship to gather data on the planet's history and wildlife, also encountering hostile aliens!

It will be up to you, as one of the science team members, whether to take the chance to collect more Data in the dangerous alien wilds, return to your Ship with the Data you've already collected, or strike out on your own to hunt the Aliens before they hunt you!

Also, when you return to the Ship, afraid to explore further, you can continue to be involved in the Mission by "analyzing" the data you've collected, using it to "bet" on the continuing Mission.

At the end of the game, after five Missions, players compare their Data totals, and the player with the most Data wins.

Strange Planet is a push-your-luck card game with elements of betting and set collection.

Roll with the punches!
There's a rowdy ruckus in the land of Leprechauns. 
Perhaps it's their penchant for porter, or maybe it's because they're giddy for gold. Either way, it's time to play. Pick up your Shillelagh and join in the frey.

Leprechaun is an all out battle for gold. It's a dice rolling game with a bit of press your luck. Players represent Leprechauns gathering gold coins, fighting over gold coins, and bolstering by drinking beer and gathering 4 leaf clovers.

Play consists of drawing 3 dice from the bag, choosing an action showing on the dice and then deciding if they wish to press their "luck of the Irish" by drawing additional dice from the bag, or they may bank what they have already acquired.

Gather, Fight or Bolster your way to the most gold to win.

A quickfire card game based on the classic confidence trick. Keep your ACE hidden while you knock out opponents by guessing theirs.

Each player has three cards on the table placed face down. One ACE card and two DECOY cards. If your ACE card is taken by an opponent you are eliminated. If your opponent guesses a DECOY card then they are eliminated.

Each player has three cards in their hand. Everyone starts with a LOOK and a GUESS card as well as a third card drawn from the deck. Each turn you play only one card. If you choose to LOOK at an opponent's card this turn you will have to wait until your next turn to make a GUESS. Maybe they will use their next turn to try and knock you out, or play a SHUFFLE or BLOCK card to keep their ACE safe.

It’s a well-worn story by now in the Land of Danger: An army attacking a castle. Somehow, though, the story keeps happening, and another bellicose mob has advanced on the imposing walls of someone else’s keep. The clash to control those walls is at hand. Lead the Blue attackers or the Red defenders, using clever card play and a touch of luck to control more sections of the wall than your opponent

The game consists of 54 cards -- 5 walls, a Red personal deck of 8 cards, a Blue personal deck of 8 cards, and a main deck of 33 cards.

Players take turns playing Archer, Cannon, Attack, Leader and Flag cards from their hands to the different walls. Archers & Cannons each have a strength value, and the player with the most total strength at a wall controls it. Attacks remove the opponent's cards, Leaders each have a unique ability that impacts how strength is calculated at a wall, and Flags lock down the control of a wall and trigger the end game.

The player controlling the most walls at the end of the game is the winner.

Enter a world where every decision you make will have lasting consequences. Customize the board, your faction and your strategies!

Factions has been designed to provide you with an accessible yet deep, highly customizable, tabletop wargame. You take on the role of a military leader in one of four (base game) competing Factions and will need all your planning, tactics and cunning to dominate in this deeply immersive game. The many levels of customization available in FACTIONS means the replay value is extremely high, and every game brings a new set of challenges for you to enjoy.

boardgame: Lacrosse Blast Pro Indoor Lacrosse Game
description:
Lacrosse Blast is an "old-school" card-chart-and-dice simulation of pro lacrosse. Primarily designed as a solitaire game for sports board game hobbyists, it can also be played head-to-head, with each player making decisions for his team.

From the publisher:

We've taken our original lacrosse game, Crash and Scramble, and made it even better by adapting it to the same game engine that powers our popular hockey game, Hockey Blast. The result is a fast-playing, fun board game simulation of this cutting-edge pro sport that captures everything fans love about it: bone-jarring hits, acrobatic passes, rocket-fast shots, and amazing saves. YOU can re-create all the thrilling action of this "ahead-of the curve" sport, right in your own home, with Lacrosse Blast Pro Indoor Lacrosse Game!

Lacrosse Blast uses a possession-by-possession system of play that keeps things moving fast. The game uses the same easily-memorized chart structure and no-nonsense method of rating players as our hockey game. Players perform based on the presence--or absence--of various qualities like speed, power and skill. Each player is also rated for his ability in creating and/or taking away scoring opportunities, as well as for shots and assists, penalties, face-offs and injury. That means you'll get table-top performances which echo each player's real-life abilities without a bunch of calculations or math gymnastics. You'll see the great assist guys dishing it off for goals and the powerhouse shooters firing the ball into the net, while the less-talented players defer to someone else. You'll be caught up in the action as you "watch" players use their power, skill and savvy to make plays. Goalies have their own special ratings, designed to make the real-life star net-minders shine on your table top the just way they do on the pro league floor! Easy-to-play, but amazingly detailed--THAT'S Lacrosse Blast Pro Indoor Lacrosse simulation board game!

Kickstarter Exclusive Means of Power Expansion

Four new special action cards that will help vary each game and give it greater replayability.

This set of 17 additional cards for Incredible Expeditions: Quest for Atlantis includes even more content inspired by the writings of H.P. Lovecraft. Add to the base game to bring more madness, Curses and Supernatural influence to Quest for Atlantis.

Take charge of your expedition as the academic-turned-cultist Dr. Psyrri. Enchant your Crew with the haunting violin song of the Musician. Face off against Shoggoths and Deep Ones in your quest to discover the secrets of R'lyeh.

boardgame: Incredible Expeditions: 1,001 Tales of Betrayal Mini-Expansion
description:
This expansion adds 17 new cards based on the Tales of the Arabian Nights to Incredible Expeditions: Quest for Atlantis. Combine with the base game for more gorgeous clockwork devices and opportunities to backstab your opponents.

Lead your expedition as the ruthlessly creative Sandbad Al-Basrah. Pull your Crew by the purse-strings with the help of the Treasurer of Kings. Face down the legendary Roc and attack your opponents head-on with Piracy to beat them to the treasures of the legendary City of Brass.

The time has come to claim your prize from the Free Captains' Regatta: the supposedly uninhabited Island of Empty Eyes. The ruins prove to be a bit more inhabited — and dangerous — than they might have first appeared. In the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Island of Empty Eyes Adventure Deck, you must tame the island, throw a rollicking feast, and gain seats on the Pirate Council.

This 110-card expansion includes new rare loot cards and five new scenarios that make up the complete "Island of Empty Eyes" adventure, along with new monsters, locations, villains, and more. Continue your character's legend with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Island of Empty Eyes Adventure Deck.

description:
Zombie Invasions is a Zombie Apocalypse Survival Horror Combat System developed by Frugal Rock Games. This board game allows for 2 - 5 players to take on the role of either Survivors or the Zombie Master in a strategic turn based fight for survival.

Survivors must employ their wits and a selection of weapons in order to defeat wave after wave of shambling nightmares. Meanwhile the Zombie Master who takes control of the horde must use superior numbers and devastating attacks to bring the Survivors down at any cost.

A game or 'wave' consists of two distinct phases. The Encounter and the Post Encounter.

The game is designed to bring fun, strategic thinking and competitiveness to the table top by employing robust Action Point and Health Point systems, as well as the scoring of Earned Points which allow Survivors and the Zombie Master to buy new weapons or zombies.

Zombie Invasions is designed for consecutive games, 'waves' to be played for as long as the Survivors can live up to their name.

Expansion to the base game which allows the Acrobatic Cat variant to be played, as well as a standalone game using just the components of the expansion.

The standalone game is for 2 players and comprises of the stacking mechanic of the original game. The actions on the dice are different though, and there aren't any cat tokens in the game.

For playing the Acrobatic Cat variant you need the components of the original game. Apply stickers from the Plus game to the backsides of the cat tokens from the original game. The stickers contain special acrobatic actions for the cats to perform.

The cards from Cat Tower Plus can also be used to play the original game with up to 6 players.
