boardgame: Zombies Keep Out: Night of the Noxious Dead
description: The goblin bodgers’ workshop is on the verge of being overrun by a teeming mob of zombies—and now foul Stinkers have joined the ranks of the shambling dead! The stench of these vile zombies is so strong it practically glows, forcing even other zombies to keep their distance. Luckily the bodgers have a stockpile of new parts to build canny new defense contraptions. That might be just what they need to make it through the night!

boardgame: Forlorn Struggles: Operations Gemse and Südwind
description:
Forlorn Struggles is a DualPak game. It comes with two separate games in one package.

Operation Südwind is a two-player/solitaire game recreating the mid-February 1945 German offensive against the Soviet Bridgehead across the Garam River, in what is now southwestern Slovakia. It commenced on the morning of February 17. The German attack spearheads included a total of 44 formidable Tiger II tanks. Subsidiary attacks from across the Danube helped weaken the Soviet positions. 7th Guard Army defended stubbornly but was barely reinforced and by February 24th the Germans cleared the bridgehead. The Germans won a clear-cut operational victory but they incurred hard-to-replace losses. In the grand scheme of things Operation Südwind was a localized, and only temporary setback ,for the Soviets.

Operation Gemse is a two-player/solitaire game recreating the fierce early March German offensive in Oberlausitz and lower Siliesia against the unsuspecting Soviet 3rd Guard Tank Army commanded by General Rybalko. Securing Lauban and the surrounding area was deemed critical for a possible relief of encircled Breslau. However the planned grand encirclement anchored on Naumburg soon proved too ambitious. Instead on March 5 the Führer-Grenadier and Führer-Begleit Panzer divisions completed their reduced pincer movement and met up in Sächsisch-Haugsdorf. By March 7th, the encircled Soviet 7th Guard Tank Corps was almost completely destroyed.

Anti-hero is a solitaire Print & Play game which has RPG style character development elements.  The player takes on the role of a fledging superhero/villain created by a freak accident at a local nuclear facility. Your character is just getting to grips with his new skills and powers which he has acquired through the mutation of his genes.

The accident is kept quiet and your character is the only survivor. The radiation has made you smarter and more dangerous than the humans in this city. The city officials must pay for what has happened to you and your colleagues, but no one wants to hear your story, not the Police Commissioner, the District Attorney or the Mayor, but you will make them pay for what they have done.

The game uses a combination of dice to create your powers, skills gained through experience points and actions which will see the player, rob banks, hire goons, investigate and blackmail city officials. Can you take control of the city before you are stopped? You are the anti-hero.

"¡Santiago y Cierra, España!" is a solitaire wargame on the Reconquista in the Iberian Peninsula.

The player is in charge of the Christian Kingdoms that tried to recover the peninsula from Islamic control. He begins in a few regions of the north, with initial troops. Each turn he decides where to attack, places the reinforcements he receives, being able to choose which type of troops are send. Leaders are an important aspect of the game, and will be appearing throughout the game, and may be eliminated by combat or a natural death by old age. The IA will try to stop our progress, replacing the cities we attack, creating troops near conflict zones, which can counterattack if you do not plan your defense well. After the last turn of the game, your level of victory or defeat depends on the number of zones you've managed to reconquer.

Available as Vassal module, will be available in Print & Play as future (along this 2014), both free versions. (Rules English).


Wargame en solitario sobre la Reconquista en la península Ibérica.
Nos pondremos al mando de los reinos cristianos, que intentaran expulsar y recuperar la peninsula del control Islamico.
Comenzaremos en unas pocas regiones del norte, con unas tropas iniciales.
Cada turno deberemos de ir decidiendo donde atacar, e ir colocando los refuerzos que recibamos, pudiendo elegír en que tipo de tropas gastarlos.
Los lideres son un aspecto importante del juego, e iran apareciendo a lo largo del juego, pudiendo ser eliminados por el combate o de muerte natural por vejez.
La IA del juego irá intentando detenernos en nuestro avance, reponiendo las ciudades que ataquemos, creando tropas cerca de las zonas en conflicto, y pudiendo llegar a hacer contrataque si no eres capaz de planificar bien tu defensa.
Al acabar los turnos del juego, tu nivel de victoria o derrota dependerá del numero de zonas que has logrado reconquistar.

Disponible como modulo de Vassal, a lo largo de este 2014 estará disponible como Print & Play, ambas versiones libres.

description:
Hex Survival is a tactic game of survival set in fantasy world of Moffee, where you'll have to be the last man standing.

PLOT 
In order to complete his masterpiece, the perfect Zombie, Dr.Skelrt trapped Moffee's heroes in his laboratory. In order to survive, the heroes will have to fight each other and avoid zombification: only the last man (or woman, or orc, or boar) standing will earn freedom!

 GAMEPLAY 
Control your hero pawns and kill your adversaries while escaping their zombie version and collecting treasure cards, in a randomly-built HEXangonal map full of bonuses and maluses.
You'll have to collect treasure cards in order to attack your fellow adversaries or heal your own zombified pawns, to fire your gun, stab your opponents' back, trick, bite and infect them, and a lot more.
Being zombified hero is not the end, but just another beginning.

 OBJECTIVE 
Eliminate all your opponents and be the last survivor at the end of a round.

Infinito is an abstract game for two player with a theoretical infinite moves per turns, i.e. theoretical infinite branching factor, but with finite number of turns. A finite version of the game is Myriades.

Infinito is played on a square board, preferably 8×8 or larger. One player owns the black stones, the other player owns the white stones. Gray neutral stones are needed.

Each player has an infinite number of stones, each printed with a unique natural number: 0, 1, 2, 3, and so on…

Players move alternately, starting with the player controlling the white stones. Each turn consists of two actions, performed in this order:

1. OPTIONAL MOVE: you can move a stone like a Queen in Chess, i.e. any number of squares horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.
If your stone ends its move (orthogonally or diagonally) next to one or more enemy stones whose value is lower than your stone, and your stone was not next to those enemy stones at the start of the move, replace any of your stones on the board — except the one you just moved — with a neutral gray stone marked “∞”.
If you end the move next to multiple enemy stones with a lower value, you must replace one of your stones for each of them. When you remove your stones from the board, they return to your supply and may be placed again later.

2. COMPULSORY PLACEMENT: you must place a stone onto any empty square.

The game ends when the board is full. The player with the lowest total wins.

Myriades is the little brother of the game Infinito, a game with a theoretical infinite number of moves per turn.

Myriades is a two-player game, played on a 10x10 square board.
 First player own the black stones, second player the white stones. Each player has 50 stones, numbered from 0 to 49. Such numbers represents the value of the stones.

Grey stones are neutral, without value and, once placed on the board, they are immovable.

Players move alternately, starting with the player controlling the white stones. Each turn consists of two actions, performed in this order:

1. OPTIONAL MOVE: you can move a stone of your color as a Queen’s moves in Chess, i.e. any number of cells horizontally, vertically or diagonally.
If your stone ends its move (orthogonally and diagonally) next to one enemy stone whose value is less, and your stone wasn’t (orthogonally and diagonally) next to that enemy stone to start its move, replace any friendly stone – but the stone just moved – on the board with a neutral gray stone. In one move you can end up close to more enemy stones whose value are less than your stone, in that case you replace your stones with neutral stones for each of those stones.
When you remove your stones from the board, those stones will be available for future placements.

2. COMPULSORY PLACEMENT: you must place a stone onto any empty space.

The game ends when the board is full, whoever has the least sum of his values wins.

Pair of Ducks is a 2-4 player game of hidden roles. However, unlike other hidden role games, the player whose identity you don’t know is your own! Players wear their character card on their forehead.

Be the first player to get to 5 points by figuring out who you are. You can see who the other players are, and who they are affects how you play the game. For instance, if you see the Angel character, you must answer any question asked of you honestly. If you see the Ninja character card, you must not answer questions verbally (instead, use silent hand gestures and/or basic grunting). Based upon the way other players are answering questions, you should be able to figure out what character you are!

Cat Tower Lite is a 2 player version of the original Cat Tower game. Cats cards have to be stacked in special ways as dictated by the dice. The first player to have placed all his cards is the winner.

Cat Tower Lite has fewer cards than the original game. It also doesn't have the cat tokens of the big game. The dice is different too and has different actions for the players to perform.

Second issue game of Wargame Japanese History #21.

This game uses the same rule and map as "Aterui saga".  But it uses different counters.

This campaign is less popular even in Japan.  It started in 1051 more than 200 years after Aterui saga.  And it ended in 1062.
Basically, this campaign was conducted by the central government against an unloyal Abe-clan.

The Visitors are coming, hellbent on world domination.

The Visitors is a cooperative 1 to 4 player game where the players are sent to a haunted manor with the objective of stop any manifestation of their arrival. Players take turn eliminating the haunting from the rooms of the manor and dealing with whatever Visitor that might appear.

The game ends after all the Visit cards have been used, meaning that they have successfully managed to stop their invasion. It will also end if 3 Visitors are present in the board at the same time, as they have become too strong to be defeated now.

Players are allocated 3 action points on their turn, and can choose to move to an adjacent room or to use a trinket in order to destroy a manifestation. Each Visitor brought on to the board makes the game harder as more Visit cards have to be drawn, with the extra effect of speeding up gameplay.

With a modular board and rules that allow for difficulty tweaking, no two playthroughs will be the same!

description:
A solo print-n-play game of tomb-raiding and jungle exploration.

Enter the temple, grab the Golden Idol and make it back to the landing site before the angry natives and hungry jungle critters catch you!

Raiders in my Pocket is a modification of Zombie in my Pocket, with a re-implemented theme and some rules variations to reinforce the new theme. Most significant of these is the special 4th time period "Storm", which triggers when you steal the Golden Idol and anger the ancient gods of the temple, starting a doomsday clock until darkness takes over... will you make it back to the plane on time?

Rcommended background music: The Indiana Jones Theme tune, which coincidentally lasts about as long as it takes to play :)

Dilemma is a two-player strategy game in which players compete by moving pawns around the board to collect tiles worth varying points.

The 49 tiles are placed in a preset pattern on a 7x7 grid, and two pawns are placed on top of two tiles in the central diagonal. One player can move the pawns along columns, while the other moves them along rows. Players cannot move the same pawn they moved the previous turn, so strategy is built around knowing which choices your opponent has next, and working to make those choices miserable — thus putting them in a dilemma. Some of the tiles affect the board when collected: moving tiles or pawns, returning pieces to the board, or stealing tiles from an opponent. When neither player can move, the game ends and whoever has the most points wins.

Skillful maneuvering and forethought are the name of the game in this engaging, classical board game.

Bunnies vs Zombies is a board game in which carrots and brains are your currency, and cuteness counts for nothing! Hordes of flesh-eating zombies have risen from their graves in search of brains, and a village of cute cuddly bunnies is on the menu. With the annual carrot harvest fast approaching, an assault from the undead could not have come at a worse possible time! Without the carrot harvest, the cute wittle bunnies will all starve. There is only one thing left to do! FIGHT BACK!.

Bunnies vs Zombies is a standalone board game that includes everything you need to survive the ensuing zombie apocalypse! Featuring over 35 detailed game pieces, 7 game modes, a fully illustrated game board, game cards, tokens, barriers, and rulebook.

Tschu-tschu-tschu...the railroad is a game with a colour die. It contains 5 wooden tiles depicting locomotives, 15 tiles depicting railroad passenger wagons and 4 smaller tiles depicting conductors. And then there is the 6-sided colour die.

From the publishers description on the back side of the game box: Tschu-tschu-tschu... the railroad is a colour die game made from wood. When the locomotive is steaming and ready the train be quite quick. Two or more build a common train or build their own one. Made from wood, so childrens hands can have a strong grip too. The game: From the wooden tiles coloured trains are built. The die sets the colour so everyone has a chance in this fast-paced tile laying game made out of wood.

The rulebook suggests two games: In one game one locomotive is put in the middle of the table. The wagons are shuffled and dealt to the players. The players take turns rolling the die and then adding to the locomotive if they have rolled the appropriate colour or black which is a joker in this game. The first player getting rid of all his/her wagon tiles is the rounds winner and gets a conductor tile. After 4 rounds the game ends, the player with the most conductors wins.

In a second variant each player gets a locomotive and the wagon tiles are put in the middle of the table. Then there must be an agreement which train will be the winning one: The longest one, the most colourful on or one in a single colour. Players take turns rolling the die and taking a wagon of the rolled colour. The rulebook is unclear if taking a wagon is optional or compulsory in this variant.

The players move on the game board through several levels, where they have to answer questions from different themed game cards or follow the instructions on the Event Cards. If a Player answers a question correctly, he continnues till he doesn't know the correct answer to a question or an Event Card ends his move. Movement is determined by a die. The first player to reach the center of the game board, wins the game.

This game was produced as a promotional giveaway for the Vivento Customer Services GmbH, a subsidiary company of Deutsche Telekom AG.

Asterisk is an abstract connection game in which players aim to create several connections at once. It is a descendant of the Game of Y.

Each player has three goals: to connect the three sides of the board with pieces of his or her own color (dark or light), shape (hexagonal or circular), and marking (asterisked or empty). But none of the pieces will serve all three of a player's goals, so every piece played potentially benefits the opponent. There are a limited number of pieces of each type.

The winner is the player to make two or more of his or her connections.

The Rock'em Sock'em Robots Dice Game tries to emulate the fast action of the Rock'em Sock'em Robots of old, with players rolling their dice as quickly as they can to get the same face on all dice — colors or icons, depending on a flip of the match coin. Whoever does this first flips one of the opponent's face-down life markers face up. Two of these markers are blank, and the other three have hits; if all three of your hits are revealed, you lose.

If you've been hit, you can try to roll "block" on all your dice. If you do, flip one of your face-up markers face down again, then shuffle it with your remaining face-down markers.
family: Series: Soda Pop Can Dice Game (Mattel)
Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
It is said that angels poured courage into a seed, then planted it into the soil of the human world, and that is how sunflowers came to be.

And so it happens that on a beautiful island a student-led social movement breaks out. If only they can find the legendary sunflower that hides great power within him, the movement can prevail, and so the student leader will use any means necessary to find it; but the government is dispatching police to hinder him, spread disinformation and sow confusion. What kind of future will the island have?

Who is the Sunflower is a microgame in which two secret teams face off, trying to discover each other's identity. Each player gets an identity card. Some characters need to identify certain other character (e.g. Student wants to find Sunflower), while others need to be identified by certain character (Banana wants to be found by the Student). After some discussion, players that need to find another character can attempt to guess who that character is. If this fulfills any player's victory condition, the round ends, with those players scoring a point each.

Dolly's Bookworm is the book lover's game of hide and seek! Like 20 Questions for books, the objective of the game is to find the Bookworm hidden by the Librarian somewhere in a bookshelf of over 30 books.  Players ask Yes or No questions to remove books from the shelves, narrowing the choices until the Bookworm is found. With simple rules, three shelves, four Bookworms, over 65 titles to pick from, and player questions limited only by your imagination, no two games will ever be the same!

Pre-readers can ask questions about the younger titles' colors, locations, and pictures. They can also use the icons to know if there is magic in the game and if the main characters are male, female, or animals.

Older players can test themselves by asking detailed plot and character questions about the older titles. They can also hide multiple bookworms, add a timer, and/or leave the books on the shelf for a serious mental challenge!

Investars is a collaborative game, where players work together to collectively achieve Big Goals, accumulate money in their retirement savings account, use their individual salaries to Invest in companies, and try to get all their pieces back Home to retire and win.

Investars is played in rounds, each simulating a decade in your life.  The team starts in their 20s with a salary, multiple Stock Tokens to invest in companies, and empty savings accounts.  Every round starts with paying salaries, doubling the value of each savings account, and rolling the dice to find which companies pay off investments - or potentially go out of business. If companies go out of business, their tiles are removed from the game, potentially blocking players' paths to the Big Goals and Home. Then, it is the team’s turn, and each player makes several actions toward winning the game: Move, Invest, Achieve, Save, or Withdraw.

With good planning, lots of teamwork, and a bit of luck, players will achieve their Big Goals and retire in style as successful Investars!

As a bonus, while you're having fun playing Investars, you'll also learn a bit about compound interest, savings accounts, and planning for long-term goals. In short, you'll learn how to be better investors!

Your grandfather started a salt manufacturing business fifty years ago. After thriving for many years, the salt business is now in trouble. Faced by tough competition and a lack of good management, there's a risk of business going bankrupt. It's up to you to take up this challenge and save this family business.

Salt Merchant is a card game of business, luck, math and strategy. Each player becomes a salt merchant and tries to outplay other players and become the first to set up the maximum number of salt manufacturing factories and hence earn maximum money. Your challenges are to acquire good labor and factories, dig more salt water wells, and automate your factories. There's tough competition, too, so you have to be smart, make the right moves, and set-up factories of all different types if you want to regain lost glory.

This card game simulates a dungeon crawl if the entire party was made up of thieves.

Each player is a thief and plays a Chamber card which gives some information about the next room the party is about to enter. This card is played face down and the current player describes its contents, generally by lying. Each player then plays a card face down to express how they intend to deal with the upcoming room, and then everything is revealed.

The players accumulate treasures which equate to victory points and also give each player some special powers depending on the treasure captured.

Double-sided promo card for Galactic Strike Force: one side depicting the Swangee Automaton Tech card, the other side showing the Broodax Repositor Ship card.

The Swangee Automaton allows you to swap the attack and defense values of an opposing ship; and the Broodax ship will automatically move to the sector with the fewest player ships.

These cards were a Kickstarter bonus.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Light and Shadow is a small-box expansion to BattleCON (about 120 cards or so) that includes higher-powered versions of all Fighters and the cards necessary to play them. These can be used for 2v1 or 3v1 games, or you can use this expansion to duel two of these super-fighters against each other one-on-one.

The box will contain powered-up Unique Ability cards for each fighter in BattleCON: War of Indines and BattleCON: Devastation of Indines, as well as four new sets of bases: 2 sets of "EX" bases (used with EX ability cards, making a fighter roughly twice as strong as a normal character) and 2 sets of "Almighty" bases (used with Almighty ability cards, making a fighter roughly three times as strong as normal).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Town Center, players build a city – in particular, the town center. They add cubes on their personal board and try to arrange them as best as possible in order to score the most victory points. Each cube represents a different type of module. Flats, shops, offices, generators, lifts, car parks, town hall can be built and stacked during the course of the game. Each module generates influence on adjacent land and on cubes directly below or above.

Each round, players will draft two cubes of various colors, build them on their game board, then eventually stack them in order to make towers according to the building rules. If the players have done their job well, some modules will evolve, becoming bigger in three dimensions. The last phase is an income phase in which players gain money from the shops and parking lots if they are supplied with electricity.

The bigger and higher your city is, the more victory points players will have at the end of the game, which lasts ten rounds – but do not forget to provide electricity to all your flats, shops, and lifts to make them more efficient.


Changes in the fourth edition:

Has a new alternative (two by four) map printed on the reverse of the boards.

Contains the Central Park solo board, previously released separately as the Manhattan t-shirt, on the back of the central board.

Has a bigger box that now fits all the cubes comfortably, and has space for expansions.

Has a bigger rulebook, in separate languages, with updated rules, including rules for up to 8 players to play together by combining two sets of the game.

16 player cubes in 8 colors in each box to allow for games with more than 4 players, by combining 2 copies of Town Center.

Revamped player maps with wider streets that are compatible with future planned expansions.

Added central board with a track around it, a simpler method to track income and points.

Improved player aid that now includes Phase V (in Editions 1–3, Phases IV and V were both encompassed in Phase IV) and more detail in Phase I explanation.

Doubled the size of the cloth bag - now all cubes can all be stored in it without spilling out, and there's space for enough cubes for 5-8 players to be pulled from it.

Note: You need two copies of the same edition* (or a copy of the 3rd edition and a copy of the 4th edition) to play with up to 8 players. Each copy contains enough building cubes and player boards for up to 4 players.

* 3rd and 4th edition are the only 2 editions with the same size of cubes. 1st edition's much smaller cubes, 2nd editions LEGO bricks, and Deluxe edition's way larger cubes are not compatible with the 3rd and 4th edition cubes; the 5–8-player rules require all players to select their cubes from the same draft towers.

In Infected, you and your friends play human characters as they encounter a pack of wounded infected.  You manage to kill them off but a few of your buddies got bit and are starting to turn.

At the start of each turn, you roll dice corresponding to your weapon or mutation. The dice show the following three symbols: Card, Heal, and Attack.

There are five card types that will add additional gear, weapons and benefits.

In order to win the game, your group must eliminate the opposing side.

Inspiration: Bang!, Shadow Hunter, King of Tokyo

This version of Dominion: Intrigue is an expansion that requires the Dominion base game or Dominion: Base Cards to play.

In Dominion: Intrigue (as with Dominion), each player starts with an identical, very small deck of cards. In the center of the table is a selection of other cards the players can "buy" as they can afford them. Through their selection of cards to buy, and how they play their hands as they draw them, the players construct their deck on the fly, striving for the most efficient path to the precious victory points by game end.

Part of the Dominion series.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Ausnahmezustand is the German edition of Nightfall: Martial Law. It is an expansion for Nightfall. It is not playable without the base game. It contains 207 cards opposed to the 312 cards of Martial Law. Martial Law is a standalone game, playable on its own.

Nightfall is a deck-building game featuring direct head-to-head combat.

Before the game begins, there is a draft to determine which cards are available for purchase, and by whom. During this draft players select two cards from the set for their personal archives, and they also select cards to put into the common area. The cards drafted into personal archives may only be purchased by the player who drafted them. After the table has been set with the private and common cards, players begin play.

The meat of Nightfall is in the chaining mechanic. Each card has a main color and two linking colors. If you can match the main color of a card to one of the linking colors of the previous card, you can chain those cards together. Once a chain is started, players all get an opportunity to link additional cards onto the chain during that turn.

Cards in the chain resolve in reverse order: first in, last out. Instant effects fire off as cards come off the chain, doing damage to your opponents or bringing characters into play to defend you and attack your opponents.

The object of Nightfall is to put wounds into your opponents’ decks and end the game with the least amount of wounds in your deck. But beware: the more wounds a person has, the more enraged they become and the faster their deck works.

Twizmo! is made up of two parts.  A Twizmo cube, which is a twistable puzzle cube with 9 faces per side, and a deck of square cards.  In Twizmo Original, the faces and cards are made up of 6 symbols.  The 54 cards have 9 of each symbol, as does the Twizmo cube.

Twizmo! Original’s simple rules bely the use of spatial and deductive reason along with strategy and a little bit of luck needed for a win.  Its thoughtful pace is great for bringing young and old minds together and its universal appeal transcends language and culture.

Twizmo! is all about creating sets of three matching icons.  Players take turns twisting the cube, with the goal of creating “three-in-a row” groups of similar icons on the top panel.  A “3-in-a-row” set on the cube allows a player to collect all of the visible cards with the matching icon.

When players have collected 3 cards with the same icon, they may turn that set face down; that card set is “closed” and cannot be taken by another player.

To win the game, collect the most card sets, or at any time during the game, collect a set of all nine matching cards for an instant win!

It's all about the big bucks with Big Buck's Football!

It’s time for your favorite team to play—all your friends are over and ready to watch—everyone has their predictions—it’s time to put your money where your mouth is—see who knows the game of football best. To win, correctly predict which play is going to be next. Each play is another chance to show your football knowledge and win BIG BUCKS!

Any American Football game will do. Game play is based on the actions of the teams and the results of each play and/or drive.

The player with the most Big Bucks at the end of the game, wins.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Game description from the publisher:

What will come of one Runner's hunt for the net's legendary source protocols? The answer to this question is embedded within the core of All That Remains, the fifth Data Pack in the "Lunar Cycle" for Android: Netrunner. Its sixty new cards (three copies of each new card) include a new Criminal Runner identity, a new shard of the source protocols, and a host of assets designed to support the ambitions and secrecy of those Corporations that have the most investment in lunar development.

Additionally, a number of other cards ensure that every action ripples outward and spurs new responses. You’ll find a trio of Corp agendas with powerful "when scored" abilities and trace cards that can hit the Runner even if their traces fail.

Data never dies. It just takes deadly new twists. Explore the ends to which they lead in All That Remains.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Secrets and Schemes
description:
Game description from the publisher:

"Lysa knew more than she had dared to put in her letter. She might have the very proof that Ned needed to bring the Lannisters to ruin, and if it came to war, they would need the Arryns and the eastern lords who owed them service." –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

Secrets and Schemes is the first Chapter Pack in the "Wardens" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game.

Lords and ladies across the Seven Kingdoms have fought for the Iron Throne. Many have died. Still others have held back, aloof and apart. In Secrets and Schemes, you will discover sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) that touch upon the schemes these patient individuals have concocted. Additionally, you’ll learn how they make their opening moves with a host of new Knights, attachments, and the agenda, Aloof and Apart, which encourages players to revisit some of the game’s most expensive, powerful, and recognizable characters.

As the first chapter of the Wardens cycle, Secrets and Schemes also begins its exploration of the House Arryn trait and the introduction of the Castle plot type, which offers powerful rewards, but only when your plot enters the used plot pile. Additionally, a number of card introduce powerful new “Limited Response” abilities, which add new dimensions to this game term by moving it to the center of more meaningful decisions.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
A shaky game dexterity game for 2-4 players from 5 years old.

The turtle is taking a nap on a rock, but five little rascals are having a party on the turtles back. The game contains two very similar variants:
- All five figures start on the turtle and the spinner determines which animal has to be moved. If the turtle tips over you lose a token. Once you lost both tokens you are out of the game.
- All figures start on the table. the spinner determines which animal must be move on/off the turtle but only blue fields may be used. f the turtle tips over you lose a token. Once you lost both tokens you are out of the game.

The Animal Farm is a team competitive board game based on an animal farm where team must try to enclose animal in our fence , collect and sell resources to be the winner .

The Animal Farm is a 2v2 or 1v1 game that will prove your ability to understand your partner's strategy without talking to him. In the meantime, you must protect yourself from the other team that attempts to overthrow your plans.

A game of The Animal Farm is divided in 20 rounds, each one consisting of four turns. Every turn, a player can perform one action: Play an Action Card , Draw some Action Cards , use Nursery to create a new animal, put a wood fence on the board (here players can enclose available animals), collect a resource generated by an owned animals or go to the market where they can sell resources or satisfy orders.

By selling goods or satisying orders in the market, a player collects Victory Point for his team. At the end of the game, the team that has the most Victory Point wins the game.

There are worlds that are ruled by creatures of the sky, great beasts of mythology that have never known anything but dominance.  Generations ago, a rift opened up between these worlds and they became infested with invisible portals that led from one to the other but not necessarily back.  The result was a macrocosm that could not be mapped and in which no one could settle.  Now, thrust together, these great creatures have been locked in an infinite war, for each species believes that it is its world that is the primary and its right to take control of the new Empyrean.

As a player of Titans of Empyrean, you control a squad of 4 titans placed on a tiny hex battlefield in the sky.  Every moving, it is your responsibility to maneuver your titans into positions of opportunity and advantage.  Each unique titan has its own special ability and is armed with a deck of maneuver cards for use throughout the game.  But be careful.  While these powerful maneuvers can be the key to victory, they also double as lifeblood of your titans.  Without them, your titan's wounds cannot be healed.

Position your titans.

Manage your maneuvers.

Attack!

Will your titans control the new Empyrean?

Super KGB contains both Project CIA: The Secret Formula and Project KGB: The Double Agent.

Called "Spy Training Games", they were originally issued in 1973 by House of Games Corporation Limited (Bramalea, Ontario, Canada). The two board games are distinct, different, and have different rules. The two board games can be united together and played following the instructions as "The Deluxe Game" or "Super KGB".

In "Project CIA: the secret formula", 17 envelopes are scattered in designated spots on the board game. One of the 17 envelopes has the secret formula. By rolling the dice, players move around the board game and as they pass over the positions where the envelopes are, they pick up the tile and see if it has the secret formula printed on the bottom of the tile. The winner of the game is the one that exits the board game from one of its four entrance/exit gates.

There is a clear moving piece representing a security guard that moves around from one room to another room. If a player finds himself inside a room with the security guard, he/she has one turn to exit the room.

In Game 2, you're either an agent trying to identify the double agent, or you're the latter, sabotaging the training camp. Deception and deduction will keep you scrambling!

Super KGB is what you get when you combine both games 1 and 2. Waddingtons included extra pieces in both sets so you could play a Deluxe version of either game using both boards. Super KGB can be played by up to six, whereas the component games are limited to four.

This is the version from Waddington's with both games in one box.

Spurs and Sprockets: Revolutionary Decks is a stand alone game for 2-3 players. It includes 3 new Spurs and Sprockets decks and a new set of rules that will allow players to combine it with the basic Spurs and Sprockets decks to allow for 4, 5 and 6 players to play at the same time. The game decks also include artwork used with the new Flanking rules.

Spurs and Sprockets is a perfect information, abstract strategy game. Players have eight matching tiles that they take turns placing orthogonally into a grid, in an attempt to link three or four in a row. There is no board in Spurs and Sprockets so the grid grows organically, based on the placement of tiles. The grid also varies in size and dimensions based on the number of players.

Spurs and Sprockets sets comes with sixteen tiles and rules for many different ways of playing, ranging from a simple Tic-Tac-Toe-like game where players gain 8 points for four in a row and 3 points for each set of three in a row, to a game where every placement of a tile may result in an attack and removal of another tile.

Heifer Heist is a family friendly board game that gives a spin on the classic alien-cownapping tale. Aliens who have crash landed on Earth find themselves in a need of organic material to power their tools to fix their ships, and fortunately they've landed in a field of cows! Of course, the farmer isn't pleased about the aliens "borrowing" his cattle. The aliens must dodge the farmer while racing to be the first to gather 10 cows and escape. The players must guide the aliens through the farm, collecting cows and returning them to their own ships. The farm is a set of tiles, initially upside down, which are revealed as the players progress. The game allows for both competitive and cooperative play.

Survivors are trying to brave the treacherous waters while stranded in the ocean awaiting their helicopter rescue, are you here to save them? NO! You're here to drown them!

Survivors are held up in a structure and players flick dice at the structure hoping to drown as many as possible.

There are 4 rounds and each person drowned is worth a different amount of points. Score the most points by the end of the 4th round and win the game.

Rough Waters Ahead is a print-on-demand dexterity game available on The Game Crafter's website.

boardgame: Alfred the Great: The Great Heathen Army 871AD – 3D Wargame
description:
A two player or solitaire game that uses Hex, dice and miniatures, a game with complexity on the lower end of the scale that has the Vikings invading Wessex in 871AD, players will have to consider when and where to fight, re-supply and other challenging factors to win the game.

Players have Warriors, Earls/Jarls and Kings to do battle with and will have to make strategic and tactical choices to win the game. 
 A turn chart of 15 moves commences in January 871AD and goes through to May 871AD.

The same game as Alfred the Great: The Great Heathen Army 871AD (Counters version) but players purchase their own 1:72 scale figures and plastic counters to make a 3D version of the game. Map will best printed in A3 size to fit miniatures on the board comfortably. UPDATE! this game is available as a download from Wargame Vault 
https://www.wargamevault.com/en/product/194743/alfred-the-great-3d-wargame
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: In Switch and Signal, you take the role of engineers, signalmen, switchmen, and yardmasters working together to load trains with cargo, navigate a congested railway network, and deliver cargo to the port city of Savannah, Georgia. Switches and signals are used to guide trains along routes. For every train that reaches the port, you gain victory points. You win the game by gaining at least 40 victory points, and 60 victory points is a perfect game.

Harbour Bridges is a light network connection game featuring a double sided board of Sydney and Auckland Harbours.

Players take on the role of city planners and build bridges to connect cycle routes between harbourside suburbs and then race the other players to get their pawns on to their target suburbs quicker than anyone else.

Movement is by collecting numbered cards, and on movement turns players play down as many cards as they want as long as the cards are the same number.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Old Europe / 51st State
description: Age of Steam Expansion: Old Europe / 51st State is a new pair of expansions for use with the Age of Steam base game. Each expansion features a non-hex-based map for use by experienced AoS players.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Argentina / The French Riviera
description:
Age of Steam Expansion: Argentina / The French Riviera is a new pair of expansions for use with Age of Steam. Each new map in this expansion includes a new type of hex, with Argentina featuring the pampas and The French Riviera showcasing the expensive French coast.

Both maps are illustrated by the talented Todd Sanders, who added flavor to them, namely the Dictatorship and Saul Bass film posters.

Compilation in a box of four Spanish classic games from CEFA:

"Misterio"
The game is about solving the mysterious disappearances by a process of elimination. The winner will be the detective that manages to solve the 3 keys: "Mis" "Te" "Rio"

"Alerta Roja"
During the voyage of the merchant ship that transports barrels with radioactive residue, one of them disappears mysteriously.

By satellite, this barrel, is directed by the sea to a far away city.
Ultimatum: In 48 hours the city will be destroyed!!

The most famous secret agent searches , using the most sophisticated equipment he has. Since the barrel, being moved by computers, can transform itself into the same type of vehicle that the agent uses for his search, the search and recovery becomes an almost impossible mission.

"Imperio Cobra"
The game is a standard roll and move fare with the added twist that one of the gamers controls the forces of evil and attempts to stop all of the other players from winning

"Dagon"
In order to win the game you have to search for the “Dagon Symbol” and carry it to one of the cities. This “Symbol” is in the center of the board.

By doing this you will be invested “Kingdom Knight”.
The first one in being invested as a Knight in three different cities will win the game
family: Series: Álbum de Oro Club de la Aventura (CEFA)

description:
Objective: be the first reaching space 42
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll & Move' game; choose one of three different routes before you start; there in no space '1' on the board - you must roll at least a '2' getting started. Ending on a red marked space follow instructions glued into the lid.

Note:
Published likely early 1920s.

Objective:
Be the first reaching space 100
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll&Move' game; ending on a red or blue marked space follow instructions.

Note:
Likely published mid 1920s.

boardgame: Todd Parr's I'm Going on a Trip...A Worldly Game of Travel and Chance
description:
From the back of the box:

Grab your bag and get ready...You're going on a trip!  How many places will you travel to and how will you get there?  Will you go to the mountains or a tropical island?  Will you visit a museum or an amusement park?  Each time you reach one of your travel destinations, you collect a souvenir to pack in your suitcase.  Whoever packs their suitcase fastest is the winner!

The suspense builds as players draw chance cards and use clever strategy to collect their souvenirs before other players get theirs.  One bad move can change the outcome, so it's a race to the finish to see who will win!

Object of the Game:
Players travel the game board hoping to collect the Souvenirs and Transportation cards they need to fill their suitcases.  The first player to finish packing his or her own suitcase wins the game!

"Viajar a lo largo de una provincia de Cuba designada, respondiendo correctamente preguntas de seis diferentes categorias. Para ganar, un jugador (o equipo) debera llegar al puerto despues de responder correctamente cuatro preguntas de la categoria "No!" consecutivas."

Translation:
Tourism along a designated province of Cuba, correctly answering questions in six different categories. To win, a player (or team) must reach the port after correctly answering four questions of the category "No!" consecutively.

Learn letters with Frida! It’s lunchtime in the rainforest and Frida is collecting letters for her little ones. Spin, find, squeeze, and set four letters in a row first to win this fruity preschool letter game. For 2-4 players.

Teaches letters and the alphabet
Develops fine motor skills
Develops strategic thinking skills
Encourages social skills and turn-taking

Alphabet game for preschoolers

boardgame: The Sneaky Snacky Squirrel Card Game
description:
Your furry forest friends are hungry and they need your help! Race the other players to squirrel away the most acorn cards and you win this nutty, fast-action, color-matching game for preschoolers.

Reinforces color learning
Develops matching, sorting, and strategic thinking skills
Encourages social skills and turn-taking
Practices memory and attention skills

Preschool color matching game
Fast-play fun
Based on The Sneaky, Snacky Squirrel game.

Howdy y’all! It’s a reading race through the southwest! Pick your cards, gallop around the board to round up all your words, and giddy up home before the other players to win! Game features the top 25 building block words.

Teaches 25 key Kindergarten words.

Objective: Be the first reaching space 100
 - moving by turns -

All players have to buy in first. Each player moves forward according die roll. Ending on certain spaces you must pay or collect play money, skip a turn or move again...

Note:
Likely published 1916 +/- one year.

Objective: be the first reaching space 64
 - moving by turns -

Simple 'Roll & Move' game; each player must buy in; ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid:
green - skip one turn; red - earn play money; purple - step 10 spaces forward; blue - step 8 spaces backwards.

Note:
Published likely mid 1930s.

Star Worlds is a collectible card game, where a player takes a role of a commander of a space fleet.

A player starts the game with a base and 50-card deck. The base can produce certain types of units, which in turn can produce other types of units. In addition to this a player can play characters and put light spaceships directly into play from his or her hand.

Spaceships can be divided into many categories: some of them can attack other spaceships, other recover used cards or cover key units from enemy fire, deliver ammunition onto the battlefield.

Once a player has gathered enough units, he or she can attack their opponent’s base. If the player’s base is destroyed, the other player wins the game. Another way to win the game is to collect twelve strategic points, produced by strategic objects. The third way includes the defense of the base until the opponent will run out of cards in their deck.

The creator and designer of the game is Vladimir Kravchenko.

The first set consists from 110 cards, 55 for each of two factions.

Included in the expansion : 

6 New Bid Boards with 4 new Influential People to choose.
1 Overlay Building Board featuring Garden Asylum and Jail
2 New Player Colors (Black & Gray) and Screens

 4 New Influential People and Abilities: 

Warden  - Four Support/Reclaim one token/Add an opponent's cube to jail.
Heretic  - One Force/One Blackmail/Influence Asylum.
Governor  - Influence any open Influence Space/Add any opponent's cube to any open space.
Anarchist  - Gain the reward in any other space.

Game description from the publisher:

The legitimate authorities have given way to mob rule — it's anarchy!

Revolution! Anarchy includes a new board overlay, new bidding boards, and two new sets of player components, allowing you to play Revolution! with up to six people. With this expansion, you'll be able to force the Warden to throw your enemies in jail, blackmail the Heretic and take over the asylum, and bribe the Anarchist, whose effects change every turn!

Meanie Rangers is a short, Pokemon style game where players catch Meanies in the wild or duel with other players. Each Meanie appears on a single card with a value of 2-7. After 4 rounds of catching fighting and trading, players are judged on the quality of their entire hand.

Points are allocated for the highest mean score, the highest median, the highest mode and the largest range. The game can be played with POD cards, or with normal playing cards of value 2,3,4,6,7 (one of each suit).

boardgame: Monopoly: Wow Brisbane Broncos Charity Edition
description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Brisbane Broncos, an Australian Footballcub; limited edition to raise money for McGuinness McDermott Foundation (now Little Heroes Foundation).

Re-fight the battle that could have ended the American Revolution on a hot summer's day in 1778. Find out if your strategies and maneuvers would have altered the course of battle. You are in command either as General Clinton, or as Generals Lee and Washington. Luck is not a factor.

boardgame: Hollywood: The Other Side of Hollywood
description:
The Other Side of Hollywood is a Kickstarter exclusive add-on which consists of 21 brand new cards. All of these cards have a special ability: you can pass a card to another player or you can use this card in your own movie.

Why would you want to pass the cards? Because they affect usual movie production rules and impose some limitations! Here is an example. Producer’s Son is not very skilled and he will decrease your box office with his terrible acting. However, he is still an Actor. The decision to keep him or to pass him is yours!

Art House Actress, on the other hand, is quite experienced but you can’t add any green crew cards to her movie. Why? Well, art house movies usually don’t need any special effects or anything that high budget team can offer.

If you got an “Other Side” card from another player you have to use it in your movie. You can’t get rid of it by discarding it! All of the “Other Side” cards have a ten-pointed star in the background to be easily recognizable.

The Other Side of Hollywood shows you the darker side of Hollywood but does it in a light-hearted manner. It is worth mentioning that all of the add-on’s cards are optional to use. You can add all of the new cards in to the deck or try any combinations.

More interaction to the game. More strategies to try. More interesting decisions to make!

Arcatype is a combination of elements from strategy card games coupled with tactical-based unit placement on a hexagonal map with a number of capture points for players to fight over.

Each turn, a player can summon a unit from his party onto the battlefield, evolve a unit already on the battlefield, move and attack with any units on the battlefield, or use a powerful spell card from the Arcana deck. Arcana cards are based on the Major Arcana cards of the Tarot, and each card has a powerful spell effect that you can play at any time to help turn the battle in your favor.

Klassekampen is a political game modelled on the classical Monopoly game. Each player represents a political party. Votes replace currency, and players strive for control over political issues instead of shares.

The mechanism of the game parallels a traditional Monopoly game. Votes are used to take control over more political issues, which again are used to gain more votes. The aim of the game is to conquer all other players by overtaking all their votes.

The game is published by Klassekampen, a Norwegian radical-left newspaper (the term Klassekampen means 'class struggle'). The game is not sold, but is rewarded to readers who helps the paper in getting more subscribers.

boardgame: Heavy Gear Blitz! Field Support Guide
description:
From the introduction (no back cover);

The Field Support Guide is a free update for Dream Pod 9’s  Heavy Gear Blitz tabletop miniatures wargame.

This downloadable pdf is meant to complement the Heavy Gear Blitz Field Manual rulebook which is the current ruleset for the game. It pulls together the universal rules updates that were added in the Perfect Storm: NuCoal Field Guide (NuCoal and PAK factions) and Forged in Fire: Southern Field Guide (all the Southern factions), which are the current army lists of those factions.

And, also includes the Combat Engineering, Medical, plus a few other combat groups from various issues of the Gear-Up e-zine.

Plus Field Manual and Forged in Fire errata, Field Testing of updated Threat Values (TV) for the CEF, Black Talon, Caprice, Eden, Leagueless and Utopian factions for Gear-Up. New and updated datacards round out the guide.

Humanity finally left the safety of Earth to settle, terraform, and extend their dominion to the other celestial bodies of the Sol system. Aided by the Maanen drive, which allows ships to travel exponentially faster between planetary bodies, and humanity's never-ending push for autonomy, the colonies soon became independent planets. In the ensuing wars, both cold and hot, two powers rose to dominance: the Republic of Mars and United Terra. The political and military impasse has existed for much of the majority of the citizens of the system...

Sol Rising focuses on the conflicts, big and small, of the Sol system primarily involving United Terra and the Martian Republic. You are a Fleet Commander put in charge of the squadrons of your star system. How will you perform your duty when war comes to the Sol system?

Instead of being tasked with merely destroying the enemy every time, every mission is crafted to have unique experiences and objectives. These include things like capturing the enemy space station with marines, escorting a merchant to safety so he can warn the fleet at the next mission, stealthily avoiding detection to plant rebels on an enemy moon, and more. In some missions, your accomplishments and choices will affect the next, triggering ripple effects that make your play through the campaign unique.

In Scenario Play, the players will participate in a single scenario pitting one side against another; the winner is determined by victory conditions set by the scenario (ex. the scenario gives the attacking side the mission of wiping out their opponent and to minimize casualties as much as possible, while the other side is trying to get a specific ship to escape the sector and then to take out as many of the attacker’s ships as possible).

Players in a Campaign game participate in a number of scenarios, each having an effect on the next as the story unfolds; the winner of one scenario will have a better starting position in the next (although care has been taken to ensure there will not be a runaway conquest because of this) and the ultimate victory condition is to win the final scenario in the campaign (if the example scenario above was used as a part of a campaign, the second scenario would change based on the outcome of the first; the number of ships the attacker gets and their starting status in scenario two is determined by the ending of scenario one, and if the defender managed to get their special ship out of the sector they get to choose the starting locations of their ships, rather than using a predefined, unorganized starting layout).

The Battle of Macysburg , a Command and Maneuver scenario, portrays a meeting engagement between two well-matched armies converging on a town that’s important as both supply point and road junction. Two players maneuver armies composed of units representing infantry, cavalry, cannon, skirmishers, and leaders, to capture and hold Macysburg, and chase off or destroy the opposing army. The battle lasts for 3 days, 36 day turns divided by 2 night turns.  Units arrive at diverse points on the field during the first 2 days. At night, the armies separate and rally some of the units lost each day.

Command and Maneuver is a simple abstract strategy rules set that allows very rules-light wargames to be constructed.  But as Macysburg demonstrates, it does not necessarily mean a light wargame. By abstracting all of the “fiddly bits” of wargames, what gets represented are the basic ideas of command control, supply, and maneuver to obtain both strategic and tactical advantage over your opponent. Since all combat is deterministic, players aren’t trying to overcome the randomness of combat results tables or dice towers or card draws. But they are trying to overcome chaos created by the combination of the combat and leader rules, which often result in units being out of command.

Armies are fragile in this game, and they “feel” that way. Combat is very bloody and unforgiving. If you are not careful in your dispositions and advances, you can lose a major chunk of your army in a very few turns, without adequate compensation. Players quickly find themselves adopting combined arms tactics and using formations and units in ways that look very much like standard tactics of the era. By doing things very differently than most wargames, the CaM games tease out and examine different aspects of war.

Macysburg, an abstract riff on the Battle of Gettysburg, is just one of the scenarios you can build with the CaM package, available in a Print and Play and in a Vassal version. The terrain (woods, hills, and town squares) is movable, allowing for a different set-up of the game each time. The elements of the scenario are broken down, laid out, and discussed for players to see how the scenario is constructed and how to vary it. Other scenarios are available and more will be provided. Players are encouraged to experiment with the system.

boardgame: Epic Death! Seven Card Bonus Pack
description:
From Epic Death! Kickstarter Page:

The Bonus Pack adds a whole new element to your game: Events! These testing trials will appear early on in your adventures and will need to be dealt with quickly if you're to become truly Epic heroes. The Booster Pack also comes with two exclusive Fate cards: the Nerf Bat and Potion of Imba... perfect if you're looking to really turn the tide!

"Attack the Dino by shooting as many balls as you can!"

That is what the box claims. In this game there is a central Tyrannosaurus Rex Head that spins around and as it does there is a series of blocking pieces of plastic that also rotate. As they do each player need to shoot his or her balls through the gate at the end of each of their ramps to try to get it in the middle. The person who wins is the one who gets all their balls in the middle.

Urosette is an abstract strategy game for 2-5 players with a Moroccan theme. It is similar to both Othello and Go.

Players first take turns placing starter tiles (coins) on the game board. Then, they draw a hand of 3 coins, each having a different ability defined by the arrows on the coin. Placing a coin points to one or more other coins not owned by the player, and that player lays claim to those coins by placing a colored token on them. On subsequent turns, claimed coins change hands.

Once a coin is completely surrounded either by other coins or the edge of the board, it is captured by the last player to successfully claim it. This means a coin captured on your turn could go to an opponent.

The game ends when all of the spaces have been filled with coins, and all of the coins have been captured. Each coin has a number on it, add those up and the player with the highest score is the winner.

A fast paced action packed motor racing board game based on the Australian V8 Supercar series.

Simulating all the realism of V8 Supercar racing, players will need to manage all aspects of their car, know when to use fuel, push hard and  jostle for position risking damage to the car. Successful management of fuel, engine condition, tyre wear and brakes is necessary to win the race.

Players must first setup their car correctly. A wise choice is needed, because once the car is setup, it cannot be changed!  Each setup will determine how fast the car can move around the circuit for each gear. Careful analysis of the circuit will help decide the correct setup.

Drivers then qualify for pole position and then race begins.

During the race each driver decides what gear the car should be in for their turn  moves  as per their chosen setup while making some important race winning (or losing) decisions:

Should I use extra fuel now and push hard?
    Should take this corner faster than recommended and wear my tyre quicker?
    Will I be able the slow down in time to take the next corner?
    How confident is my driver right now, do I have enough confidence to avoid any nasty instances?
    Do I need to pit this lap or can make it around one more time?

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – S'Gorn Expansion Pack
description:
A Gorn starship is a type of vessel utilized by the Gorn Hegemony during the mid-23rd century for conducting raids. The Gorn ship is designed with four nacelles, and its class configuration is unfamiliar to the Federation prior to the late 2260s.

In 2267, a Gorn starship approached the Earth outpost on Cestus III at space-normal speed using the outpost's regular approach route. Before the outpost knew what hit them, the Gorn ship knocked out the outpost's phaser batteries with its first salvo, leaving the outpost defenseless. The weapon used was described by a survivor as being "like phasers, only worse". The Gorn ship continued to assault the outpost, until it was obliterated.

The Gorn starship later attacked the USS Enterprise, which had been lured to the colony by false signals. The Enterprise's counterattack with full phaser and photon torpedo banks proved ineffective against the deflector screen of the Gorn ship, which was located just outside visual range.

The Star Trek: Attack Wing – S'Gorn Expansion Pack is the competitive prize for the "Arena" episode during Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play.

At the center is a board game tiles, abstract, strategic and competitive, 2 to 4 players, about 10 to 40 minutes.

In this game players must wear the tile color by pushing the center of the board with other tiles. To push necessary to match the figures of the tiles in shape and color. It looks easy but you only have a minute and just 5 tiles in hand per turn.
The player who wins 3 rounds will be the winner.

Components: 120 tiles, 1 rule booklet, 1 timer.

Printable files are here and the BSK.

This game will be as p & p for a trial period.

boardgame: Heavy Gear Blitz! Operation: Drop Bears Dive!
description:
Operation: Drop Bears Dive!

[From the back cover;]

"Barrington Gates. Baja. Red Cliffs. Battles that the 08th had paid for in blood against the CEF over thirty cycles ago and now the enemy had returned. Corporal Vandenbosche gripped the control sticks on either side of him in the dark cockpit of his Koala. Anger welled up inside of him as he thought of the loss of his family in the orbital bombardments dropped so casually on his home town.

Today, he would repay the invaders for their crimes. His regiment would drop from the skies and rain hell on the enemy. Looking through his gear's omni-camera, Vandenbosche saw the green light and his squadron commander readying himself to jump from their Orca transport. As Sergeant Mason engaged his gear's SMS and leaped out of the open bay door, he called over the radio, "Boys, I'll meet ya on the ground. Give 'em hell!"

Vandenbosche grinned at his sergeant's order and moved to position himself on the launch track. The clear blue skies seemed incredibly inappropriate for the bloody work in store for the Crazy Eights."

The WFPA 08th Airborne Regiment is one of the oldest regiments in the Western Frontier Protectorate. As a distinguished regiment, the unit is instrumental in many of the key battles the league has waged and represents among the best examples of the WFPA's soldiers and traditions.

Operation: Drop Bears Dive! explores this unique regiment's history and the developments they introduced onto the battlefield. In this book you will find:

- Unit History and Traditions
- WFPA Ranks, Insignia, and Uniforms
- 10 New Campaign Scenarios
- War of the Alliance Rules for Northern Leagues and the CEF
- HACS-01HG-PARA/AST Koala Development History, Technical Data, and Variants
- Five Special Characters with RPG and Blitz! rules for fielding them.
- And a Bonus City Militia Army List to be fielded by the South.

A sourcebook for the Western Frontier Protectorate Army 08th Airborne Regiment, this book requires use of 
Heavy Gear Blitz! Locked & Loaded, Return to Cat's Eye, and Heavy Gear RPG 2nd & 3rd editions.

The Game of Legends is a unique RPG with an important financial part. Gametime is flexible and depends on which one of the possible three endings the players choose. Regain the occupied land from the Dragon, improve your character, fight monsters, attack other players, invest and make good deals. And be the first to be strong enough to attack the Dragon and rule the land! Beautifully drawn characters and awesome graphics provide the base for the unique experience.

boardgame: Heavy Gear Silhouette Core Miniature Rules
description:
From the back cover;

To celebrate the 10th Anniversary of Heavy Gear, we bring you the new 3rd Edition of miniature rules! The new Silhouette Core Heavy Gear Miniature Rules takes the acclaimed Silhouette game mechanics to the next level, with game play from one on one Melee Scale to massive Fleet Scale battles. The Tactical Scale has been revamped to speed up the game and save time on record keeping. Rules updates include improved Command Point
usage, streamlined ammunition tracking, plus new artillery, air and orbital strikes rules.

The best in Giant Robot miniature games just got better!

The Silhouette Core Heavy Gear Miniature Rules includes rules for the following:
• Tactical Scale, the main Heavy Gear miniature battle scale.
• Melee Scale, character-scale battles with rules for converting SilCORE RPG characters into miniature stats.
• Duelist Scale, for one on one duelist gear battles in close quarters skirmishes.
• Fleet Scale, massive battles with landships and squad stands.
• Weapon tables, combat tables and datacards included.

A core miniature game rulebook for all players; a few six-sided dice, miniatures, measuring tape, pen and paper are required to play the game.

For two to six players, age 8 and older.

80 Pages; August, 2005

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Ströer, a company in Germany.

Crisis Averted After listening to the community of BGG we have decided to make the game free to print and play. We have taken the time to ensure the game is a fun experience for all ages and enjoy a healthy debate on your favorite comic book icons. Welcome to Crisis Averted! Players will be dealt cards representing different iconic characters from popular culture, using knowledge of these characters they will avert the crisis set before them. Crisis Averted can be best described as a role-playing multiplayer party card game; where creativity is pivotal to winning. Crisis Averted fuses traditional role-playing storytelling elements with a competitive party game format. The game uses a Judge System, one player per round is the Construct, from their hand of five Crisis Cards they must devise of scenario to the other players.

description:
What’s better than getting yourself as a survivor? How about a pet! This deck includes many of the animal submissions from the backers from our first Kickstarter and a few of our own devising. Some of them are walking time bombs, and others are just plain adorable. Either way having a fifth member of your Squad can come in handy when holding back a zombie horde. Whether it’s man’s best friend or a polar bear, you never know what they will bring to the Squad dynamic. This deck also includes new special abilities.

Each deck comes with 25 Survivors and 2 Blank cards for you to create your own.

Totalbiscuit A.K.A the Cynical Brit, is a refined English gentleman of acerbic wit and regal authority. His commentary on video games was watched by millions, but with the collapse of civilization, Biscuit sought a new vocation. He can only assume that slaughtering zombies will prove less frustrating than some of the video games he’s played.

Mild-mannered comic book reviewer Linkara has always found himself in world-threatening crises. Armed with an indomitable will, quick wits, a star ship, and an arsenal of hi-tech and magical weapons, he triumphed. But not even Linkara could stop the coming of the Zpocalypse.

Fancy man about town Jim Sterling was once the celebrated reviews editor of Destructoid.com, as well as a consumer advocate on the Jimquisition. A man of controversial opinion, Jim was no stranger to being attacked by mindless hordes, even before the zombie outbreak occurred. With an imposing frame that belies his uncanny agility, Jim joins up with Angry Joe to get the undead sorted out once and for all.

Thanks to the amazing backers from our first Kickstarter, we were given an army of downtrodden, desperate Survivors to add to the Zpocalypse struggle. We selected a number of them to make Survivor Packs, which include new special abilities and a host of people who think they’re ready to face the onslaught.

Each deck comes with 25 Survivors and 2 Blank cards for you to create your own.

Before the Zpocalypse “Angry” Joe Vargas was a video game critic with a global military organization dedicated to protecting consumers from corporate greed. When even his mighty forces fell before the zombie hordes, Joe put out the call to his most powerful allies determined to form a Squad of the finest post-Zpocalyptic Warriors in the world! Now without his beloved video games and filled with rage, Joe has only one purpose. Kill them all!

When internet film and game critic Spoony succumbs to madness, he becomes a creature of chaos and science: Doctor Insano! Approached by his arch-enemies, Angry Joe and Linkara, the mad scientist agrees to help fight the zombie threat – if only so he can destroy the world himself!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Axis Kampfgruppe Stefan Starter Set
description:
The year is 1947. The war that started a decade  ago has now spread worldwide. The discovery of a crashed alien spacecraft in the Antarctic has led to the development of revolutionary combat walkers that surpass the most advanced tank technology. Old alliances have fragmented and new ones arisen as three major blocs, the Allies, the Axis and the Sino-Soviet Union (SSU) fight the world over for VK, a valuable new resource that power their new fighting machines.

Dust Tactics is a miniatures board game for 2 or more players. The Axis Kampfgruppe Stefan starter set contains everything you need to start playing Dust Tactics, including 10 highly-detailed plastic miniatures, unit cards, terrain elements, special Dust dice, and the Dust rulebook. Enter the world of Dust and lead your Axis Kampfgruppe into battle against Allied or SSU Battlegroups.

Note: The rules and cards in this box are based on the version 2 rules, but are slightly different from and not completely compatible with the full Dust Tactics version 2 rulebook.

This game consist of ten different game variants to learn numbers and some basic math. The main components are foam numbers from 0 to 9. There are two pieces of every number and plus, minus and equality signs. Basic idea is to recognize numbers by feeling them in your hand. To prevent you from seeing the numbers, you construct bigger box from the game box and two cardboard walls. Those walls have an hole to put your hand into the box. Hole is covered with cloth.

Basic idea of the games is to find the wanted number(s) from the box. Some examples: 1. You have to pick up numbers in ascending order. 2. You throw 12 sided die and you have to find two numbers that add up to the result. 3. You pick up a tile with a two digit number and find corresponding numbers. And so on. In most advanced games you have to add or subtract numbers or you have to recognize number sequence.

The entire rules:

Each player chooses a particular coloured Aeroplane.

No player can start until he has thrown a 6 with the dice, this counting as his first move.

The game proceeds by moving the Aeroplanes according to the number shown by the throw of the dice.

The player must observe all the hazards his Aeroplane alights upon, whether in his favour or otherwise.

The exact number required to reach the 100 must be thrown and the first player to do so is the winner.

The board comprises a track of 100 spaces spiraling around a painting of a Gloster Gladiator with RAF roundels.  There can have been little playtesting: a player can never land upon the 3 space but it bears the penalty "Fail to rise: miss one throw".

The Gloster Meteor depicted was notable from 1944 to 1955.

Insane Traffic is a card and dice game that simulates the traffic situations in Metro Manila, Philippines. You are an everyday driver living in Metro Manila and the goal of the game is to get the most number of points by reaching check points in different areas of Metro Manila.

The game has a great balance of dice rolling for movement (to depict the randomness of traffic) and hand management to add chaos and skill to the game. Insane Traffic is also an educational game wherein players must be able to recognize the different traffic signs in the game to increase their chances of winning.

Trilemma is an abstract game, where players make simultaneous moves in order to create a pattern of stones in their color.

The players each secretly choose a numbered stone they wish to place on the board and then reveal simultaneously. Uncontested stones are placed in the desired spot. If players choices conflict, a non-conflicting player claims the spot according to a simple deterministic rule.

First player to create a line of three stones in his color, wins the round and thereby two poins. First player to 10 points wins the game.

Playing the game involves bluffing, smart choices and banter. It offers the feel of a 'dilemma for three', hence Trilemma!

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – VT-49 Decimator Expansion Pack
description:
To be granted command of a VT-49 Decimator is seen as a significant promotion for a middling officer of the Imperial Navy. A heavily armed transport, the VT-49 Decimator is one of the Empire’s most feared warships, often used to provide long-range reconnaissance or to deploy raiding parties past enemy forces.

The VT-49 Decimator Expansion Pack brings this intimidating Imperial gunboat to X-Wing as the game’s largest ship yet designed for Standard Play. Even at the game’s signature 1/270 scale, the expansion pack’s detailed miniature towers over its base and smaller starfighters.

In addition to its imposing, pre-painted miniature, the VT-49 Decimator Expansion Pack introduces four ship cards, three debris cloud tokens, a new mission, a maneuver dial, and all the tokens you need to fly your Decimator into the thick of combat. Finally, you’ll find thirteen upgrade cards, which introduce a variety of crew members like Mara Jade and Fleet Officer designed to help you fill out the Decimator’s three crew member slots.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – YT-2400 Freighter Expansion Pack
description:
A fast and resilient light freighter, the YT-2400 features no fewer than thirteen weapon emplacement points, making it an attractive vessel for smugglers, mercenaries, and other individuals looking for a heavily armed “transport.” Although a stock YT-2400 light freighter has plenty of space for cargo, much of that space is often annexed to support modified weapon systems and oversized engines.

The YT-2400 Freighter Expansion Pack brings this formidable light freighter to your table as a Rebel starship with two attack, two agility, five hull, and five shields.

The highlight of the YT-2400 Freighter Expansion Pack is its detailed miniature starship, which is enhanced by one new mission, three debris cloud tokens, a maneuver dial, all requisite tokens, and four ship cards, including one for the famed smuggler Dash Rendar.

Of course, a ship as readily customizable as the YT-2400 needs to come with a host of upgrade options, and the YT-2400 Freighter Expansion Pack comes with thirteen upgrade cards, including a Lando Calrissian crew upgrade, the Outrider Title, and upgrade cards designed by each of the game’s first two World Champions.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game
