A promo card for Lost Legacy. It's a card with investigation value 8, which can win you the game if it's in your hand at the end of the game and someone investigates it.

Handed out at Tokyo Game Market in spring 2013, fall 2013 and spring 2014.

Duel of the Magi is a deck-building game that combines fantasy board games, drafting, and TCG fighting mechanics for a complete and unique experience. Players start by choosing one of the four magi classes, each of which have a unique ability to be used during the game. While taking turns players choose sets of spells to include or exclude in the game. Once this phase is complete, the chosen cards of each spell type are then shuffled together and placed on the board in the appropriately labeled spots. Staged face-up, the cards are drafted one at a time from the board as each player builds their own customized deck of spells. Once deck building is complete, the game board is flipped over to the dueling ground side and players engage in a spell casting fight to determine the winner. Part of the dueling strategy is gamesmanship in the form of alliances and negotiations. Do what you must to ensure you survive to the end.

Over 40,000 sold. Published in Shanghai, in Chinese, with English names and slogans as an added-on educational element.

Car racing game using cards. Three different games using the same cards: a two player card 'duel', lasting 10-30 minutes; a 2-10 player moving-around-the-track game for younger players; and a combo of the two, a 2-10 player moving-around-the-track game using the cards to interact with the others.

Backs of the cards represent a straight section of track. Fronts: left and right wide and left and right tight curves, an S-curve, a finish line, a dry/wet tire card, a sun/rain weather card, a thick perforated card with 5 two-sided cars, and 45 playing cards, in 26 types and 4 different colours representing maneuvers, special ations and anti-card actions. Also included is a handy 14-page full-colour fold-out booklet with all the rules, illustrated examples and a list of the game components.

On the accompanying website (now defunct) the games could be played substituting pictured cards for the real ones.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Game Night Kit 2014 Fall Season
description:
From publisher website:
Game Night Kits for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game contain promotional material for up to four players and are designed to encourage play and build communities. Each kit includes new sets of cards, called Nightmare Decks, that enhance existing scenarios and challenge players in new ways. Prizes are also included to reward players for succeeding. As players set forth from the Shire, fight spiders, and confront Smaug, they will have to overcome all-new threats and evil tricks in the newest Nightmare Decks.

Nightmare Decks invite players to experience past adventures in an entirely new way, facing new challenges along the way. By removing certain cards from a pre-existing Encounter deck and replacing them with the cards in the Nightmare Deck, players can experience an entirely new version of the quest, taking their adventures into new realms of nightmare!
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Decks: On the Doorstep
description:
Nightmare Decks for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game allow players to revisit their favorite scenarios cast in the shadows of the game's challenging Nightmare Mode. Featuring deadlier enemies, darker locations, and more terrifying surprises, each Nightmare Deck introduces new, alternate encounter cards and instructions for using them to reshape a scenario's encounter deck. The result? A nightmarish journey through territory players thought they knew, but with new twists and turns that will have them on the edges of their seats!

On the Doorstep Nightmare Decks is a single set of three twenty-card Nightmare Decks, one for each of the three scenarios from On the Doorstep, a saga expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
You are a bear, and the goal of every bear to eat a balanced diet. You've collected some food, but now you need to protect it from the other hungry bears.

The game is played in as many rounds as there are players. Each player is the "parent" bear for one round, during which the other players try to steal food tokens from the parent's table using one of four food cards. The parent can defend one type of food each round, and if the other bears get too greedy, none of them will steal anything.

boardgame: Mare Balticum: The Fate of an Empire
description:
Mare Balticum - the Board Game© is a board game about production, trade and war around the Baltic Sea. The players take on the roles of noble lines during the Swedish Empire Era. Together, they will populate the kingdom, build ships for overseas trade, build armies for overseas war and build forts for defending the gains. Instead of fighting each other, they will have to fight the enemies around the Baltic Sea, competing for the same provinces. However, the best for the kingdom is not necessarily the best for your noble line. You have to decide what benefits you most: production or trade, consolidation or expansion, war or peace. But will your objectives coincide with your opponents' objectives? Beware, if you fight internally, your external enemies will conquer the entire kingdom and put an end to both the Swedish Empire Era and the game!

Game mechanisms include

1. Player balance: The players have a limited number of units, preventing one from growing too strong. 
2. Game balance: The rate that the enemies grow is related both to the number of players and to their current strength, preventing them from getting too strong or too weak. 
3. Production vs Trade vs War balance: The players may choose different strategies for growing and one strategy chosen will bring negative effects to the other strategies. 
4. Cooperation: The players build fleets, armies and forts together and they also fight battles together. 
5. Prisoners' dilemma. One player may benefit by not cooperating but if all do so, all will lose.

In Cults of Arkham you control a gang of deranged cultists trying to summon their Lovecraftian god of choice and thus end the world.
 You use action dice to visit different places in Arkham, activate location abilities, and initiate fights with the other players. Your aim is to fulfil sinister plots, defeat the feared investigators, and/or control key locations in order to gain Victory Points. The first player to reach 12 Victory Points wins the game and gets to end the world his way.

The core of the game is the custom action dice you use to move your cultists and to do stuff in Arkham; learn spells, corrupt the citizens, and more. And the farther you get into the game, and the madder your cultists get, the less chance you have of the dice actually showing the results you need. Madness may be a stepping stone, but it’s also an obstacle you somehow have to manage if you want to be the best cult in Arkham.

description:
Take control of skaters across the ages as they compete to be the first to place their tags on various points in history.

Players take turns moving one space across the board, taking any actions they move across in the process. Tags require paint cards to complete, match the cards you pick up to the ones shown on the tags then move past a painting spot to leave your mark and gain points. Create longer tags for more points, use special abilities to alter the cards in your hand or position yourself to block off other players routes.

The game ends when all paint spots are taken, then the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Yummy Scrummy, What's in My Tummy?
description: A children's game where you spin a wheel and choose a monster. Each monster has one of five colours in his tummy. You are trying to collect one of each colour as a healthy fruit, while avoiding junk foods, called "oh no's".

2, 3 or 4 players

Materials

A hexagonal board with 5 hexagons per side, 64 stones in four colors, 16 red, 16 yellow, 16 green and 16 blue.

You will also need a small drawstring bag.

Definitions

Group – A set of like color connected stones, a single stone is also considered a group.

Rules

The board is placed between the players and starts out empty.

Place 4 stones 1 each of the 4 colors (red,green,blue,yellow) in a bag and then the youngest player draws a stone and passes the bag to the next player.

After all the stones are drawn players look at their stones without revealing them to the other players.

All stones will remain hidden until the end of the game.

The color of drawn stones corresponds to the color of the stones that belong to that player.

The remaining 60 stones are placed nearby.

The youngest player starts by dropping a stone of any color onto an empty hexagon, then turn moves clockwise.

From now on, each player on his turn must drop 1 stone of any color onto an empty hexagon and then move a different stone already on the board to another empty hexagon.

Restrictions

• A stone may not be dropped onto a cell that has no adjacent empty cell.
• A stone is moved in a straight line either orthogonally until another stone in its path or the edge of the board blocks it. 
• Stones cannot jump over other stones.

The game ends when a player is unable to take his turn.

Players then reveal their hidden stones, the player with the largest group in her color wins.

If two or more players are tied when comparing their largest groups then look at their second largest groups as a tie-breaker.

When playing a 2 or 3 player game the rules are the same as above with but one exception, leave the undrawn stones in the bag.
A set of 25 wooden cubes with letters to replace the colored cubes from the base game. With these, the goal of the game changes; players should now align the cubes to make a 5 letter word.

This is old and pretty simple roll and move boardgame, where you are a customer in a department store. In Finnish Tavaratalo means department store. There is three departments in the store, Paper department, food department and household department. Players start from the lobby and throw the die to decide in which department they are going. After that you just roll and move throught chosen department. All the spaces in the departments sell one special thing and you just buy it. If you roll five or six, you take a random card and you either get money, or things or loose money. When you come back to Lobby you sell all the things you bought with random prize (from zero to two and half times the buying prize) decided by the dice. Game ends after certain amount of rounds and the player with most money wins.

This is a word game, where you try to name things that start with different letters. On your turn you roll a die and one other player takes a card and reads a category from the corresponding line. Now you try to name as many things from that category as you can. You have on note block with alfabet and you can cross out all the starting letters that you could use. When you run out of time, it's next players turn. On your next turn you get new category and try to get the remaining letters. First player who can cross out all the letters is the winner.

This is educational childrens game about water. You roll and move and follow the instructions where you land. Basically you go forward, backward or just stay where you landed. On every space there is information about water and things you can do with the water. Winner is the first player to arrive in the end zone.

This is a roll and move board game, where you try to catch fish. Every player has a boat, which he/she uses to go round the board fishing. There is 40 fishes in the board and the game ends, when there is only four fishes left on the board. The winner is the player who has the biggest catch at the end. Some spaces in the board causes player to lose his/her turn until he/she roll a certain number with the die.

Victory Under Sail (VUS) is a quick-playing miniatures game of fleet naval battle during the last century of the age of sailing warships.  It is designed for a small number of players to recreate the larger battles of this era in a reasonable time.  It is not an overly simple game, but it is designed to have simple and quick mechanics to speed play.

Each side has a fleet of ships (represented by counters or miniatures) subdivided into squadrons.  VUS is based on squadron maneuvers rather than individual ship movement, although each ship is individually moved within the requirements of the planned maneuver.  The movement system uses a true 12-direction movement system on a hex grid that is unique among games of this genre.  Squadron maneuver cards and random event cards recreate the challenges of command & control of a fleet of sailing warships.

VUS uses a “Hordes o’Dice System” for combat that is based on the use of different types of dice to represent various types of cannon batteries.  The dice mimic the results of a traditional combat results table without the need to reference charts.

The game includes more than 30 scenarios from 3 wars.

The game is involving pattern recognition & building. Players act as students of the school of magic and wizardry, who compete with one another to learn new magic.  Each player will join three different magical circles, and determine the number of times of path conversion made within the time limit, to quickly change the magical element into the correct magical circles.  It is a game where speed of thought is contested.
The number of times of path conversion is controlled by cards, so players need to be aware whether or not there are suitable cards to use, or if one thinks too fast but lacks a usable card, he or she will have a small price to pay.

Trouble Maker is a pattern building game.  Players will exchange game tokens within 8 rounds to get rid of the trouble maker and earn points. When Trouble Makers of the same color form a line, then that line counts as one riddance.  If after a game token is flipped over, more Trouble Makers create a line, then the player can continue to flip over more tokens to count as riddance points.  The fun of the games is to create multiple lines everywhere and earn very high scores. Trouble Maker is a two player game, so enjoy the fun of continuous good riddance and win points!

The cars of the players (or teams) have to pass 9 different performance tests. For each test they get awarded points, which are determined by a spinner. A second car of the same color marks the points on the circle at the center of the game board. The player who gets the most points after 9 tests wins the game. Terrain is a game of bluffing, luck and landscapes, a game of hex tiles representing land types. Each player has a secret tile that denotes which type he scores. Everyone takes turns picking a new tile from the bag and placing it. The winner is the one with the largest connected region of his type. The game of Terrain consists of a few more rules than this, as well as an advanced game in which the same mechanisms are played out in rounds, with each player having a hand of tiles.

The animals are out for a fun ride.

Move your bus around your game board searching for the one winning solution that matches how the animals are sitting in the bus.  Be the first player to find it... and you win!

This is a children's learning game that focuses on: matching, image recognition, and concentration.

Poochie challenges players to a friendly game of tug-of-war.  The object of play is to build the longest length of rope between Poochie and her boyfriend, Fastball.  Players take turns drawing cards and placing them in a row in front of them.  When a player has a Poochie card at one end and a Fastball card at the other end, she is out of play.  The remaining players continue play until they have also completed their ropes and dropped out of play.  The winner is the player with the most rope cards between the characters.

—description from the back of the box

Game Play Variants: There are 2 game play variants of the game, 1 vs 1 and the free-for-all 3 way battle. The Recommended Variant is the 3 player free-for-all game which is the basic setup of the game's gameplay. The game can be played 1 versus 1 which will still use the play mat and will still use the unit positioning area such as the left/right flank unit placement.

Game Objective: Reduce all opponents’ Leader life Points to zero.

Game Setup:

1. Select Race Players choose which race they wish to play and thoroughly shuffle their decks. Pass each deck counter-clockwise to next player for reshuffling until it goes back to the designated player.

2. Place Leader Card Each player must place their leader Card in the field (playmat designated area). A Leader Card starts with 30 Health Points. (If a leader’s life points drops to zero, that player is eliminated from the game).

3. Determine Play Order    Randomly select which player will go first. After the first player’s turn, player turn order will resume in a counter-clockwise direction.

4. Draw Hand Cards    All players must draw 6 cards from their chosen deck. Every play turn, players can draw 2 cards in their library. All players can assign up to 2 cards on the mining placement area which will produce 1 gold per card per turn. The more cards assigned to the mining area, the more gold it produces. However, re-assigning the cards/using the cards from the mining area has an additional cost of 4 gold. It is the penalty for the cards being re-assigned as unit in the field.

5. First Turn    The first player begins their turn and performs the actions described in the Play Sequence section of these rules.

Game Play Sequence:

These are all the actions a player may perform during their turn. Each action must be completed in the order designated below. In a free-for-all game, the player chosen to go first takes their turn. Next, in a consecutive order (going in a counter-clockwise direction), the other players will fulfill their turns.

1.    Re-draw (Optional) A player can elect to declare a Re-Draw action at the start of their turn. This action is performed in place of any of the regular actions associated with a typical turn. When a player has no useful cards in their hand, they can choose to use the Re-draw action. The Re-Draw action allows a player to discard their hand of cards and draw 6 new cards from their deck. This action ends their turn. The player is not allowed to take any more actions, and will not be able to produce any Gold until their next turn. Note: If you don’t need to Re-Draw, skip this action and take a normal turn. Simply begin your turn with the collect Gold action and precede through the subsequent action steps until you wrap up your turn with the Upkeep action.

2.    Collect Gold Collect the amount of Gold equal to the total Gold on all of the cards placed on the mining area.

3.    Draw Card Draw 2 cards from your Race deck.

4.    Activate Cards This action lets you establish and activate any type of cards, use    Command cards, and/or assign new units.    During this play sequence, a player may perform all four of these options: - Play 2 Card to the mining field. Note: All cards in the mining field should be placed facedown. - Play any number of Passive and Instant Command Cards.    - Play any unit cards to assign any number of new units on the left/right flank.

5.    Re-assign Once the unit is re-assigned from mining area to any of the field flank, it cannot go back to the mining area. However, once a unit is assigned to any flank, you can re-assign it the other flank with a cost of 2 gold.

For further information of the game's "How to play", please download the game's Rule Book.

From the publisher:

Pocket Rugby League is head smashing, scrum packing, ball and all Rugby - played with dice.
 We've made this game to give plenty of scoring chances...in fact, not scoring in an attacking raid might be more painful than the huge hits throughout the game.
 The gameplay focuses on each side taking turns in an attacking set of 6 tackles.
 You are in control of 5 playing positions - the scrum half always starts each passage of play. He will either pass to another position, kick for a field goal or try and gain ground. On the other 4 dice, there is a 1 in 6 chance of 'busting' the line of defense, at which point, your opponent has one more roll of the 'Defensive' die to try and stop the try scoring effort. 
 If he succeeds, it is recorded as a tackle in the set of 6 and the attack continues. If he can't stop the try, the attackers then gets to roll the TRY / NO TRY dice.
 3 in 6 chance of a TRY / 2 in 6 chance of NO TRY and 1 chance the VIDEO ref is called upon to review the tape - in which a re-roll of the TRY die is made.
 You can also kick for field goals, penalties and scrums. All of these are dependent on rolling a certain number to be successful.

From the publisher:

Pocket Basketball has all 5 player dice on the court with the Referee die, a Defensive set die and a standard six sided dice for shot accuracy. Depending on if your player is OPEN or the defense has double teamed him, will change how easy or hard it is to score.

Games consist of 5 min quarters, or however long you wish to play.    There are dice for each of the five court positions, plus 1 die for defense, 1 die for referee and 1 die for shot accuracy.

At tip off, both players roll the D6 with the highest roll winning possession.  Every play begins with the PG being rolled first. He will either PASS to a team mate, SHOOT for 3pts or DRIBBLE

PASSING from the PG will be to the team mate shown by a letter. You can pass as many times before the SHOT CLOCK times out.    
Each other player has a PASS option which they can CHOOSE which player to pass the ball to. The CENTRE and SMALL FORWARD have a special ability after they pass, however, it can only be used if the receiving player takes a shot on his next action.    

    CENTER - Pass/tip in - if the player that receives this shoots and misses, the player can roll again to try and meet the DEF level as a tip in attempt    
    SMALL FORWARD - Pass/rebound - if the player that receives this shoots and misses, the player can roll again with the SF die as the rebound was made    


If either of these two pass, and the receiving player also passes, the tip off/rebound are no longer able to be used.

SHOOTING - when a type of shot is shown on a player's dice, this indicates he is attempting to score. Your opponent will then roll the DEFENSE die to set his defense. Depending on the DEFENSE level, the SHOOTER must roll equivalent or above to score a basket.

DRIBBLE - just reroll the same dice again. If you roll DRIBBLE 3 times before you take a shot, the shot clock has timed out and it's a turnover to your opponent's PG

UNCONTESTED SHOT - except for your PG, all 4 other team mates have a special shot indicated with a STAR. The DEFENSE can not roll and the Shooter scores an automatic basket.

DRAW - when rolled on the DEFENSE, means a FOUL has occurred on the shot. If the shot is good, the player will go to the free throw line for an extra point chance.    If the shot misses, the player then goes to the line for either 2 or 3 FREE THROW attempts.

FOUL - when rolled on the DEFENSE, your opponent will roll the REFEREE dice to determine the infringement. Travel/Dbl dribble/Charge are all Offensive fouls and result in a TURNOVER to your opponent's PG.    Personal/Flagrant foul results in a stop in play and restart by the OFFENSIVE player's PG
Ball up - both players roll their dice to see who gains possession from the jump.

Across the Void is a Space Trader board game for one to four players that focuses on trade negotiations with alien merchants, as well as management of and interaction with crew members.

You are the captain of a small deep void trade ship. Under your command are five crew members; each with specific skills. You must manage your crew so their overall Morale, Loyalty, and Efficiency Ratings are as high as you can get them. That way, your crew will perform their tasks more efficiency when needed.

Travel through Hyperspace is dangerous, and requires successful Skill Tests of your crew. Sustaining damage is costly, in terms of spending money on repairs, and the potential loss of crew, ship's systems, and cargo.

The object of the game is to be the first Captain to accumulate 1000 Galactic Credits by buying Goods, transporting them safely through Hyperspace, and then selling them at negotiated prices on distant worlds.

Player vs Player interaction occurs, as well as card-driven NPC combat events. In addition to accommodating multiple players, this game is designed for Solitaire play.

INTRODUCTION

Futuristic game of missions and agenda for two to three players set in a dystopian CyberGlam city hovering in the upper atmosphere.

Players are leaders of different factions vying for control of the hovering city of Lithica.

GAME OBJECTIVE

The object of the game is to complete missions that allow you to realize your faction's agenda. Doing so allows you to expend resources in order to add buildings, statuary, bill boards, and groups throughout the city. These installations expand your influence, giving you control of city sectors.

The first player to control a predetermined number of city sectors wins the game.

ENACTING AGENDA

At the start of the game, each player selects a faction-oriented Agenda Card to put into play. This is called enacting an agenda.

Agenda Cards list five different types of Missions your agents must complete. Once they have completed three or more different missions from the list, the agenda is considered realized, and you are awarded a specified number of Influence Points. These points can then be spent on placing installations on the game board.

COMPLETING MISSIONS

Mission Cards list five different types of Actions our agents must complete while in neutral- or enemy-controlled locations. Once three or more different actions from the list are completed, the mission is completed. You gain the listed benefits from the mission, and the Mission Card is then placed face-up under the Agenda Card (for scorekeeping purposes).

COMPLETING ACTIONS

Each agent in the game has a primary function; such as: eliminating, seducing, capturing, corrupting, or turning enemy agents. To perform its function, an agent must be in a city sector, or sector location, that contains a keyword matching the same keyword on the agent's card.

CyberPsi utilizes polyhedron. An action attempt may require you to roll 1d4 or 1d12 to determine how many Action Points an agent musters when attempting the action. Asset Cards that are equipped to the agent sometimes adds to an action attempt's Action Point total.

Thus, a Rank 1 Hacker in a location with the keyword INTERFACE may try to "Steal (1) Data Card from target enemy Database." The Action Point Value needed to successfully perform this action is (12). In order to be able to reach this, you roll 1d8 (as indicated on his card) and add (2) Action Points provided by his "Jac-in-Boxx" Asset Card and (1d4) Action Points provided by his "Virtualization Headgear" Asset Card. The total number of Action Points your Hacker gets is (13), which exceeds the target value of (12). Thus, the action attempt is successful, and your agents enables you to take anyone Data Card from the target player's Database.

RESOURCES

During the course of the game, you will assign agents to undertake activities that generate resources. You can then expend these resources to put other agents into play, to buy Asset Cards, and to unlock cards that can be placed into your draw deck.

COMBAT

Combat in CyberPsi is resolved by rolling each combatant's die type (d4, d6, d8, etc), adding any modifiers provided by Asset Cards, and comparing the results to each other. Damage is determined and applied, and the battle continues or ends, depending on combat situational factors.

Some agents have Combat Abilities that impact attack order, targeting, and damage.

The Beavers have to build lodges and collect winter stores, but always have to expect flooding and interference by other beavers from the neighboring areas.

Each player starts with a pair of beavers and a set of trees in different colors. On your turn you roll a dice and move a beaver. If he comes to a tree field, he may take the tree in his stock or build it in the lodge. Two Beavers on marginal fields carry out a turf war: This is decided with dice: who rolls higher wins. The winner can take a tree in the lodge or a tree as a food supply from the loser. Who first completes his lodge and fills his sfood supply wins.

Misión Imposible features up to four competing Intelligence Agencies (players) in a frantic battle of wits and resources for the ultimate espionage feat.

The Agencies' goal is to be the first to acquire classified reports on six different Government areas (Military, Space, Air Force, State, Naval and Nuclear secrets). On each turn, Agencies roll their dice and move their tokens around the board, acquiring the reports "naturally" by landing on the proper spaces. Reports can also be stolen from rival Agencies who have already acquired that particular piece of the puzzle. This is achieved by sending spies to their facilities. Players must balance spy missions with the need to conduct counter-espionage against the other agencies' spies.

But beware -- not all of the reports collected are authentic, so it'll require the powers of observation and skills of a true agent to smoke out the false data and avoid spending precious resources on mere decoys!

As they race against the clock, Agencies will recruit the help of Super-Agents and Super-Spies, get mission-disrupting directives from Headquarters, and receive situation updates via state-of-the-art Telex communications!

The first agency to get their hands on six authentic classified reports (one for each Government area) will be the winner.

Seas of Glory plunges you back into Merchants & Marauders where you can live the life of an influential merchant or a dreaded pirate in the Caribbean during the Golden Age of Piracy.

This hefty expansion offers a modular experience with eleven new challenges you can mix and match to constantly renew your search for glory at sea. With many new mechanisms such as changing winds, smuggling, and crew satisfaction as well as five variants, Merchants & Marauders will never be the same. Will your captain still gain eternal glory and immense wealth – or will he find his wet grave under the stormy surface of the Caribbean Sea? Only time will tell...

description:
In Totem!, you need to build the higher totem.  The game ends when a six-head totem is built or if no more cards can be drawn (the common deck is empty) and one player has no more cards in his hands. 
 On each turn, each player either plays a totem head (which have an effect), a "low blow" card (which is discarded after the effect has been applied) or draws two cards. Of course, as you do this the other tribes are trying to do the same to you, wrecking your totem while building theirs. 
Cards allow to combinate effects and you can manage to play more than once during your turn. Team play and bluff is highly recommended in order not become a target for other players.
That's why cards have action not only to upset players, but also to make alliances and help others !
You can play several rounds to win points (when you win a round you score as much as the totem! height). A board where all players can score their points is provided. After each round you can then decide your next better target (which can be the leader of the race if it is not you).

description: Move & Groove is the perfect first game for toddlers! Designed to inspire movement, creativity, laughter and learning, it’s a fun way to introduce the basics of game play and the joy of movement without the pressure of competition. The rules are simple—toss the plush cube to determine color, choose a matching colored card and perform the given move. There’s no right or wrong way to boogie—it’s all up to interpretation—so players can use their imagination while improving balance, coordination and flexibility. The perfect game to build confidence and celebrate all the things your little one does so well!

Game Over is a tabletop card game set within the realm of the digital gaming world where each player must control 3 characters in order to fight for survival.  It is designed for quick and casual gameplay with layers of various strategic elements to keep each round feeling fresh and new.

Players select their characters and then take turns building their inventory by randomly drawing cards from the play deck.  Each turn will allow for a player to either equip an item, attack, perform a special ability, use an action card, or redeem gems for special bonuses throughout the round.  Knowing your enemies from your allies is imperative as each player can "beat the game" in several different ways.  The game is over when a player reaches one of three different win scenarios:

Last Man Standing - Be the last survivor by knocking all other players out of the game
High Score - Be the first to collect 25 bonus points by accumulating gems throughout the round
Quest Complete - Be the first to find the 3 hidden enchanted items within the play deck

A new game from the makers of The Art of Conversation: The Art of Christian Conversation

From the website:
 The Art of Christian Conversation is an innovative and inspiring way for Christians, (and those exploring) to get to know themselves - and God - better. Carefully researched, tested, trialled and refined, TAOC Christian offers new angles and avenues to think about, and discuss, Christian faith and life.

Louise Howland and Keith Lamb, authors of the original best-selling titles, TAOC The Art of Conversation, have collaborated for this special edition with Ross and Suzette Bowerman.

With 300 Christian Communication-builders ranging from the profound to the light-hearted, plus a comprehensive Christian Communication Guidebook, TAOC makes it easy to share and discuss feelings, ideas and experiences related to faith and religious living in a harmonious way.

Like all TAOC editions, it’s not a quiz – and whilst there are myriad of opportunities to learn, it’s never about who knows the most. Rather, it’s a communication tool that draws on personal experiences, thoughts and reactions to facilitate fascinating discussions and new insights. It also encourages clear communication and respectful listening.

TAOC is a handy icebreaker when a new group of people come together and helps people who already know each other take their relationships to new levels. Ideal for small groups, bible study, fellowship, education and the home, TAOC can be played by family, friends, workmates, students or even solitary players. TAOC is suitable for players aged from four to 104.

A new game from the makers of The Art of Conversation: The Art of Food Conversation.

From the website:
Do you eat to live, or are you one of those passionate souls who lives to eat? Food is certainly enjoying a high profile at present. With hit TV shows, social sites, glossy ranks of gastro-porn at every newsagent, and the return of the dinner party, many of us have headed back towards the kitchen as well as continuing our passion for dining out.

TAOC serves up 300 culinary entrees on topics like food destinations, cooking classes, slow food, fusion, indulgent food, leftovers and bizarre food ideas. It includes a comprehensive guidebook to culinary communication, complete with fascinating endnotes.

Like all TAOC editions, it’s not a quiz – it’s not about who knows the most. Rather, it’s a communication tool that draws on your own experiences, thoughts and reactions to facilitate fascinating discussions. Even wannabe foodies and reluctant eaters will love it, because let’s face it – we all eat!

description:
From the inside of the lid:
"Object of the game is to make as many words as possible from the ten (10) letters obtained by spinning the wheels"

Each player has a set of markers and a mini-roulette wheel of the same color. Each hole on the wheel corresponds to two letters of the alphabet and a number.  The board is divided into quadrants, each having a color matching a wheel and a hole where the wheel sets into. Players play only on their like-colored quadrant.

Each quadrant shows an alphabet list.  Players spin their own wheels 5 times to get a set of 10 letters, marked with a disk on the board.  When the 10 letters are obtained, the sand timer is flipped and players race to make the most words from their letters.

The game advertised 6 games total that can be played.  However, the main game, described above, requires some skill.  The other 5 (including one using the numbers on the wheels) are completely luck driven.

boardgame: To The Ends of the Earth
description:
To the Ends of the Earth is a fully-cooperative adventure game based on the 1930's adventure serials. Players take on the roles of intrepid explorers traversing the globe in search of ancient artifacts while trying to outwit and outrun enemy agents.

The game begins with the players choosing a mythical artifact, which will serve as the McGuffin for the game. Each artifact will have a series of clues that are distributed around the world.
Clues take several forms: They can be excavation sites, scraps of historical documents, ancient relics, or even people who may be able to help the players deduce the location of the artifact. Each clue that is collected brings the players on step closer to discovering the artifact’s final resting place. Players will have to act fast as enemy agents (controlled by the game) will be hot on their heels collecting clues and setting ambushes at every turn!

Combat in EotE takes the form of action sequences, or confrontations with the enemy villain that happen periodically throughout the game. Players work together to defeat enemy minions by using their weapons, special abilities, teamwork, and most importantly, the environment around them. Furniture such as crates, explosive barrels, tables, and even bottles of whiskey can be used and manipulated by the players to give them the upper hand in any battle. Find yourself staring down the barrel of a Tommy Gun? Flip over the nearest table for cover! Cornered by a band of hired muscle? Grab the nearest whiskey bottle, set it alight, and toss it at an explosive crate to create the perfect getaway!

To the Ends of the Earth is the board game where you, the players, truly determine the course of your adventure! Fight vigilantly to defeat the enemy villain, recover the artifact, and win the game!

EotE features a unique Action Dice allocation system to determine the options available to each player during their turn. Each player will choose an adventurer to control for the duration of the game. Each adventurer has three "stats", which determine their proficiency for different tasks:

Brawn determines a character’s Strength, Speed, and Endurance, and is used for physical tasks such as brawling, climbing, or using melee weapons.

Skill describes a character’s Dexterity, Coordination, and Awareness. Using firearms, disarming traps, and driving vehicles are made much easier with a high Skill Stat.

Wits relates to a character’s Intelligence, Problem-Solving, and Inter-Personal skills. A high Wits Stat is needed to conduct research, excavate dig sites, or issue commands to allies and other characters.

At the beginning of each turn, a player will roll their available Action Dice (five six-sided dice if their character is healthy). Each die rolled below a Stat may be used for an action that requires that Stat.

Actions include movement, researching artifacts, brawling with enemies, using firearms, or manipulating the environment. Special abilities and equipment broaden the choices available to each player, and with so much potential to strategize, there is never a ‘bad roll’. Every action you take will have consequences that ripple through the rest of the game, creating a unique adventure each time you play!

Das Feuerrote Teufelchen (The Fire-Red Devil) is an abstract chase designed for Spiel sets.

The "devil" player attempts to overtake the "angel" player, while the angel aims to keep moving until the cubes run out. Each time either player moves, the vacated space is filled with a neutral cube from a limited supply.

April 1947. When the Blutkreuz Korps unearthed records of an ancient alien energy source hidden in Mesopotamia, they started working on plans to recover it. Now their plans are coming to fruition.

Operation Babylon thrusts the Neue Deutches Afrikakorps (NDAK), the New German Africa Corps, into Allied territory in central Iraq. Opposing them are the elite 4th Marine Division of the United States Marine Corps and the Spetsnaz special forces of the Sino-Soviet Union (SSU) raid both sides, intent on setting the region ablaze.

Operation Bablyon is a campaign book for Dust Tactics.

The Kebab Shop replaces the Fountain from the original game.

The Kebab Shop has to be placed as one of the inner spaces of the bazaar.

Note: this expansion is produced on card stock rather than the thick cardboard used for the original game tiles.

This extension pack was exclusive to Kickstarter backers.

The Royalty pack includes 30+ cards created by people around the globe to extend Villages with fun new cards.

From the back of the box:

The Cathedrals of the Brotherhood stand as citadels of faith before the overwhelming tide of the Dark Legion. Raised as places of prayer they have become the focus for some of the fiercest battles. Both sides fight for possession of the sacred walls and the powerful relics that lie within the Cathedral vaults. Where one side fights to keep the Light of Faith alive, the other side fights to see it extinguished, replaced forever with Blessed Night.

This Box-set Contains:

A full colour card model of the Brotherhood Cathedral: on eight A4 sheets of card for you to construct.
    Two exclusive new miniatures only available with this box set: The Brotherhood's - "Visionary" and the Dark Legion's - "Angel of Mercy".
    A 32 page booklet, containing 10 scenarios that can be played as stand alone battles or as part of an extended campaign, where the fate of the Cathedral depends upon the success or failure of each scenario. There is separate descriptive text and maps for each scenario and battle photographs throughout to put the struggle for the Cathedral and the two new characters in context.

boardgame: Khrysos Hunters: Ruins of Abetan Expansion
description:
Watch your step! The Ruins of Abetan are a treacherous labyrinth of steep cliffs and rickety bridges, but the fabulous Khrysos treasure inside is all that matters to the Khrysos Hunters.

Expand your hunt to up to six players with Khrysos Hunters: Ruins of Abetan Expansion, which includes ten new Hunters to make your team. Jump the gaps, but don't get pushed in!

About the Game

M.I.N.D is a tactical strategy board game set in a sci-fi setting in which players are in control of a colony and have to manage and expand it while facing different crisis and invaders. M.I.N.D can be played solo or in two players, but also with up to four players with the help of an extra copy. The goal is to build your colony and complete the set of five achievements required by the game before the last turn ends. Players can play as a purely cooperative game where everyone wins or loses together. Or they can play competitively, in which case they can sabotage or attack their opponents.

Players build new sectors, train new citizens and manage their command to do as many actions as they can to maximize production of credits, influence, equipment or overpower while also progressing. The game requires lots of planning since there are many things that can intervene and ruin your plan if not paying attention. Players interact and can either help or sabotage each other. Units can be sent to start fires, break structures, close doors, start an epidemic or even unpower structures. To resolve all these players need to assign command to their units and use them to repair, build or interact with different things from the colony. Players can improve their units by equipping them with items or assigning them influence thus enabling them access to the faction's unique super power.

If you like strategy games, games that can be played solo, games that have confrontation or simply that let you cooperate with a friend while facing a great challenge then M.I.N.D may be the perfect choice for you.

Spiritual Warfare is everything you want in a game! It is strategic, engaging, innovative and offers players the chance to use a different strategy EVERY TIME they play. Acquire Spirit Points to win! Defend yourself with the help of Angels! Demolish your opponents with twelve different Demons.

Each player starts the game with an equal number of Character Cards, 4 Orb Cards, 1 Angel and 1 Demon. The board is set with 5 Orb Cards to draw from, and the rest of the Orb Cards are placed to the left of the pool. There are Black, Red, Blue White, Green and WILD Orb Cards. Black & Red Orbs can be used to purchase Demons. Blue & White Orbs can be used to purchase Angels. Green Orbs are used to by Action Cards, and a combination of all 5 colors gets you a Gate Key.

On a players turn, they may choose to do one of the following actions: 1) pulling Orb Cards 2) spend Orb Cards 3) Attack a player

You obtain Spirit Points by: Purchasing Angels and Demons, going through Gates using Gate Keys and White Orbs, buying Action Cards (some of them are just Spirit Points), or Battling other players!

In a battle, the attacking player uses their Demon Cards and the defending player uses their Angel Cards. Each player starts with 10 hit points. Players take turns rolling dice to try and damage the other player. 5's and 6's are hits wile 1-4 miss. Angel and Demon Cards consist of either Battle Cards that last for the entire battle (i.e. Armored Angel - You cannot be hit by 5's) or Attack Cards that are played before a player rolls the dice (i.e. Fire Demon - On your next roll, 6'd hit for 2 damage). The first player to bring their opponent to zero Hit Points wins.

Finally, the Character Cards in the game all give slight advantages and manipulate all of the mechanics listed above!
 Now...Bow your head, say your prayers, and prepare for Spiritual Warfare!

description:
In order to improve trade, quality of life, and his militia’s ability to quickly respond to threats, the king has commissioned you and your team to complete highways between the towns and villages throughout his Kingdom.

The king’s highway will be constructed with dice having highway segments on their faces. Players work from a communal pool of dice and take turns placing sections of the road to earn prestige. Of course, whoever completes the highway earns a boost of prestige. Be careful, though, completing the highway before all of its paths are finished will cause you to lose points.

Because the king is too busy to pay attention to who did the actual work, whoever has the most prestige at the end of the project is given the credit, the glory, and the money!

Ender Dungeon's Last Crawl is a light game with simple mechanics meant for casual gamers or even as a gateway game. Players each become 1 of 3 heroes sent by the king to exact revenge upon the local terror, Ender Dungeon.

You and your friends will delve deep into Ender’s caves by rolling dice and slaying wave after wave of the boss’ ferocious minions. After 45 to 60 minutes of fierce battle, the surviving heroes will finally come face-to-paper-face with Ender Dungeon himself.

Can you slay the fiend and save the kingdom?

This is a light game with very light mechanics. Players fight against progressively more difficult monster decks. Fighting takes place by rolling dice in an attempt to roll higher than the monsters.

A game of guessing, bluffing, and betting to out-wit your opponents and collect the most points.

Comes with everything you need for 2 - 6 players. You can play a couple of matches for 15 – 20 minutes of fun, or spend up to an hour playing a full game. Top Deck is designed so that inexperienced gamers can rely on dumb-luck to get by, but experienced gamers can develop specific strategies in hopes of out-thinking other players.

You will each use a personal themed deck, Bonus cards, and character abilities to compete for individual Slots. The highest bidder wins, but each slot has an increasing value, so the stakes get higher as the game goes on.

Terra Mystica: Fire & Ice has the original factions brace themselves for new competitors: Yetis – the masters of power, Ice Maidens – who adore their Temples, Acolytes – whose entire life is focused on the cults, and Dragonlords – who use their power to create volcanoes. And as if this wasn't enough, there are two more factions, Shapeshifters and Riverwalkers, who ignore the most basic of rule of one faction, one terrain type. (Inconceivable to the Halflings!)

A two player game played on 3 standard chess boards arranged vertically one above the other. The "light" squares are transparent to help see what pieces are where.  Each player starts with 16 pieces on each board in almost the same setup as standard chess.

There is only one king and he starts in the normal position on the centre board. Kings, Queens and Bishops move as normal but extended into 3 dimensions. Knights move as normal and stay on the board they started on. Pawns and rooks move as normal but can also move levels. There are five new pieces, Prince (which replaces king on top and bottom boards), Princess, Abbey, Cannon and Galley, each having there own special movement rules.  The object of the game is to take the king.

Based on by 3dchess program for GNU/Linux. This variant is played on three standard 8×8 boards, stacked vertically. The middle board features the standard pieces, while the following new pieces populate the other two boards:

Prince (King), Princess (Queen), Abbey (Bishop), Cannon (Knight), Galley (Rook)

The movements of various pieces have been modified to allow them to move across boards (for example the Cannon must move three spaces in one direction, two in a perpendicular direction and one in the remaining perpendicular).

This version was developed and produced in a plastic board game format by Mind Games Manufacturing Limited.

description:
Haven: Titan Command is a 2-4 player sci-fi strategy card game set in an new and exciting galaxy where each player takes command of a hulking futuristic battle Titan. There are four rivalling factions currently at war, and each player must choose their allegiance, construct a deck and then charge into battle to claim victory for their faction.

The Core Set features over 190 cards, allowing players to build their own decks using a combination of faction and neutral cards to fully customise their Titan build. Each Titan build must carefully balance its reliance on combat equipment armed and activated during the fight, pilot manoeuvres which are activated as surprise actions to evade or ambush, and field skills which provide support from other battlefield units.

Players battle it out over a number of rounds until only one Titan is left standing victorious.

Simple Formula 1 race game.

It's the only one game published by "El taller del ocio", a little spanish publisher.

The player attempts to complete the circuit, overcoming obstacles in race, trying to make your team add more points than opponents or come first.

It is a game of rolling dice and move forward with some obstacles and a penalty die.

Druglord is a thematic war-game based on the history of Cartel's wars, with a strong commitment pro-legalization.

4-6 Players will impersonate and control the most powerful and dangerous Cartels in the world: Mafia, Yakuza, Triads, the Cartel of Medellín, the National Crime Syndicate and the Bratva (Russian Mafia). They'll have to distribute their forces all over the world, conquer the production places of drugs (for example: Colombia or Afghanistan) and the markets (cities like Los Angeles, Amsterdam or Tokio), organizing the flow of 4 kinds of substances that have different perks. This way players will gain soldiers that can be used to attack or defend; cooperation is acceptable, regulated and sometimes very useful. There are also cards to be drawn at each turn, inspired by historical or fictitious events related to the criminal world. They can have good or bad effects, and often there are choices to be made.

The goal is known at the beginning of the game, when each player draws a goal card (all different but balanced) that has to remain secret until the conditions of victory are fulfilled. There's also a semi-randomized countdown towards legalization: an event that in our point of view (and according to the ideas of many economists) would terminate the business of Cartels. When this happens (after 2-3 hours, according to the players' choices) the game is over and victory is assigned by points.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other (fancy) elements of Juicy Couture, a brand name.

Full Attack (Greatbow): Ranged 1-2, 4 Dice

When Bloodwave Greatbow is affected by a friendly command spell, his Greatbow attack gains +2 until the end of the round.

boardgame: Hit It! Tactical Card Game of Tennis
description:
Hit It! is a quick print & play card game of tennis for two players.

Each player has a deck of 25 cards, of three types to represent different tennis shots, plus two types of cards that modify shots. Each player sets out four cards face down to form the playing surface. (Choosing these cards adds an element of deck building to the game.) Each player takes six of the remaining cards from their deck. The first player "serves" by placing a card on one of the opponent's hitting zones and draws a replacement card from his deck. The opponent then moves his player marker to the ball and selects what card he will use to hit the ball back. This continues until one player manages to place a fourth card in a particular hitting zone, or a player is unable to return the ball.

Play continues until one player has won the set, i.e. six games.

Hit It! has abstracted tennis into a quick tactical card game, but retains the feel of the sport.

The long, lost island of Jungle Heart has just been discovered.  What mysteries lie beneath its shadowy canopies?

Monstrous animals!

Vicious tribes!

Malicious militants!

Deadly traps and hazards!

Ghosts and demons!

Yet beyond all of these obstacles lies crumbling temples brimming with gold, jewels, and priceless relics.

In Jungle Heart, you and your opponents take on the role of an investor in the 1940s, sending adventurers, scientists, big game hunters and other pulp standards to the jagged rock shores of Jungle Heart to find the clues necessary to tap into the unlimited wealth of the island.

Game play is simple:

1) Recruit and equip your adventuring party... then it's "off we go" to the island.

2) Use supply tokens to pay for actions while on the island.

3) When the supplies run dry, your adventurers return to the mainland to regroup and reequip.

4) Play a victory card to earn points.

Jungle Heart is the second game in development from Wine Key Games and is capable of bringing hours--nay! weeks of indepth play.

Are you prepared to visit Jungle Heart?

A two player game played on an 18x24 board. Players each place two 2x6"mountains" on the board and then each place 54 soldiers(25mm plastic figures) on the 3 rows nearest them. Each player secretly selects a restricted set of 5 squares on his opponents rear row. 
  The winner is the first player to get his own soldiers on all five of these squares.
 On his turn a plyer takes a card and follows its instructions. He then rolls two dice, one die number represents the number of units he will move and the other die represnts the number of squares units move. He then moves his units and executes capture by replacement, some decided by die roll.
 As far as I can tell all the "Action" A-Toys are the same game on the same board but with different figures.

description:
From inside the game: "At last the grand old Army game of shooting dice (craps) has been lifted into respectability- to be enjoyed often when friends meet."

Checker Dice consists of 126 cards (the game calls them "checkers") numbered from 2 to 12. The cards have pictures of die rolls, but the frequency of the numbers does not quite match the statistical likelihood of rolling those numbers on 2D6. For example, there are equal numbers of sixes, sevens, and eights.

To play, players turn over cards and hope to get certain numbers and avoid others, just as in Craps. Once a card is turned over, it remains face up for the remainder the player's turn, which slightly affects the statistical probability of finding another card with the same number.

A Trivial game about soccer and soccer-players. During your turn you have to draw 3 soccer-player counters from a draw pile of 72 and order them according to a random trait that could be: age, height, caps in the national team, caps in mundial, goal in the national team and goal in mundial.

Other players have to agree or disagrre to your decision.

All the players have also bet counter to use during the game.

boardgame: Knights of Arthur: A Game of Knightly Adventure in the Age of King Arthur.
description:
BACKGROUND: Kinghts of Arthur is an adventure game where the players assume the role of a Heroic Knights in the age of King Arthur. The Player Knights are young, eager Knights anxious to prove themselves in order to become a Knight of the Round Table. In order to accomplish this, the Player Knights must seek out adventures and quests to improve their attributes and receive a grant from King Arthur to join the Knights of the Round Table.

Game-Play
Each Player creates their own Player Knight Character composed of the following Attributes: Virtue, Resolve, Chivalry, Stamina, Combat and Defense. Each Player Knight takes their turn individually, using a Player-Knight Counter to represent themselves on the Map. Each Player's Turn consists of the following actions:
SEQUENCE OF PLAY
1. Move, Heal wounds or Stay Put. Party may move, Heal or remain in the current location. 
2. Encounter Check: Roll on the Encounter Table for each area entered to determine if an Encounter occurs.
3. Resolve Encounter. If an Encounter occurs, roll on the Reaction Chart to determine the Encounter’s reaction. Resolve the Encounter to conclusion, including combat.
4. Conduct Knightly Action: The Player Knight may conduct one of the following Actions: Enter Town/Castle, Shop, Seek Entry to a Castle,Resolve Quest, Acquire a Ship, and several other actions.
5. Attribute Improvement: Player Knights may Trade in earned Valor Points to Improve their Attributes.

Combat is resovled on a Battle Mat representing a Castle and surroundings. The combat system is man-to-man and has the feel of skirmish fighting, but, resolved quickly without slowing the game.

Knights of Arthur is full of Arthurian Themes from various sources, but, especially from Sir Thomas Mallory's Le Morte D'Arthur. As a Knight, you will perform many Knightly duties including completing quests, dispatching bandits and brigands, participate in battles and sieges, rescuing damsels and possibly serving them. There are Tournaments to participate in and Monsters to slay!

If you enjoy books like Le Morte D'Arthur or movies like Excalibur, you will enjoy this game.

Knights of Arthur can be found at http://www.berserkergames.net

English:
The King wants to know which of his battalions is the strongest and faithful. They will be tested and the best one, will become the royal guard and will get the king's favor.

Each battalion is composed by 5 soldiers, 3 swordsman, 1 squire and 1 knight.
Each soldier has a different force value and movement, so players would need to combine them adequately in order to conquest the king's districts and capture the enemy battalions.

On each turn, player has up 3 movements to be distributed on 
his soldiers. It should move at least one soldier per turn.

The king will announce 3 districts to conquest and then, the battalions will enter into the citadel to conquest it. The first one to have presence in the 3 districts will be the winner. Capturing soldiers enemies can also give the victory, as once 3 solders from a battalion are capture, they loses the conquest.

Once the winner of a conquest is declared. He take the district card and there is a new announcement. Then, a new district card is placed on the board to start a new conquest round.

The battalion that gets first 3 conquests (3 district cards), will become the king’s royal guard.

Spanish:
El Rey quiere saber cual de sus batallones es el mas fuerte y leal. Estos serán sometidos a prueba para determinar quien es el mejor, quien se convertirá en la guardia real y tendrán el favor del rey.

Cada batallón esta compuesta por 5 soldados: 3 espadachines, 1 escudero y 1 caballero.
Cada soldado tiene un valor de fuerza y movimiento diferente, por lo que los jugadores tendrán que combinarlos adecuadamente a fin de conquistar los distritos y capturar los batallones enemigos.

En cada turno, los jugadores tendrán hasta 3 movimientos para distribuir entre sus soldados. Deberá mover al menos 1 soldado una vez.

El rey anunciará 3 distritos para conquistar y es cuando los batallones entraran en la ciudadela para conquistarlo. El primero en tener presencia en los 3 distritos será el ganador. Capturar soldados enemigos también podría dar la victoria, pues una vez 3 soldados de un batallón son capturados pierden la conquista.

Una vez el ganador de la conquista es declarado, el jugador guarda la carta de distrito y nuevo anuncio del rey tendrá lugar. Entonces se saca una nueva carta de distrito y comienza una nueva ronda de conquista.

El batallón que consiga primero los 3 conquistas (3 cartas de distritos) será nombrado nueva guardia real.

Spanish 80´s clone of the popular game Trivial.

From the spanish box:

"¡Este juego contiene más de 3000 preguntas, participa y que las fuerzas del saber te acompañen!"

"This game has more than 3000 questions, play and may the forces of knowledge be with you!"

Vienga is a deck of cards that captures and simplifies the English language. Each depicts a letter, a number, and a clearly illustrated part of speech (noun, tense, adverb...) or sentence form. The back of the card has example sentences to show how each grammar point is used.

The Vienga cards can be used to play a variety of games. Simply use the cards to create equations, or words, or sentences. Vienga can be a challenging group game or a solo learning aid. The Vienga cards help you visualize sentence structures, recombine and replace words, and develop creative thinking. Challenging for native speakers and students with a good command of English wishing to improve their language skills.

Made entirely of wood and designed to be self-contained, Tabula is its own box. The game closes in on itself to become an sleek tabula, "tablet" in Latin.  The name Tabula  also comes from "Tabula Rasa", meaning "blank slate" - the board starts as a blank slate that then becomes filled with the tiles.

Tabula is a a simple turn-based strategy game in which the objective is to occupy the most spaces of the board. In this game, each player has access to four different type of special tiles that have unique abilities: Attack, Defense, Expansion, Jump. The game-play consists of playing either 1 special tile or two regular tiles (that have no abilities). The game ends when all the spaces of the board have been filled. Whoever has the most tiles is the winner.

Not only is this game a source of tremendous fun, but also is a beautiful aesthetic addition to the home.

The King is looking for adventurers to fill his ranks of champions, an elite fighting force charged with protecting the King and doing his more “hands on” work.
In an attempt to weed out the weak from the application pool, the King conscripted the Royal Academy of Magi to construct a magical labyrinth.  Loaded with deadly traps, vicious monsters and other contestants, you must live long enough to escape in order to become one of the chosen few.  One of the Kings Champions!

Labyrintheus is a brand new dungeon survival card game that introduces a unique Persona Card System where you compete against you fellow party members to defeat Monsters and Avoid Traps while collecting Gate Stones.  Gate Stones are used to activate the portal to the outside world.

One of the things that makes Labyrintheus unique is the persona card system.  When you chose a character to play, you will have their persona card.  More of a tile than a card, you use this for your inventory system and to track your health.  Treasure cards collected in Labyrintheus are equipped by placing them along the appropriate edges of the persona card.  And once you die, flip the persona card over to reveal your stats and abilities as an undead servant of the King.  If you can't win, why should anyone else?!?

SUNDAY! SUNDAY! SUNDAY! Monster Truck Mayhem is invading the SuperMegaDome! Brought to you by Ridback Pub, 101 The Animal, and Big Al’s Sound Emporium! This thrill show spectacular will BLOW YOUR MIND! All your favorite trucks and more will be there… AND SO SHOULD YOU! Car crushing, Mud Sliding, Bus Jumping, and general mayhem for only $10! TEN BUCKS gets you the whole seat... but you will only need the edge Edge EDGE!

Race around the SuperMegaDome and be the first to cross the finish line!

Monster Truck Mayhem is a real-time dice rolling racing game. Players simultaneously roll their dice to race around the SuperMegaDome track and the winner is the first player to cross the finish line!

Players use custom dice to move 1, 2, or 3 spaces at a time. “Tire” and “Boost” are used to navigate course obstacles like the Car Crush, Bus Jump, Mud Pit, and more! Three “Boost” is an instant movement bonus. Three “Tires” causes another player to spin out!

Crush obstacles AND the competition and be the first Monster Truck to cross the finish line and take the checkered flag!

description: Party game where players have to guess which song is being hummed.  Part of a series of games published by John Lewis and Ginger Fox Ltd.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other (fancy) elements of the Great Canadian Gaming Cooperation.

From the rules:
Investo is a game of High Finance for three or more players. Play consists of investing In various companies, and receiving from the "INVESTO" Bank, Dividends and Bonuses in accordance with Instructions on a number of cards drawn by the players in rotation. Companies become bankrupt by the drawing of one of the Bankrupt cards.

TO BEGIN. One of the players is selected as Banker, and issues each of the players with £1,000 with which to make his or her investments; retaining the balance of the money as the "INVESTO" Bank. (The Banker is entitled to join in the game, and should also receive the initial £1,000.) The cards are then shuffled and placed face downwards in the centre of the table. The cards representing the ten companies are shuffled separately and placed face downwards beside the Banker, who is at all times in charge of the game. A number of company cards are then turned face upwards, in front of the Banker; the number so faced being one less than the number of players. (e.g. If five people are playing, then four company cards are turned up.) Players now invest in one or more of the companies as desired. All investments must be in multiples of £100. The Banker keeps a record of all investments.
 THE PLAY. The first player (determined by any method desired) then draws the top card and places it face up in front of him. Each card has a number of the top representing one of the companies. If the player drawing the card has invested in that company, he announces the dividend (e.g. No. 4 —30% or 6/- in the £). The Banker thereupon pays ALL players who have invested in No. 4 company, 30% of their investment, from the money in the Bank. Should the player not have invested in the company whose card is drawn, the play passes to the person next on his left, and so on round the table; each player announcing the dividend or bonus to be paid. ONLY If he has money invested in the company.

BANKRUPTCY. On a player drawing a "BANKRUPT" card for the company in which he has invested, he announces "No. 7 —Bankrupt", and then reads the directions as to payment to or from the Bank (e.g. "Shareholders must pay in 10/- for every £ invested, to meet the liabilities of the Company.") or "Shareholders receive 5/- for every £ invested in the Company, from the sale of assets," as the case may be. The Banker then collects the money from, or pays the money to, the shareholders in accordance with the instructions. After all the players have paid or received their money, the Banker removes the bankrupt company from play and exposes the next company card. Any player who had invested in the company that was just bankrupt now has the opportunity to invest in the new company. There is no compulsion, and a player who has money invested in another company may elect not to invest in the new company. On oil investments being completed and recorded by the Banker, play continues with the next player.

CHANGE OF INVESTMENT. Changes in investments can only be made when all the cards have been drown. All changes must be recorded by the banker who makes a charge of 10% on the total investment for the issue of the new "Script". (e.g. a player increases his investment as one of the companies from £200 to £500. The Banker charges £50.). 
GENERAL. After all the cards have been drawn, they are re-shuffled, placed face down, and commeacing with the player on the left of the person who drew the last card, play re-commences. When a company is bankrupt and there are no more companies to be exposed, the players who had invested in the last company to go bankrupt may choose any ONE of the remaining companies in which to invest. The game is cancluded when the last company is bankrupt. The winner is the player who finishes the game with the most money.
