boardgame: Zombicide: Special Guest Box – Miguel Coimbra
description: This expansion includes two survivors and their Zombivor counterparts both designed by Miguel Coimbra.

This is the day of the great horse show! Our riders prepare theirs horses and stay focused despite unexpected competition and traps set by their opponents. A game of combinations and love which unites the rider and his horse will overcome all the trials!

In order to win you have to prepare your pony for the competition before others competitors. To do this, each player must collect the following cards in order: Halter, Pony, Grooming, Fillet, Saddle Pad, Saddle

The First Player to complete these six steps wins.


C'est le jour du grand concours équestre ! Nos cavalières doivent préparer leurs montures et rester concentrées malgré les imprévus de la compétition et les pièges tendus par leurs adversaires. Un jeu de combinaisons ou l'amour, qui uni la cavalière et sa monture, saura triompher de toutes les épreuves !

Pour gagner, il faut préparer son poney pour le concours avant les autres concurrents. Pour ce faire, chaque joueur doit collecter, dans l'ordre, les cartes suivantes : Licol, Poney, Pansage, Filet, Tapis, Selle.

Le premier joueur qui parvient à accomplir ces 6 étapes remporte la manche.

Où se cache le Corbeau ?
Avec le Renard...
La Cigale va-t-elle retrouver sa fourmi ?

Animé par les personnages des célèbres Fables de La Fontaine, Misti’zzle est un jeu de cartes basé sur l’observation au graphisme adapté pour les enfants.

Le but du jeu est d’assembler un maximum de cartes par paire pour former des mini-scènes des Fables de La Fontaine, en évitant d’avoir en main à la fin de la partie, la carte Mistigri représentée par le Chat Botté du conte de Perrault.

Les mini-scènes s’assemblent ensemble à la manière d’un puzzle pour créer le Misti’zzle (un puzzle de 4 cartes de haut sur 10 cartes de large).

--

Each player organises and places all their existing pairs face up on the table. Then the youngest player begins by taking at random any card from the player to his left in the hope of matching a new pair. If he makes a pair with this new card, he places it face up on the table, and takes another card from the same player to his left. If not he keeps the card and the next player takes his turn and play continues thus, until there are no more pairs. Gradually as 
the game progresses the players are able to assemble the Misti’zzle a tableau presenting images of the stories of Jean de La Fontaine.

boardgame: Philippines Ablaze: A Solitaire Game of the Philippine Insurrection (1899-1902)
description:
BACKGROUND: Philippines Ablaze is semi-historical simulation of the United States war in the Philippines to put down the insurrection of General Aguinaldo. The Game begins shortly after the Insurrectionist Leader, General Emilio Aguinaldo, declares independence from the United States controlled government of the Philippines.

OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the United States Forces in the Philippines. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to capture four of the five Key Insurrecto Controlled Settlements (San Fernando, Tuao, San Jose, Puerto Galera, Cagayan) before the last Campaign Card is played, the Insurrectionists Win.

GAME FEATURES: The game features a fun and simplistic method for resolving solitaire combat tactically, rules for Heroes that can make an impact on the outcome of battles, and lots of colorful game counters.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Bill Furlong & Siegfried
description: A promotional mini-expansion, including two new heroes, one for the US forces and another for the German forces.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

boardgame: Tiger Attack!  The Battle of the Bulge, Ardennes 1944
description:
A relatively fast, very playable point-to-point game at roughly regiment/combat command/brigade unit scale and 1-day per turn time scale, using my simple Dark Crusade combat table and system, plus Disruption and fortification.  U.S. starts with 19 units, Germans with many more.  Playing time is a couple hours.

Special Peiper, Bayerlein, and Abrams leader counters and a Skorzeny 150th Brigade Brandenburgers unit are included, as well as the 2 historical Tiger tank battalions.  (Some offensive-shocked GIs were reporting any panzer as being a Tiger tank.)

Fuel is unlimited for the Americans and crucial and weather-dependent for the Germans - vs. Allied airpower.  My Patton's Prayer rule (for better weather) from my hex-and-counter Tigers in the Ardennes is included, as is a rule for the chance of a "blonde and beautiful" U.S. Army nurse diverting Bayerlein's attention, as occurred historically and possibly decisively on his/Panzer Lehr Division's way to Bastogne.

Map and units can alternatively be used with Major Battles and Campaigns combat system.

description:
Was kommt dazu (What's added now) is a tile laying game.

It consists of 72 square cardboard tiles, similar to the tiles of a memory game, and a rulebook. There are 6 tile series with different pictures. 3 series of 16 tiles each (steamboat, wonder machine, sun flower), 2 series of 9 tiles each (flowers of the spring, bird), 1 series of 6 tiles (helicopter). The basic idea is that one tile of each series has the least amount of small details, another one the greatest amount of details (like with or without doorknow depicted, a bee with or without wings etc.). These cards must be sorted according to the amount of details picted from least to most in ascending order. A check is possible by turing the tiles around. On each tile back is one half of an animal or thing - horse, train car etc. If the cards are in correct order those halves match.

The rulebook suggests 3 games:
1) Solo game: The solo player takes one or more tile series, shuffles them and must lay them in the correct order. 
2) Group game: The short helicopter series is done away with. Each player gets one tile series (well shuffled). Players start to put their series in order. The first player to do this wins - if he did correctly. This can be done with one round or in several rounds. If rounds are played the player who finishes first and has his order correct he gets victory points equal to the number of tiles in the series plus 5 extra points. The other players get one victory point for each tile that is in correct order. For the next round the series are shuffled again and each player gets another series than before.
3) What is disappeared: A series is laid on the table in random order. The picture with the most details is laid in the middle. Players look out for the tile where just one little detail disappeared then the next one etc.

boardgame: Kings of War: The Basilean Legacy
description:
The Basilean Legacy is a supplement for Mantic's Kings of War (KoW).  KoW is a miniatures wargame set in a fantasy world and Basilean Legacy expands both setting's background and wargame by providing three new army lists.  The new army lists are the Basilean Army (good aligned humans backed by angelic beings); Forces of Nature (neutral aligned fey and elementals) and Forces of the Abyss (evil aligned monsters and demons).

In addition to the background information and new army lists, the supplement also expands magic artefacts and one of the original spells (Zap!) in the original KoW rules.  These changes to magic provide additional variety in gameplay and approaches to magic strategy in the game.

The supplement is available as a hard copy version and in electronic PDF format.

In the game Flying Kung-Fu Frogs, players try to launch their colored frogs onto the game base's spinning platforms to score points. Each platform is worth a different point value from 10 to 40 points. If you land on a gray base or get stuck on a branch, you score no points. If your frog lands on the table or anywhere besides the game base, pick it up and try again. To launch a frog, press down on its rear to fling it forward. As your finger slips off, the frog should jump toward the platforms. Once all frogs are somewhere on the game base, the game ends. Count up your points, and the player with the most points wins. This game is for two to four players. It comes with a Kung Fu Training Center game base, 20 frogs (five of each color), and a sticker sheet.

Potions Class is a press-your-luck dice game where players compete to be the top student in potions class. Be the first to reach 100 points and end the class with a higher score than anyone else to win. Of course in a potions class students can also craft concoctions to give themselves an edge on the other students.

Roll matching symbols to rack up points but if you press it too much you may end up failing the testing and getting none of the points, and fizzling any partially crafted potions.

Class is in session.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Yeager Expansion Pack
description:
This ship is one of the five ships players purchase as an entrance fee to parcipate in the Star Trek Attack Wing "The Collective Month 1" tournaments.

A Federation ship that is a 'Saber Class' starship.  Can be seen depicted in episodes of TV show DS9.

description: This ship is one of the ships players purchase as an entrance fee to participate in the Star Trek Attack Wing "The Collective Month 1" tournaments.

This ship is one of the ships players purchase as an entrance fee to participate in the Star Trek Attack Wing "The Collective Month 1" tournaments.

I.R.W. Vorta Vor (with generic Romulan Bird-of-Prey and maneuver card)

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Gavroche Independent Expansion Pack
description: This ship is one of the ships players purchase as an entrance fee to participate in the Star Trek Attack Wing "The Collective Month 1" tournaments.

Pits heroes and villains from classic novels against one another in a race to collect the Books of Power, and a battle for all mankind.  In The Keeper Board Game you can join forces with Shay, his girlfriend Becky, Grandfather Nolan, or a cast of heroes from classic novels such as Peter Pan and Pinocchio.

OR

Work with the Master and his cast of the world's most infamous villains in a race to the Books of Power. When a book is collected you unlock one of the characters from that book to use in part two. Good unlocks good. Evil unlocks evil. For example if Shay were to reach the novel Dracula first he'd unlock Van Helsing! If the Master were to reach Dracula first he'd unlock Dracula. Once all of the books are collected, and their characters unlocked, part two begins.

Careful there are skirmishes along the way that may set you back! Play a Battle Point Card, and draw your enemy into battle. Loser loses a turn. Winner gets an extra chance to draw an unlock-able card. Weapons and characters are hidden within the deck. A chance at unlocking them makes drawing from the deck even more exciting.

In part two you take all of your unlocked characters and battle by rolling dice. Last man/woman/team standing wins!

Age of Steam Expansion: Ukraine, an expansion for Age of Steam that's part of the Winsome Games 2014 Essen set, includes a new action called "Civil Disorder" that can be selected in addition to the usual actions. This action affects the ability to deliver cubes to/from a hex of the selector's choosing.

boardgame: Celebrity: Race to the A-List Board Game
description:
The ultimate celebrity board game.
Race your family and friends around the celebrity board, collecting fan club members along the way.  The first person to finish with at least one million fans wins.

Sing, dance and act tasks with celebrity trivia, impersonations and fun celebrity challenges.

Pink squares are celebrity trivia.
Blue squares are Which Celebrity am I? questions.
Green squares are celebrity impersonations.
Orange squares are various celebrity tasks and rewards.
Sing / Dance / Act / Media and Talk Show circles are performing tasks.  
All cards are either winning or sometimes losing fan club members. First person to the finish with at least one million fans is the winner.

Further information is on the website www.giveitagogames.com.au

This game covers the battle of Płowce, fought between the Kingdom of Poland and the Teutonic Order. Despite the Polish victory on the field, the battle is traditionally regarded as inconclusive given that the Teutonic Order was not destroyed. Nevertheless, it was an important battle for Poland, which was just regaining its stature as a country on the international scene, and held its own against a powerful military force.

It was published in 27th issue of "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine. It contains board and 92 counters, along with two scenarios:

- Hasło "Kraków" (early stage of the battle)
- Płowce (late stage of the battle)

description:
Crossing the Moro is a Scenario / Campaign Game pack which depicts the Princess Patricia’s Canadian Light Infantry's battle to capture Villa Rogatti  in December 1943. it contains:


    One colour 22" x 33" historical map with one-inch hexes of the Villa Rogatti 
    Four Scenarios played on various parts of the map, 
    A Four-Scenario Date, Campaign Game; and 
    Chapter CM that includes rules to play the Campaign Game. 


The 1st Canadian Infantry Division, having captured Campobosso in late October, began withdrawing in late November, bound for the Adriatic coast, where they were to replace a British division that had been badly mauled in the crossing of the lower Sangro River. The Canadians were assigned the far right flank of the British Eighth Army, on the coast, and given the task of driving northwards to capture the small port town of Ortona. Before they could do that, however, they would have to cross the heavily defended Moro River, about three-kilometres South of Ortona. The Canadians' plan called for the crossing the river and the securing of a bridgehead at three points; on the right along the coast, the Hastings and Prince Edward Regiment was to capture San Donato; in the centre, the Seaforth Highlanders were to capture San Leonardo, and on the left flank, the Princess Patricia’s Canadian Light Infantry was to capture Villa Rogatti.

Crossing the Moro is not a complete game and ownership of a tactical wargaming system such as MMP’s Advanced Squad Leader, Critical Hit’s Advanced Tobruk System, or Firefly WWII Battlegroup Actions is required.

From back of the box:

Tabletop fun for all ages! This curling game uses real ice - not as the playing surface, but as the curling stones themselves! The set includes a 6 well ice cube tray with which to make your curling stones, 6 curling stone handles, 2 brushes and a 1.5 metre curling lane.

Zombicide Angry Neighbors challenges you to face off on a whole new breed of zombies! These Seeker zombies have you and your friends in their sights and will chase you to your bitter end!

Every Seeker zombie get an additional activation every time a seeker zombie spawn card is drawn.

Angry Neighbors brings four new survivors to your apocalypse, each with their own special abilities, and their Zombivor counterpart!

Angry Neighbors brings more than just new survivors to your party, it brings you companions! These four special human companion miniatures are here to support you! These companions may be strong enough to survive on their own, but can't commit zombicide like you do! Rescue them, lead them, and add their firepower and special abilities to your party!

Angry Neighbors wants you enter the next era of the apocalypse! Set at the same time as Rue Morgue, buildings have crumbled and roads have collapsed. Now gaping holes litter the city's streets! With poor decision making you may find your survivors slowed down in these holes, and with a wrong move you may just end up in zombie filled pit from hell!

With these new zombies and environmental hazards, you're going to need something to keep you safe! Luckily for you, Angry Neighbors adds all new weapons to your core game as well as a collection of Ultrared equipment! These equipment cards offer the most powerful and potent zombie-obliterating weapons in the Zombicide universe to date! Perfect to up your survivors' survivability!

Fend Off the Angry Neighbors!

Zombicide Angry Neighbors Contains:

18 seeker Zombie Miniatures.
    4 Survivor miniatures with ID cards.
    4 Zombivor miniatures.
    8 Companion miniatures (4 types, 2 each)
    3 Double sided tiles.
    63 Cards: 27 Equipment cards, 12 Zombie cards, 8 Companion cards, 16 Wounded cards.
    27 Tokens: Barricades, Companion makers, Helicopter etc.
    Experience trackers and a Rulebook.

Very simple spin and move game. Players have little Choo Choo Train pieces that move along a track through a zoo. Spaces are divided into (four) colours and Zoo Gates. If a player lands on a Zoo Gate, he or she draws an Animal Card. There are 32 cards with eight types of animal, but which animals a player has (or how many of each) has no effect on the game.

The player that has the most Animal Cards once all players have reached the end (back at Sesame Street) is the winner.

Once upon a time, an enigma and a race's map for a new kind of web race. 
One full year of mystery, call it an adventure, a quest or a race  ! 
Raiders & miners sail right through the map, solve several enigma and dive into the web to discover the lost Bitcoin...

Remember everything you'll need is in the map ! I swear it on the hidden Bitcoin wallet.

But do you really want to win the Raider's of The Lost Bitcoin's Trophy ?

Mexico is known for being one of the countries with most botanical diversity in the world. Regions such as Tehuacan, in the mexican state of Puebla, have a great variety of plant species adapted to extreme conditions such as cactus and agave.

In this game, players will help the conservation of this species by creating the biggest and most biodiverse bothanical garden. In their turn, each player selects cards two cards from four different decks depending on which plants they need on their garden, then they pass a card to the player on the right and they can plant a specimen. The game ends when all the cards from the decks are placed in each botanical garden and then the players score their sets.

Spanish description:

México se caracteriza por ser uno de los países con mayor diversidad botánica del mundo. Regiones como la de Tehuacán en el estado de Puebla, tienen gran variedad de especies de plantas adaptadas a condiciones extremas como gran insolación y poca precipitación, predominando plantas como los cactus o los agaves.

En este desafío, los jugadores se suman al esfuerzo por la conservación de estas especies de una manera divertida y dinámica para lograr formar el jardín botánico más grande y con mayor diversidad de especies. En su turno, cada jugador toma dos cartas de los mazos de cartas dependiendo de la especie que le haga falta en su jardín y posteriormente pasa una carta al jugador de su derecha, finalmente puede plantar plantas en su jardín para completar las especies. El juego termina cuando las cartas de los mazos se acaban y después se hace la puntuación dependiendo de los sets que hayan completado.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – A Deadly Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

“When the snows fall and the white winds blow, the lone wolf dies, but the pack survives.”
   –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

Play the game of thrones for higher stakes than ever before with A Deadly Game, the second Chapter Pack in the Wardens cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! In this expansion, you’ll find new support for some of the Wardens cycle’s key themes: trait-based decks, limited responses, and cards that reward you for using some of the game’s most powerful and recognizable characters.

This Chapter Pack offers sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards), including new versions of cards such as Brienne of Tarth, Khal Drogo, and The Eyrie. Whether you choose to bide your time with Castle plots, create decks based on traits such as Dothraki or House Tully, or observe and strike from afar with House Arryn, you’ll find the cards you need in A Deadly Game.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Odkrywcy Internetu (Explorers of the Internet) is an educational board game designed for children and teenagers. During the game the players will learn some tips about staying secure in the Internet but also how to not break various laws using available resources on-line.
In the game the players will gain levels of knowledge in various skills, useful while using the Internet. These skills will be helpful when some dangerous events occur. So one should better be ready and score as many points as they can while they still can! They will also need some luck to get the most wanted benefits and they should always have some free space in their memory to remember some very important facts. The player with most points at the end of the game will win.
publishers: Wojewódzka Biblioteka Publiczna i Centrum Animacji Kultury w Poznaniu

description:
X-Hour: XenoStrike is an action point-based dice allocation game in which you command an elite combat team in Advanced Personnel Exo-Suits, affectionately known as APEs. Your mission is to exterminate Xenomorphs that have infested a facility before it gets overwhelmed by their incessant spawning.

The Xenomorphs have anticipated your team's insertion and have positioned Xeno Sentinels to guard their spawn sites, while a Xeno Prowler roams the facility, hunting your APEs.

Tactical wargame designed for military training, played by two players or teams plus an umpire. The full game consists of a rulebook, 8 geomorphic map 1x1 meter boards with 3D terrain, and 1000 game pieces representing military units of company size and below.

This is a free wargame, meaning there are very few rules. Players on each side write orders on map sheets and the umpire then makes all decisions on what the outcomes are, falling back to roll dice when not sure about something.

boardgame: Zombicide: Box of Zombies – Set #9: VIP #1 Very Infected People
description:
VIP - Very Infected People
Very Infected People are just like normal walkers, they come with special spawn cards which you shuffle into your spawn deck. When you kill one you can keep it on your card. If you discard a set of 5 VIPs with different sculpts you get a pimpweapon of your choice.

This set consists of 20 VIP zombie miniatures, 12 Zombie spawn cards, 3 equipment cards and rules.

The Londonderry Mini-Expansion adds 4 new Guild Cards for use with the Londonderry map on Mercury Games' "The Walled City".

These 4 Guild Cards can also be mixed into the Borderlands deck as per the original game rules.

The Borderlands Mini-Expansion contains 4 new Guild Cards for use with the Borderlands map in Mercury Games' "The Walled City".

The 4 Guild Cards can also be mixed into the Londonderry cards as per the original rules.  The cards are as follows:

- Masons (Build Wall/Road instead of Road/Wall)
- Gamers (Your citizens are untouchable)
- Cutlers (Score full points on neighborhood tie)
- Pewterers (May place all on same side of Road)

Killer Croquet is a thematic arena board game with tactical positioning, risk management, and multiple paths to victory. Fast paced and strategic, Killer Croquet is quick to learn and dramatic enough to match it's absurd theme.

6 Six of the Worlds Greatest Croquet Players have been kidnapped by a Shadow Organization known as the Pall Mall. Take on the role of an ex-soviet super spy, a time traveling steampunk cyborg, or the nordic God of Thunder as you play for the highest of stakes: your lives.

In Killer Croquet, you control a player piece and a ball piece. You get two actions on you turn that can be split up between moving, hitting your ball, and hitting the other players. When you move you can choose to move to your ball or 3 spaces in any direction. When you hit your ball, you choose a direction and a strength and then roll custom dice to see how far the ball goes: Softly (1,1,1,2,2,3), Fairly (1,3,4,5,6,8), or Wicked Hard (Both dice added together)

Pass your ball through each wicket in the correct order and direction and hit the ending post to gain the "Poison" Power Boost. Afterwards, pass your ball through the wickets in the reverse order and direction and hit the start post to win the game. Or simply knock enough players down to win.

Paper Football is a Print and Play dexterity game, where you and an opponent use your fingers to “kick” a paper ball around, over, or through obstacles, and into the other teams goal. You make your own custom obstacles using pieces cut from the components pages, and use two fingers to run around the field and kick the ball.

boardgame: BANG! High Noon/A Fistful of Cards
description:
BANG! High Noon/A Fistful of Cards combines two small expansions in one box.

BANG! High Noon consists of thirteen cards, with twelve being shuffled and the "High Noon" card placed on the bottom of the deck. At the start of each of his turns, the Sheriff reveals a new card which every player must obey for the entire turn. For "Hangover", all characters lose their special abilities, while with "Blessing" the suit of every card becomes Hearts, meaning that Barrels always work and Dynamite never explodes. Even if you've been killed and are out of the game, you can step back into play during "Ghost Town" and try to win.

BANG! A Fistful of Cards functions similarly to High Noon with fifteen cards, fourteen of which are shuffled at random into a deck with the final card being "A Fistful of Cards", which has players facing a number of Bang!s each turn equal to the number of cards in hand.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Greatest and Worst 'Most likely to...' game you will ever play
 The Rules:
1. Flip over a 'Most likely to...' card
2. On the count of 3, point to the person who's most likely to fit that description
3. The player chosen then flips over an ACTION card and has to follow the instructions
4. Once completed, that player flips over the next 'Most likely to...' card

Card Types:
There are 2 main types of cards: 'Most likely to...' cards and ACTION cards. Within the 'Most likely to...' category, there are 3 types of cards:
• ALL: All players can be chosen in this round
• GUYS: Only guys can be chosen in this round
• GIRLS: Only girls can be chosen in this round
Action Cards:
• ACTION: Player chosen has to follow the instructions

“According to Norse myth, Fenrir the wolf was the third son of Loki and the giantess Angrboda. It was prophesied that Fenrir would one day devour Odin ushering in Ragnarok. Tyr the god of war took it upon himself to feed Fenrir while he was still a pup, but as time went on Fenrir grew larger as did the gods' fear of him. The gods then decided to bind the wolf with a fetter they called Leyding. The wolf easily broke free of his bonds. The gods then used another fetter called Dromi and once again to no avail. Receiving word of this Odin called upon the dwarves of Svartalfheim to fashion a fetter made up of six magic ingredients, they called this ribbon-like material Gleipnir. Once bound with Gleipnir, Fenrir was unable to break free…”

Object of the game
The players “Viking gods” win if they manage to collect 3 of the 4 sets of Fetter tiles; otherwise the players lose if Fenrir reaches Odin first.

Components
1 Fenrir wolf figure

4 chips in four colors, 1-red, 1-blue, 1-green and 1-yellow

4 Viking god figures in four colors, 1-red, 1-blue, 1-green and 1-yellow

25 square tiles comprised of 1 Odin tile and 24 Fetter tiles in four colors, 6-red, 6-blue, 6-green and 6-yellow

1 draw bag

Setup
Pick a color you’d like to play as and place a chip in that color in front of yourself.

The chips color also corresponds to the color of the Viking god that you will move in the game.

The Odin tile is the start tile place this tile face up onto the table.

Then place all 24 fetter tiles into the draw bag and shuffle them; draw one tile at a time from the bag and form a path of face up adjacent tiles that lead away from the start tile.

Next place the four gods into the draw bag, draw a god from the bag placing one on every 5th tile along the path.

Lastly place the wolf figure onto the last tile of the path; the wolf figure should face the path looking towards the Odin tile.

Now you’re ready to play!

How to play
The youngest player starts.

A player on his turn must, following the movement rules…

• Move his piece either forwards or backwards along the path and ending the move on an empty tile not of his color or
• Move forwards or backwards along the path and collect a tile in his color.

After a player’s turn, he calls out who’s turn is next by player color only, although this is a co-operative game players are not to help each other by verbally coordinating there moves.

Tile collection
A player on his turn collects a tile in their color by landing on it with their piece. Tiles are not removed from the path but instead are flipped over and the god that last moved is placed on top.

The game ends when any three of the four sets of color tiles have been collected or when Fenrir reaches the Odin tile.

Movement
The gods can move in three possible ways:

• Walking
• Long jump
• Leapfrog

Walking

To walk, simply move your god to an empty adjacent tile.

Long jump

A god may long jump over another god or even the wolf.

• When performing a long jump you must land an equal distance after the jumped piece as at the start of the jump, so for example, if the piece doing the jumping is four spaces away from the piece you're jumping over, you must land four spaces away from the piece being jumped.

• You can only jump one god at the start of a long jump.

• Multiple long jumps in a single direction are allowed per turn.

Leapfrog

A god may leapfrog over another adjacent god or the wolf ending the jump on an empty tile immediately beyond the jumped piece.

Multiple leapfrog jumps in a single direction are allowed per turn.

Restrictions
No two gods may occupy the same tile nor may a god move onto a tile that the wolf is on.

The gods are also forbidden to leave the path.

Wolf movement

The wolf moves either two tiles forwards or one tile back along the path, but its movements are dictated by what the player’s actions are after a turn.

• If a player’s piece moves onto a tile that’s not in his color, the wolf moves two tiles forward.
• If a player’s piece moves onto a tile that’s in the players color, the wolf moves one tile back.

When Fenrir moves onto a tile occupied by a god the wolf takes his place and the god is moved to the start tile.

Adult board and beer drinking game.

From the rules: All players start at Home. Each player throws the dice, moves their piece, and follows the instructions in the box where they land. If you take a beer card, you must immediately carry out the task given on it, after which you place the card on the bottom of the deck. Each finger of beer that you drink (approx. 2cm) counts as one point. A Killer counts as five points if you empty the glass in one go, or three points if you take your time. If you ail a task, it will cost you five points. Leaving the table without the permission if the other players or beyond the scope of a task costs you ten points. The winner is the player with the highest score.

Box contents: playing board, dice, 4 beer pieces (beer caps), and 32 beer cards.

In this two player card drafting game, both players compete to get the most points from their single row of animals. However, each animal has various effects that can cause you to rearrange your row for more points or to interfere with your opponent, among other things. When both players have 6 cards in a row in front of them, all game ending effects from animals are applied and the player with the most points wins.

From the back of the box:

...And there they go! In the Crash Motor Melee, contestants maneuver around the course while avoiding the walls, other combatants, or their exhaust trails (which consist of potent chemicals that once burned will disable engines of vehicles which take in the fumes through their air intake system.) At the end of each round the smoke clears, vehicles are repaired, and the survivors who avoided the hazards successfully are awarded points. The first driver to reach enough points will be crowned Champion of the Crash Melee!

User summary:

Motor Crash Melee is played on a square grid. Each player controls a car on the grid. At the start of each round, each player selects a new direction for their car (ahead, left or right). Then, they draw a speed card that determines how many squares they move. Each car that crashes into the wall, another car or an exhaust trail is wrecked.

Mars 04:45 
In Mars 4:45 each player is a Mission Director, racing to establish the first colony on the Red Planet. Mission Directors compete to construct all the modules necessary to start a Mars settlement, sourcing as many parts internally as possible to maximize profitability.

During game play, players will use their deck and tableau set up to add cards to the middle of the table. Players can start a new module or add to an existing module. All modules in the middle of the table are public and can be started or added to by anyone at anytime. Players are playing simultaneously so don't wait to play your cards or another player will beat you to it! When a unit is completed, it is added to a player's colony, waiting to be scored at the end of the round...that is.....if you were not sabotaged!

Mars 04:45 can be played by as many people as there are unique decks, 4-6 players works best. Mars 04:45 has a play time of 15-20 minutes. First player to 150 points wins the game and play can be adjusted depending on how many points the group sets as the goal.

Parasprite Panic! is a fan-made cooperative board game based upon Hasbro's My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic franchise. The game, which draws upon events from the television series (particularly the  Season 1 episode, "Swarm of the Century"), can support from 1  to 6 players and has an approximate running time of 45 - 60 minutes. Players assume the roles of the series' primary ponies (the 'Mane 6'), who must, through the use of an action point allowance system, stem the spreading tide of devastation by abating the swelling swarm of cute but calamitous parasprites while simultaneously attempting to rescue the injured citizens of Ponyville and preventing the utter destruction of their town.

boardgame: Spartacus: A Game of Blood & Treachery – House Calavius
description: The mini-expansion Spartacus: A Game of Blood & Treachery – House Calavius consists of a new single house card for use with Spartacus. Initially available only to winners of Spartacus tournaments at Gen Con 2014, House Calavius is also included in Spartacus: The Shadow of Death.

Hellfire & Stone is a supplement for Mantic's fantasy mass combat game, Kings of War (KoW).  In addition to Kings of War scenarios and background, it also provides a scenario for Dwarf Kings Hold, a dungeon exploration/fighting game of Mantic's set in the same fantasy world.

From the publisher:  Hellfire & Stone is a narrative campaign for Kings of War, featuring an epic conflict between Dwarfs and Abyssal Dwarfs. As well as six scenarios that link up the story of the campaign, you will find an optional Dwarf King’s Hold scenario plus full rules for three new models: Arhak Soulbinder, Berserker Lord Sveri Egilax and the mighty Dwarf Bombard, as well as painting guides for some of the units involved in the war.

I Know New York challenges your knowledge of the Empire State! Collect category markers by answering multiple choice questions as you 'round the clover-leaf board. If you can answer the brainiac-level "I Know It All" questions, you have your pick of markers. Steal an opponent's marker on "Chip Raid" and win when you've gathered all the markers! With 785 unique questions from all regions of New York, this game will allow you to be a part of it. Now go out and spread the news, "I Know New York" is THE new trivia game for everyone who loves New York. Questions cover a broad range of fun and challenging topics, including: Geography, People, History, Sports, Arts, Natural History, & Pop Culture.


    Using logic and strategic thinking, "I Know New York" players are rewarded with markers by correctly answering questions from the game cards, which are presented in multiple-choice format.
    As players progress through the game board and cross different topics, they find themselves journeying through the rich and diverse history of New York State.
    A "Know It All" category for brainiacs!
    Multiple-choice questions for the timid.
    Even a "Raider" option for the ruthlessly competitive!!!

Kampion is a football card game featuring beautiful illustrations from artists across the globe.

It has 4 game modes, 2 top-trumps based and 2 originals, simulating football matches.

Game description from the publisher:

Shadowrun: Crossfire – Character Expansion Pack 1 has character cards with new art, a starting deck for each character that includes one new piece of art per deck, and a full set of upgrade stickers. With this pack, players can branch out, creating whatever shadowrunner concept comes to mind, starting them on the path to being a legend. Or they can bring new players into the game, sending them on the path to designing their own character and discovering the story the mean streets are waiting to tell.

Replace a Skilled Worker set with this one during game setup.

Brewmaster
Skilled Worker
Work Units: 1
Cost: 4 Gold
Discard: Lose a Tourist from your Resort. Gain a Lazy Peon to your discard pile.
Quantity: 4 Cards
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Into Battle - WWII Tactical is a set of rules for WWII tactical combat using hexes and specifically designed to play within a small area.

The rules are presented for use with miniatures on hexed terrain and can be played on a grid that is only 8 hexes wide by 6 hexes deep.

‘Into Battle’ is a free download that includes six scenarios for the east front in 1943 / 44. Additional expansion modules will rely on this core set.

boardgame: Depths of Courage: The H.L. Hunley's Attack, February 17, 1864
description:
The H.L. Hunley's Attack, February 17, 1864.  LPS / ATO minigame from 2014. Released as a sheet of paper (half board, half rules) and counters.

A solo game where the player is the captain of the Hunley, the Confederate Submarine that saw action off Charleston, South Carolina, in 1864. The goal of the game is to leave port, sink a blockading Union ship, and then return to port, without being sunk yourself. Uses a die roll to make checks (modified by the result of a card flip and the state of various elements on the board) that determine the status of the journey and attack.
family: Series: Depths of Courage (High Flying Dice)

boardgame: Great War at Sea: High Seas Fleet
description:
Description from the publisher:

During the brief period between 1906 and 1914, so the story goes, Britain and Germany engaged in a furious naval arms race sparked by the launch of the Royal Navy’s revolutionary battleship Dreadnought. This rivalry, egged on in Germany by Grand Admiral Alfred von Tirpitz, then helped ignite the First World War.

As usual, reality is a little more complicated than popular history. Tirpitz’s enemy lay not across the North Sea but rather in the War Ministry offices just on the other side of the Brandenburger Tor. Army and Navy battled furiously for funding, inventing, respectively, the (possibly mythical) Schlieffen Plan and the Risk Theory (less mythical, but just as detached from reality) to justify ever-higher levels of spending. In the event, neither got what they wanted: the Navy did not build a new class of dreadnoughts every fiscal year, nor did the Army get to muster two dozen new divisions to “reinforce the right.”

But what if Tirpitz had won his real battle? Germany had the financial and industrial resources to build a fleet to challenge the British. What might this fleet have looked like, and how would it have stood up to the Grand Fleet in battle?

High Seas Fleet is a supplement for the Great War at Sea series that studies this question with background essays, over two dozen new scenarios, and 70 new scorchless, sootless laser-cut playing pieces. It is not playable by itself; you’ll need our Jutland game (and only our Jutland game) to play the scenarios. You can, of course, just read the essays and fondle the pieces without owning Jutland. We won’t tell.

New pieces include German battleships designed but never built: the 1904 semi-dreadnought, the 1905 dreadnought, the 1912 dreadnought with 13.8-inch main guns, and the repeat Baden class super-dreadnoughts. There are additional cruisers and battleships to fill out the classes of the German program, and the full Blücher class of six armored cruisers.

This is a powerful fleet that can stand toe-to-toe with the British Grand Fleet – the fleet the propagandists boasted of but Tirpitz feared to actually build. Now you can lead it into battle.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

boardgame: Iron Curtain: Red and White – A Panzer Grenadier Scenario Book
description:
From the Avalanche Press website:

Shortly after the Allies jointly vanquished Nazi terror, the former partners settled into two mutually opposing camps and began a four-decade-long Cold War. At the heart of that protracted struggle lay the fate of the central and eastern European countries "liberated" by the Soviet Union. One of those unfortunate countries, Poland, had been cynically divided between the Soviet Union and Nazi Germany via the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact, as the opener for World War II. Despite the valor and sacrifice of over 200,000 Poles fighting for the Allies, their interests were betrayed at the Yalta and Potsdam conference, and as a consequence, the Soviet Union ruled Poland through a puppet Socialist government for the next 45 years.

Unlike most other Panzer Grenadier titles, Red & White explores one of the "what ifs" of history; in this case, what if the Poles had been able to return home and set up their own government in the postwar era? Looking at the turbulent history of uprisings in the territories under Soviet rule, perhaps a conflict would have emerged pitting a rearmed Poland, supported again by America and the rest of NATO, against the Red Army. We offer you some more historical background, and 40 scenarios to see if Poland can keep its sovereignty against the Soviet Union.

Red & White includes 77 mounted and die-cut Polish playing pieces. The 40 scenarios require the use of the pieces and boards from our Cassino, Elsenborn Ridge, Hammer & Sickle, Patton's Nightmare and Road to Berlin games.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The game involved players traveling from the centre outwards on a circular board to escape the mysterious "Laken" while trying to get to a mysterious crystal to stop it.
The players moved pieces shaped like strange creatures to escape the Laken. Cards and dice were involved.

Starship: Panic is a Print & Play 1-7 player, co-operative game about a team of Space Explorers attempting to survive disaster. To survive, the team must work together and keep the pile of metal and bolts known as their Starship intact. The players will have to deal with internal and external threats by activating various stations aboard the ship.

The game features a tile-based ship and multiple player characters creating a large amount of variation.

The players win by completing a set number of Red Level Events which can change depending on the desired challenge and length.

Starship: Panic takes place over a series of rounds. Each round consists of each player taking a turn.
A player's turn involves: taking actions, activating event cards, and drawing new event cards.

Each player is permitted 2 actions: Move, Station Action, Combat, Repair, or Trade. Then any event cards the player possesses, is activated. Finally, a new event card is drawn.

The Starship: Panic PnP files includes 2 pages of rules and 17 pages of printable pieces included tiles, cards, tokens and player sheets.

Additional Components needed: 6 blank white dice (16mm), 7 blank black dice (16mm), 1 d8, 2 x 1cm wooden cubes

Optional Components: A lot of red cubes to mark damage, At least 12 black cubes to represent Invaders, 7 Player Pawns- one in each color.

This supplement includes additional scenarios and rules for the Skirmish Sangin miniatures game. The book includes four new orders of battle for Canadians, Germans, Afghan National Police, and the 611 Militia. There are seven new scenarios, campaign & character experience rules, a game play primer, and tactics articles from ex-military personnel.

This is a promotional game from a line of similar ones, each named and themed for a Swedish town, and self-described as a cross between Monopoly and Trivial Pursuit. Players roll and move around the board. At specific points they have the opportunity to acquire properties that allow them to collect rent from other players, or to collect dividends on owned properties; answering trivia questions may be necessary to take these actions or increase their effectiveness. There are also event cards that may require players to answer questions to determine what happens, event spaces that require players to play a game of chance for property ownership, and more. The game may be played until one player goes bankrupt, until all players but one go bankrupt, or for a predetermined amount of time.

300 fets de llengua ("300 language events") is a word/linguistics game on the history of Catalan language. It consists in 300 round cards, each one containing a brief note on a historical event related to the Catalan language and a challenge for the players. There are 10 different kinds of challenges: to create anagrams, to find words, to define words, to identify acronyms, to guess word meanings, etc.

In a game, 30 cards are selected at random: these will be the challenges to the players; and each player begins with 300 points. The game is played in turns. In a player's turn, he/she draws one of the 30 cards and reads the brief historical note and the kind of challenge. Then all players bet points to solve the challenge, before actually knowing the challenge (they just know its kind). After deducting the points bet of each player, the active player reads the challenge and the players try to solve it. All players having solved the challenge receive double the points they bet, while the higher better receives three times the points bet; the players not having solved the challenge receive nothing. After the 30 challenges have been played, the player with most points is the winner.
publishers: Departament de Cultura. Generalitat de Catalunya

description:
RULES
2-4 Players. Age +6 years

DESCRIPTION
At first sight, memm appears to be a simple game as it is composed by only 36 geometric cubes of different colours. However, memm goes far beyond this apparent simplicity. memm is a dynamic, visually entertaining and aesthetically attractive  game, but above all, memm  is game full of possibilities and lots of game play. These characteristics makes memm the ideal cognitive development game to play with the whole family.

PREPARATION
The 36 coloured cubes are placed on the table, mixed and shared equally amongst the participants. Then a cube with all the six colours is rolled. The player having the cube with the resulting colour on all its 6 sides will start the game by placing this cube in the centre of the table.

GAMEPLAY
Clockwise, the following players will place one cube so that joining sides match colours.  A cube can be placed on top of the starting cube. However, this move will be subject to a special rule that will be discussed in the SPECIAL RULE section.

ABILITY TO REPEAT TURN
A player gains the ability to repeat turn if he joins a cube by two sides. Please note this is only physically possible as from the fourth turn.
A player gains the ability to repeat turn and get rid of one of his cubes by handing it to a player of his choice if he joins a cube by three sides.
A player gains the ability to repeat turn and get rid of two of his cubes by handing them to a player of his choice if he joins a cube by four sides.

If a player joins a cube by five sides, gains the ability to repeat turn and get rid of three of his cubes by handing them to a player of his choice.

SPECIAL RULE
No more than three cubes will be placed in the same direction in a row of turns. This rule prevents the formation of continuous lines of cubes and is not to be confused with a limitation of the central structure being developed during the game. When a player places a cube on top of another, the player will have, on his next turn, the possibility of you can also put it in one of the free sides of any of the cubes that are in the same vertical.

OBJECTIVE
The first player who gets rid of all his cubes wins the game. However, unless this player is the last to play in the round, the remaining players will be placing their cubes until the full round of turns is completed. At that point, the player left with more cubes loses. In a tie in number of cubes , you will lose more different colours have. If they have the same number of colours , the result will be a tie.

VARIATIONS OF THE GAME
This is the basic and general form of the game. Nevertheless, a number of variants were also explored to create a series of dimensional figures that would condition the placement of cubes. Some examples are detailed in the photo section. 
A rotating circular table can be added to the game to facilitate players with the different angles and spatial perspective of the figure being created and gain fluency and aesthetics in the game.
Specification (Components, forms, materials, sizes):
Components : game box , instructions , manual with different figures to make and 36 cubes with the following distribution of coloured sides :
6 cubes with all sides the same colour.
6 cubes with 5 sides of the same colour and with a different side.
6 cubes with 4 sides of the same colour and with 2 different sides.
6 cubes with 3 sides of the same colour and with 3 different sides.
6 cubes with 2 sides of the same colour and with 4 different sides.
6 cubes with all sides of different colour.

In principle, the chosen colours are red , yellow , orange , violet, blue and green.

Each cube will have on each side a roman number that determines the number of sides with that colour. For example, a "V" on the a red face of a cube means that there are 5 red sides on that cube.



1 Descripción de memm: Jugadores: de 2 a 4. Edad: +6 años.
memm aparenta ser un juego sencillo por tratarse sólo de 36 cubos geométricos de diferentes colores, pero va más allá de esa aparente sencillez. memm es un juego con multitud de posibilidades, dinámico, muy estético (al quedar figuras de colores increíbles), entretenido y, sobre todo, con muchísima jugabilidad.

2 Instrucciones
Se colocan los 36 cubos de colores en la mesa, se remueven y se reparten equitativamente entre los jugadores participantes. Se lanza un cubo con un color diferente en cada cara y empieza el que tenga el cubo con las 6 caras del color que haya salido. Se comienza la partida situando el cubo en el centro de la mesa. El siguiente jugador (los turnos se realizan en orden del sentido de las agujas del reloj) tendrá que colocar una de las caras de los cubos que le ha tocado en una de las caras libres del último cubo puesto en la mesa. Esta unión de caras sólo se podrá realizar si coinciden en su color. Los jugadores podrán situar el cubo en la cara superior, pero esta acción estará sujeta a una regla especial de la sección 3. Se procederá de la misma manera en turnos siguientes con las siguientes condiciones:
 3 Posibilidad de repetición de turno

Cubo toca una sola cara con último cubo puesto: no coloca ningún cubo más y pasa a siguiente jugador según turno.

Cubo en la mesa toca dos caras con último cubo puesto: posibilidad de colocar otro cubo de nuevo.

Cubo en la mesa toca tres caras con último cubo puesto: posibilidad de colocar otro cubo de nuevo y puede deshacerse de un cubo suyo, entregándoselo al jugador que crea conveniente.

Cubo en la mesa toca cuatro caras con último cubo puesto: posibilidad de colocar otro cubo de nuevo y puede deshacerse de dos cubos suyos, entregándoselos al jugador o jugadores que crea conveniente.

Cubo en la mesa toca cinco caras con último cubo puesto: posibilidad de colocar otro cubo de nuevo y puede deshacerse de tres cubos suyos, entregándoselos al jugador o jugadores  que crea conveniente.

4 Reglas especiales
No se podrá colocar más de tres cubos en turnos seguidos en la misma dirección. Esta regla evita la formación de una línea interminable de cubos y no debe ser confundida con una limitación de las dimensiones de la estructura a formar durante el juego.

Cuando un jugador coloca un cubo sobre otro cubo en cualquier nivel de la estructura, el mismo jugador (en caso de unión de 2 o más caras) o el siguiente jugador (en caso de unión de una sola cara) deberá colocar su cubo de tal manera que haga contacto con la vertical sobre la que descansa ese cubo, incluyendo éste.

5 Objetivo final
Un jugador ganará la partida cuando sea el primero en deshacerse de todos sus cubos, aunque deberá esperar a que el último jugador de esa ronda haya completado su turno. Es decir, el resto de jugadores  podrán colocar sus cubos e incluso entregar sus cubos a cualquier contrincante hasta la finalización esa ronda.. En ese momento, perderá el que se haya quedado con más cubos. Si hay empate en número de cubos, perderá el que más colores diferentes posea. Si tienen el mismo número de colores, habrá empate en dicho jugadores.

Esta es la modalidad básica y general del juego. Pero se pensó en las miles de posibilidades de unas piezas tan versátiles. Por lo que se empezó a crear una serie de figuras ficticias para delimitar la colocación de los cubos. Se detallará algún ejemplo en la sección de fotos. También se puede incorporar al juego una 
tabla circular giratoria para así facilitar la visión espacial de la figura desde cualquier perspectiva y de paso agilizar el juego.

Memoria descriptiva (Componentes, formas, materiales, medidas):

6 Componentes
Caja de juego, instrucciones, manual con las diferentes figuras a realizar y 36 cubos con la siguiente distribución de caras de colores:

6 cubos con todas las caras de un mismo color.
6 cubos con 5 caras de un mismo color y con una cara de color diferente.
6 cubos con 4 caras de un mismo color y con 2 caras de color diferente.
6 cubos con 3 caras de un mismo color y con 3 caras de color diferente.
6 cubos con 2 caras de un mismo color y con 4 caras de color diferente.
6 cubos con todas sus caras de color diferente.
En principio, los colores elegidos son el rojo, amarillo, naranja, violeta, azul y  verde.

Cada cubo tendrá en cada cara un número en romano que determina el número de caras que tiene ese color en el cubo. Por ejemplo, si en una cara roja de un cubo se encuentra una “V”, significará que en ese cubo hay 5 caras rojas.

A game of Zoophoria takes place over seven rounds, representing one week.  Players start with four animals, three employees and two secret targets.  Each day, a new selection of animals, employees, buildings, food supplies, targets and more will be available.  Players  will draft three items from the 19 available as new additions to their habitat. Strategy and planning must be used to create a habitat that can manage the daily food, virus and trash production.  If the growing collection of animals is fed and remain healthy, and the habitat is kept clean, visitors will come to enjoy the zoo.

Over 90 unique animals from around the world available to build your habitat. Earn extra visitors by creating themed exhibits containing mammals, reptiles, birds, endangered species and more.

Over 50 unique employees and buildings are available to provide assistance.  Upgrade or add a new Keeper to produce more food.  Install trash cans and recycling bins to keep the zoo clean.  Enlist Veterinarians to ensure animals don’t get sick.  Add restaurants, photo spots, souvenir stands or other guest services to attract even more visitors.

The player who attracts the most visitors to the zoo will win the challenge and earn the title, Director of Zoophoria.

boardgame: Zombicide: Box of Zombies – Set #8: Murder of Crowz
description:
It was inevitable that crows would get infected, scavenging all those zombie bodies. It was also inevitable that it would be very bad news. Crowz may be easy to kill, but these flying vermin can move awfully fast and, worst of all, they can ignore any obstacles, either flying over them or squeezing through broken windows, holes in the roof or gaps on crumbling walls!

This box of Zombies implements infected Crows into the Zombicide games. The box contains 15 miniatures and the spawn cards to play them.

Note: This item, although functioning in a similar way to Zombicide: Black Plague – Murder of Crowz, does not expand Zombicide: Black Plague, and Zombicide: Black Plague – Murder of Crowz does not expand Zombicide, because of the card backs. This entry allows for the correct items to be linked as expansions to the correct entries.

Game covers two battle of the american civil war. First one, fought on July 21, 1861 was also first major battle of this war as well. Second,  fought August 28–30, 1862, was the culmination of an offensive campaign waged by Confederate Gen. Robert E. Lee's Army of Northern Virginia against Union Maj. Gen. John Pope's Army of Virginia.

Game was published in the 35 issue of the "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine. It uses "Napoleon" game system.

Bumper Golf is a deluxe version of the carom golf game, you might play at a Parks and Recreation building. The game board depicts an entire 18-hole golf course. Players shoot their 'balls' (wooden disks) from the numbered tee spots and aim for the equivalent numbered 'hole' (cup depression.)

There are two ways to play. In the first version, each player plays each hole one at a time keeping track of his shots. At the end of 18 holes the player with the fewest strokes wins. For a more interactive game, players alternate taking shots, thus allowing chances to block off holes or knock other players balls out of position. In this version the first player to complete the course is the winner.

This original game by World Wide Games would later be republished as Bumper Golf after an acquisition by S&S Worldwide.

Double Check is a game of Checkers adapted for four players in teams of two.  It is played on a 14 x 14 square grid with nine squares of each corner removed, resulting in a cross-shaped checkered board.  The game is played on the dark squares only, and each wing of the board has twelve dark squares to host twelve pieces in their starting positions.  There are pieces of four colors: black, white, red, and green.  Each player chooses a partner and their own color, and sets up their pieces in starting positions opposite the board from each other.  Play proceeds clockwise around the board.

Players move their pieces and capture other players the same as in Checkers, moving only towards the opposite side of the board until their pieces can be crowned in their partner's home row, and moving in any direction after.  Only opponent's pieces may be jumped or captured.  Pieces cannot be crowned in opponent's home rows.  Movements are not otherwise restricted on any part of the board.  The team who eliminates their opponents' pieces, or prevents them from moving, wins the game.

There is one variant provided for in the game's rules, in which players are allowed to jump their partner's pieces without capturing them.

This wargame tells a valorous page of the Russian Revolution: the revolt of the sailors and workers of the fleet base of Kronstadt against the rude attitude and program of the Bolshevik Soviet. Even if the rebellion was put by an end by violence, Lenin adapted some of the requests with the NEP at the X Congress.

ATTENTION: it is a diceless game!

Gravellona 1944 è la simulazione della battaglia di Gravellona Toce, svoltasi tra i partigiani della Repubblica dell'Ossola e i reparti repubblichini nel Settembre 1944.

Una simulazione tattica con diversi spunti interessanti!

Regole in italiano, pedine di gioco e tabelle!!

The game represents the battle between Italian partisans and republican fascists in September 1944. It is a tactical simulation with interesting ideas; units can do Actions by using Action Points, which are gained at the end of each turn and which must be planned carefully by the player. 
Combat units are usually at platoon level or single veichle; each unit has a different capacity of fire, depending from the type of fire (Infantry, Tanks or Artillery) and the type of target (Infantry, Tanks and Veichles, Concrete targets). Morale tests and Hit results can apply on Fire, as well as during Melèe.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In the year 2220, criminal gangs and organizations have moved from the streets and back alleys of the physical world to the fiber optics of cyberspace. The two most infamous hacker crews, the ruthless Black_Skulls and the guileful White_Wolves are on the front lines of this new digital war; devastating security systems and vying for notoriety. When warfare is virtual, reputation is everything.

Overview
In #VECTOR, two players take control of rival hacker crews in a constant struggle to rule the grid. As the leader of a crew, you will increase your reputation and damage your opponent’s in three key ways:
1) Controlling the grid by converting it to your crew’s color
2) Using remote exploits to damage your opponent’s system
3) Hacking into your opponent’s mainframe and stealing valuable data

Objective
There are a limited amount of REP tokens in the general supply. The player with the most REP when the token supply runs out wins!

Like all members of the 18xx family, this game is played in alternating stock rounds, where players buy and sell shares in multiple corporations, and sets of operating rounds, where the corporations operate on the map and possibly pay dividends to their shareholders. In this game, there's also a development round before the operating rounds where infrastructure is built on the map. At the end of the game the player with the greatest net worth in stock value and cash holdings wins.

This is 18xx on a large scale - 40 railroad companies, two oversize map sheets, 825 tiles, 480 cards (stock shares, trains, private corporations), and a play time to match.

This game takes the "D" variant of 18C2C: Manifest Destiny and reimplements it on an even larger map (the US, Canada, and Mexico) with a variety of rule changes to provide high replay value and balance, most notably player-driven upgrade paths for cities (so that cities will grow to different values in different games) providing new sources of tension in the stock and development rounds.

Game features taken from 18C2C: Manifest Destiny include a reservation auction for private companies (similar to 1830), partial capitalization, company destinations (somewhat similar to the destinations in 1870: Railroading Across the Trans Mississippi from 1870 but also player driven), corporate share redemption, and mergers. This game has 4 different kinds of tokens and 5 different classes of railroads. Steam trains run routes as in most 18XX games, whereas Diesel trains run abstract routes counting only cities where you have stations and without the need to trace routes - simplifying route building and calculation in the later stages of the game.

Intended publication is set for 2030 as a full commercial production similar to 18OE.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Arkhangelsk you play a team of soldiers sent to Arkhangelsk to find out why it is no longer responding to communications.  At the beginning of the game you just arrived in the camp. You need to get the power on, radio working, and call for evacuation.

On each turn you will draw cards from your deck to gain time to take actions like moving, firing, repairing and searching. You can also use the time to refine your tactics by purchasing cards. The cards in your hand  give you both time to take actions and better or more efficient actions.

As the game progresses your deck will become stronger, but the storm will increase in intensity, the sun will set, and stronger creatures will venture forth from the surrounding Siberian wastes.

You will need to balance all your actions carefully to survive the elements, repair the prison, kill the winter creatures and finally make a dash for the airstrip when the plane lands!

The Peides Curse: Earth is a strategic and tactical game in which players take on the role of heroes who are embarking on an audacious and dangerous rescue mission. They face the uncertainty of time travel that bring them to the future year of 2223 where they will join the Spirit and fight a tough battle to stop the power of Evil. In order to accomplish the objective of their mission, the players shall participate in the race of their lives to find 9 secret codes that will stop Evil from wielding its power and destroying Earth.

Each player is required to put into motion his or her most strategic plan and creative cunningness to plot successfully and beat the other players to be the first to find all the secret codes. The game uses two different decks of power card play / power travel to help the players navigate to their designated mission destination. In this phase, players must cleverly maneuver their moves to put into motion their plans and strategy. The game play takes on an infusion of race and prior planning. High levels of strategy deployment or plotting, combined with tactical maneuvers, is required.

In the quest to ﬁnd the secret codes, the players are required to follow the clues which will lead them to where they are hidden. They then need to decipher the code with the decoder dices. Successful attempts shall be rewarded with energy points and battle ﬂags. They are both critical game changing components that will propel the players to lead in the game.

The last phase of the race shall see them being pitted in the battle between the Spirit and Evil. Here, the players will be able to deploy actions or movements that will keep them safe. The battle card may also be a game killer if it is played correctly. The players need to be extra cautious at this stage because they may well fall prey to 3 ‘surprise’ cards in the stack. This can cause them to deplete their Vourton power and lose a turn when they meet face to face with Evil. However, all is not lost. There are saviour cards that will restore the players’ power immediately without losing a turn.

Darkfast Dungeons is a print and play tabletop game designed to provide a robust dungeon crawl experience in a couple of hours. A typical dungeon crawl will last 2-3 hours and provide a unique adventure with each game played.

There are many ways to play Darkfast Dungeons with rules and scenarios for co-operative games, competitive games and solo play.

The Darkfast Dungeons basic set includes 6 heroes, 12 monsters, a full set of game cards, rulebook and 26 customizable 2d tiles.
In this children's dexterity game, players use hooks on strings to move plastic fish vertically onto a tree-like structure. The player who completes the task first is the winner.

Munchkin Holidazed, Steve Jackson Games' holiday-themed Munchkin booster pack for 2014, contains 15 cards (eleven new cards and four reprinted cards that previously appeared only in the Munchkin Holiday Surprise boxed set), a peppermint-swirled level counter, and six Munchkin dice.

Europe, c. 450 A.D. Barbarians are running amok, raiding villages for women (rapine), weapons (pillage) and gold (plunder). Will your tribe reign supreme in this savage contest for survival?

Game play alternates between the players. On your turn, you have a choice of three actions:


    Rapine, pillage or plunder (RPP)
    Move
    Discard a card


The cards serve the three above purposes. Most of the time, players will move to villages and perform RPP. Sometimes, however, when a barbarian tribe gets a little cocky, a battle will occur between the players. These are settled smoothly using the symbols on the cards in hand, and rest assured--tribute will be paid by the loser!

I designed Rapine, Pillage & Plunder because I feel there's a dearth of fun, light, yet not-too-simple two-player games. I believe that this game helps fill that void. (Publishers take note). It's a simple game aimed at casual gamers but has enough in the way of tactics and choices to appeal to more seasoned gamers, as well.

boardgame: Krigs-spelet, grundadt på Bülows nyare krigs-systems esprit
description: Game about military operations played on a grid with terrain drawn on. Units are brigades formed into three different armies for each player, plus Generals that are needed to activate units. There are also forts and magazines on the map. Tracing of supplies and communication are important to winning the game. The goal is to capture the opponent's main fort. Combat is deterministic, decided by having more units attacking or defending, modified by terrain, forts, or having elite units in the battle.

"Able Archer" is wargaming systém, which will put players into role of company or battalion commanders durning fictional conflict in 1983. You will assume command of NATO or WARPAC ground forces. You will lead tanks,infantry but even artillery or airforces. You will be fighting in conflict that was greatest fear of every politician and commander durning Cold war. Can you defeat Soviets before they could reach the Rhine? Or will you lead final push towards the Paris as Soviet commander?
Able Archer unlike many games situated in Cold war gone hot scenário, is played in 1:1 scale. That means 1 vehicle represent one vehicle, base of infantry represents team and so on.
In smaller games you will lead companies, while in larger games you can command battalions or even regiment worth of troops.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Air Fleet: Knights of the Sky
description: Air Fleet is a game of aerial combat of the second world war. The players control a squadron of aircraft. This squadron is generally composed of two or three aircraft. The goal is to maneuver on the game table in order to destroy the greatest number of enemy aircraft. There is no limit to the number of players, however it is advised to have only one squadron per player.

Counter-Terrorist Force is a pure “player vs. player” (1 on 1) game where the main focus is on tactics and realistic combat. The game is played from both sides of the conflict. One person plays the terrorists and the other takes the role of counter-terrorist.
Not only do you execute missions, you also run your own counter terrorist corporation and try to work your way up from scratch. You will also fight occasionally as a terrorist so that your friends can
play as counter terrorist troopers too. Game play can either be single missions to improve your corporation, or otherwise in campaign mode where two campaigns are interwoven – you and your opponent take turns as terrorists - and you both have your own counter-terrorist unit to run every second game in your individual campaign.

Tonnage War Solitaire is a solitaire game simulating the great North Atlantic convoy battles of 1942-43. The player controls the anti-submarine escorts protecting a convoy of Allied merchantmen, while the German U-boats attacking the convoy are portrayed through the mechanics of the game system.

These wargame rules have been designed so that players can fight typical small battles between European Colonial armies and their opponents during the period between 1875 and 1910. They use a square gridded battlefield, which removes the need to measure both move distances and weapon ranges, thus speeding up the whole process of fighting a wargame. It also removes any doubts or possible disputes as to whether or not a unit has moved too far or if it is in range or not. They also incorporate simple combat resolution systems that likewise speed up game play. The armies needed to fight battles with these rules are small by most wargames standards. This means that players can ‘raise’ lots of small armies rather than a couple of big ones.

- Description from Publisher

Typhoon is a set of miniatures rules for modern jet combat, specifically designed as an introductory level game for those gamers who wish to use the most current aircraft, but to not get bogged down in the complexity of hi-tech jet warfare. The game’s systems allow each player to control several jets in a fast paced game that can be easily completed in a few hours.

Modern jet combat, particularly with aircraft such as the F-22, Eurofighter, and SU-35 can be extremely complex with the wide variety of radars, ECM, DDS, weapons, etc., which can bring a game to a crawl when using these planes. Typhoon attempts to incorporate the technology into the various game systems which lets the players fly and fight their jets without having to worry about whether or not you have enough chaff or flares still left in your dispensers to defeat the enemy’s next attack.

This makes Typhoon an ideal way to get new gamers into the modern jet combat era as they can focus on the basics of jet
combat instead of memorizing a two page sequence of play. The game is designed to be used without a hex mat or special stands, so this will let gamers use any miniatures that they have and however they are currently mounted for the game.

Hopefully, Typhoon will introduce gamers to the exciting world of hitech jet combat and let them use some of the most powerful aircraft in aviation history in a fun and fast moving game.

These are rules for playing a wargame with toy soldiers. It is intended for several players - say 4 or more. Players control significant leaders – the key samurai of a clan, who in turn have contingents of fighters under them. These brave samurai find themselves opposed by hordes of undead horrors – the Oyoma.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
War Games Rules for the Great War.

The Great War has never attracted the attention of war-gamers in the same way that conflicts both before and since have done. There are probably a number of reasons for this but the common perception, especially in the UK, that Great War battles were little more than exercises in mass slaughter ordered by remote and uncaring generals probably has much to do with the reluctance to game this period.
These views are now being re-appraised by a fresh generation of historians and researchers who, without playing down the horrors of the trenches, the scale of the suffering or the terrible cost of a "Big Push", are challenging many previously held ideas.

Flanders Fields is a set of rules which allows players to war game actions from Regiment (Brigade), to Divisional level actions from the war on the western front between 1914 and 1918.

The rules seek to provide an enjoyable gaming experience for all players, regardless of the final result. As such a degree of sportsmanship is necessary between players, it is perfectly acceptable to seek to slaughter the toy soldiers on the table top but in dealings with other players please maintain a degree of sportsmanship. In cases where the interpretation of a rule might be in doubt, for instance whether a unit is in or out of range let the roll of a die or toss of a coin decide.
The rules are divided into four main sections
A brief consideration of those aspects of the Great War which serve to distinguish it from previous and later conflicts.
The main body of the rules.
Generic tables of divisional organisation which may be used to pick forces for a game.
A number of appendices providing extra information and examples.

Coal, and the Kaiser is a naval warfare simulation portraying naval surface warfare in the period 1904-1918. The simulation presents aspects of this warfare through a series of scenarios.

Scenario generators provided with the game can be used to create scenarios typical for the time and place that the game covers.

The game presents these battles on a tactical, ship-to-ship level. The game is for two sides: each controlling the ships of a contending naval force. Ships are represented by 1/6000 scale miniatures, with any 4 foot by 6 foot level surface serving as a playing area. Movement and combat are conducted in accordance with these rules of play.

These rules are designed for a player to control many capital ships in a major fleet action. They are designed to allow players to refight the huge space battles of the Exterminator War.

There are a couple of other sets of Universe-based rules already in existence.

    HELLFIRE, which allows players to control a single ship in considerable detail.
    Big Burn is for small fleet actions with a degree of detail as to what damage is done.


This set of rules are another step in the abstraction process, designed to make a battle playable in an hour or less, and several battles in a campaign context practical in an evening.
The basic rules concept is a direct steal from the excellent WRG game 'Seastrike' published in, I think 1974.

Inevitably, that original system has mutated somewhat in its conversion to the Universe, and this system is an attempt to reflect the doctrine and practice of 300+ years of space combat in the Universe.

Space battles in the Universe generally take place in the orbital space around a colony world. Combat is generally not 'to the death', and fleets will usually withdraw if overmatched.

Combat is also very greatly influenced by political constraints and the consequent rules of engagement. Similarly, the loss of a capital ship is a major economic loss as it takes a year and a half to build a replacement, and it has significant credit value. (See the Macro Rules for the Humanity Will Prevail Campaign). Captains and admirals are schooled with the prevailing ethos – which is that if an enemy ship breaks off combat you let it go (after all, it might be you next time).
Much of this was challenged during the Exterminator War – where the enemy was an implacable machine intelligence – so when fighting Exterminators the loss of ships is expected. However, that specific threat has now receded.

Braiiiins is a 1-6 player game where you control one of six different zombie characters.  There are currently 4 different missions to be completed and 2 different modes (Co-op and an indirect player versus player.

Each turn is broken up into three rounds: spawn, locate, and then feed.

During the spawn round, players will all look at the spawning rules for the zombie character they have choosen.  Then will then draw a number of zombie allies from the risen deck based on those rules.
