During the locate round, players will draw living cards from the living deck. The number of living cards drawn is based on the number of players who started the game.  These are who the zombies will feed on!

During the feed round, the players then assign their zombie character and risen cards up against the living cards that are currently in play.  Attack and defense characteristics are compared to one another to see who wins the fight!  After all fighting actions are resolved, the game turn starts over again.

There are several ways to win: in player versus player mode, you want to be the zombie who has taken the least amount of damage.  In our co-op mode, you must successfully complete the choosen scenario before running out of hit points.

Our scenarios range from eliminating the entire town of Risington, to picking off specific members of the town!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Story:
  Long ago, the power of five elements was operating in the whole land. Metal manufactured tools. Wood bred creations. Water moisturized lives. Fire warmed world. And Earth created land. Five elements mutually generated and overcame each other. In this circle, all creations within heaven and earth never ended.
  In the vast land, only a few people could master all five elements. People called them “The Alchemist”.
  One day, the empire held a competition of five elements to find the best alchemist. And you are one of them.
  You have to follow the secret book, and use your magic spell and ancient artifact to collect specific element stones. Once you succeed, you will be the best alchemist in the world. But be careful, all your element stones may suddenly token by other alchemists. And your soul and body may be torn apart because of wrong element stones…



Introduction:
  In the beginning of the game, players choose their target cards. And in each turn, player moves on the five element map and uses the function card to collect element stones or to do the special function. Players have to reach the alchemy condition on his target card. Once a player reaches his target, then he wins, and the game is over. But if someone reaches the soul-broken condition on his target card, then he has to restart.So, hide you target carefully. And start to enjoy the magic power of Chinese five elements!

boardgame: Myrmidons Invasion of the Giant Mutant Ants
description:
Myrmidons pits two players or two teams of players against each other.  One player/team plays the US military forces circa 1950s.  The other player/team commands a force of giant mutant ants ala the atomic horror movie classic, Them!

The game is designed for tabletop miniatures play (15mm scale human troops are suggested such as Battlefront Games' WW2 Flames of War (FoW).  Counters are provided with the game so that new players or those who don't wish to use miniatures can dive right in.

From the Cover:

Giant mutant ants are in the city sewers and the army must save the day! Play the army commander and use your rifle squads, bazookas, flame throwers - - even mechanized units to eliminate the Ant Queen and her minions; or feast on the human intruders as you defend your colony by playing the carnivorous giant ants!  
This package contains everything you need to play except six sided die!

Here’s what you get:
•A complete set of play tested rules, using easy-to-learn game mechanics.
•A partial sample turn with accompanying illustrations.
•2-D copies of all required game pieces for first-time players.
•Extensive diagrams and illustrations for “visual learners” to get playing fast!
•SEVEN unique scenarios for hours of gaming fun; play from two to six players!
•Comb binding for ease of play.
•Compatible with Flames of War basing.

Stax is basically a Pachisi variant played by 2 players (each playing with 4 counters each of two colours which start in their respective "jails") or 4 players in partnership each playing one colour. The first player/team to get all 8 counters home wins.
 Players alway throw two dice and must always move by exact count. It requires a six to move a counter out of jail.If a player's counter finishes on an opponent's counter then the opponents counter is returned to its jail. Once a player has got all of one colour home, he then requires a six and thereafter may move his other colour or partners colour on his colour's turn turn.
 If two or more counters of the same colour land on the same square they form a stack. No opponent's counter may pass a stack. To break a stack a player must first land on the square immediately before that stack. He must then throw successive sixes equal to the number of stack counters plus one. If successful the stack counters are returned to their jail, otherwise the turn ends.
 If a multicoloured stack is formed, this does not block movement, it may not attempt to break a stack and if landed on  its counters are returned to their jails.

The Red Death is running rampant throughout the country, claiming countless lives. In an attempt to avoid this plague, Prince Prospero and a thousand of his closest friends have shut themselves inside the seven rooms of his castellated abbey. One night, the prince decides to throw a masquerade ball to keep his friends entertained. But while this ball is in full swing, a masked figure, of who had not been seen before, has started to make its way throughout the rooms of the abbey. Angered by this figure’s mask of a plague ridden corpse, the prince tried to kill the figure but was killed in the process. Now the prince’s nobles are hunting for the masked figure that at some point covered its mask with another. Now nobody knows who is who in this game of bluffing, deduction, and hand management.

In this game for 2 to 6 players, the game begins with the abbey of seven rooms being built and each player receiving one role card. The roles are kept hidden as they will be revealed during game play. Each player then selects one of the seven rooms where they will start the game. On each player's turn they will have a variety of actions that will allow them to trap, kill, or reveal other players, move throughout the abbey, or use a key to unlock a trap. They can perform these actions by using the cards that they draw. The goal of the game is to be the first collect five victory points and can do so by being a Noble alive at the end of the round or by not being trapped at the end of the round if playing as The Red Death.

Awards
2014 MicroGame Contest Best Theme
2014 MicroGame Contest People's Choice - 4th Place

Hansa Teutonica: Britannia is an expansion for Hansa Teutonica that consists of a new map of Britannia and revised two-player rules.

The special permission in Carlisle, Cardiff and London means that you are allowed to do one of your normal placement or displacement actions in Wales and Scotland. You do not get an extra action.

MALPRACTICE! is an original card game filled with sabotage and surgery. Players act as doctors in an extremely poorly run hospital; every round a new patient is admitted, and players take turn treating him. The goal of the game is to heal the patient on your turn, while sabotaging your friend's treatments until they lose and get sued. The game is for 3 to 6 people, and takes approximately an hour per game. Rated 16 and up due to drug references.

Three Kings is a three-player chess puzzle game.  Each player represents an "Alliance" of two of the three colors of chess pieces in the game (red, black and white).  On each player's turn, they may move a piece prior to adding a new piece to the board.

In Three Kings, check and checkmate are not means to an end, as the object of the game is not to render checkmate, but to have the most points.  When a King is put into check or is checkmated, the opposing color(s) involved score points.  Because each player represents two colors of pieces, his or her score is mostly the sum of points scored by those two colors.  However, each player also has a Hidden Agenda which may increase their score, and each player scores points for captures they make throughout the game.

Benny - The Children's Performer
"And now, boys and girls, let's give a big hand to Benny the Bear!"

Needing beer money, Benny answered an ad from a company specializing in children's parties.  A star was born that day.  a born thespian, Benny's repertoire included a bear, a knight, a giant, a talking zebra, and a friendly commando.  He was at a children's party dressed as Captain Jack Action, the star of a popular children's TV show, when the zombies came.  Benny's gun was a toy but like all great performers he improvised, fleeing the scene.  He wears the costume to keep people from screwing with him and his acting skills make him much in demand amongst survivor groups...for awhile.

Benny is a promotional Survivor that was available to those that pledged extra during the Season 3 Kickstarter campaign.  A Zombivor version is also included.

Oksana - The Stunt Woman
"I got two fractures, a concussion and a broken nose doing that stunt.  It was awesome!"

Leaping from her roof onto a trampoline at the age of six, Oksana began her career as a stunt woman early.  The broken arm set, and so did her resolve.  Brushing off the minor nicks and abrasions that ensued over the years, Oksana found her way to Hollywood where her daredevil exploits quickly made her a star.  No stunt was too dangerous, no risk too great, no height too high.  Like all adrenaline junkies, Oksana felt that the price of fame was well worth the occasional fractured skull or hospital stay.  The zombie outbreak has, if anything, made her even more reckless.

Oksana is a promotional Survivor that was available to those that pledged extra during the Season 3 Kickstarter campaign.  A Zombivor version is also included.

Patrick - The Hipster
"God, I'd kill for a soy bean caramel peppermint latte right now."

Settling in a rapidly gentrifying neighborhood with 1.3 cafes per block and a vibrant arts scene, Patrick set about renovating his brownstone apartment.  Wearing thrift-store shirts and stonewashed jeans, listening to the latest prog rock bands, and sipping lattes to his heart's content, Patrick lived an idyllic existence until the zombies came.  He found inner reserves of strength he didn't know he had protecting his beloved neighborhood from the dead.  It was a losing battle and soon Patrick was forced to leave, joining other survivors in their desperate struggle.

Patrick is a promotional Survivor that was available to those that pledged extra during the Season 3 Kickstarter campaign.  A Zombivor version is also included.

This book has 4 board games and a crossword with some easy games used to explain Ancient Egypt to kids. The book also has punchable hard stock counters and even a die.

The four board games are:

Before the river floods. 2 players. Two farmers compete to bring all of their harvest to the granary before the river Nile floods. They must follow a circuit and can have encounters with gods disguised as animals that can help them or harm them.

Chariot race in Thebes. 2 players. Two teams of two chariots race for victory in a circuit full of bifurcations, barriers and even a herd of goats that moves really slow.

Finding Ra's crown. 1 player. Choose your adventure game in which you read the description for each chamber inside a pyramid and must choose your next action. Many lead to death, only one leads to victory.

Senet. 2 players. The original ancient Egypt Senet game.

There's also a crossword made with Egyptian hieroglyphic instead of latin letters.

This started as a reworking of the Meka Tac - Giant Stompy Robots Edition rules into a starship combat game, along with the grafting of elements from a lifetime of pushing miniatures around a table while rolling dice.
That didn’t work as planned; the rules did the job, but not well enough to command anything more than a task force of a half-dozen ships per player. The ultimate goal was to handle the battle of fleets but still make the individual ship and how it was maneuvered matter. Some compromises were made, other things were simplified, and few items simply tossed out. The desire was to have a game that would entice new players to starship combat gaming and give an option for battles with larger numbers of ships to those who have fleets at the ready.

Fleet of Battle was developed out of Field of Battle by Brent Oman (published by Piquet Inc.).  The rules are for ancient naval warfare from Salamis to Actium. They are best for the Punic, Successor and Roman Civil Wars where the quinquireme (five) was the most common 'line of battle' ship. Fleet of Battle is intended for use with fleets of between six and 20 ships per player with an optimum of a dozen or so for an evening’s play. Larger battles can be fought without difficulty, but more than an evening should be set aside for such games.

There is no set ground scale or ship scale. The rules were devised for what felt right using 1:600 model ships. Fleets in classical times were very large, often several hundred ships strong, manned by tens of thousands.With this in mind it is suggested that historical scenarios should be fought using a ratio of one model to 10 - 20 ships.

If using 1:1200 scale models or smaller, use centimetres instead of inches.

The rules are about small unit actions of a group or so, deployed planetside for VIP guarding, raiding, hostage rescue, arrest and capture and similar missions.

The rules are intended for use with 15mm scale models, or suitable counters of similar size.
Ground scale: 1 cm = 5 metres
Time scale: 1 game turn = 2-4 minutes
Figure scale: 1 figure = 1 soldier

Take your Robot Turtles play to the next level with this limited edition Adventure Pack! Extend your young programmer’s skills with Frog Favorite Cards, designed to teach children to use and combine functions, a key coding concept. Bonus Jewel Tokens add a new programming challenge, as players learn to create increasingly complex code to collect them all. Put these new skills to work and take your Turtles on one of 10 new Adventure Quests, imaginative board setups each with a creative story line.

boardgame: Tajemnicze Domostwo: Lost Letter Promo Set
description:
Promo Set is available at a variety of events, exhibitions and competitions. The set contains a new character, new location and new subject.

Tramp.
Grotto.
Poker.

The expansion is a part of regular Polish edition of base game.

The Kickstarter Limited Exclusive Mini-Expansion is contained in the box of the Deluxe edition for Kickstarter backers, but may also be available separately in the future. From the Kickstarter page,

Kickstarter Limited Exclusive - VERY limited quantity printed. Will never enter regular distribution channels but may become available via contests, conventions, and/or the BGG store.

It introduces 11 cards not found in the Base edition:

Giant Snake (Dire Enemy)
Basilisk (Dire Enemy)
Fire Elemental (Epic Foe)
Overlord (Epic Foe)
Baal's Bait (Artifact)
Gavel of Gamelyn (Artifact)
Necromancer (Hero)
Assassin (Hero)
Avenger (Legendary Hero #1)
Revenant (Legendary Hero #2)
Guardian (Legendary Hero #3)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Gothic Doctor: Dual Treatment Kickstarter Exclusive Cards
description: Kickstarter exclusive that includes 10 dual treatment cards

Money makes the world go round. It also helps secret agents pay their rent.

In [redacted]: Mercenaries, a whole new level of spycraft is necessary, not for the glory of the revolution or to defend fast food and reality TV, but to end your career with a nice chunk of change. With a variety of conflicting missions, players must use the skills they picked up during the base game of [redacted] to work together (or not) and complete assignments for their shadowy employers.

Changing loyalties, shifting objectives, conflicting assignments. Nothing ever stays the same, and players must balance short-term gain and long-term strategy to win, in this advanced expansion to [redacted].

Coopopoly is a board game with a baseball/Cooperstown theme. Choose a player piece and start playing...as you buy, sell, and trade The National Baseball Hall of Fame, Farmers' Museum, Otesaga Hotel and all of your favorite businesses and museums in Cooperstown, NY! It's a new addition to complete every baseball collection and the perfect gift for any baseball fan, Cooperstown entusiast, and board game lover. Designed by elementary teacher this game can also encourage math skills for young children!

A ship full of scallywags and a hold full of loot... it’s time to divvy up the booty.  Keep an eye on the crew though, because everyone knows pirates be cheaters! Try to keep the best loot for yourself while giving the worst to your mates! Once all pirates have five treasures, the pirate with the most valuable loot wins.

Me Booty! is a card game for 3 to 6 scallywags. On your turn you must decide to take treasure for yourself, give it to another pirate, or use some sneaky back-stabbing to sabotage your mates.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – The Collective Month 1 Organized Play Borg Cube and tokens
description:
Comes with the following for use with the "First Contact" scenario as a part of Month 1 of "The Collective" organized play tournaments:

1 Borg Cube (ship) Token with unique stats (6/0/8/8)
8 Resistance Tokens
4 Hidden Movement Tokens

Napolatronics is a wargame for 2-4 players, set in a wacky world of smartly uniformed robots launching bold assaults from their rocky, island bases.

This is a complete wargame! All YOU need to add are some dice.

Features of this game include...
1) 4 complete armies of robots! Cavalry, Artilery and Infantry.
2) 4 Battle Mats representing the floating island bases
3) A sheet of Terrain tiles! These randomly drawn tiles will give the gameboard a different layout EVERY game!
4) Easy to follow Rules booklet!
5) Cheatsheets! With these you wont have to reference the rule book at all!

boardgame: Green Lantern Power of the Ring Game
description:
The path is filled with excitement and danger: will you master the four constructs that you need to defeat Parallax? When you arrive at the lantern with the ring, press on the lantern to see the result. An unlit ring means you have to go back on the track. A lit ring means... you've won! Light up the ring and recite the Green Lantern oath!

Players roll a pip die to move around a circular board. Only after collecting all four types of Green Lantern Constructs will a player leave the main board and have an opportunity to win the game. Varying spaces along the way distribute Constructs or force players to discard Constructs, while other spaces allow a player to send themselves or other players to a space of his or her choosing.

Two notable components also affect game play. By landing on a ring space, a player might take the Lantern's ring (an actual light up ring component), allowing him or her to roll the pip die twice and pick from the outcomes. Finally, even after collecting all four Constructs, a player must successfully charge their ring to win. A large Lantern battery piece gives a player a roughly 2/3 chance of victory by spinning a plastic piece, pressing a button, and awaiting the random response.

boardgame: BattleGroup: Modern Wargaming Rules – For Battalion Level Miniature Based Games
description:
Battlegroup has been written because of modern wargamers, dissatisfaction with the commercial rules available.

Battlegroup has been play-tested over the last few years and were used successfully between 1997 and 2008 in the Ultramodern World Wargaming Championships, the 1997 Royal Air Force Wargames Association Championships and the modern competition at Roll Call 2001-2007.

The feedback from all those experienced players helped the rules evolve into this edition.

Although these rules offer nothing revolutionary in the field of wargames rules you will find Battlegroup faster and easier to follow than previous modern rules, rules that due to the complexities of modern warfare ended up either too complicated or far too simplistic.

Battlegroup is designed to give the feel of modern combat, taking into account all the latest technologies being fielded, yet still remain an enjoyable game.

These rules are ideal for quick battles, competitions, and campaign games or even for new players to learn about modern warfare.

—description from game introduction by Ian Clarke - 2009

AMERICAN CIVIL WAR IN 10MM SCALE MINIATURES RULES

The Civil War lasted from 1861 to 1865 and cost the lives of 600,000 Americans; more than in all Americas' other wars combined. Its immediate cause was the decision of several southern states to leave the Union of States. This 'secession' was the result of years of rivalry between north and south and friction between the federal Government and State governments, particularly over the issue of slavery. Some 50,000 books and articles have been written about this epic conflict that re-forged the American nation.
This is a rules system that enables players to re-fight some of the 2,500 battles and skirmishes that made up of this dramatic episode of history. They are based on modern historical research, which dispels

These rules are intended for use with 1/144 scale aircraft miniatures. Each aircraft model should be mounted on an hexagon shaped flying stand which has one side designated with an arrow to indicate the direction of forward movement (see Figure 14a) and a collar assembly which allows the insertion and removal of the multi-length altitude dowels.

description:
The aim of these rules is to simulate combat in the period 1789-1815.

COURSE OF A GAME
Each game is assumed to be one of the following formats
a) Competitive - each player has a force which is balanced using a points system. The game commences with the opposing forces (starting on or off table) and advancing towards each other, with intent to give battle and destroy the opposing force. Starting with initial contacts from patrols and/or vanguards prompting their commanders to respond. The main forces are deployed in attempts to seize and/or maintain the initiative. Finally one force is defeated and the victors may attempt pursuit. The rules are primarily designed around this type of game.
b) Scenario – each player has a force defined for the scenario which may be based on fictional or historical events. The objectives and victory conditions being defined by the scenario.

REPRESENTATIVE SCALES
There are variable ground and troop scales to allow for varying sizes of engagements. So in effect the ground scale will vary from 1:2500 (i.e. 1mm on the table = 2.5m in life) to 1:30,000 (1mm = 30m). However in any game the ground and troop scales are directly proportionate to each other.

The game is broken into bounds of varying length. For game purposes these are expressed are periods of minutes. The activity of all troops within any bound is directly related to the time span of the bound. For example a five minute bound allows all troops to complete five minutes of activities. However a 500 minute bound allows all troops five hundred minutes of activities. The reason for the variability is to allow for those periods where little or no activity occurs to be passed over.

Troops are represented by figurines and or models based on varying size bases.

Cold Infinity is a tabletop ship-to-ship space combat game, focusing on small engagements at the tactical level.The Main Rulebook and the Ship Systems Book (abbreviated SSB) provide all the rules and tables required
to create and play in virtually any space-faring science fiction universe you can imagine.

Take control of a rampaging monster and destroy other monsters or just crush a city and puny Humans.

To begin playing Destroy All Monsters, you need the following things:

    A Gaming Board. Preferably with some kind of urban terrain, but use whatever you have.
    2 of 9 Monsters. One per player. These can be of any scale that the players desire, but should be in scale with each other and with the terrain.
    Cotton, for representing smoke, ooze, webbing, or other, less mentionable, substances.
    A Record Sheet for each Monster. These should be prepared sometime before actual play begins.


Pencils, for recording changes to the above mentioned record sheets.

    A Vast Quantity of Six-Sided Dice. You're gonna need 'em.


Something to Represent Power Points. Small tokens of some sort. Beads or Glass Blebs would be ideal. These will be kept in a common pool, and distributed to individual players
as necessary.

    Numbered Chits, and Something to mix them in, so that players may determine when it is their turn to move.


To beat all other Monsters present into unconsciousness. And to step on a few humans, while you're at it.

boardgame: Firepower Naval wargaming Rules: Naval Gaming with Rules and Model Ships for the Period 1900 to 1945
description:
Naval gaming with rules and model ships for the period 1900 to 1945 These rules can be used for any of the popular scales, though most players will prefer the smaller scales for space and cost reasons. For
the purpose of this game, scales are divided into two classes; 1/700th and smaller (sub-1/700th). The most popular small scale is 1/3000th. At the end of the rules there is a special section for players (including myself) who prefer the 1/700th - the real scale.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Guardians of Deephall
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The stronghold of Deephall was once home to a thriving, peaceful community, but now the mountain city lies under constant threat of invasion. A horde of undead, led by dark priests, attacks Deephall constantly. These unending attacks deplete the city's guards and erode the peoples' resolve. Four heroes are all that stands between Deephall and the city's extinction.

Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Guardians of Deephall contains four heroes and three monster groups; these figures were previously available in the first edition of Descent, but are now brought in line with second edition aesthetics through reimagined figure sculpts and artwork. You'll find the orc warrior Mordrog battling in the front lines alongside Lord Hawthorne. At the same time, Sahla, a gifted Healer, focuses his power by channeling the strength of other heroes, even as the deadly assassin known as Silhouette uses her stealth and cunning to cut her enemies down.

The battle for Deephall's fate plays out in two new quests. In "Guardians of Deephall", the heroes must defend the walls of Deephall against a seemingly endless assault of undead and dark priests. Then, in "The Curse of Iona", the hero party must venture into the icy lair of a dragon sorceress to reclaim a pendant that may be Deephall's salvation.

description:
Mech Deck is a light skirmish game in which players draft and build customizable Mechs and deploy them on a modular battlefield. These Mechs are made up of five plastic parts that fit together to form the players piece in the game. Each part of the Mech has unique stats and abilities displayed on their corresponding Mech cards. After taking some damage these parts will be destroyed and the player must remove them from the mini.

Deck cards are drawn each turn giving extra abilities and maneuvers for the players to use. Action cards are played on the player's turn and reaction cards are played on the opponent's turn.

description: Rail Baron Rivals is a game where you and a rival baron are competing to have the most cash after 12 years of development in the golden age of railroading in the U.S. Northeast. Each year is represented by a turn in which you will roll 5 special dice that are used by one or more railroads to build track, expand cities and improve infrastructure. You may invest in any of these companies by acquiring shares - the more shares you have of the successful railroads the richer you will be. If you can get the right combination of shares and each railroad has increased in value then you have won the game.

Montoro is a deduction game where two players compete to get all the goods available. On each turn a player takes the role of "reporting" and his opponent of "manager" where the declarant will announce the number of tricks that will take, and the manager will have to try to thwart the plans of the declarant and make it do more or assets less the number I said. This is a tactical, fast and very pissed game.


Montoro es un juego de deducción donde dos jugadores competirán por conseguir todos los bienes disponibles. description:
You are one of “Crust” – the neighborhood pizzeria delivery drivers.

The Pizzeria owner knows that a good pizza delivery is when the pizza arrive to the customer Fast and Hot.

That will make the customer happy and a loyal one.
How can the pizzeria owner know the pizza was served fast and hot?
By the tips the delivery Driver gets from the customers.

That’s why at the end of each day the pizzeria owner checks which delivery driver got the most tips and he give him a bonus and the title “Employee of the day”.

Your mission is to earn the most tips at the end of the day and become “employee of the day”.

How to play the game:

ROUND SETUP
The player picks up a delivery card showing the delivery destination. 
The player calculates which the fastest route is for him.
The player throws the dice and advances the number steps indicated by the dice.
The player moves by one count the "pizza marker" that indicates the progress of the delivery.
Each "traffic line" on the road and each “road sign" are progress steps included in the play.
Traffic is only allowed in the direction indicated by the arrows on the road signs.
After this, the next player draws a card and repeats the steps.

PIZZA DELIVERY
Arrival at the delivery destination is considered done when a player threw the dice and moved according to the number on the dice to one of the “traffic lines " located across the entrance (address number) of the same house and between the two road signs on its sides.
 After a player has arrived to the house and delivered the pizza he will receive a "tip" in money bills, which depends on how fast he has brought the pizza to the customers. The calculation will be based on the number on which the "pizza marker" is located, that counts of the number of turns that the player played until he arrived to the house.

When the player arrives back to the pizzeria, he picks up another delivery card and repeats the action.

Clankball Legends is a turn-based, dexterity sports game coming soon to Kickstarter. It combines the strategic disc-flicking of Carrom or Crokinole with the excitement of tabletop hockey.

You are the player leading a team of Legends from across the galaxy to victory in a futuristic and brutal version of hockey. You guide your Legends by flicking them throughout the arena while scoring goals, using power-ups, and pummeling your opponent into the ground. The goal is to score more points than your opponent by the end of the match.

description:
Street Dice is a 2 - 4 Player game. Players all start with a car and use the dice to race each other. If you lose your car in a Race you must Borrow, Earn, or Steal another.

Setting the Qualifying Combo: On the player’s turn they are automatically in the dig(the race). Player 1 rolls their 3 qualifying dice to set the combo for the others to get into the race. After the combo is set, player 1 says, “On your mark, get set, go!”

Qualifying: The other players roll their 3 qualifying dice as quickly as they can to qualify for the dig. Once someone rolls the right combo they yell out that they have it. If there is a tie Player 1 re-rolls another combo and the tied parties re-roll.

Only 2 players race in a dig. Once the qualifying is done the 2 players each draw a Start Stage Card ad place it face down in front of them. They also get the 9 start stage dice (Red, Yellow, & Green) and place them in front of them.

To win the game a player must hold 4 cars in their garage at the same time. After you have 4 cars you are the Street Dice Champion.

Game description from the publisher:

Fantasy Flight Games is proud to announce the upcoming release of The Source, the sixth and final Data Pack in the Lunar Cycle for Android: Netrunner!

In The Source, cyber-explorer Nasir Meidan departs Heinlein for Earth and the relative safety of his apartment. There, he stares at the drive in his hand before booting up his console. Was he able to complete his download? Does this drive truly contain the net’s legendary source protocols? What will he find? After a series of thrilling encounters with Corporate security measures and security officers, all he has to do is pull up the files.

At the end of your lunar expedition, the sixty new cards in The Source (three copies each of twenty different cards) reveal surprising new takes on life, evolution, and death in the virtual world and the cyberstruggles of Android: Netrunner. You’ll find assets that reward Corps for birthing new servers and others that can self-destruct. You’ll find self-propagating viruses waiting to explode into the network, and you’ll find programs that reward you for jacking out of your runs.

description: Polter-Heist is for 2-4 players, ages 6+, with a playtime of 45-60 minutes or more.  The object of the game is to explore the haunted mansion, uncovering rooms and searching for the hidden Treasure.  Along the way you’ll make discoveries that can help you out and encounter ghosts that will deplete your courage or hinder your search.  Occasionally you may encounter a benevolent spirit that will help you along the way.  Be careful though, because if you get scared to death you’ll become a ghost that can chase down your former friends!  You win the game by acquiring the Treasure and getting it out of the house.  The ghosts win if everyone in the house gets turned to a ghost.  You lose if you are a ghost and one or more players are able to make it out of the house with the Treasure.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Counter Attack Die Resource
description:
The Counter Attack Die is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 1, "First Contact," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, The Collective. It is considered a Resource, and costs 5 Squadron Points.
				
				
					1) The Counter Attack Die is considered a defense die.
2) Once during every Combat Phase, a player may choose to exchange 1 of their defense dice with the Counter Attack Die.  Players may not roll this die more than once per round, regardless of how many ships they have in their fleet.
3) The pips on the Counter Attack Die are: [Evade],[Evade],[Evade],[Battle Stations],[Battle Stations],[Hit],[Hit],[Critical Hit]
4) If a [Hit] or a [Critical Hit] result is rolled, the attacking ship suffers damage as normal during the Deal Damage step of the Combat Phase.  The attacking ship does not roll defense dice against this damage.

Armory is an expansion to BattleCON War, Devastation, and Fate which allows you to create customized fighters using all sorts of enchanted gear, potions, and trinkets. It's perfect for players who want to test their skills at creating fighters on the fly.

description: BattleCON: War Extended Edition is an expansion to BattleCON: War that gives you new costumes, extra game systems, and more arenas.

The 2nd expansion set for Bezzerwizzer.

The game contains 2,000 new questions on 100 cards in the same categories and layout as the base game.

A burning torch falls upon the damp rock of the crypt where you, Fortunato, are now trapped after being lured with the promise of Amontillado. As Montresor forces the final stone into place, there is a faint ring of metal hitting the floor. Now you face a race against time to find the key that Montresor dropped inside your tomb before the air runs out.

This is a solitaire game with memory as the main mechanic. As you search for the two key tiles, you must balance your other traits as best you can in order to give yourself the best chance to escape and survive. The only additional components needed would be markers for each trait category and to track your movement on the tiles.

boardgame: Snowdonia: The Necropolis Line & Neuhauser Bockerlbahn
description:
Snowdonia: The Necropolis Line & Neuhauser Bockerlbahn consists of two new scenarios for Snowdonia that, again, takes players away from the damp North of Wales and transports them (and their shovels) to London and the forests of Germany...and both scenarios find a new way to use your Surveyor piece!

The Necropolis Line, designed by Tony Boydell, follows the specially-built 19th century railway for the transportation of London's dead to the leafy suburbs and eternal rest. Your Surveyor has passed away (R.I.P) — probably pneumonia-related given the places she's been sent up to now — and you must a) build the line and b) build a grave in which to put her to rest.

The Bocklerlbahn, designed by Sebastian Bleasdale, re-enacts the consequences of a great storm that leveled hundreds of thounsands of trees; the railway was built specifically to transport this useful wood to lumber mills! Rubble is now "fallen trees" and won't score you any contract card bonuses until it's been "converted" by your mill!

This expansion is comprised of rules and a single deck of 33 cards containing:


    Alternative versions of contract cards for the Necropolis Line scenario
    New trains just for the Bockerlbahn scenario
    New trains for general use...including an Elephant and a caterpillar-tracked Traction Engine (!)
    New station cards!

description:
Lords of Conquest is a strategy game of territorial struggle.  You begin by taking turns selecting territories on a game map.  Because of their locations and wealth, some of these territories will be important and powerful, while others will be insignificant and weak.  The rich ones contain "resources" such as Gold, Horses, Iron, Coal, or Timber, which you will work to acquire because of the powerful weapons and cities they can buy.

The action in Lords of Conquest takes place in yearly cycles.  Each year has four of five phases.  During each phase, you will have an opportunity to make decisions in preparation for that year's action.  The first phase is Development, during which you can invest in new weapons and cities.  Development begins during the second year, and is the first phase of all subsequent years.  The other phases are 1. Productions, in which your resource-bearing territories produce wealth; 2. Trading; 3. Shipment, in which you have an opportunity to move either your wealth or some of your forces to a more strategic location; and 4. Conquest, in which you relentlessly assault your neighbors' territories.

Battles between territories are decided on the basis of the number of supporting territories and forces (in the form of weapons, horses, boats and cities) held by each side.  During the year, all the players will have a chance to expand their territories and wealth at the expense of their neighbors.  Ultimately, you become strong enough to start building cities. The first player to build three cities, and successfully defend them for a year, is the winner.  One last word of advice, show no mercy, and expect none!  Not everyone has what it takes rules the world.

A previously unpublished expansion for the ASL system. Included as an insert to Tactical Wargamer's Journal, Issue 1.

Kruglowka Railway is a two-player scenario depicting the fighting near the Dneiper River in July 1941.  In addition to the scenario, extensive playtest notes and historical articles are included.

Tactical Wargamer's Journal (ISSN 1918-9729), published on a non-regular schedule, covers topics of interest on all aspects of the world of commercial tactical wargaming, with its focus set on warfare at the man-to-man, squad and platoon level in the 20th Century and contemporary era. Tactical Wargamer's Journal (TWJ) places emphasis on both military history and the history of tactical level gaming itself.

Issue 1 focuses on the history of the Großdeutschland Regiment from 1939-1942, and the GD is featured prominently in this scenario.

boardgame: Time Wars: Strike Team First Edition
description:
The original game of superpowered time traveling vampire hunters.

Players form Strike Teams of unique Time Corps Agents while the Game Master sends them on brief missions into the past with explicit goals which change from adventure to adventure. Characters have Stats, which represent their physical body, and Skills, which represent their learned behaviors. As the Game Master plays out the Timeline of the adventure, the players must creatively apply their powers, try to avoid using the dice to check against their Stats, and increase their Skills, as they navigate the past and meet the goals set for them by Time Corps Supreme Command. But the window into the past is quickly closing - if you don't accomplish your mission in time, you never will!

The First Edition of the game is no longer available for download, and is no longer supported by the publisher.

In the revised Second Edition rules you will find an improved Skill Point Distribution System, new Powers including the new category of Emergent Powers, over 200 new possible animal hybrids, improved Power rules, an overhauled combat system, and improved language, among many other things!

Players form Strike Teams of unique Time Corps Agents while the Game Master sends them on brief missions into the past with explicit goals which change from adventure to adventure.
