Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Kempten, a city in Germany.

Nomads No More (NNM) is a free game system that includes two different games: Ungern’s Mongolia and Pasha’s Bokhara. Both games in the NNM system use the same core rules but use separate maps and pieces to cover concurrent conflicts that erupted on the eastern perimeter of Soviet Russia soon after the Russian Civil War – the early 1920s. Ungern’s Mongolia simulates the invasion of Mongolia by fleeing Whites, their clash with the Chinese and subsequent pursuit by Reds. Pasha’s Bokhara covers the Basmachi uprising against Soviet rule around Bokhara - present day Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan.

Published in Paper Wars #86 (Summer 2017 issue); publisher Compass Games.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Offenbach, in Germany.

Dead Apocalypse is one of the first fully cooperative deckbuilding games of strategic survival, set during a present day zombie apocalypse. Staying alive is your only goal in this game, and you must work with your allies to search for human survivors, abandoned locations, and lifesaving equipment to fend off the zombie horde.  Teamwork is the key to victory, for if you can't trust your fellow players none of you will reach the CDC... alive!

Dead Apocalypse is a fully cooperative deckbuilding game, meaning all players strive for the same goal and either win together or lose together. Players will begin the game with the same starting decks consisting of a handful of survivors, a base camp location and some equipment. Players will take turns playing their hand to gain damage, occupy locations, use abilities and cure infections. Gaining damage allows the player to defeat ghastly Zombies who are protecting invaluable equipment, or surrounding a location, or even trapping other survivors. Once a Zombie is defeated, add the resources to your personal deck to be used for another turn. Though don't think Zombies will go down without a fight. The Zombie stack of cards holds surprise attack events which will force a player to have to defeat Zombies that match the type of event. If a player does not have the sufficient resources to defeat all Zombies, other players have the opportunity to assist in combat to help even the odds!

Dead Apocalypse is  1-4 players so if there isn't someone in your company you still have the opportunity to try to fight for survival all alone. Don't fret though because there are other game variants for those seeking an easier game or a more competitive game.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Melle, a community in Germany.

Pot O' Gold is a colorful, fast-paced family card game. Each player takes on the role of a leprechaun trying to be the first to complete their rainbow and get the Pot O' Gold - but they have to be wary of the other devious tricksters who have the same goal!

Pot O' Gold includes nine different cards, each of which have actions associated with them. These include seven different color cards - Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, and Violet - that may be played sequentially to form a player's rainbow or to take an action that will help them manage their cards, get new cards, or attack another player. There are also two special cards: Pot O' Gold acts as a Wild Card as well as the winning card; Leprechauns are tricksters who undo the last action played.

On their turns, players may play in one (or both) of two ways: to add to or to solidify their rainbow in front of them, or as an action. Hand order matters, and players must play the first card from their hand every turn; they may choose to play the second if possible. Play continues until one player has played all colors of the rainbow in order and plays a Pot O' Gold to win the game.

In RARRR!!, players first draft monster power cards in order to build their perfect monster, then draft power bid cards to fuel their monsters' rampages through cities. Cities are destroyed using a bidding system, and players are limited in their bids by the amount of each type of power that their monster possesses.

Will you build a super fire-spewing dragon monster, or a more balanced monster that can flatten cities time and time again for the long haul? Either way, it's not a good day to live in the metropolis.

This set of 12 bonus cards was included with the 2014 Kickstarter edition. It includes 4 new monsters and 8 new cities.

From the developer:

5-6 Player Expansion to Noueni. You need the base game to play.

Noueni - Japanese for "On top of" describes this abstract game.

In Noueni each player has their own unique deck of 18 cards. Each card is broken up into two halves and each half has a scoring orb on it. The goal of the game is to have the most visible scoring orbs.

Laying new cards follow a simple set of rules:

1) Connectors on connecting edges must match in number (0, 1, 2, or 3 lines.)
2) New tiles can be placed next to a tile, or overlapping one half of other tiles.
3) A tile may not completely cover another fully exposed tile.

Just because the rules are simple doesn't mean that finding the best place to put a tile to cover up your opponent’s orbs and protect your own is easy.

Make sure you end up on top.

Evolutionary Tabletop Game

Galapagos is a resource management game in which players take on the roles of competing species in a rainforest. Each turn, players will harvest genetic material: the currency with which they may buy mutations to change their species or propagate to increase their presence in the ecosystem. Players must balance strategy, tactics, and guile to compete in the pursuit of several unique win conditions; ultimately to ensure the survival of their genetic footprint.

-- Developer's description

Things Change.
You are a species. That's not an insult, it's the premise of [the] game. In this game, you take on the role of a unique species in a rainforest ecosystem. Your goal is to out-compete your opponents to obtain the largest ecological footprint; and I have given you several win conditions so that you can prove it.

The core mechanic of the game is called harvesting. Each turn, you will harvest genetic material (The game's currency) which you can use to buy mutations to make your creatures better equipped to compete, or propagate to increase your presence in the forest. The amount of genes you harvest depends on your presence and position in the forest. The more you have, the more genetic capital you have to work with. Neat, huh?

Win Conditions.
If you want to win the game, you'll need to be the first to achieve any one of these win conditions:

Survival of the Fittest. This one is straightforward. If your creatures are the only ones alive in the forest, you win. Do this by clashing with opponents' creatures.

Top of the Food Chain. If, at the beginning of your turn, you control three carnivores, you win. Easy, right? Go ahead, make that third carnivore. See what your opponents do.

Ecological Niche. If, at the beginning of your turn, you control a creature in each Floor tile (Blue-green), you win. Who says barnacles can't be winners? Seriously, who says that?

Okay, I think that's it. The game is designed for 2-3 players, but if you have a fourth, you should play in teams. Oh, and one more thing:

It's time to get squishy.

-- from the game manual

The game is for 2-4 players. Each have 8 bottles and the winner is the one who get rid of his/her bottles fastest.

The game board have eight spaces for each players bottles plus five spaces in the middle.
The players roll the die by turn and do as following:
Roll 1-5 - The player move his/her bottle to the numbered space in the middle.
If there is already a bottle on this space the player takes this into his/her own box.
Roll 6: The player removes the bottle from the game - one down!

For each time you can either place a bottle in the middle or roll a 6 to remove a bottle completly, you can choose to roll again.
Each player have to have at least one roll per turn.

In Q4, players take the role of board game publishers, trying to make a name for themselves in the competitive world of hobby board games. The objective of the game is not necessarily to make the most money – although it helps – but to get the most satisfaction out of your career as a publisher by releasing the kind of games that you love.
Over the course of a game players will seek funding and take out loans in order to license games from designers, hire artists and sell their games; boring parts of the process such as dealing with printers, shipping, warehousing and losing your house are abstracted away, but release deadlines are not!

Each game design a player licenses (a Contract) is placed onto that player’s production schedule – the player must then source enough artwork to make that game before it hits its release date, as games which cannot be released on time get bad buzz and won’t make them so much money. There are a limited number of artists working on board game art, however, so competition for art in particular styles can be fierce, and players will have to outbid their rivals to include it in their games.

In a game turn, players take turns to place their company's workers (discs) on the action spaces around the board. Some spaces function as traditional worker-placement action spaces and can be occupied only by a single disc at once. Other spaces - such as the artists or the game-design contract spaces - act as auctions; players place their discs on top of the stack of other discs already on the space, and once all discs have been placed, the players take turns from the top down to pay money equal to the number of discs in the stack if they want to commission art from that artist, or contract a game design from the submission queue. Players who place late pay the highest price but get first choice; players who place early could get a bargain, but only if nobody else outbids them for the thing they wanted.

At the end of each turn, each player's catalogue of completed games sell to distributors and give him some income, but only a small number of games can be kept in print at any one time, and if a player's company has too much money in the bank the directors take the excess in dividends and blow it all on a weekend in Vegas.

At the end of the game, players score each contract they've completed, getting bonus points if it aligns with their company's goals.

The indy RPG storytelling game 99 chances consists of a deck of cards, each with three distinct features: numbers from 1-99, opposing sets of words (good/bad, open/closed, wise/foolish, etc.), and genres (western, zombies, sci-fi, murder mystery, etc.)

Players are dealt a hand of cards and choose two genres from among them to mash up and pitch as a story. The group decides on a story by consensus, then builds characters. Players play cards on themselves with the positive side up to represent resources they have within the context of the story; they play cards negative side up on other players as problems.

Players take turns telling tales about how they will use their resources and the story itself to overcome their problems. Other players select number cards from their hands to represent the plausibility of the active storyteller's chance of success. The player who selects the median number is rewarded with a bonus resource and the median number becomes the chance of success for the storyteller. If the turn of a card is below this chance of success, the problem is turned into a resource.

After all players have had two turns to tackle their problems, the player with the most resources frames the climax. Players wager their resources on the final turn of the card.

Objective: be the first gaining a victory point(=marker) each from all different topics
 - acting by turns -
On a circuit all meeples are moved forward by die; ending on an uncolored space - next player's turn; airport symbol - move to space of your choice; colored space - neighbor draws appropriate card:
roll the die again - number determines question/task. 
Successful - get matching marker; wrong - new start out from the centre of the board.
Different topics: true/false; 2 x multiple choice; find wanted person using 3 (out of 6) random hints; find 7 matching terms within one minute; guess matching profession

Business on the Move is an educational business board game.
Players run a business moving different products from China to their domestic market by land, air and sea. The game can be won in 3 ways:-

    the business that delivers orders the quickest
    the business with the greatest profit
    the business with the lowest carbon footprint


Players must make the same decisions businesses make every day.  How do I deliver? Will I make a profit? How should I grow? How can I cut my carbon footprint?

Each turn a player must choose between taking an Air/Sea turn or a Rail/Road turn. You can only do one or the other. Playing your turn then requires 4 same steps in strict order:-

    Step 1 - Deciding whether to buy new assets- planes, ships, trains & trucks
    Step 2 - Paying operational costs
    Step 3 - Picking up an Air/Sea or Rail/Road card
    Step 4 - The last thing you do in your turn is roll the dice to make deliveries. Income is earned by completing deliveries for the amount shown on your Order cards.


These 4 easy-to-follow steps are summarised on an A6 card.
Business on the Move  is extremely versatile and can be played at seven different levels, changing how to play and how to win.
Extensive trials across the UK have seen the game played by nearly 600 young people aged 9-19, teachers and business professionals.

description:
Objective: Using the detailed street map of Stuttgart each player is given five companies (each divided in 4 pieces/tiles) to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.
note:
game approach is identical with the 'cityprogame' family; game production was financed by advertisements of locally based companies

Commercial game, related to Monopoly, for the purpose of promoting Rendsburg, a city in Germany.

The players move around a board consisting of 40 squares. Most of the squares are showing companies to be bought - with values between 1000 and 8000. Three companies are forming a set with the same colour. Whenever a player lands on a win&loss-square, he/she must draw a card following instructions.
As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends, all company cards are sold for their original price. Player with most money is the winner.

Another version of the classic Monopoly. This time the cases represent the 25th anniversary of the Dutch professional association of ship piloting.

Keyflower: Trader, a new village tile for the game Keyflower, enables a skill tile to be exchanged for a green "keyple" or (if upgraded at the cost of one gold) for two green keyples. If upgraded, the tile is also worth 3 points at the end of the game.

One copy of Keyflower: Trader is available for a small donation for each copy of Keyflower: The Merchants purchased at Spiel in October 2014. All proceeds will be donated to the East Sheen Chengannur Trust, of which Keyflower co-designer Richard Breese is a trustee. The Trust aims to help the needy in Chengannur, Kerala State, India.

Two to four players fight for mastery of the galaxy! Explore a galaxy filled with planets and other wonders! Build specialized fleets of star ships. Organize massive invasion fleets and armies. Use powerful leaders to command your forces, make and break alliances, conduct battles between ships and rise or fall on the tides of fortune and failure. Battle for Gallion's Reach is the classic eXplore, eXpand, eXploit and eXterminate space game for your table top!

- Build fleets of star ships such as Battle Fleets, Invasion Fleets and Siege Fleets and even planet killers that can destroy entire worlds!

- Defend your worlds with powerful Planetary Defenses!

- Build Star Bases where you can build your fleets, defend your empire and gather your invasion armies!

- Research advanced technologies to give your faction the edge!

- Attempt to usurp the Imperial Throne and become the Emperor!

- Space hex cards make the game map different each time you play! From huge spiral galaxies to smaller layouts for shorter, sharper games, Gallion's Reach makes sure no two gaming experiences are the same!

- Detailed and yet simple star ship combat simulation where you, as the admiral, place your fleets into battle and fight it out with your enemies!

- Simple economic system lets players concentrate on conquest strategy and fleet battles.

- Random Events keep things interesting.

- Action Cards give players secret weapons to disarm enemies, steal ships, make and break alliances and much more!

- Simple die roll-based combat.

User Summary

A strategic card game for two or more players, each using their own deck, who must battle to defeat their opponent. It is a stand-alone game, not collectible, but is designed to be expanded and may include expansions with custom cards. Game requires one complete deck per player and each includes 40 full color cards with different pictures.

Rip It is a silly, humorous game about farting.  Players sit around a table with a food deck at each seat.  Players choose to eat (draw) from the food at their seat, roll to fart, move to another seat, or bump a player to an empty seat.

If their fart succeeds, they will cause players on their left and right to lose "air". Players that lose enough "air" will "pass out".

Players use card combinations for greater fart effect, and Object cards such as a "Fan" to blow a fart to another seat. Players compete for the Window seat which helps regain "air", and the Gas Mask which provides protection.

Those that cause enough "pass outs" win!

boardgame: I Don't Need to Outrun the Zombie
description:
I Don't Need to Outrun the Zombie is a game that combines press your luck dice rolling with the need to anticipate your friend's choices. Will they try to come to your aid, leave you to fend for yourself, or outright stab you in the back? Every player must choose what will best help them survive.

Game play happens in phases. In the first phase of each round, the players (in turn order) roll their dice and attempt to get the best score possible. A given die may be rerolled any number of times until a) the player accepts the number on the die or b) the die rolls a 1. If the die rolls a one, that die is locked at one for the round.

Once each player has their starting roll for the round, the scheming begins. Each player must choose to help or hinder an opponent with five possible outcomes (3 outcomes if there are only two players left).

Outcome 1 - Player A helps Player B: Player B adds 3 points to their score for the round.
Outcome 2 - Player A hinders Player B: Player B subtracts 3 points.
Outcome 3 - Player A helps Player B and Player B helps Player A: Each player adds 6 points.
Outcome 4 - Player A hinders Player B and Player B hinders Player A: Each player subtracts 6 points.
Outcome 5 - Player A helps Player B and Player B hinders Player A: Player A subtracts 6 Points and Player B adds 6 points.

At the end of the second phase, the player with the lowest score in the round loses a die. Players gain bonus chips adding 1 point to their score for the next round for each player with a higher score in the current round. The player with the highest score goes first next round.

The game ends when only one player is left with dice. All others have been eaten by the zombie.

boardgame: Infamy: Welcome to The Big Smoke
description:
Summary of information found on the game website, in Kickstarter updates and in the tutorial videos:

Infamy: Welcome to The Big Smoke is an action-packed skirmish game and a range of steampunk miniatures. Join the revolution and battle across an alternate Victorian London!

Infamy: Welcome to The Big Smoke uses playing-card-controlled skill management and opposed-dice combat creating strategic, hybrid gameplay. Each character uses playing cards for a pontoon/blackjack mini game, drawing cards as close to 21 as possible to increase the selection of special actions that are available for use.  Bust hands only allow the most basic attacks, but increase the character's defensive abilities.  Attacks are resolved with opposed dice rolls and players alternate activation of their characters, so the players are continuously engaged in the game.

Infamy: Welcome to The Big Smoke has a range of intricately detailed, resin-cast, 32mm scale miniatures. Many Infamy: Welcome to The Big Smoke characters are based on famous individuals from literature and history, but given a steampunk twist.

It is set in an alternate Victorian London in turmoil through the development of advanced new technology. The city is packed with intriguing stories, fascinating characters and a deep history, making for a rich steampunk setting. Character cards describe the unique abilities of each character, creating games that have an exciting cinematic style.

boardgame: Monopoly: Fairmont Hotel & Resorts Centennial Edition
description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Another version of the classic Monopoly. This time the cases represent the company Fairmont.

Conquista Bíblica is a question and answer game about the Bible. There is a total of 996 questions. The game is for 2 to 6 players, starting at the age they can read.
You roll the dice and navigate over the board. The cell where you land has 6 questions associated. You roll the dice a second time, to determine the number of the question you will have to answer. You may use the Bible to look up your answer. If you answer it correctly, within 30 seconds, you get 3 “denarios” (coins). If you need the biblical reference, and answer it correctly you only get 1 “denario”.
When you reach the total of 6 “denarios”, you may conquer a book. If the cell where you land belongs to a a biblical book, you can pay 6 “denarios” in order to try to answer correctly 3 questions. If you do so, you conquered the book and receive the corresponding card! The player that conquers the more books wins!

Original Spanish description:
Conquista Bíblica é um jogo de perguntas e respostas sobre a Bíblia. Perguntas sobre reis, profetas, sacerdotes, homens e mulheres, nações, cidades, apóstolos, alimentos, armas, habitações, batalhas, viagens, etc, irão testar os seus conhecimentos e ajudá-lo a conhecer melhor a Palavra de Deus.

Take part in a rebellion in this all vs. one game! Overthrow Enderlin or defend it - your choice.

'Empires rise and empires fall. Whether through mistreatment of the locale, mismanagement of responsibility, or outside invaders all empires eventually meet their reckoning.

The reckoning day of the city of Enderlin has arrived. Civil unrest has led to rebellion. Rebel leaders take up the sword in the name of freedom. They raise their battlecry in the hopes that the people will join them in taking up arms against the King and Empire.

This is the story of Enderlin. This is the story of its Uprising...'

In Uprising, players take on the role of either the King of Enderlin or one of the Rebels working towards toppling the Empire. In this all vs. one scenario, players must defeat the opposing forces through the use of influence and diplomacy - swaying the ten unique Districts of Enderlin to their cause by the actions that they take. The Districts of Enderlin are the key to victory. Only through his influence on the people can the King squelch the rebellion that has arisen. Only through their influence on the masses can the Rebels hope to topple the tyrant. Who the player influences determines their bonuses and abilities as well as the ultimate fate of Enderlin. Use your Influence wisely to sway the Districts of Enderlin for your forces!

Race through an obstacle course by reading passages and answering questions about the order of events!

Adventure Camp is an educational roll-and-move boardgame where players must answer reading comprehension questions in order to progress along the track to the finish.  The theme involves a wilderness trail with various shortcuts, hazards and obstacles which can move a player forward, cause the loss of a turn, etc.

From the insert: A highly competitive and most compelling game for all ages.

The game comprises two dice and a scoring pad, with simple rules.

The six-sided red die has pot x4, miss, or foul. The other dice contains the colours of other balls.

There is no skill in this simplistic simulation of snooker. Just roll the red die, then attempt to pot a colour, and continue until all 15 red balls and all colours are potted, or until boredom sets in.

Ancients incorporates everything which was in the earlier games Ancients I and Ancients II (which is why, for instance, there are two scenario booklets).

Ancients is a game of tactical combat in the age before gunpowder. The game contains 64 historical scenarios. Also included are guidelines to match different historical armies against one another, or to build a larger campaign game.

boardgame: Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #9
description:
The Mayfair Games Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #9 is composed of 2 expansions:

Patrician: The Messengers (part 1)
The patricians have messengers to stay informed about the activities of the others. By that they can achieve additional prestige points or lose some.

Hot Tin Roof: Deluxe Catwalks
Adds 4 tiles and the following rules to the base game:

1. During setup, each player must take 1 deluxe catwalk in his own color.
2. As soon as ALL players have earned at least 1 fish tile from the fish market, you may place your deluxe catwalk instead of 1 of your regular catwalks. Any cat that crosses a deluxe catwalk must pay the owner 2 instead of the usual 1.

This is available in Game Trade Magazine (Issue 174 - Aug 2014).

A mini-expansion of 18 (or 12 ... see NOTE further below) artifacts for Fantastiqa.

These artifacts can be used to expand your Fantastiqa game.

How they work: At the beginning of the game, shuffle all Audacious Artifact cards into the Artifacts deck. As always, include all and only those artifact cards you enjoy playing with - that includes including or removing cards from the base set as well. You might want to change the cards you include each game.

The artifacts included in this mini-expansion include:


     2 x Cassens’ Uncanny Clasp of Unfastening (Cost: 4 Gems): Choose one card from your hand or discard pile and tuck it part-way beneath your current Region. A player who ends his turn in this Region must add this card to his discard pile.
     2 x The Complete Troll-Gurgles of Beng Erber (Cost: 3 Gems): Discard as many cards from your hand as you wish, and then draw that many more again.
     2 x Dozer’s Deranged Digging Stick (Cost: 4 Gems): Draw five cards from your draw deck and keep two. Discard the others.
     2 x Paco’s Paranormal Pencilbox (Cost: 4 Gems): Draw one Beast card and add it to your hand.
     2 x The Summerer’s Guide to Winter Wonderland (Cost: 3 Gems): If you are in the Frozen Wastelands, draw one Artifact or one Beast card and add it to your hand.
     2 x Tox’s Ill-Tempered Tortoise of Directed Rigor Mortis (Cost: 4 Gems): Choose one card in your hand or discard pile and remove it from play.
     2 x Vasel’s Disconcerting Dice of Mischievous Melody (Cost: 4 Gems): Add an additional +1 Point Bonus Token to any Open Quest. (Limit: 3 Tokens total on any Open Quest.)
     2 x Ender’s Tollbooth of Never-Ending Adventure (Cost: 4 Gems): Travel to the Region most distant from your current location. (If more than one Region ties, choose either one.)
     2 x The Ever-Clucking Chicken Cup of Rahdo’s Bravado (Cost: 4 Gems): Subdue any adjacent single-symbol Creature card and then travel to the Region just beyond it.


NOTE:
If you purchased this pack from the BoardGameGeek store prior to January 2016, you only received the following 12 cards:

     2 x Cassens’ Uncanny Clasp of Unfastening
     2 x The Complete Troll-Gurgles of Beng Erber
     2 x Dozer’s Deranged Digging Stick
     2 x Paco’s Paranormal Pencilbox
     2 x The Summerer’s Guide to Winter Wonderland
     2 x Tox’s Ill-Tempered Tortoise of Directed Rigor Mortis

Hedron is a tiny epic space battle that combines abstract strategy with thematic gameplay and dynamic mechanics. The game uses polyhedral gaming dice to represent factions of warring energy beings. The core mechanic of the game has players make a single move each turn which they can build into a chain of movement allowing you to move your fleet across the battlefield. Players will also gain access to dynamic powers through flux cards, allowing them to use their fallen warriors to fuel more powerful attacks. Players will gain victory by showing they’re the most powerful set of hedrons in the whole sector through dominating areas of space and destroying your opponent’s hedrons.

Throughout gameplay, players are faced with balancing their current options and opportunities with the need to gain resources and maintain a strong board presence. Is it better to use this hedron as a warrior on the board, or better to keep it for dynamic attacks? At the end of the game, players will add their total energy on the board to their points total to determine the final victor.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Stars are born and die. Great empires rise and fall. Does your civilization have what it takes to stand the test of time?

Tiny Little Empires is a fast card game, easy to pick up and have fun with friends.

The game is played over a series of turns in which each player lays down cards in front of them. These cards have special abilities, bonuses and victory points that make the game unique each time.

The goal of the game is to have the most victory points at the end  of the game.

Small limited edition of Trivial Pursuit sold with a french TV magazine

Categories:
 blue: geography
 pink: entertainment
 yellow: history
 purple: arts and literature
 green: science and nature
 orange: sports and recreation

A simple Barbie themed matching game. Players turn over the top card of their stack of cards and the first person to call out "Gems!" wins the two piles of cards under the matching cards. Play continues until one person is left with all the cards.

Classic UNO with Phineas and Ferb artwork on the cards.

WHAT MAKES IT DIFFERENT FROM TRADITIONAL UNO:

Agent P Card - Play Agent P Card whenever someone plays a Draw 2 or Wild Draw 4 on you and that person must draw the designated amount of cards from the discard pile.  The Agent P Card is also a wild card where you choose the color that resumes play.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Doozy is a life-sized board game that encourages players to move, interact, and create with one another--away from a screen. It's a great way to meet strangers, get to know new friends, or just be silly with old friends! You can play Doozy outdoors, at a party, or for your office icebreaker!

The gameplay is simple. You take the circular spaces and create any closed loop of your choice. You roll the die. You go to the next space of that color. You draw a card, and you do what it tells you to do! The goal is to go all the way around the closed loop.

boardgame: 10 Die Survival Games Public Domain Promo Cards
description:
From publisher blurb:

These are 6 exclusive cards with recognizable horror icons from public domain properties; 4 from the tales of H.P. Lovecraft and 2 from the movie Manos: The Hands of Fate.

Death Ride Kursk - Leibstandarte is an expansion game for Death Ride Kursk - Totenkopf.  This game focuses on the battle for the actual advance on Prokhorovka and the Red Army counter-attack that hit it square on the head during 12 July 1943.

The game contains a supplemental rule book with some specific items related to the Leibstandarte's operation the base game is simply an extension of the base rules in Totenkopf.  Units are modeled as German Platoons or Companies and Red Army Companies.  The game includes the components for the Command and Control and Fire Support Enhancements.  These make the game very interested and give you a real feel for the problems of ensuring your radio line of sight for communications are well sited.

There are extensive obstacles to overcome and combat engineering is well modeled.  There are rules covering all the Battlefield Operating Systems and they integrate well together forcing players to make critical decisions that will effect the outcome of the battle and of each smaller engagement.

This game comes with 5 daily scenarios for 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13 July and 2 small scenarios that use extremely small footprints and can be played in an afternoon sitting or two.  If you already own Totenkopf then you will be able connect this game with it and play the battle from the Psel crossing to the town of Prokhorovka.  You'll experience nothing like it anywhere else.

Magical Square is a 'paper and pencil’ puzzle game started from the 'Match 3' games.

Each player takes a sheet with a 7x7 table and plays the game simultaneously. The game master draws and reveals 3 number cards from the deck, then each player records them in order. After that, players can check 3 or more numbers which are same or continuous in a row horizontally, or vertically, and gain some points according to the quantity of the numbers he/she checked. The player checked numbers take a bonus number, then gets a chance of additional check. When a player checks fixed times, he/she gain some special ability which would be useful for the game. The game ends when every player couldn't record the numbers, then the player with the most points wins.

Compatible Boards/Tiles for the Game "HEROES OF NORMANDIE"

New Boards / Tiles compatible 
to add new grounds to originals, and so to increase the possibilities of scenarios.

In Bake the Cake!, the players both cooperate and compete to bake the best cake. Players score by buying ingredients, preparing parts and baking cakes. Although the cakes belong to specific players, the players may freely use each others' ingredients and parts to bake them. All players participating in a cake will score but only when it is completed and only if the cake wins the final contest.

Bake the Cake! is not about baking your own cake first but rather contribute most to the winning cake. To stop other players, you may take the ingredients they want, use the kitchen tools they need and even eat their ingredients (but not too many or you will get a stomache ache).

The gameplay is quick and intensive as the players fight to get hold of the ingredients and the tools, not unlike what it would be in a real kitchen with too many bakers. Behind the playful theme hides a tactical game where each player must play her cards well to spread her colors to all cakes and at the same get as many other players to them as possible. Please don't try the game recipes at home and don't eat the winning cake!

Game meachanisms include:

Economy: The players use ingredients and tools to accomplish their objective. 
Interdependence: The players compete about the same resources but may also earn points for each other. 
Take that: Players may interfere each other by blocking tools or eating ingredients. 
Balanced victory condition: To win, a player must ensure that other players score as well.

"This action packed soccer card game is the next best thing to being on the field!  It's a race to be the first to assemble your winner soccer team. Recruits and Substitutions are allowed.  Watch out for penalty calls! Fun for the entire family!" -- From The Manufacturer

Using a card drawing mechanic, players attempt to complete their "team,"by assembling player cards for each position (of the same color). Different cards represent the different positions -- Forward, Midfield, Defensive Line, and Goalie, as well as special cards which cause different game effects, and wild cards which can replace other player cards on your "team."

From the publisher:

Shelter is a hybrid of card-duelling games and the castle defence genre. Can you survive against the zombie hordes?

Fight for your life against wave after wave of zombies. Place, modify and use guns! Reinforce your defences! Fight hand to hand! Shelter combines that strategic gameplay of card-duelling games with a simple dice roll based enemy to create a unique single-player experience.

Based on the hugely popular Android, iOS and Windows Phone game, Shelter will provide you with hours of exciting play.

From the developer's website:

Shelter is a single player game where you build a deck of duelling cards and take them into battle against a zombie deck that “plays itself”. The zombie deck is not controlled by a hideously complex AI, but plays based on a simple set of rules and dice rolls for that element of chance. You must fight off the waves of zombies before they smash your shelter’s barricades and start munching on your brains.

You choose your survival strategy when you build your deck. Do you pack as many guns into your deck as you can? Do you use traps and reinforce your defences? Do you stun stronger zombies and pick off the weak ones? You have the freedom to play as you want.

As you win duels, you will be rewarded with new cards to work into your deck and streamline your play style.

Sex Toy Poker is a simple bluffing game for adults only. The game contains 80 cards: 8 copies of 10 different sex toys.

1. Anal Beads
2. Dildo
3. Strap-On
4. Nipple Clamps
5. Penis Sleeve
6. Ben Wah Balls
7. Fisting Gloves
8. Auto Suck
9. Butt Plug
10. Cock Ring

On a turn, a player will pass one sex toy card face down to another player and either tell the truth and proclaim the toy being passed or lie and claim a different sex toy.

The player being passed to may agree or challenge the player passing the card. If correct, the player passing the card loses and must place it face up. If incorrect, the player being passed to must place it face up.
A player may also choose to not guess and pass it to a player who has not yet seen the card.

If a player gets 3 of the same sex toy face up, they lose and should be referred to as "Butt Plug" or "Mr. Plug" in mixed company, until another player loses and claims the title.

A player can win the game if he or she gets 7 different sex toys face up without getting 3 of the same type.

There is either 1 winner or 1 loser in a game of Sex Toy Poker.

A collectible card game based on the international Discworld Conventions.

There are three main game types playable:

1) A top-trumps style game based in various people associated with the convention.

2) A short game where players attempt to run the best single event.

3) A long game where players try and run the best events as the convention timetable progresses.

In game types 2 and 3. Players build a deck consisting of event, role and modifier cards. Players in turn play events based on timetable slots, and then play roles and modifiers to improve or disrupt the events. Once all events are played (or the last event is played) players add up their score; the player with the most points wins.

Adds 4 tiles and the following rules to the base game:

1. During setup, each player must take 1 deluxe catwalk in his own color.
2. As soon as ALL players have earned at least 1 fish tile from the fish market, you may place your deluxe catwalk instead of one of your regular catwalks. Any cat that crosses a deluxe catwalk must pay the owner 2 instead of the usual 1.

Interrupt is a 2-7 player connection-style game using differently shaped 3d wooden blocks. The objective of the game is to create a continuous connection with one set of blocks that spans the length of the game board.

The game board is a modular square; prior to the game players place "terrain" upon the board using neutral blocks. Each player then must decide how to bridge and/or tunnel through pieces to reach the other side. Once per turn, a player may either place a new piece into play or move one of their existing pieces on the board, as long as it isn't trapped by an opponent's piece. Alternatively, players can use a small selection of "wildcard" pieces that provide a connection for all the players.

A game is won once the first person successfully bridges the board with no edge connections between pieces.

Multiple rule variants possible - this is a family of games about building geometric connections in 3d space.

In Find the Treasure!, the players take on the roles of pirates searching a secret treasure. There are nine possible places on the island but each map only shows what is hidden along a specific longitude or latitude. To dig randomly is risky as each place may contain unknown dangers. Instead, the players must challenge each other for the maps or watch where the others dig and then steal whatever they find. To win Find the Treasure!, you must find out the other players' clues while guarding your own, lure them into digging where there are no diamonds and escape from the island before they realize that you have the treasure.

The gameplay is tense and interactive as the players struggle to seek through the island with one eye and watching the other players' hunt with the other. As more and more of the island's secrets are dug up, the pirates will close in on each other, some eager to fight, others to escape, and tactics and deduction rather than brute force will be decisive. May the most ruthless pirat win!

Game mechanisms include:

1. Changing and hidden game board: The island is different in each game and the location of the treasure hidden to the players. 
2. Player balance: The players have similar resources (1 pirate and 1 ship) and are always equally strong. 
3. Deduction: By acquiring maps, the players can deduce where the treasure is located. 
4. Hidden score: The players do not know what the others have found.

In the swamp, the hungry crocodiles wait for fat booty. Totally motionless, they observe their surroundings and wait for something tasty to swim past their nose. Is the right moment there, they bite quickly! But not everything that ends up between their teeth, is really a treat. Those who manage to get the most food at the end, win the game.

The cards show either one, two or three animals or an alarm clock. They come in four colours, which are also found on the board. The players look at the three cards, to that they know where the most points can be found. Then, a spinner is turned. Depending on how much luck you want to introduce in the game, it is spun fast or slow.

When a player believes that the colour of a card he wants to gain is under his mouth, he pushes the head of the crocodile down, stopping the spinner. Now is checked, what the each player, not only the one who stopped the disc, touches with his alligator mouth.

If a crocodile touches a colour from the available cards, that player takes all those cards. If the crocodile touches nothing or a colour that is unavailable, he gains nothing.

Then, the cards are replenished, the crocodiles are placed back in their starting positions, and a new round begins.

Original German description:

Im Sumpf lauern die hungrigen Krokodile auf fette Beute. Völlig reglos beobachten sie ihre Umgebung und warten darauf, dass etwas Köstliches an ihrer Nase vorbei schwimmt. Ist der richtige Moment gekommen, schnappen sie schnell zu! Doch nicht alles, was mit einem Happs zwischen ihren Zähnen landet, ist auch wirklich ein Leckerbissen. Wer es schafft, gezielt zuzubeißen und am Ende die meisten Häppchen hat, gewinnt das Spiel.

Die Karten zeigen entweder ein, zwei oder drei Tiere oder einen Wecker. Das alles gibt es in den vier Farben, die auch auf dem Spielplan zu finden sind. Die Spieler schauen sich die drei Karten an, damit sie wissen, wo sie den Kreisel am besten stoppen müssen, um Punkte zu bekommen. Dann wird der Kreisel gedreht. Je nachdem, wieviel Zufall man im Spiel haben möchte, dreht man den Kreisel schnell oder langsam. Wenn ein Spieler glaubt, dass die Farbe einer Karte, die er haben möchte unter seinem Maul ist, drückt er den Kopf des Krokodils schnell nach unten und stoppt so die Strudelscheibe. Jetzt wird überprüft, was die einzelnen Spieler, nicht nur der, der die Scheibe angehalten hat, mit seinem Krokodilmaul berührt. Berührt ein Krokodilmaul eine Farbe, von der auch Karten offen ausliegen, so nimmt der Spieler alle davon an sich. Zeigt sein Maul auf eine Farbe, zu der es keine Karte gibt, oder ins Leere, so bekommt er eben in dieser Runde nichts. Die Karten werden dann wieder auf drei offene Karten aufgefüllt, die Krokodile wieder in die Ausgangsstellung gekippt und die nächste Runde beginnt.

The Thief Die is an expansion die for Dungeon Dice.  It was previously only available in the Five-Player Booster Pack, but is now available on its own to add to your games of Dungeon Dice!

The Thief has already opened the chest, and is now using its contents against you. When fighting the Thief, you draw a die of  the appropriate type (as shown on the bottom right of the Thief die) and roll it together with the Thiefs level dice. If you defeat him, you get to keep what the Thief tried to steal!

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Fall 2014 Game Night Kit
description:
Game description from the publisher:

In the Fall 2014 Game Night Kit for Descent: Journeys in the Dark Second Edition, you will find a new adventure, Nature’s Ire, enabling one to four players to experience a mini campaign over the course of a single evening.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
You start with 10d6, then draw a card each turn. The cards can be adversaries to battle, lasting effects or one of three items needed to win the game. When you loose a monster battle you loose dice. When you run out of dice you are dead. The trilogy is comprised of three games:

The House of George Walter Michaels
The Tomb of the Damned Pharaoh
The Abyss of the Lurid Cove

The games are best suited for solo play, but they can accommodate up to 6 players.

The game takes place in Valachia in Czech republic. Player becomes chief shepherd and works with his sheep during four seasons.

Each season represents one phase of a round. During spring, players roll dice to move their sheep to grazing lands. During summer season players roll dice to see which areas produce milk (which serves as a currency) or sheep. 
Come autumn, the players have to move sheep back to the shepherd otherwise they freeze to death in winter. During winter the players can also buy more sheep and buildings or sell excess sheep.

The goal of the game is to be the first player to own herd of eight sheep or become the owner of building "fojství".

Place a thief next to the treasure and it's your - Easy! But watch out for the caliph! To win, you'll have to play your card right in this tactical challenge!

Each players is dealt 3 cards, the rest of the cards are placed face down on the table. The top fours card is turned over and put face-up in the middle of the table. The playing area is formed by 4 cards laid out 2x2.

In each round, the player chooses one of the three cards in their hand and places it in the playing area. Then they pick up a new card from the pack.

One card cannot be put next to (vertically or horizontally) a more powerful card: you cannot put a treasure next to a thief, or you cannot put a thief next to a vizier.
If the card put down is adjacent to one or two weaker cards, the players wins the adjacent cards or pile of several cards.

When the pack has all be taken and all cards held have been played, the game is over. The winner is the player who has won the most cards.

The Game of Nancy is a betting, trick taking, and partially co-operative card game played with a seventy-eight card tarot deck. The game is frequently played by people of the video game universe of Dishonored (published by Bethesda Softworks). The official rules are included with the Dishonored Tarot Deck which was included as a pre-order bonus of the video game in some regions.

It is mostly co-operative in so much as it is a turn-based game in which players change teams from round to round based on which player bets they can win the most tricks with the card they have been dealt. The winner of the betting round, or the Taker, then plays against the other players to win as many elected tricks before the round's end. At the conclusion of a round, the Taker's ability to completed win the required tricks is scored and the inverse of the Taker's score is given to each of the remaining players. The player left of the previous round's Taker deals a new round and bidding begins once again.

The Game of Nancy has no set winning end score and thus, duration is unknown. The rules state that a target score should be set, and agreed upon, prior to game commencement.

Sometimes when a family moves into a new house, it's not the best place to live. It does, however, turn out to be the best place to stay forever...

Darkness Comes is a co-operative board game that tells a story of a family trying to escape from a haunted house. The game begins quickly. The set-up is gamified as the players use their cards to create the initial situation on the board. They decide which areas are haunted and which doors are closed, how much time they need to escape, where they can find useful items, what they should do before leaving the place. Every card they play increases the tension, and strange things start to happen: Menacing shadows gather, and family members' faces disappear from photographs. All these events prepare us for the forthcoming nightmare.

Then the main phase of the game begins. The house tries to ensnare the characters inside or kill them. Trying to find their way out, the family members encounter many kinds of horrors. Every time it happens, they decide whether to strengthen the house, hurt themselves, or change their plans.

Characters co-operate and support each other by transferring love points — but sometimes they have to risk their sanity to survive. And sometimes they lose their minds. That's why the rules of the game can change. The possessed character may decide to side with the sinister house. It comes as no surprise for horror fans when the father of the family gets mad and finds the axe!

Chance and Strategy for the whole family!

This game is about trying to place more money in your bank than your opponents!

Rules are simple and the game is easy to play. At the same time, game is fun to play over and over.


     The Thief steal from one's bank.
     The Banker steal cards from one's hand.
     Exchange hands if you dare!
     If you have the King in your hand, stop your attacker! Unless he has a King too!


Strategically play your Action Cards or your Coin Cards! Make sure to be the last player to play!

Igramul the Banisher
Cost: 3 Crystals
Prestige Value: 7
Come-Into-Play Effect: Name a Card: Opponents reveal their hands and discard all copies of the named card.  If at least one card was discarded, receive the energy present in that card's summoning cost.

This card was created by Federico Latini for a contest by Libellud. It was only included in the first printing of Seasons: Path of Destiny expansion.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Christina Regina is a card game version of Christina Regina - the Board Game. It is a game about politics and intrigues in 17th century Sweden where each player represents a faction with a hidden agenda. The end of their intrigues is the newly appointed queen Christina of Sweden and their means include persuasion, agitation, manipulation and even assassination. They achieve this by carefully steering the queen towards influence of their own faction and away from influence of other factions. At the same time, they must closely monitor the other players' actions to guess their agendas. But beware, if your own agenda is too obvious, you may yourself become the victim of your opponents' intrigues!
 Game mechanisms include:
 1. Card drawing: Cards are drawn or discarded so that each new player in turn is faced with new options. 
2. Asymmetric scoring: There are no "good" or "bad" cards but they all score differently for different players. 
3. Hidden agendas: The players do not know how much the other players score until the very end. 
4. Guessing game: Based on how players play, the other players may gain points by guessing their agendas (or lose points by guessing wrong).

Christina Regina is the third game in the Nova Suecia series.

Match, count, and race your dinosaur to the finish line.

An exciting game where all the dinosaurs move simultaneously towards the finish line. Each turn, a four-arrowed roulette is spun and each of the players has to look at their result and find how many matching dinosaur tokens they have. Then all players move simultaneously their resulting number in the circuit. Afterwards, all the drawn dinosaur tokens are returned to the pool while turning them upside down, so a different pool is configured and next turn can start.

Race for your position on the podium to collect a shiny medal!

boardgame: Het grote erger je wel voetbalspel
description:
During which match did Hans Kraay Jr. throw away his jacket? Which Dutch player was among the top goal scorers during the World Cup 2010?

Those are some of the over 450 questions in this football game. Test your football knowledge in general, but also of events from the very popular football show on Dutch television. Play as Wilfred Genee, René van der Gijp, Johan Derksen or Jan Boskamp and answer questions about Star players, WC football and more. And don't forget to annoy your opponents. Have a good match!

In the deepest depths of the ocean lie scientific discoveries yet to me made, ancient mysteries still to be solved and vast treasures to be gained by those willing to take the greatest risks.  But it will take a brave soul to challenge the crushing pressure and terrors to be found at the bottom of the ocean.

Come aboard the fantastical Nautilus and meet her intrepid crew and the brave Captain Nemo, as they risk their lives for fame, fortune and glory - 20,000 Leagues under the sea.

Bathysphere is a 24 card microgame played across several rounds with each player having an opportunity to be Captain of the Nautilus.

Each turn players simultaneously vote whether to Descend (dive deeper), Ascend (return to the surface) or Abandon Ship! (leave in an escape pod with a small share).  Majority rules.

After the vote the Nautilus dives and may face many dangers or crises by turning over a dive card. The crew may elect to use their skills and fix these crises or let them pass, however, if 3 crises go unresolved, Nautilus is destroyed and all hands lost to the crushing depths. Resolving a crisis places it back in the dive deck, meaning it may resurface again.

The deeper Nautilus and her crew travel, the greater the rewards but the greatest rewards are shared only with those demonstrating the most loyalty to the Captain.

boardgame: Musketeers: The Search for the Queen's Gems
description:
Queen Anne has given her precious gems to The Duke of Buckingham, with whom she is secretly in love.

But Cardinal Richelieu, the most powerful man in France, is aware of the Queen’s affections and has convinced the King to hold gala fete where the Queen may show-off her dazzling gems – a gift from the oblivious King Louis.

In desperation, the Queen calls upon her valiant Musketeers and loyal servants to come to her aid – to find and return the gems to her before Richelieu and his nefarious spies can find them and reveal her to the King.

Musketeers: The Search for the Queen's Gems is a social deduction/bluffing 24-card microgame where 4-8 players assume an alliance on one of two sides – the Cardinalists or the Musketeers, each vying to outwit, outmanoeuvre and out-bluff each other to be the first group to collect together all four of the Queen’s gems and return them safely to the Queen, or to present them to the King.

boardgame: Just Add Ice SportACTION Hockey Card Game
description:
"A fast paced card game that has all the action of real hockey game. It's a race to assemble your team. Player trades and recruits allowed but, watch out for penalties and flying pucks!" -- From The Manufacturer

Using a card drawing mechanic, players attempt to complete their "team,"by assembling player cards for each position (of the same color). Different cards represent the different positions -- Offense, Defense, and Goalie, as well as special cards which cause different game effects, and wild cards which can replace other player cards on your "team."

boardgame: Rally Point Volume 8: Sons of the Rising Sun
description: A special study of the Pacific Theater of Operations. It includes 10 new scenarios from the Tampa ASL Group. This special study of the PTO is intended to compliment the ASL module Rising Sun by providing players with tournament-level scenarios designed with the modern-era style of ASL play in mind.

From the box: "Enter the world of the Twilight Saga with this collection of games based on the epic movie series. The more you know about Bella, Edward, Jacob, the Saga movies & your friends, the better your chance of winning. Combine all three games for the ultimate Twilight Saga experience."

Borscht! is a set collection and strategy card game for 3-4 players ages 8 and up.

Players become Cossack peasants competing to complete a pot of traditional borscht (beet soup) while fending off the marauding tartars and sandy mole rats. In this society, economy is king--the Cossack with the cheapest borscht wins.

Game components include 101 Borscht! cards, 4 action and recipe cards,4 player identification cards, 20 coins in an old sock, and a six-sided die.

Each character identity has special abilities (such as the "Baboushka" (wise woman) whose negotiating skills allow her to buy commodities below market value. After all players have used their identities, the cards are rotated, creating a new balance of power in the game.

This game fuses the game Blue and Gray with checkers.  In it, players attempt to navigate their Captain piece to the middle, moving along a winding road on the board to be the first to get their Captain to the middle of the board.  In addition, players can promote their Private pieces to Sergeants, which adds backwards mobility to a Private.

Edit: Playable here: Playable on Tabletopia in this collection:
https://tabletopia.com/games/alquerque-and-friends

The grand feast for the nest is tonight! A group of fancy rats are sent out into town to scavenge for the ingredients that are needed to make the feast a success. However, a cat and a ratcatcher are looking to catch the rats. Each rat has a special ability that can help ensure victory. With a little luck and some skill, the rats will find what they need in town, all the while wearing dapper hats, of course!

Players, as rats, collect Food cards to place in their buffets to complete Entrees given to them at the start of the game. To collect Food cards, players roll a die and move into different locations, such as a bakery or a butcher shop. There are five different types of food: Bread, Fruit, Meat, Vegetable, and Cheese. The different locations offer these types of Food. For example, when a rat enters the Cheese Shop, he or she draws a card from the Cheese deck. Or, when a rat enters the Pastry Shop, he or she draws a card from either the Bread deck OR the Fruit deck.

Then, the rats add cards to their buffets from their hands, but only one at a time. After that, the player switches into the role of the cat and/or ratcatcher. He or she rolls the Enemy die and moves the cat and/or ratcatcher accordingly. When the cat enters the same tile as a rat, he or she must discard a card in hand. When the ratcatcher enters the same tile as a rat, he or she must lose a card from his or her buffet. So, players can use the cat and ratcatcher to keep other rats in check if a player is getting too far ahead.

The game moves to a final turn when a player has ten Food cards in his or her buffet OR when any Food deck runs out of cards. Then, the player with the most points, when adding up all of his or her Entrees and Food cards, wins the game.

Knights & Damosels takes you back to the Age of Chivalry and the intricate code of conduct between the King's men and the ladies of the court. In the game, the players play both knights and damosels from the Arthurian legend - each with his or her different objectives. As a knight, compete with the other knights to become the most noble of them all or cooperate in quests and battles to earn glory in the eyes of King Arthur. As a damosel, provide another knight with the arms and virtues he needs until he is wrapped around your finger. But beware, if the Land falls you will all fall with it!

Game mechanisms include:
 
     Double deck building: The players build decks in two ways, different types (knights) or similar colors (damosels)
     Hidden information: The damosel do not build their own deck but try to get cards of their (hidden) color to the same knight
     Card placement: The players place cards to earn points but may only place cards not already placed by another player
     Simultaneous actions: The success of an action depends on a player's ability to predict the card played of another player
     Cooperation: The players fight wars and obtain quests together.
     Prisoners' dilemma: One player may benefit by not cooperating but if all do so, all will lose.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
A bluffing game where the aim is collect the most maki and sushi possible by bluffing.

Contents: 18 Fish cards numbered 0-3
Rule cards for 10 languages.
21 Maki tokens.
11 Sushi tokens.

The deck of cards are shuffled and the first player draws the top three cards and secretly
announces a score lower than or equal to 9.
There are then the 2 possibilities.

A.The following player believes the announced score and takes the cards, whilst the first player wins a maki tile.The following player then draws the top card from the draw deck and announces a score still lower or equal to 9.

B.The following player does not believe the score announced.
If the first players announced score was right he shows the cards, puts them beneath the draw deck and takes a maki tile. The following player then misses a turn.
The next player then draws 3 cards from the deck and play starts again.

If a player announces 9 without bluffing he wins 2 Maki tiles.

When a player has 5 maki tiles, he exchanges them for 1 sushi Tile.

Depending on the players and time available the winner of the game is the first to obtain 1 or 2 Sushi tiles.

Inspired by Perudo

boardgame: Patton's Third Army: Spearhead of Victory
description:
Patton’s Third Army is a solitaire game system where the player takes command of General George Patton’s Third Army during the campaign across France in 1944. The game begins immediately following the Falaise Battle and continues through to 15 December, at which point Third Army was sent to fight In the Battle of the Bulge. Your goal as a player is to surpass Patton’s original record, thereby possibly forestalling Germany’s Ardennes Offensive and thereby gaining glory as a great general.

In the game, you control US and other Allied units assigned to the Third Army. The game system plays the Germans. You will have to deal with a wide variety of situations, from battling against fierce panzer counterattacks, to figuring out ways to extend your lines of supply. Your objective: Germany!

The game has two levels: the Standard Rules, which gives a quick wargame, and the Optional Rules, which give more realism for more complexity.

While Patton’s Third Army is a solitaire game, multiple players can play as the US team, making decisions by consensus, or each managing operations along one of the “Axes of Advance”. However, one player should always be the Commander of Third Army, and his decision is final!

description:
A box you say? Yes, an official box for your Meepile!
Since the first edition of Meepile did not come in a box, we decided to create one. Yes, we've created a nice little home for your beloved Meepile. Now your Meepile no longer needs to be dusted before play, and can rest snuggly on the shelf, between your other Simi Game Knights small box games.

While we are at it, we might as well add some bits. Because you can never have too many bits.

Oh, and a few cards, because there was still a little room in the box.

How about a new way to play? Because you can never have too many ways to play with your Meepile.

Bits - 
Each player has a collection of 6 interestingly shaped wooden bits to place on the Meepile. Each player also is given a deck of 6 cards that represents each of their 6 wooden bits. The cards are shuffled and on a player's turn one is revealed. The player then is required to place that piece.

As usual, all pieces knocked off of the Meepile are gathered into your stockpile, so if you turn over a card and you have more than one of the matching shaped bits, you may attempt to place all of them. Work your way through the 6 cards and then shuffle and start again, until someone has placed all of his/her stockpile and wins the round.

Additional way to use these bits and cards are included in the game box.

An Old Dwarven Dice Game for 1 to a lot of players.

You are a dwarf and you are digging a hole. You want to gather up all the precious ores and gems without blowing yourself up (too much) or hitting a lava stream.

You start your digging with 0 points and 5 lives.

Roll 6 Dice 3 times. On each roll you can keep some, none or all of the dice 
After the third roll you tally up your score.

Emeralds 10 points 
Diamonds 5 points 
Coal 2 points 
Lapis 1/2 point

TNT: for each TNT loose one life but gain 10 points 
(you blew up good but you also dislodged some precious gems)

Lava: for each Lava destroy the highest scoring Ore or Gem

If you loose all of your lives your score is reset to 0 and you are given 3 lives. 
If you loose all your lives after that your score is reset to 0 and you are given 1 life.

The first digger to 300 wins.

Both the PrintAndPlay and Tabletop Simulator versions of Diggy Diggy Dice are licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

In each round of play, one player is considered to be the Judge. The Judge draws a Battlefield card, that determines where the battles will take place. The other players split into teams, and play Warrior cards from their hand onto the table. Then the battle commences, with players on each team using their imagination to tell the Judge why their warriors would win in a fight against their opponents, or how they would defend against their opponents' attacks. Examples from the rulebook:

"Police Dog reads you your rights!"

The Judge is the final arbiter of what is and isn't permissible, and also decides any challenges. Players can also use Item cards from their hand to add more collateral to the fight ("Alien uses this Jetpack to escape!"). When one team is completely defeated, or when the Judge is ready to rule in one side's favor, the round is over. Winning teams keep their used Warriors as victory cards. The game ends when everyone has had a chance to be the Judge once or twice (depending on the number of players), and the teams with the most victory cards is the winner.

description:
The power of games to teach abstract concepts is more and more acknowledged. Find the Bug! offers an introduction to IT testing through a fast and simple board game.

The application is simulated by a game board divided into a number of components. Each component has a unique number of bugs with a unique level of severity. The test leads take turn to analyse the application (to learn where bugs are likely to find) and to place testers in the application (to find those bugs). Each bug scores a value according to its severity level.

Once a set of components is perceived to be bug free, a test lead may let a tester cover the entire set and thus increase the probability of finding bugs in the next round. However, if the test lead is wrong, the tester's effort is lost so don't be too fast!

The winner is the one who finds most bugs.

Find the Bug! is mainly designed for IT professionals who want to learn testing in a fun way. However, ordinary gamers may also enjoy this deductive challenge where you must use your limited resources efficiently and monitor the other player's actions carefully to Find the Bug!

Game mechanisms include:

1. Changing and hidden game board: The number, value and location of the bugs is different in each game and they are hidden to the players.
2. Player balance: The players have the same amount of testers and are always equally strong.
3. Predictable odds: To find a bug requires luck but the more pre-analysis a player does, the better are the odds for finding them.
4. Open end: The potentially high end game score makes every game open until the very end.
5. Educational: The game teaches IT testing in a game context

Carcassonne is a tile-placement game in which the players draw and place a tile with a piece of southern French landscape on it. The tile might feature a city, a road, a cloister, grassland or some combination thereof, and it must be placed adjacent to tiles that have already been played, in such a way that cities are connected to cities, roads to roads, etcetera. Having placed a tile, the player can then decide to place one of his meeples on one of the areas on it: on the city as a knight, on the road as a robber, on a cloister as a monk, or on the grass as a farmer. When that area is complete, that meeple scores points for its owner.

During a game of Carcassonne, players are faced with decisions like: "Is it really worth putting my last meeple there?" or "Should I use this tile to expand my city, or should I place it near my opponent instead, giving him a hard time to complete his project and score points?" Since players place only one tile and have the option to place one meeple on it, turns proceed quickly even if it is a game full of options and possibilities.

This Big Box is the same size as other Big Boxes, but with a slightly different component mix. This big box includes the basic game, Inns & Cathedrals, Traders & Builders, Wheel of Fortune, Hills & Sheep and The River. It also includes two extra sets of meeples - one pink and one purple.

description:
A 2025 renewed version of Snow the Savior Promotional Expansion, Snow the Savior is now a Legendary Character!

Snow uniquely designed box includes:

4 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base

Install guide

Legendary characters are born with a story to tell. Included character cards foretell a journey on a grand scale. Use only one legendary character card per campaign. This character's story unfolds throughout their lifetime.

When Snow was born she cried out, inconsolable and sleepless. In the hovel, the lanterns dimmed and the baby fell asleep. She dreamed only once as a baby and woke up a ruddy-faced youth. Clutching a staff bedecked with bright ribbons appearing from out of nowhere. Her feet black and tough from an unseen journey. When she awoke she was possessed of strange wisdom beyond her lantern years. When next she slept, she awoke a warrior in her prime, lean with hunger and robust with experience, clutching the staff topped with a vicious stone. When she awoke again she was in the full bloom of her power, hair streaked with grey, possessed of the power to fulfil the dreams of her settlement, her tenuous existence anchored to the settlement that bore her. Finally, she awoke, gnarled and papery, a husk whose substance lives vividly in another place.

Scary Cape Creatures is a card game for 2-4 players, ages 8 to adult. The object of the game is to score the most points after every player's hand is empty.

Each player uses the same deck, which is separated into four suits that consist of cards representing various monsters and legends from the Cape Cod area. Each player selects all the cards from a given suit, or all the cards from 2 suits for a 2-player game.

Cards have a symbol in each corner, representing seasons and elements. Points are scored by placing cards with matching symbols beside cards currently in play. Cards also have numerical values that affect scoring and gameplay. Each suit also has special characteristics or powers that can be used to affect gameplay.

From the publisher:

A rollickin' fun card game using 52 of Cape Cod's spookiest characters.

Gather around the campfire with this game of strategy using Cape Cod's spookiest entities as pawns - altogether 52 of the peninsula's scariest and most infamous pirates, ghosts, witches and indigenous spirits, calling upon their mastery of the fourfold powers of nature to defeat your opponents(s). Boldness, skill, and timing are needed to enter a mysterious realm populated by a host of weird creatures drawn from the Cape's eerie legends, history, and lore.

Um Himmels Willen (For Heaven's Sake) is the game of the crazy cards. The rules are simple: Play a card, comply the task and score the points.  There are three kinds of tasks:
 *A trivia question: Ask the question to another player. If his answer is correct, both you and that player gain points.
 *A puzzle: Make a certain card combination to score points.
 *A dexterity challenge: Complete some action that requires balance or sleight of hand.

Based on a unique concept, Um Himmels Willen garantees fun, tension and variety for gamers of all ages.

Um Himmels Willen is very interactive and contains also a special variant for Teams.

Original description (German): 
Tante Lise hat gerade ein Farbenpaar ausgespielt, Oma Frieda der kleinen Hanna eine Quizfrage gestellt und Papa Ferdi versucht 10 Karten auf seinem Körper zu verteilen, während er eine Spielkarte auf seinem Kopf balanciert.

Um Himmels Willen ist das Spiel der verrückten Karten. Denkbar einfach sind dabei die Regeln. Karte spielen, Aufgabe erfüllen und Punkte kassieren. Aus drei Kategorien können die Spieler eine Aufgabe wählen: Quizfragen, Knobel- und Geschicklichkeitsaufgaben.

Eine Quizfrage stellt man einem beliebigen Mitspieler. Kann dieser die Frage richtig beantworten, bekommt auch der Fragensteller Punkte. Man muss also kein Quizmaster sein, um Punkte zu bekommen. Die Mitspieler richtig einschätzen zu können, ist viel wichtiger. Bei den Knobelaufgaben gilt es Kombinationen mit den verschiedenen Eigenschaften der Karten zu bilden: Farbe, Buchstabe oder Zahl zu Drillingen, Wörtern oder Straßen formen und Punkte kassieren. Die Geschicklichkeitsaufgaben fordern Balance, Fingerfertigkeit und Spontanität von den Spielern. Aber auch eine gute Beobachtungsgabe kann hilfreich sein um Punkte zu machen, gibt es ja noch diese „Geheim“-Karten. Passt man nicht gut auf, landet so eine fiese Karte schnell auf dem eigenen Punktestapel. Oder man verpasst eine Blitzaufgabe und kassiert Minuspunkte.

Interaktivität wird bei „Um Himmels Willen“ groß geschrieben. Ein Großteil der Aufgaben bindet mehrere Spieler mit ein. Jeder kann immer mitmachen, ist aber nicht gezwungen dazu.

Durch das einzigartige Konzept wird für alle Altersgruppen Abwechslung, Spaß und Spannung geboten.

Für 3-6 Spieler, ab 8 Jahren.

Eine spezielle Team/Familien-Variante ermöglicht es auch größeren Gruppen Freude an "Um Himmels Willen" zu haben.

Für 2-6 Teams, ab 8 Jahren.
