description: Munchkin Apocalypse: Judge Dredd is a fifteen-card non-random booster pack expansion for Munchkin Apocalypse and other standalone Munchkin games that includes the title character as well as "a very Munchkin take on playing in Mega-City One", according to the publisher.

boardgame: Munchkin Adventure Time 2: It's a Dungeon Crawl!
description: Munchkin Adventure Time 2: It's a Dungeon Crawl! is a small boxed expansion for Munchkin Adventure Time and other standalone Munchkin games.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Munchkin Love Shark Baby is a Valentine's Day-themed fifteen-card non-random booster pack for any standalone Munchkin game.

The card backs are the same theme as Munchkin, but the fronts are various shades of pink. It consists of 7 Doors and 8 Treasures.

boardgame: The Rumplebum Academy for Bug-Based Baking
description:
Welcome to the Rumplebum Academy for Bug-Based Baking! You have enrolled at the finest culinary school for young Trolls in all the Kingdoms! Under the guidance of Chef Cordelia Rumplebum, you will be taught the fine art of baking delicious foods with only the finest ingredients: crunchy, juicy, savory bugs!

The Rumplebum Academy for Bug-Based Baking is a lighthearted memory and set-collection game. The players are trolls who are going out into the forest to turn over rocks, looking for bugs. They'll use those bugs in the recipes they bake back at the school. But they'd better be careful, because sometimes they'll turn over a Knight who might attack! Then again, they might find a beautiful Princess to help with the next dish.

From the box: The Baseball Game is a computer generated, mathematically pure game that enables you to replay Big League baseball games by transferring the performance records of Big League players from the playing field to playing cards.

Each Pitcher's card is divided into strikes, balls or spaces where the Batter hits the ball.  Each Batter's card is divided into four columns that match the Ground Ball, Fly Ball, Line Drive and Long Drive spaces on the Pitcher's card.  Simply go to the matching column on the Batter's card and roll the dice to find if the Batter is out, hit safely or reached base on an error.  After the Batter hits the ball, each play is completed by referring to the simplified Fielding and Base Running Charts on the playing board.

This game is licensed by Little League Baseball.

Shinkukan is a deck building, head-to-head collectible card game, akin to Magic or Pokemon. With the world, art, and visual designs created by artist John Hathway, and game system designer Takashi Sakaue, this is JH Lab's latest attempt to expand "true understanding" of otaku culture.

The game is set in a possible future world where science and spirituality have merged, causing a certain regression of technology. Mainly, it is found that spirits could be contained in now-defunct vacuum tubes, which could in turn be used to power various robots and androids. Human beings could transfer their consciousness into androids, and man-made androids could develop human intelligence. The world has embraced this discovery, doing away with any national, religious or ethnic segregation. Instead, people are identified by their intelligence.

The game utilizes 4 different types of cards. 1, The Android Cards are your main cards which are built up and strengthened by the other cards. 2, The Resource Cards strengthen the androids. 3, The Unique Resrouce Cards are one time use cards capable of changing the properties of gameplay. And, 4, Assist Cards are one time use robots that have the ability to shift the flow of battle! Using these cards, your goal is either to destroy all your opponent's androids, or completely deplete their main card deck.
Be the first player to connect 4 or more Pipe Cards (5 or more in 2 player game) from the bathtub to the spout (with no clogs).

Promo hero for Boss Monster given out at Gencon 2013 and also distributed through The Tinker's Pack. Proceeds from the Quothe sales went to Heifer International.

The card itself in an homage to Kvothe, the hero of Patrick Rothfuss's Kingkiller Chronicle series.

In MindMaze: Verzwickte Rätsel - Rabenschwarze Geschichten, as in other MindMaze sets, players take turns asking questions to try to solve mysteries, in this case dark, morbid tales.

In general, a referee tells a strange story, then the players must determine why and how the story happened by asking "Yes" or "No" questions, with "Not Important" also being a plausible answer. Players take turns asking questions, with a point being subtracted for each question. Players are awarded points for asking certain key questions that eliminate faux versions of the story. The list of useful key questions is attached to every riddle. This sort of gaming procedure effectively encourages players to develop careful analytical questions and eliminates random questions.

boardgame: Boss Monster: Bom-Boy Factory Promo Card
description:
A boss monster promo card given away at Origins 2014.

Bom-Boy Factory was a preview of Boss Monster 2: The Next Level, and is also included in that game.

The factory is a room that can be added to the deck of rooms for the Boss Monster base game.

Published about the German City of Oberhausen.

By playing the players get to know a little bit about the city and its shops.

During each player's turn they roll a six-sided dice to move along a game-board depicting a map of Bielefeld. At certain spaces players can acquire cards of the shops or they can go sightseeing by following the instructions on the back of the card. Sometimes there is only information written, sometimes you have to pay an amount of money or at other times you may earn some cash.

The game ends when the last player reaches the finish space with his playing piece. The winner is the person who has acquired the most sightseeing and/or shop cards.

During each player's turn they roll a six-sided dice to move along a game-board depicting a map of Bielefeld. At certain spaces players can acquire cards of the shops or they can go sightseeing by following the instructions on the back of the card. Sometimes there is only information written, sometimes you have to pay an amount of money or at other times you may earn some cash.

The game ends when the last player reaches the finish space with his playing piece. The winner is the person who has acquired the most sightseeing and/or shop cards.

By playing the players get to know a little bit about the city and its shops.

During each player's turn they roll a six-sided dice to move along a game-board depicting a map of Bielefeld. At certain spaces players can acquire cards of the shops or they can go sightseeing by following the instructions on the back of the card. Sometimes there is only information written, sometimes you have to pay an amount of money or at other times you may earn some cash.

The game ends when the last player reaches the finish space with his playing piece. The winner is the person who has acquired the most sightseeing and/or shop cards.

Objective: be the first to get the permanent majority of votes for the coming election of the mayor - learn more about the city of Peine
- moving/acting by turns.

Each player rolls the die, moves according the number, follows instructions of space landed on; buys, if possible and/or (preferable) votes.

"Chance" cards are created with circumstances out of the history of Detmold. Correct answers give economic advantages.

Voting process about the next mayor: Each owned company corresponds to a number of votes. The results of each voting are kept and the final winner is the first player who reaches the target.

boardgame: Ice Age Thrills & Spills Adventure Game
description: Join an unlikely group of friends on an exciting quest of ice age adventure. Race against the elements and each other  to see who can return the baby Rodhan home first. pass the baby back and forth with other players while sliding on icy paths dodging lurking predators.

Spice Winds is a card game, where the cards, and the whole table become the board. Players play ship captains and traders in a wild and hardly explored archipelago, exploring and trading between the islands, trying to make their fortunes.

The game is made up from a custom card deck:
Some cards are scattered on the playing surface and become the islands to trade between.
Some form an event deck - controlling random events that may beset players journeying between islands
Others form a marketplace that is used to control the price of goods throughout the game
The remainder are used to record player wealth, ships' cargo or provide player information and determine other events through the game.

Players seek to generate wealth through trade, and convert this into stockpiles of spices ready to ship back to the Old World.  The first to amass a stockpile of 5 chests of spice is the winner.

SuperPower SmackDown is a fast-and-furious dice combat game for two or more super-powered heroes!

In SuperPower SmackDown!, each player rolls a set of 5 themed dice -- you can be a Strength hero, a Speed hero, a Gear hero, a Mind hero, or a Mutation hero -- looking for matching icons. Get at least one match, and you can reroll the unmatched dice. Get all 5 to match, and you can roll again.

Whoever gets the most matches "seizes the initiative" and is on the attack that turn. Everybody else rolls for defense. If you roll too low, you lose a die. Reduce your opponents to a single die, and you win the game!

Each die icon has a special power, and the hero cards will also bear the definition of each power.

Sublimation is a card game that blends familiar elements found in game such as Uno or War and combines them into one entertainingly fun game. Players are dealt a specific number of cards and are placed in a specific arrangement. The first player to get rid of all their cards is the winner, however it's not that easy, players will have to play strategically by setting themselves up for advantageous plays or by setting up their opponents by causing them to pick up extra cards.

The games always start off polite, but as soon as it looks like someone is going to win, the claws come out. Enjoy watching your opponents squirm as you force their hands, and relish in the victory when you triumphantly play your last card.

boardgame: Boss Monster: Killa, Man Eating Gorilla Promo
description:
Promo card given away at Gencon 2014.

Killa is a new Boss to add to the standard selection of bosses for the game, giving more versatility to the boss deck.

This card is a preview for the upcoming Boss Monster 2, but is compatable with the base game.

boardgame: The Pacific War: From Pearl Harbor to Philippine Sea
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Can right strategy alone change history?

Was defeat in the Pacific War inevitable for Japan? Could you set the goals needed to end the war and use the Imperial Japanese Navy in greater effect, which was the strongest in the world at that point, in order to achieve different results? An "if" scenario that history could not forgive, but may be investigated through a simulation game.

[Pacific War] is a strategic level simulation game of the naval operations in the Pacific from 1941 to the first half of 1944. Players become commanders of the Japanese Imperial Navy and the Allied navy, which is centered around the US navy, and have to plan a strategy and execute it in order to win the war.

Even though the rules are simple and cover only 4 pages, a unique gaming system is adopted. Similarly to the actual warfare in order to maximize the efficiency of your aircraft carrier task forces will require experience and a significant amount of trial and error. The designers notes and detailed example inside the provided play book, are designed to help the players get a grasp of the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: A Crowning Mercy: A Solitaire Game of the English Civil Wars (1642-1651)
description:
BACKGROUND: A CROWINING MERCY is semi-historical simulation of the English Civil War. The game begins just after King Charles I dissolved Parliament. The Player represents the Commander of the Parliament Forces with the goal to overthrow King Charles. As the Commander of the Parliament Forces, you must control a significant number of Cities and Towns before the last Campaign Card is Played.

GAME OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the Parliament Forces. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to control at least 16 Cities and 14 Towns before the last Campaign Card is Played. If the Royalist Army controls 16 Cities and 14 Towns at the end of their Turn, the Royalists win.

GAME FEATURES: Game includes many colorful, graphic Counters, a period flavored map, and Campaign Cards. Counters include Pike and Musket, Musketeers, Cavalry, Cuirassier, Cannons and Leaders. The Game is Card Driven where the Player draws a number of Campaign Cards that may be Forces, Actions or Events. The Player then takes his turn carrying out actions with his Leaders and Forces.

GAME MECHANICS: The Player moves his Leader Counters on the Map along with their Troop Points. If a Battle occurs, Troop Points are exchanged for Tactical Units and combat is carried out in a Tactical manner on a Battle Mat. If there are any Reserve Units in the "Play Area", he may add them to a Battle Force.

boardgame: The Game of Where on Earth?
description:
"Find yourself in this game of guesswork!"

The box contains 80 cards, each with the name and brief description of a location, real or fictional.
One player looks at a card, and the player whose turn it is must ask yes or no questions to work out where they are, for example "Am I in a famous building?"

They may guess where they are as many times as they like, but have a 2 minute time limit. If they successfully guess within that time, they keep the card. If not, it is discarded.

The player with the most cards at the end of the agreed number of rounds wins.

"The fast and furious name-guessing game!"

Players divide into two teams, and decide on a time limit for rounds, which adjusts the difficulty.

Each card contains the names of a celebrity, a sportsperson and a historical character, and there is spinner with these on, plus three wilds.

One member of each team takes a card, spins the spinner, and sees who they must describe. They then have to describe that person to their team. If guessed correctly, they gain a point. If not, the card is discarded.

The first team to 15 points wins.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: Commonwealth Army Box
description:
Expansion for the Heroes of Normandie base game. contains:

- Three new units: Suffolk Regiment (Rifle platoon), Regiment de la Chaudiere (Weapon platoon) and 4th County of London Yeomanry (Tank platoon).
- High command: Artillery, airstrike, smoke, directives and more.
- Field command and additional recruitment options.
- Three new heroes.
- 14 wooden order tokens for the Commonwealth army.
- 76 Action cards.
- New rules, including Woodsmen, Winnie, Urban fighter, Knife, Arrow and Bagpipes!
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Windscorers Doubles is an expansion for Windscorers.

This expansions adds:

    The doubles match mode
    6 new characters 
    A biggest court 
    More maneuver cards
    A new maneuver card; the pass. With the pass card you can pass the frisbee to the other character of your team.

Explorers of the Lower Vistula region is a family and educational board game for 2-4 people. In the game the players are put in the roles of travellers who step by step discover and combine in pairs the most interesting and and the most beautiful places of Sztum county.

The rules are very simple and you can learn them in 5 minutes. The game will provide plenty of fun for the whole family. By playing this game you will discover historical, natural and cultural attractions that are worth visiting and exploring.

In the game there are two options available: for kids aged 4+ and 8+.

Game covers battle of Lake Peipus (also known as "Battle on ice"). This was a battle between the Republic of Novgorod and the Livonian branch of the Teutonic Knights (whose army consisted mostly of Estonians) on April 5, 1242, at Lake Peipus. The battle is notable for having been fought largely on the frozen lake.

Game contains board and 128 counters, along with one scenario. It was published in 34 issue of "Taktyka i Strategia" magazine. Game uses "Średniowiecze" system rules.

Mimikawa no Tatakai is the fourth entry in the Sengoku Folio battle game series. It's a doujinshi (self-published) game similar in tone to the early SPI folio games-low numbers of counters, compact map, and an easy-to-learn game system used for all the entries in the series. The full name is Mimikawa No Tatakai: Otomo (Tawara) X Shimazu (Takajogawa No Tatakai) 1578.11.12 (耳川の戦い: 大友 (田原) X 島津 (高城川の戦い) 1578.11.12, Battle of Mimikawa: Otomo (Tawara) Vs. Shimazu (Battle Of Takajogawa) 1578/11/12).

Historically, the battle took place in Hyuga province (in the eastern part of Kyushu, Japan's southernmost major island). The Shimazu's increasingly expansionist ways were making the daimyo (feudal lords) in the north of Kyushu uneasy. Otomo Sorin of Bungo province in northeast Kyushu put together a　sizable army and sent it south to deal with the Shimazu. The Shimazu in turn dispatched reinforcements north to their outpost at Taka castle. Shimazu Iehisa, Yoshihiro, and Yoshihisa consolidated their forces (along with some troops from their allies in the Ijuuin) at Taka, numbering about 50,000. The Otomo forces dispatched to meet them were made up of about 30,000 troops mainly from the Tawara, Takita, Saeki, and Kuwano families. They were commanded by Otomo Sorin's son-in-law, Tawara Chikataka. On the 12th day of the 11th month of the sixth year of Tensho (December 10, 1578) Tawara made an unwise frontal assault on the castle. After some bitter fighting featuring some of the earliest usage of large numbers of arquebus in Japan, the Otomo forces broke. The Shimazu pursued and many of the Otomo were drowned in the rapid waters of the Mimikawa river to the north. The Otomo lost about twice as many men as the Shimazu, probably about 4,000 or so. The Shimazu continued to push the Otomo back in the ensuing months, eventually forcing the Otomo to request aid from Toyotomi Hideyoshi, giving the warlord and his massive army a handy excuse to add Kyushu to his list of conquests.

The battle is played out in eight turns. Victory is determined by both enemy casualties and possession of Taka Castle. Special rules account for the Otomo cannon (a rare instance during the Sengoku of cannon being used in the field). Counters are rated for morale, combat strength (melee), and marksmanship (in this case, the arquebus). While the Otomo have the numerical advantage in overall combat strength and counters and a couple of cannon, the Shimazu have better morale and more leaders, making things more or less even. An enjoyable play that rewards individual skill.

Can the robots collect enough crystals to escape planet Pook?
The robot's ship has run out of fuel and crash landed on the mysterious planet of Pook. They need to collect enough crystals so they can leave. The only problem is the aliens that live there want the crystals for themselves and don't feel like sharing.

Escape From Planet Pook has 4 game modes for up to 16 players!

    Play as the robots (1-8 players), get enough crystals to escape the planet!
    Play as the aliens (1-8 players), stop the robots from stealing all your crystals!
    Play as aliens vs. robots (2-16 players), robots try to take crystals to their ship and the aliens try to take the crystals to their cave (they use them as night lights).
    Play Crystalball! (2-16 players), a super cool sport played by the aliens and robots, just for fun!


Escape From Planet Pook features modular game tiles, awesome artwork, an easy to remember alien/robot A.I. (in the Aliens or Robots modes all the players play against the game and the A.I. controls the opposing models for you!). Each alien and robot has their own unique power as well! Escape From Planet Pook also features a bonus micro card deck as well, so you can play your favorite card games on the go!

The game also has many features and suggestions for scalable difficulty. Don't let the cute artwork fool you, this is a complete game (or complete set of 5+ games!). You can make this game easy enough for any kid to beat it or impossibly hard for an adult. Are you ready to explore the planet of Pook?

boardgame: La Bataille de Friedland 1807 et le siège de Danzig 1807
description:
Another game of the great La Bat-Series created by "The old guys" of Marshal Enterprise : 
FRIEDLAND 1807 and the Siege of Danzig

Two games in one package to decide the fate of Prussia and Poland.
5 maps, 5 full color diecut countersheets, 2 informational countersheets, allocation tables, increment charts and a full CD of historical opinions, rules and game aides. Fight on the field of honor or dig your way to the city walls, what is your fate?

"The La Bataille game series are games simulating grand tactical Napoleonic combat. The games are based on historical battles, have detailed Orders of Battles, and can range from small (the space of a coffee table) to "monster game" size (4 or more full maps.) They use the battalion as the primary unit size and 125 yards per hexagon. Units are measured in steps, each step being 100 infantry, 50 cavalry or 1 gun."

--BGG Series Description

Produced in a limited quantity of just 400.

Standard scale is:

     scale = 100 m/hex; 
     time = 20 min/GT; 
     strength = 50-100 men/SP (Regimental/Brigade Size)
family: Series: La Bataille (Marshal Enterprises / Clash of Arms Games)

description:
Side Effects May Include... is a Screw Your Neighbor, party game for the over and under medicated. Players take on the role of pharmaceutical companies attempting to pass their drugs through a government review process or sorts.

Each round a condition is flipped face up. Players research medication cards to treat the condition, but medications always come with side effects, and corporate spies are rampant.

Side effects are dealt to players and they choose whether to steal results from a neighboring pharma company keep the results they have. Once each player has 5 side effects, the medications are passed to the dealer who is the reviewer for that round. The dealer decides which medication is the least harmful; that medication is passed and treats the condition. The first player to treat 3 conditions wins.

And sometimes the treatment is worse than the cure.

Warning: Side Effects May Include... Uncontrollable laughter.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Many of the Doctor's most feared opponents have teleported into the TARDIS to steal the last copy of the Black Scroll of Rassilon from the TARDIS library. With no one else, the Doctor's past regenerations have been gathered to help. Whoever finds the Black Scroll and escapes first wins!

TARDIS Run is a 3D printed, Print 'n Play, two-player game as inspired by Backgammon, Parcheesi and Senet, a game played by the Ancient Egyptians. This game turns the standard "roll and move" upside down by making you choose which character to move before you throw the paddles and add them up to move the piece you've chosen. But the very uneven distribution of the paddles means you can rely on the pieces probably moving 3 spaces. However, when the black scroll of Rassilon is found (by rolling all blanks) with it's 2x multiplier on one side the statistics become considerably harder to predict.

The board for this game is designed to fold into a neat carrying case that holds everything necessary to play inside.

Another Sekigahara game from Game Journal. But, this one is really unique and innovative.

Each player is secretly assigned a role as a secondary daimyo: Kobayakawa, Kikkawa, Chosokabe, or Shimazu. Neither player knows the identity of their opponent. Since identities are hidden, control of the Eastern and Western forces alternates each turn until players reveal their identity.

Victory is decided by victory points for eliminating the other side's (Eastern or Western) units. More points are earned by the side who wins the battle. It is possible that both players belong to the same side at the end of the game.

Time scale: ?
Game period: September 15, 1600
Counters: 108 
Map: half standard



Published in Game Journal #51.(from BGG user)

F**ktionary is a dirty word game. In each round, a player reads a word from a card out loud and the other players attempt to come up with plausible definitions. Points are scored for then guessing the correct definition or for opposing players guessing that your definition was the correct one.

From the website:

"Contains 300 obscene, crude and lascivious words. Players generate credible unwholesome definitions. Score points by guessing the correct definition, or by others voting for your repugnant answer."

From the website Example of Play:

"1 person plays the light alpaca, 1 plays the dark alpaca.

Each turn you roll the dice.

The 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6 are movement numbers- how many hexes you can move your counter through. The last hex you walk on you flip over. If it was dark alpaca territory, flip it to light alpaca territory or vice versa.

The House icon means that you put a red house token on the tile you’re standing on. The tile now cannot be flipped.

Once you have 10/16 tiles in your colour, you win immediately!"

Additional rules inferred from draft image of game:

If you roll a 1, you do not move but, instead, place a red house token on your current tile.

To setup the game, place 8 tiles black-side up on the left and then 8 white-side up on the right. Place the light colored alpaca counter in the top right of the white section and the black alpaca counter in the top left of the black section of hex tile layout.

Objective: Gain most bonus chips; learn more about the federal state NRW (Nordrhein-Westfalen) of Germany.
 - acting by turns -
On a board with six nested circles starting in the outer circle  each player moves around according die roll. At the end of a circle you cross over to the next inner one. Ending on yellow, green or orange space left neighbor must draw a card and reads 1 (yellow), 2 (green) or 3 (orange) questions, which must be answered. 
Correct? Get 1/2/3 bonus chips; wrong? Depending on position (circle no.) you may ransom yourself (with matching no. of chips) or step back/skip turns. Ending on a blue space you are not allowed drawing a card.
After on player has entered target zone game ends immidiately.

Picaroon emphasizes chaotic randomness, unpredictability, and variable outcomes, yet our new game also incorporates the tactical challenge and excitement of a great strategy game. In order to win Picaroon, you must channel your “inner most pirate”, to use strategy, negotiation, money management, memorization, and like most other board games a little bit of luck.

Florenza: Captains of Fortune consists of six cards that represent the "Captains of Fortune", with their correlated tiles for the draw from the bag. This new kind of character allows you to take the field in Renaissance battlefields to gain glory...and some extra resources!

This expansion also includes resource multipliers to make it impossible for players to run short on tokens during the game.

Hetsugigawa no Tatakai is the fifth entry in the Sengoku Folio battle game series. It's a doujinshi (self-published) game similar in tone to the early SPI folio games-low numbers of counters, compact map, and an easy-to-learn game system used for all the entries in the series. The full name is "Hetsugigawa No Tatakai: Toyotomi (Sengoku) X Shimazu-1587.1.20 (戸次川の戦い: 豊臣　(仙石) X 島津　1587.1.20, Battle Of Hetsugigawa: Toyotomi (Sengoku) Vs. Shimazu-1587/1/20)". In this case, the 'Sengoku' in parenthesis doesn't mean 'Warring States' (pronounced the same, different kanji) but rather is the name of the Toyotomi commander who provoked the battle with a foolhardy assault.

After responding to a request for aid against the Shimazu by the Otomo, Toyotomi Hideyoshi prepared a huge army to take the island of Kyushu. His vanguard consisted of forces from the Sengoku and Chosokabe (around 20,000 in all)-they entered Bungo province in NE Kyushu and were instructed to stay on the defensive until the Toyotomi main body arrived. Instead, samurai being samurai and not inclined to listen to anyone, Sengoku Hisahide decided to attempt the relief of nearby Toshimitsu Castle (then being besieged by the Shimazu). The Chosokabe protested but eventually joined the assault, figuring that if Sengoku was determined to go through with the assault it would be better to join in than to sit by doing nothing. However, the Shimazu were aware of their advance and increased their efforts to take the castle, laying claim to it before the Toyotomi forces arrived. Undeterred, the Toyotomi forces (also with elements of the Otomo and Sogou, about 7,000 in all) gathered for battle on the 12th day of the 12th month of the 14th year of Tenshou (January 20, 1587). The Shimazu (with a large number of allies from the Ijuuin, the force adding up to about 15,000) provoked an attack with a feigned retreat (a favorite Shimazu tactic), luring the Toyotomi into an ambush and routing their forces. The Toyotomi withdrew from Bungo and this was to be the high point of the Shimazu-things would be far different when the Toyotomi main force arrived.

The battle is played out in eight turns. Victory is determined by both enemy casualties and possession of the two castles on the field. Counters are rated for morale, combat strength (melee), and marksmanship (in this case, the arquebus). The Toyotomi forces face a tough fight as they're outnumbered almost two to one by a Shimazu force with superior morale. Thankfully, they aren't required to be be as stupid as Sengoku Hisahide.

Two player ice hockey game, authorized to use the official 1974 Ice Hockey World Championship Tournament Emblems.

Each player selects a national team, the teams are identical - only names and picture vary. The players are set in a rink and then players alter turns to play action card (such as attack, shot, save, off side etc) from their hand and try to achieve a position where they can score. Only one card per turn can be played, a new card is drawn after the played card is resolved.

The idea is to score goals and win the match. When the deck runs out, the period is over. After the third period is over, the match ends.

Rules say that the maximum players is six, but that is only for tournaments - only two players can play at the same time.

This is a three-dimensional dexterity game with a military theme published in the late 1950s or early 1960s. Terrain is represented with a cardboard base and plastic landscape features. A colorful background depicts a WWII battle scene. A plastic tank is guided along a road network using a magnetic control device. The tank commander scores points for removing obstacles but receives a penalty for entering a minefield or running off the road. Players take turns driving the armored vehicle and compare scores.

Includes the landscape board, a tank, and several plastic terrain features. The rules are printed inside the box lid.

Asamblea is a game about dialog and discussion. The players must talk about different polemic topics and then vote on them.
Sometimes the players must advocate or attack a position opposite of his own opinion. It is a dialectical and personal challenge!

The goal of the game is convince other players that vote your position about the topic. The game has 5 rounds of debates; at the end of the rounds the players with the most points receive congratulations from other players.


Asamblea es un juego de dialogo y debate. Los jugadores deben debatir sobre diferentes polémicos temas y luego votarlos. Puede darse el caso de que los jugadores se vean en la tesitura de defender o atacar posturas en la que ellos mismos no estén de acuerdo, ofreciendo al jugador un reto dialéctico y personal. Asamblea no busca la confrontación ni los enfrentamientos directos; se trata de un ejercicio de dialogo y debate, donde se pongan en común los diferentes puntos de vista siempre teniendo en cuenta el respeto por el resto de jugadores y la palabra como única arma.

El objetivo del juego es tratar diferentes temas y posicionarse en los distintos puntos de vista mediante un debate conjunto. Aflorar nuestra capacidad de entendimiento y aprender a convencer a otras personas de nuestra postura por medio del dialogo y la palabra. Cada partida de Asamblea consta de 5 debates/temas, los cuales tras finalizar un tiempo acordado serán votados. En función de la votación los jugadores recibirán unos puntos. Al finalizar los 5 debates el jugador con más puntos recibe las felicitaciones del resto del grupo.

User Description

Space Wars is a short Tactical Space Combat Game utillising 2 decks of playing cards (through large game of 6-8 players will need 3 decks).

The game is started by making up space ships from "Fighters" to "Mother Ships" by placing sets of cards on the table.

Then the game can start which has a four turn sequence where players can launch attacks on the other players ships by playing cards to fire missilles, lasers, board enemy ships and repair their own ships.

The winner is the player who has the last ship left.

First published in Valkyrie (Volume 1, Issue 9 - 1995) magazine.

boardgame: By Fire and Sword: A Solitaire Game of the Thirty Years War (1618-1648)
description:
BACKGROUND: By Fire and Sword is semi-historical simulation of the Thirty Years War. The game begins just after the Bohemian Revolt where the very Catholic Ferdinand II (future Holy Roman Emperor) of Austria was elected to the Thrones of Bohemia and Hungary. Many Protestants felt they would lose their religious rights and a small revolt occurred. This revolt soon spread as other Protestant leaders felt emboldened.  The Player represents the Commander of the Protestant Forces with the goal to overthrow the Holy Roman Empire. As the Commander of the Protestant Forces, you must control a significant number of Cities and Towns throughout Europe before the last Campaign Card is Played.

GAME OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the Protestant Forces. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to control at least 16 Cities and 14 Towns before the “Peace of Westphalia” Card is Played. If the Catholics control 16 Cities and 14 Towns at the end of their Turn, the Holy Roman Empire wins.

By Fire and Sword is a fast and fun game with lots of colorful game counters representing the various military units of the time, including Pike & Musket, Musketeers, Cavalry, Cuirassiers and Cannon. Major leaders of the war are represented and lead forces on the map. Battles and Sieges have, almost, a "miniatures" feel to them as they are resolved on a Battle Mat.

GAME MECHANICS: The Player moves his Leader Counters on the Map along with their Troop Points. If a Battle occurs, Troop Points are exchanged for Tactical Units and combat is carried out in a Tactical manner on a Battle Mat. If there are any Reserve Units in the "Play Area", he may add them to a Battle Force.
Spin and move game where the object is collect all five sections of a newspaper before proceeding to the finish.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Do you feel lucky?

Clover has a special way of stacking the cards, with the cards forming a clover. Gameplay is similar to Crazy Eights, but with short term and long term effects as you play the cards. If you are lucky, you will find the clover with its magical effects...

Tank War is an expansion of the Bolt Action World War II miniatures game allowing players to recreate tank battles on the tabletop.  Tank War includes new scenarios, lists and special rules covering armies from the United States, Germany, Italy, the British Commonwealth, Finland, the Soviet Union, and the Empire of Japan.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A Trap the Cap adaption - 2 different games.

Instead of a die, this game uses a spinning top to reach results between 1 and 6. The game board has two sides. Side 1 is a standard "Trap the Cap" game with an altered game board. Side 2 uses different rules. Each space shows the first part of a mathematical expression, which is completed by the result of the spinning top. The amount of the whole expression is the number of spaces the cap can move forward. This game can be played like "Pachisi" or "Trap the Cap".

Game 1: Be the first catching all other caps/acting by turns 
Board is entered on an 'A'-space. Players move their "caps" around the board according spinner. If a player lands on another cap or a stack of caps, that player captures the stack by nesting his/her cap on top of the stack. The game ends when there is only one active color of caps left on the board. Caps on spaces with an 'F' or matching color can't be caught. Ending on a 'F'-space all captured caps must be released.

Game 2: More like Pachisi (kicking out other ones), but additionally players must perform one of the 4 basic arithmetic operations (according spinner & space) moving their meeples.

Pickup season is open! All thanks to Demoralizer, a love battle where no holds are barred.

This card game by Krzysztof Miller overcomes the classic scheme of game for couples, and makes a perfect example of playing with the form and breaking stereotypes. The game can be played by 2 – 6 people, who have only one goal – to demoralize the opponents. Each player takes the role of one of the six available characters: Blondi, Gangster, XXX Actor, Vamp, Scientist, or…  Grandma. Each character is described by four indicators: sex appeal, composure, cleverness, and morality. The level of these features, however, can be changed while playing the game by the action of cards, which players use during gameplay, as well as a direct result of actions taken by the opponents. To perform a successful pickup, thus depriving the other player his morality points, the level of attacking player’s sex appeal must be higher than the composure of defending player. Only adequate calculation and analysis of indicators’ levels can protect a player against demoralization.

The game is divided into players’ turns. Each player’s turn is divided into six phases: discarding, drawing, activation, playing cards, seduction, and final phase. During the playing cards phase, players play on the table their cards from hand: action, helper, item, event or skill. To do this, however, a player has to pay the cost with his cleverness points. In addition, the character cards determine how many cards of a particular type the player can handle. Seduction phase is optional. A player may, but don’t have to, try to attack any number of opponents during his turn. Futhermore, a player can attack not only with his character card, but also with helper cards, if he has got them. The same applies to defense.

When the level of player character’s morality drops to zero, that player loses and he is automatically eliminated from further play. The game continues until only one player remains and becomes the ultimate demoralizer.

Demoralizer is a game for those who in the heat of battle do not forget about strategic thinking. Thanks to unique characters’ features, playing each of them requires a slightly different approach. The differences in the attributes determine strategy - sometimes player's attacks will be more frequent, or on the contrary - he will have to raise composure level and wait for the right moment to pickup the opponent.

The average gameplay takes approx. 30-45 minutes.

Expansion for Moskwa 1941 that covers fights around Tula in second half of November 1941.

Contains board and 84 new counters.
family: Series: Wielkie Bitwy 1939-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

boardgame: Admiral's Order: Naval Tactics in the Age of Sail – All Hands!
description:
Admiral's Order: Naval Tactics in the Age of Sail – All Hands! is a simulation of naval engagements between ships-of-the-line and frigates in the Age of Fighting Sail. The focus of the game lies on the wars between Britain and France and her allies in the years 1775 to 1815. Each player takes on the role of an admiral, commander or captain whose command could include a single ship or a fleet of several vessels. The core game ("All Hands!") has five scenarios to choose from, everything from a small, fast and furious encounter between two frigates to a strategic action involving a total of 16 ships-of–the-line.

A game turn equates to a ½-hour watch on the day of battle. In each turn, players will play cards to execute sailing maneuvers such as tacking, wearing, or changing sail configuration in order to put their ships into position for devastating broadsides or even rakes on the enemy vessels.

In larger engagements, ships are grouped together in squadrons and usually form a "Line of Battle", a formation that provides the commander with the most control over his fleet. Using Admiral's Order cards players signal general commands to their squadrons ranging from "Form a Keel Line" and "Wearing in succession" to "Prepare for Close Action". Keeping the ships in formation and effectively deploying the fleet in the heat of battle while also retaining the initiative is the main challenge for a commander.

Occasionally, ships will engage each other in brutally destructive Close Quarter combat. In such situations, the game "zooms" into the action: Using a set of "Close Action" cards, players can employ grapeshot, grenades, carronades, and sharpshooters to wreak destruction on the opposing vessel's upper deck. Through a clever use of grappling hooks and boarding crews it is possible to overcome the enemy's defenses to take the ship as the ultimate prize.

All scenarios can be played by two players, but most also provide the option to split command between two players per side. Playing time depends on the scenario. While an engagement between two frigates should last no more than 1-2 hours, fighting the Battle of Cape Henry will take four hours or longer.

The game is produced in a small edition and comes in a beautiful box, designed with a picture from Thomas Whitcombe. It has a wooden presentation box with 36 hand-crafted, wooden ship models. Each ship comes with removable masts and ship names burned into each side.

Project Football is a game that tries to deliver the full feeling of football. The game starts with a draft to fill the 18 man squad with every player having their abilities. Players alternate performing actions by activating one of their footballers. The game simulates almost all possible real life situations from fouls, throw ins and corners to penalties, injuries, hand balls or yellow and red cards.

Attribute test are made in one of two ways (agreed before kick off):
1. By rolling a 20-sided die
2. By flipping the top card of Your "fate" deck.

The first thought of this game was in April 2013, but not until February 2014 that I found the mechanics that I want to use to fuel my ideas. After that a working prototype has been made, and during several test the game had gotten a good reception. Now it's in a very advance test shape.

Recently a 4th prototype version was printed with minor changes in game mechanics, and with prototype card layouts, and new game board preparing the title for release.

Massilia: Bacchus consists of four copies of one card, which are added to the other "Divinity" cards.

Card effect: The player can choose which color of goods a client buys from their stall. The player earns sesterces as usual, and reputation points based on the goods actually sold. Once played, the card is discarded.

In Massilia: The Thief, a mini expansion for Massilia, a thief stalks the market. Can you manipulate him in tandem with the consul to serve your own aims?

During the game set-up, place the thief pawn on the same space as the consul; place the THIEF tile near the board. During play, whenever a client is moved by a player, the thief pawn is moved an equal number of spaces in the same direction. Whenever the thief pawn stops in front of a stall holding goods, he steals one good cube from the tile. (If the thief pawn moves to the same space as the consul pawn, there is no effect.) The player who moved the thief chooses the good and places it on the thief tile.

Whenever a player moves the consul pawn to the same space as the thief pawn, she scores reputation points equal to the total
number of goods cubes on the thief tile, then returns the goods cubes to the reserve.

boardgame: Imperium Chronicles: Fighter Wing – Planetary Defense
description: Planetary Defense is the first expansion for the Fleets at War: Fighter Wing tactical fighter combat game. Fighter Wing (and this expansion) focuses on fighters and other small craft as they engage in dogfights among the stars.

Nations Promo Pack 2014: Inspiration consists of six promotional cards for Nations: four progress cards and two event cards. Seven of the games that were important inspiration during the development of Nations are directly or indirectly referenced by these cards.

User summary:

An 1800 Tokyo (Edo) themed, dice-driven point collecting game. Players are merchants in Edo, going around in the street, earning money, acquiring various vendors and makers, and buying product to gain them victory points. Each turn, players roll certain coloured dice, based on what vendors and makers they have, and use these dice to perform certain actions.

The game is based on accurate paintings and research from the era, about downtown Edo.
publishers: イマジンゲームズ (imagine GAMES)

description:
Nation rises against nation... They’re just not always up front about it.
You represent a nation’s interests and ambitions in the next Cold War.

Spying, sanctions and nuclear weapons.
These are your tools. Use them as you push for war, or try to prevent it.

This is a quick card game where the goal is to secretly stockpile the most nukes and/or money cards before war is declared. Players compete using strategy and of course the luck of the draw.

Easy to learn and endlessly replayable.

Players take turns drawing a card, then playing 1 card face down and/or 1 card face up.  Only Money or Nuke cards are played face down, since they are secret. Opponents can play a variety of face up cards, like Spy, Sanctions or Economic Failure, to see the secret cards and possibly remove them.
There are also War and Peace cards.  They are played in a shared space called the War Line.  The game is over when there are 7 War cards in the War Line, or when the deck runs out of cards.  Score is based on the values on the Money and Nuke cards, as well as a few bonus modifiers.

The game can found as print-on-demand on The Game Crafter.
https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/global-crisis

It is also available for playtesting using TableTop Simulator on the Steam Workshop.

A word game, in Catalan, with just 7 syllable dice and 1 numerical die. A player rolls the dice and all players write down the longest word with the syllables shown on the dice (each die can be used just once). After a fixed time all players receive as many points as syllables their respective words while the rolling player receives that many points multiplied by the value of the numerical die. Then the turn passes to the next player. Once all players have rolled one or two times, the player with most points is the winner.

A word game, in Catalan, with 110 cards, each one with a letter (distributed according to their relative abundance in Catalan). Each player is dealt six cards and six more cards are placed face up on the table. In his turn, a player places a card from his hand  below one of the six face up cards, forming a column, or expanding an already created column, with just one condition: all the letters in a column must always form a correct word (not taking into account the order of the cards); then receives a new card from the draw pile. When a column reaches five cards, the cards are retired and placed horizontally in the correct order; then all players can attempt to form a longer word with their hand cards, and whoever makes the longest word gains the cards. When the draw pile is empty the game ends and the winner is the player who has formed most words.
Players try to make a bingo vertically, horizontally, or diagonally on 5x5 bingo cards depicting US States.  One or more clues are read from a clue card to help identify each state.

boardgame: Kings of War: Kings and Legends
description:
Kings and Legends is an expansion supplement for Kings of War which contains rules for a host of Living Legends, mighty heroes and villains who bring even more character to the world of Mantica.

Most of the Living Legends from the Kings of War book make an appearance, with expanded backgrounds and additional rules which let you customize units in their army to reflect the forces they fought alongside. There are also several new Living Legends, including several who were created with the direct input of the Kings of War community.

Finally, the book contains the brand-new Ogre force list. No longer confined to the Kingdoms of Men force list as mercenaries, the Ogres can now take to the field as an entire army in their own right!

Each player gets 3 round tokens (small, medium, and large), all of the same color. On their turn, each player rolls 3 wooden "dice" (small, medium, and large) that have colors on them. They then move each of their "pigs" (tokens) according to the colors they rolled (similar to Candy Land). There are other "fine print" rules pertaining to the Big Bad Wolf. The first one to get all three "pigs" to the Brick House wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
Trobil Makers is an expansion to Asking for Trobils.

The small Sagan Colony, full of the galaxy's most ridiculously intelligent people, has taken it upon themselves to get rid of all the Trobils in Paradise by creating robot Trobil-eaters!  Of course, their programming went a little awry (as programming tends to do in these situations) and are now just as much trouble as real Trobils!

As if your job as a Trobil Hunter wasn't difficult enough, now you also have to get rid of these mechanical monstrosities!

Trobil Makers adds a new Location to the board called the Sagan Colony.  The expansion also adds new Trobil cards, purple Shard resources, Trobot tokens, and a small board. The eighth Ship that was promised with an extra punchboard in the original game will also be provided.

Fierce Battle! Rommel's Corps is one of "40 Series". 40 Series is simulation games having the concept of "using 40 playing pieces and taking 40 minutes to finish".

The theme of Fierce Battle! Rommel's Corps is "Operation Crusader" launched from 18 November 1941 by the British Eighth Army. In this operation, British Army succeeded in the raising of the siege of Tobruk that had been besieged by the Axis Army.
One person commands the British Army, the other person the Axis Army.

Fierce Battle! Kawanakajima is a game of the battle of Kawanakajima which occurred on 10 September 1561 (Fourth Battle of Kawanakajima). 
One player commands the Takeda forces, the other the Uesugi forces.

Families and Bloodlines is a fast-paced 20-30 minute card game, for 2-4 players, in which, each player takes the role of either a Prince or Princess aspiring for greatness! These individuals are tasked with creating an everlasting legacy, with the hopes of maintaining the power of the royalty, as well as facing the arduous challenges of their time.

Published as  Vampire: the Masquerade - Heritage.

A new, terrifying opponent has appeared in the corridors of the space station Theseus. What initially was thought of as strange, unknown devices clinging to the walls or closed in mysterious crates in the hold, all disappeared in a moment. At the same time you could hear shrill, metallic squeaks in the dark corridors. Someone saw beady, lifeless eyes in the tech bay. When steel blades emerged from the darkness to the accompaniment of a mechanical, grinding sound, it was too late for the rescue...

Theseus: The Dark Orbit – Bots introduces the Bots faction to the game Theseus: The Dark Orbit. Bots can be used instead of any other faction from the base game. This expansion does not increase the number of players who can play the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Corporate Chaos is a new independent card game. You and your fellow 'corporate drones' struggle to keep your job. You also don't want to get promoted or else you will become part of the soulless corporate machine. Try not to get too stressed out though or you'll burn out.

Each player (drone) is given a tracker for his/her HR Warnings, Career, and Stress levels. If any of those trackers reach 5, that drone is out of the game (sort of).

Each round (workday) goes like this. Each drone chooses 1-3 cards to play from his/her hand of 7 cards and places the face down on the table. Once everyone is ready, the drones take turns revealing his/her cards. Everydrone adds up his/her productivity for the day. The drone with the highest productivity gets "praise from the boss" and has to draw a career card (which will most likely increase the career stat) and the drone(s) with the lowest productivity gets "yelled at by the boss" and increases their HR Warnings stat; all other drones are safe for the day. Everydrone draws back up to 7 hand cards and the game goes until there is only one drone left.

Race your opponent to defeat monsters and rescue the princess, before she meets a gruesome demise.

This is a game of luck where you will use dice rolls to attack monsters and lower their health levels while protecting your own.

If you kill the monster, you gain life equal to the excess damage used in the killing blow to the monster, and move to the next monster location. If any monster kills you, you must restart with the first monster.

You must be the first to make it past all monsters and save the princess to beat your opponent!

boardgame: Star Wars: Face Off Dice Game – Luke Skywalker vs. Darth Vader
description: This is a push-your-luck dice game. The game contains a molded plastic dice cup/container molded to show the image of Darth Vader and Luke Skywalker, five identical dice (each with the image of Star Wars characters, four heroic and two villainous), as well as a short rules booklet. Players roll the dice, then can choose to "Go Hero" or "Go Villain". Players who "go hero" can reroll up to three dice any number of times to collect a scoring combination of hero faces (four of a kind, five of a kind, &c.), but if all the dice they reroll show villain faces, they lose their turn and do not score. Players who "go villain" get a single reroll, but can keep the results secret and attempt to bluff other players for more points.

boardgame: Ultimate Spider-Man: Face Off Dice Game – Spider-Man vs. Rhino
description: This push-your-luck dice game contains a molded plastic container/dice cup and five dice with the faces of heroes or villains. After a player rolls their dice, they can "go hero" (rerolling dice to build scoring combinations of heroes) or "go villain (rerolling only once, but allowing them to bluff other players for a higher score).

It's like Blackjack, with dice! Secretly roll for your hand, then press your luck to get closer to 21 and use your bluffing skills to score victory points each round.


     Full Set has enough components for 2 players. For Four-Player games get the Full Set + Extras.
Put stacks of 2 dice on each white square to act as pins, then slide other dice down the lane to knock off the top pieces. Bonuses for spares & strikes!
Place crates (dice) on the board, slide your marker and kick crates horizontally or vertically to block your opponent from moving before you yourself end up trapped by a wall of crates.

You get 4 attempts to score before possession goes to the opposing team. Roll multiple dice secretly against your opponent to run, punt or tackle. Score a touchdown and try for the extra point or 2-point conversion. Strategically use your re-roll points to gain an edge with bad rolls. Score points to win. Game ends when you run out of re-roll points.

Each player controls 2 horses. Use luck for total distance, and skill to split your movement points between your two horses while avoiding obstacles. Be the first to complete a lap with both horses to win.

Tennis is played on 2 boards (included). Roll 1-3 dice to serve and try to land on your opponent's board. Sum the points but watch out - fault by missing the board and roll to return the ball after your opponent serves. Play doubles for big group fun!

Roll to move your players around the board and to defend. Then pass and throw the ball to eliminate members of the opposing team. You'll be pleasantly surprised by how well the mechanics of this game emulate a real game of dodgeball!

6 pieces per player, 6 chess-like movement types, and a special twist: you must roll to determine which piece to move, or optionally, move your King in the same way as another piece. Capture the opposing King and win.

The fifth official expansion* to Cards Against Humanity, with 75 White Cards, 25 Black Cards, 8 blank White Cards, and 4 blank Black Cards.


    We give up.  Go ahead, play it without the main game.  Drink and drive while you're at it.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Movement is determined by 2 dice. There are spaces were virtues are rewarded and spaces were sins are punished. The first player to reach space 100 wins the game.

The game was published 1902 or up to a few years later.

Armies of Riddle is a Battle Fantasy RPG Trading Card Game for physical play as well as mobile play (Both games are separate entities but work the same way).

GOAL OF GAME: The goal of the game is to destroy your opponent's crystal shard.

PLAYERS: 2 Players

MAIN GAME ITEMS: COLLECTABLE CARDS AND DICE

QUICK OVERVIEW OF GAME PLAY:
1. Players select crystal shards
2. Players shuffle army deck and select randomly 3 army cards each
3. Players then place their desired army cards into battle and roll the dice
4. As the game rounds grow, players can increase their units, into multiple battles and combo battles to try and decrease their opponents powers.
5. The winner is the player who successfully defeats their opponents Crystal Shard

It’s a race against the hunter. Your turkey must reach the house before it gets caught. To win, your turkey must also be the biggest one.

How to play
A game is composed of 3 runs of 6 turns or less. A turn is played by having each player rolling the dices and try to obtain a sequence.

To play a sequence:

    Roll the 6 dices
    Reroll one or more dices (optional)
    Reroll one or more dices a second time (optional)


End of a run
The run ends if one of the following happens:

    The hunter reaches the house (6 turns completed).
    All turkeys have been caught by the hunter.


End of the game
The game ends after 3 runs. The winner is the player with the most points.

BOP! is like Scrabble without the board and without the vowels.
There are 58 letter tiles and 2 BOP! tiles. Each letter tile is one of 5 colors. BOP! tiles do not count as any color. With 5 tiles in your hand, you compete to score the most points by adding a WRD or WRDS to the grid. A WRD is a word found in a standard English Language dictionary with all the letters in their correct order MINUS THE VOWELS. Points are awarded for each tile in WRDS either created or extended on your turn. Extra points are awarded for adjacent tiles of the same color or WRDS 5 LTTRS or longer. Once the draw pile is exhausted and one player has played his/her last tile, each player takes one more turn. One point is deducted for each tile remaining on your hand. High score wins!

D!Git is a number and color based tiling game. There are 50 number/color tiles (0-9 in each of 5 different colors), 5 color specific Wild tiles, and 5 D!Git tiles (can represent any number in any color). Each player draws one random tile. High number goes first. All tiles are then jumbled and distributed evenly and stacked face down in front of each player. First player lays 1,2,3,4 or 5 tiles from his/her hand in a straight line to establish the grid. Tiles laid down must be either different colors of the same number OR a sequence of numbers (up or down) of the same color. Sequences may also roll around e.g. 8,9,0,1,2 etc is OK. If, on your turn, you place a 5th or 10th tile on a line, you get to replenish your hand. Rather than using your turn to add tiles to the grid, you may choose to swap tiles from your hand for already played Wild tiles as long as they work there. The first player to get rid of all of his/her tiles wins.

SnapGammon is a unique new board game inspired by Backgammon where two cowpokes race to move their cows to their home stations and onto the train, hitting other cows along the way and sending them into the cow pen. 
Special power cards let players bend the rules of the game for their strategic advantage.
There are different variations included which include power cards, crazy dice that replace the regular dice that have different values on their faces including an 8, 3+3 and a even -1. 
And even another variant for players to hit spaces even though there are 2 or more cows on the spaces.

Think five. Five colors, five shapes, five minutes to learn. In Q!nto you add tiles from your 5 tile hand to the grid in a single line. All lines must contain tiles in which the colors are either all the same OR all different, AND the shapes are also either all the same or all different. There are also shape-specific Wild tiles which can represent any color, and color-specific Wild tiles which can represent any shape. In addition, there are 2 Q!nto tiles which can represent any shape in any color. Instead of adding tiles to the grid, a player may use his/her turn to swap tiles from his/her hand for already played Wild tiles, as long as they work in that position. Q!nto can be played a few different ways. In Q!nto Classic, players keep score. Q!nto Plus allows players to count valid diagonal lines of 3 or more tiles. In Q!nto Light, the tiles are divided evenly among the players who then race to get rid of all their tiles.

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: Game Geek / Ernie the Geek
description:
Pixel Tactics Promo card that is a kickstarter/convention promo

Card text: Heroes in this unit have +1 attack strength and +1 life for each game of Pixel Tactics you have lost against the opponent since you last won a game against him/her. When you win a game with this leader, you pick the next game.

Pixel Tactics Promo card that is a kickstarter/convention promo

Card text: You can attack with any hero in your unit, regardless of the current wave. Each of your heroes can only attack once per round. You can attack during the round 1 ceasefire

Pixel Tactics Promo card that is a kickstarter/convention promo

Card text: You may alter the game state freely when your opponent is not watching. If your opponent notices the changes, you must undo them and this leader loses 4 life. After a complete wave passes, all changes stand.

Pixel Tactics Promo card that is a kickstarter/convention promo

Card text: At the end of each round, you may propose an alteration to the game's rules that will affect all players. If all players agree, the rule stands for this game. Otherwise, you may draw 2 cards

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: HD Leader Card – Udstad
description:
Pixel Tactics: Udstad is a single leader card for use in either Pixel Tactics or Pixel Tactics 2

Card text: Whenever an opponent recruits a hero, that hero restructures as far backwards as possible in the same column of his unit

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: HD Leader Card – Shekhtur Lenmorre
description:
Pixel Tactics: Shekhtur Lenmorre is a single leader card for use in either Pixel Tactics or Pixel Tactics 2

Card text: Whenever a hero in this unit is defeated, that hero immediately makes a free melee attack. ( The damage from this attack occurs after checking all casualties if it happens during the end of a wave. )

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: HD Leader Card – Kaitlyn Van Sorrel
description:
Pixel Tactics: Kaitlyn Van Sorrel is a single leader card for use in either Pixel Tactics or Pixel Tactics 2

Card text: You can use Restructure actions to affect your opponent's unit. It takes two actions to restructure enemy heroes with the Intercept ability.

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: HD Leader Card – Cadenza
description:
Pixel Tactics: Cadenza is a single leader card for use in either Pixel Tactics or Pixel Tactics 2

Card text: Your heroes and leader take 1 less damage from attacks

Murder has occurred, but clues are scarce. You must use your wits to discover the identity of the brutally disfigured corpse. From where was it displaced? And why were they killed?

A game of memory, strategy, and deductive reasoning for 2-4 players.

Players take turns revealing clues and trying to whittle down their evidence in order to deduce why, where, and who their crime was committed against.

Every set of clues the players can find give them special advantages and gains them points; however, the observant player who can summate the Mystery can gain extra points for victory!

User Description

A fan set of miniatures rules are inspired on the Pod Racing from the movie Star Wars - The Phanton Menace.

The rules require some models of pod racers, some six side dice, deck of cards, tape measure and some scenery.

Each turn in the game has four phases which each player must complete before the next players turn.

Each turn of racing the player can speed up and move his Pod in a straight line or turn it, but there is a chance he will Spin-Out his Pod or even stall its engine.  There are even rules on how to ram other Pods (just like the movie).

At anytime in the game each player can play a card which can benefit him or play on anther player (dirty tricks).

The winner is the player who crosses the finish line first.

The rules are free and avaliable from The Armchair General website.

boardgame: Run, Fight, or Die!: Cat and Lenny Character Boards
description:
Two unique Character Boards for Run, Fight, or Die!

    Cat, The Sassy Secretary
    Lenny, The Butcher


These Character Boards were distributed only to Kickstarter backers.

In Recruiters, two players compete to recruit the best group of candidates for the armed forces of their nation Uber Superb. Players need to spend their recruiting power wisely, balancing their efforts for each of the four service branches: Army, Navy, Marines and Air Force. It’s an advantage to recruit the most people, and it’s an advantage to recruit the best people. But that’s just the beginning, since allocating your recruits to hotspots around the globe is even more important, and... there are two ways to play!

In the standard game, there are two decks: one with all the Recruiting Power cards plus two Special cards, and the other with all the Recruit cards. Each player gets a hand of Recruiting Power cards, which are used to sway (i.e., buy) recruits which are constantly coming and going throughout the peak recruiting season. After this first part of the game, 2 VPs are awarded to the player with the most recruits. Then, four further and crucial competitions between the players will take place, and the player who best allocates his/her recruits to the aforementioned hotspots will earn the most VPs and win the game.

The "friendly" version of the game is a lighter, press your luck design, with unfortunate results for a too-greedy player.

boardgame: Castle: The Detective Card Game – Murder Board Promo
description:
Set of three promo cards to add to the base game.
Includes Murder Board Discussion, Murder Board Evaluation, and Murder Board Review.

The game provides a unique experience for families. The challenge is adaptable to the players desire and can be played in Single or Cooperative mode.

THE OBJECTIVE
In the begining of the game, players receive 4 objective cards of different type that indicate witch tokens they have to find (the tokens in the game setup are placed face down in random order) in the different game-boards. The 4 tokens must be found and placed on each player character sheet in order to a player (or players in coop mode) be able to chalenge the vilain in the end phase of the game.

HOW TO PLAY
To move around, in the begining of the turn, a player must take a random encounter card that tells wich character (good or bad) he encounters in his voyage. These cards must be resolved first according to the symbols printed on them. After this, each player moves its marker the number of spaces indicated on that same card.

Players then explore the boards turning the tokens around and seeing what lies beneath, looking for their 4 objectives.

Hidden beneath the tokens may be be good things, objects or enemys. Players use the power of food and good nutrition to overcome obstacles trought matching food cards color sequences.

MEANWHILE
At the same time that players ares trying to find their objectives, the clock is ticking and they have only 10 rounds to prepare for the final battle against the bad guy....he has is hown character sheet that plays automatically against the players.

Players must find their objectives and at the same time try to find out the villains hidden location in the factory for the final battle, if they fail to do this, even if the their objectives are met, the bad guy wins the game.

THE STORY
Our story takes place in a land called NUTRILAND, where food had enormous powers, called NUTRI-POWERS. People lived happily and had a healthy and balanced life. The big NUTRILAND FACTORY, managed by MURDOCH and a team of good nutritionists, led by NEUS, made ​​the processing and distribution of food to the entire population.
ALEX GRAND, the terrible villain, seized control of his father’s factory with the help of NOSE, a despicable bootlicker and his right arm, and subverted MURDOCH’s dream using the Factory to produce and distribute GENEX-100, a caloric compound of his making.

Before ALEX destroyed everything, NEUS instructed its seven most experienced nutritionists to flee, so that each one would preserve a food group. Thus they became Nutri-Masters and created the seven kingdoms of nutrition where even today they retain the foods that were entrusted to them and the secrets of their incredible NUTRI-POWERS.

30 years later NUTRILAND became gray and entirely dominated by ALEX GRAND, which gave it a new name - GRANDLAND. The population forgot food over time and ALEX became an businessman adored by all and the GENEX-100 the only food available.

Having realized ALEX’s scheme, and having discovered the existence of food, TEO, LENA, BEN and NINA take on the exciting mission to discover the seven kingdoms of nutrition and restore food diversity to GRANDLAND.

Härjapõlvlaste Riid (Quarrel of the Leprechauns) is based on an Estonian fairytale.

There are berry-cards with values 1-10, the Leprechauns with values 1-10 and some special cards in this game.
Also a die that shows how many cards player must draw before the round.

In her turn player plays a card on the table. The next player must play card with higher value on that card.
If a stronger leprechaun was played on the berry, he eats it and all cards played in this round are discarded. Then a new round starts.
If a player can´t play a suitable card he must pick up all cards from the table and a new round starts.

The objective is to get rid of all cards first.

A Myth promo card released at Gencon 2014.

"Play this card immediately after another hero fails to hit with an Attack Action with an AP cost of 2. That hero may reroll his dice pool. If he fails to hit again, a Darkness Cycle activates immediately."

This card may be played as a reaction, adds 1 AP and can be used no matter what your movement has been.
