description:
Princess Emmy and her four-and-a-half favorite horses Caesar, Otto, Rose, Sunny and Xavier are playing an exciting game of hide-and-seek.

In Die Pferde sind los, you need to help Emmy find her horses. Are they on the river? In the market? Or have they moved inside the barn? The die gives you a hint of where you are allowed to search, and whoever goes on this quest with a good memory and a little luck of the die will win.

Custodial Trap: The Game is a game created to show off the Custodial Trap move type seen in a number of games designed by Rich Hutnik. The move type is akin to Custodial Capture seen in Tafl family of games, or Custodial Conversion seen in Othello/Reversi. The custodial trap move involves two pieces bookending a line of enemy pieces, and freezing them so they can't move. In a future turn, one of the two pieces freezing enemy pieces, which isn't also trapped, can then move one space and occupy an enemy piece that it is freezing, and capture it.

Custodial Trap: The Game, is played on an 8x8 chessboard with 18 pieces each in two different colors that are identical.  Chess pawns or checkers work.  In the basic game, the pieces are set up in a 6x6 alternating pattern of of opposing pieces, that form a checkerboard pattern.  Pieces on the corners are the only ones free.   Object of the basic game is to either prevent one's opponent from having no legal moves or capturing at least 12 of their opponent's pieces.

The advanced version of the game, introduces a new win condition of a player connecting all their not capture pieces into one orthogonally connected group of piece.  In addition, players also can start with pieces off the board, and one player sets up the pieces for both players, and the other player picks what side to play, utilizing a Limited Dutch Offer (this is an extended version of the pie rule).

boardgame: Run, Fight, or Die!: Jasper City Expansion
description:
This expansion consists of 9 new Follower Cards

They can be identified by the a little "JC" near the red car in the lower right of the card.

The list of cards is:

Bothersome Gas Clerk
the Bowler
HS Physics Teacher
the Gun Nut
Ice Cream Vendor
the Lounge Singer
the Mad Doctor
Nostalgic Punk Rocker
Soldier on Leave

These came with the kickstarter version. Future availability is unknown.

Objective: collect most of those insects (20) placed on the board;
learn more about insects 
- moving by turn -
simple 'Roll & Move' game
roll the die & move forward; ending on a card space collect it if available; game ends after all cards are taken.

boardgame: Soldiers of Glory: An Epic Game of Swashbuckling Adventure during the 1600s.
description:
BACKGROUND: Soldiers of Glory is an adventure game for 1-4 Players, where players assume the roles of young Soldiers of Fortune during the 1600s. Each Player creates a Hero Character in search of Wealth, Adventure and Renown. To survive and thrive in a land torn apart by Religious Wars, Intrigue, Piracy and Plague, the Player Heroes must seek employment from a variety of Patrons and perform Assignments such as escorting envoys, participating in Battles, dueling rivals, espionage, political intrigue, rescuing damsels, and assassinations. As your Hero gains experience and renown they can improve their attributes and status.

GAME OBJECTIVE: The Objective of each Player Hero is to Gain as much Renown Points (RP) to become a Nobleman. Game length is determined by the Nobility Title desired: Baron (60RP), Count (80RP) or Duke (100RP). In order to become a Nobleman, the Player Hero must complete Assignments to earn enough RP to purchase the various Titles.

GAME FEATURES: Soldiers of Glory features lots and lots of colorful game counters including Swordsmen, Cavaliers, Brigands, Soldiers, Assassins, Nobles, Femmes Fatal, fierce animals, and much more. The game is played on a 6 Sheet Map with Large Hexes and a variety of terrain. Game includes Duels, Skirmishes, Battles, Fortress Assaults, Combat at Sea, Pirates and more.

If you are a fan of the Three Musketeers, any Errol Flynn movie, Alatriste, and other swashbuckling tales, this game is for you.

ASSEMBLY TIME: This game, since there are a lot of counters, may take longer than the designer's other games. It took me about 4 hours at industrious pace.

boardgame: The Three Pillars: Allies & Adversaries Expansion Set
description:
With six additional locations to investigate, and twenty-four new event cards, including a sub-class of Personalities to encounter, this fun expansion will certainly add a new twist to the game. Now you will be able to utilize abilities from a Support Team to help you close cases, but be advised your opponent will also have access to them as well. And beware, not everyone you encounter will be on your on your side!
 You can add these new cards to your core set, or choose to supplement the Cases and Events Decks with the Allies & Adversaries expansion.

The ultimate party game, Don’t Be a Loser was designed specifically for highly social situations.  It not only provides continual laughs and non-stop "smack talk", but it also allows players to freely flow in and out as they wish without interrupting the game.  This flexibility, mixed with a uniquely interactive game play, make Don’t Be a Loser an absolute hit among the fun-loving party crowd.

The goal of the game is to be the Biggest Winner, but more importantly, to not be the Biggest Loser.  n order to advance down the board players compete in a wide variety of one-on-one challenges including physical challenges, Mathlete questions, Super Sleuth puzzles, acting challenges, creativity challenges and much more.  Other players are able to bet on which player they think will win, keeping everyone involved as much as they wish to be and making the competition to not be a Loser fierce.

The exciting twist: before starting the game players all decide on the penalty the Biggest Loser will pay.  It could be as simple as having to sing a silly song or to buy the next round of beverages, to more serious stakes such as the Biggest Loser having to wash the Biggest Winner’s car.  Needless to say, you don’t want to be a loser!

Round Ten is a 2 player table top card game. Players try to gain favor of the crowd to win rounds.

Players each have a deck with the same number of punches. Each round players press their luck by trying to take as many hit points without going over a hit point limit.

The primary game mechanics are a combination of strategy in bluffing along with pressing your luck. This game is casual and compact, its portable, (cards). You can set it up and play it quickly.

Dogsitter is a fast dexterity game for families. Fido is a really playful dog: he wants to go out for a walk, play with the ball, gnaw a bone. Players will literally throw the toys to the barking dog, trying to center the "bark tiles" with their items, with a mix of aim and manual skill, in order to give the dog exactly what he wants. Being a dogsitter is not an easy work, but how nice is to see Fido happy!

Let's take a ride on Circus Express, the first circus on rails! But... what happened? Who mixed all the coaches?

In Circus Express, players play tiles from their hand to try to build the same pattern on their hand and on the table. The first player that builds the right train is the winner!

Worldcup is a simple game of rolling dice for 2 players with theme football. The game board represents a football-field divided into zones with a black number (in favour of the orange player) and zones with a white number (for the red player). Players have to reach their opponent’s goal by throwing 2 dice at once. Starting from the middle they put the ball forward on the opponent’s half as many places as they have thrown. A player may continue during the same turn until the ball lands on a zone with a number in the opponent’s colour. The ball keeps moving to and from until a goal has been scored (reaching no. 45 exactly). Then the other player starts in the middle again.Some zones are yellow-coloured which represent certain events like free kick, penalty or corner with their respective special actions. The player who scored most goals during playing time of 2x15 minutes wins the game. If both players scored an equal amount of goals it is a draw.

More or Less is a quiz game in which players have to answer (numerical) questions. The player whose guess come closest to the actual answers gains a point. But there is a catch! For each question, it is determined whether only guesses that are higher than the actual answer will count (more), or only guesses that are lower (less).

The first player to gain five points wins the game.

Right or Wrong is a quiz game in which the players have to guess whether statements are right or wrong. Each round, a card is drawn with three statements on it. The players make their guesses; the more they have right, the more points they score.

More or Less 2 is the sequel to More or Less. While the latter game is most suited for a large number of players, More or Less 2 is designed as a game for up to four players or teams.

More or Less 2 is a quiz game in which players have to answer (numerical) questions. The player whose guess come closest to the actual answers gains a point. But there is a catch! For each question, it is determined whether only guesses that are higher than the actual answer will count (more), or only guesses that are lower (less).

The first player to gain five points wins the game.

Het Groot Vermakelijk Schapekoppenspel (“Large Entertaining Sheepheads Game” in Dutch) is a Game of Goose-like game which is actually a children’s game, but is nice to play for everyone interested in the city of Dordrecht in The Netherlands. “Schapekop” is the nickname for a citizen of Dordrecht because of a saga about trying to smuggle a sheep dressed as a man into the city in order to prevent paying toll for cattle without succes.
Players follow a walking route through Dordrecht by dice throwing. Landing on some spots on the route means prosperity, on others adversity (gaining / losing chips and/or going forward/backward), with a description concerning Dordrecht. The first player to reach the end of the route wins the pot of chips / the game.

From the box:

"Welcome to Vienna!
This city is not only a world famous cultural hub, it is in fact the city with the highest quality of urban life on our planet. A huge amount of museums await the public with unique art and science exhibitions. From the world famous opera to clubs, from shopping to recreational areas - all are easily reached by public transport. There are many charming cafés which serve cake specialities or the viennese favourite, the "Melange" to strengthen tourists on their way. Vienna has something to offer any taste - day or night!"

This simple game is played on a city map of Vienna. The map features stations of public transport facilites: underground, bus and tramway. At the beginning of the game, a player's pawn is put on a random station. He then draws a number of destination cards showing the stations he must visit in order to win the game.

On his turn, a player rolls five special dice, showing the symbols "underground", "bus" and "tramway". He then moves his pawn from station to station, using only the transport facilities his dice offers him. If he reaches a station which corresponds with one of his destination cards, he may discard that destination card.
 You win the game if you have visited all destinations on all your destination cards.

De Sang et de Tourbe (Of Blood & Peat) is a wargame about the conquest of Estonia by the Sword Brothers in the early XIIIth century. It includes a rules booklet of 12 pages, 108 counters, one A3 map.

The players (one Estonian and one Sword Brother) first calculate their ressources points (PR).
Then starting with the player who won the initiative, the players can buy stratagems that will influence the game. They can also buy reinforcements with these PRs.
With their remaining PRs, both players can alternately launch raids or conquests of the enemy territories.

The game ends when Riga is taken by the Estonians, when all Estonian has fallen under the Sword Brothers rule or at the end of the last turn (year 1225).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Vermeer Spel is a promotional Monopoly-like game developed by Identity Games in commission of the Vermeer Groep (projects in construction and infrastructure). Players try to earn money by realizing building projects. A project can be realized if both development card and realization card for that particular project are in use. Cooperation is important as these cards can be in use by different players when realizing a project. Additional money can be earned by players if an opponent lands on their building project already  realized. If all construction sites are built up, the game ends. The player who owns most money at that moment wins the game.

Cosmic Rally is a fast paced, space racing card game for 2-6 players. Average laps can finish in less than 30 seconds! Race as many or few laps as you like. Tournament play (5-20 laps) lasts 30 minutes.

Players select a pilot and ship which determines the number of cards they must discard per lap. Players win laps by getting rid of their cards first. Watch out for other pilots as they try to slow you down with rockets and mines. Each lap will require you to dodge space hazards as well.

In this simple children's game players take their turn by drawing a coloured wink from a bag and moving a piggybank piece to the corresponding coloured field on the board. Drawing a wink of the same colour as the field the pig is already on triggers a payout for that player, which takes the form of matching winks accumulated in the pig to sets on drawn cards. The goal is to have the most cards when they run out. (It is not actually necessary to use the pig and board at all.)

boardgame: Boom Bokken: "Perfect" and "3 Hit Combo" promo cards
description:
Those promo cards are special goodies to Essen 2014.

The "Perfect" card can be played at any moment. Indicate a player, choose a number and make a call (more or less), and the game goes on.

The "3 Hit Combo" card is played as a Timeout : indicate a player who place the previously played cards under the draw pile, then draw 3 cards, and restarts the game

Black Hands of Hathoway is a quick, family-friendly game of Court intrigue, dastardly underhanded plots and cunning strategy. With elements of take that and card taking, you must out wit other players to claim the most treasure. As a thief of the Black Hands, you can coerce members of Court into being your unwitting accomplices in this classic game of Battle of the Wits. Pull off great heists and save the King's gold ... by stealing it first.

Unique game mechanic!   
Fun, fast and easy to learn!   
Great replayability!
Vibrant, hand-drawn artwork!
Perfect for all ages!

Gameplay Overview:

The game is played over the course of turns called thefts. On a given theft, one player known as the Thief, will secretly choose a card to steal from the Castle.

All other players will try to predict what card the Thief will take and attempt to stop them. If predicted correctly, the Thief is caught and a reward is given to the captor. Otherwise, the treasure is taken.

Along with Treasures, Officials will be corruptible and make future thefts even more cunning and deceitful. Be warned though, as more Treasure is taken from the Castle, the stakes continue to go up and each heist becomes that much trickier.

The thief with the most treasure at the end of the game is the winner.

Munchkin Legenden 2: Fauntastische Spiele consists of German versions of the two small English-language Munchkin Legends expansions Munchkin Legends 2: Faun and Games and Munchkin Legends 3: Myth Prints.

Thus, Fauntastische Spiele introduces a new race — the Faun — as well as the Gladiator, who gets tougher when his life is at stake! This 112-card set has new monsters to fight, new Treasures to gain, and jokes and puns based on figures from mythology and history. You can use the "Spear of Destiny" to fight "J.R.R. Trollkin" or fend off the "Wendigo" with your "Hell Toupee".
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Munchkin Turbobooster 1 contains three out-of-print German-language Munchkin boosters — Star Munchkin: Raumschiffe, Munchkin: Feenstaub and Munchkin: Zum Töten freigegeben — as well as the three notebooks of Munchkin Journal Pack 1 and three Munchkin dice.

Munchkin Turbobooster 2 contains three out-of-print German-language Munchkin boosters — Munchkin Reloaded!, Munchkinomicon and Munchkin: Monsterverstärker — as well as the three notebooks of Munchkin Journal Pack 2 and three Munchkin dice.

This is a five card Super Tooth expansion exclusive to those who backed the Super Tooth Kickstarter.  This expansion includes the cards: Stampede, Cave, Oviraptor, Lost, and T-Rex. Two additional cards, the Kickstarter Exclusive Cards reference card and the T-Rex reference card, provide an explanation and rules for each card in the expansion.

boardgame: Super Tooth: Kickstarter Backer Inspired Cards
description: This is a five card Super Tooth expansion inspired by backers of the Super Tooth Kickstarter.  This expansion includes the cards: Abydosaurus, Pack Hunters, Tectonic Shift, Brood Parasite, and Dino Bones. Two additional cards, the Kickstarter Backer Inspired Cards reference card and the Dino Bones reference card, provide an explanation and rules for each card in the expansion.

boardgame: Super Tooth: Titanic Tree Eaters Expansion
description: This expansion to Super Tooth consists of nine cards: 1 Foliage Event Card, 2 awesome Meat-Eating Ceratosaurus Cards, and 6 Plant-Eating Diplodocus Cards.  The explanation and rules for each card in this expansion can be found on the Expansion Events reference card.

This expansion to Super Tooth consists of nine cards: 1 Hot Sun Event Card, 2 awesome Meat-Eating Spinosaurus Cards, and 6 Plant-Eating Corythosaurus Cards. The explanation and rules for each card in this expansion can be found on the Expansion Events reference card.

boardgame: Super Tooth: End of Days Expansion
description: This expansion to Super Tooth is comprised of nine cards: 1 Asteroid Event Card, 2 fearsome Meat-Eating Gigantosaurus cards, and 6 Plant-Eating Plateosaurus cards.  The explanation and rules for each card may be found on the Expansion Events reference card.

This expansion to Super Tooth is comprised of 6 unique armored and spiked Plant-Eater cards: Ankylosaurus, Einiosaurus, Kentrosaurus, Sauropelta, Stegosaurus, and Styracosaurus.  The explanation for their special ability may be found on the Expansion Events reference card.

This expansion to Super Tooth is comprised of 7 cards: 1 Time Portal Event card and 6 unique Time Traveler cards.  The Time Travelers can be very helpful to players during the game, but are susceptible to Landscape-clearing Events and will get sucked back in time by the Time Portal Event.  The explanation of the Time Portal Event and each of the Time Traveler abilities may be found on the 3 Time Traveler reference cards.

This expansion to Super Tooth is comprised of a plastic card stand and three cards: 1 Roaming Daspletosaurus Event card and 2 cutout figure cards for you to create your own stand-up cutout of this fearsome predator.  The explanation and rules for this expansion may be found on the Roaming Daspletosaurus reference card.

This promo card was originally made available at PretzCon as part of the Super Tooth 2014 PretzCon edition.  The card shares the special ability of the Triceratops and the Herbivorous Heroes, the ability to chase off Meat-Eaters.

boardgame: Super Tooth: Dino Bones Promo Card
description: This promo card for Super Tooth is marked OSFest 2014.

This game takes place during the 1920's prohibition era. Players are professional bootleggers tasked with moving alcohol regularly shipped to their ports to the different establishments around town through a combination of their runner units, dogface or muscle units, and their special units, the latter of which differs from player to player. Opposing players will also try to supply establishments as well as take over establishments already being supplied so players need to balance expanding their empire with defending it. The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach 5,000 rubes, the in-game currency. Money is earned from the different establishments around town as well as start up money.

You are a Storm Chaser in Tornado Alley. Get pictures of the twisters to earn money, upgrade, and expand your Storm Research Vehicle fleet. The player with the most money at the end of Tornado Season wins.

StormChasers takes place over 10 rounds or at the end of the round in which all tornadoes have dissipated, whichever comes first. Each round starts with the Tornado Phase, in which tornadoes are spawned and moved according to the Tornado Deck. Following the Tornado Phase is the Action Phase in which players spend action points to move vehicles and take pictures of tornadoes. Money is gained from taking pictures with the first pictures earning the most money, and further pictures earning successively less. Following the Action Phase is the Upgrade/Cleanup phase. Here you must balance end game money with upgrading to better protected and faster vehicles.

Gameplay occurs on a 20x20 hexagonal grid. The board is littered with many different type of "Landscape" tiles [house, barn, and field tiles] that inhibit movement, but also provide bonus points if a picture is captured during destruction. These tiles are placed randomly during setup. Road tiles are also included and provide bonuses to movement.  Road tiles are placed either according to scenario rules or by each player in turn order.

The tornadoes are spawned and moved via the "Storm Deck" which is built during setup. The game's speed is dictated by the number of spawning cards, with the higher ratio equaling a faster game. Tornadoes dissipate randomly via dice and time track mechanic.

At the end of the game, players count only money on hand. The player with the most wins, ties are broken in favor of the player with the most pictures taken.

Players take turns to move their magicians over field, reflected at an angle of 90 degrees from the mirrors on the field and passing through the portals. Their goal - to collect charms and runes, making of them required magical combination. Combinations will vary from game to game. Charms contain ancient spell that can be used during the game to influence the field, a magician and opponents. At end players compute VP.

A core game mechanic is similar to Ricochet Robots, but turn based, contains a shared quests and other additional elements.

Игроки по очереди перемещают своих магов по разлинованому полю, отражаясь под углом 90 градусов от зеркал на поле и проходя через порталы. Их цель - собрать амулеты и руны, составив из них необходимые магические комбинации. Комбинации будут отличаться от игры к игре. Амулеты содержат древние заклятья, которые можно применять по ходу игры, чтобы влиять на поле, своего мага и оппонентов.

Magikaboom is a dice game of sorcery and potions. Dice represent ingredients; players can add ingredients, activating their effects and rolling the dice to score points. But potions can be...explosive! One ingredient too much can blow the whole cauldron! Would you take the risk and become the most famous magician of the realm?

Today is the big day!  Team leaders in Monsterland will choose their teams to go infest dungeons and castles. Will you pick the best team?

Players must play tiles face down. If a player forms a line of four tiles is allowed to reveal one tile; when tiles are over, players choose a row and a column to score points: the monster leader with the most powerful team is the winner... but be careful! Some heroes are trying to infiltrate Monsterland to put a spoke in your wheel!

Mostrilli is a tile placement involving a bit of memory and basic strategy.

Squirrels are stocking on food supplies for the winter, but Ghiotto, the most gluttonous and lazy squirrel of the forest, goes from hole to hole to grab some acorn from other squirrels!

In the funny tile game Ghiotto di Ghiande, players must choose each turn a bunch of nuts - without knowing how many leaves will be needed to cover it - or a group of leaves - without knowing how many nuts it contains - to stock on supplies before hibernation, avoiding carefully to drag Ghiotto's attention by having the biggest stack of leaves at the end of the turn. There are three types of nuts, and players will score only two, so... choose carefully!

Potje Duizend (Game of Thousand in Dutch) is a simple game of dice throwing with a calculating aspect. Every player gets a sheet with a matrix of 9 cells, divided in 3 rows of 3 cells each. On his turn a player throws 1 die and notes his result in one of the cells on his sheet. Doing this he should try to achieve a result of one thousand (or as close as possible) when making a final addition if the matrix is full (by considering each row of 3 cells as a number and adding those 3 numbers). The player closest to thousand is the winner. Attention! If one of the diagonals shows 3 same numbers, this number may be multiplied by 10 and used as a bonus to come closer to thousand.

Otto the Octopus really needs a snack. Using their fingers like the tentacles of Otto, children try to catch a lot of colored fishes — just one for each finger! — before they swim away, due to the arrival of the clumsy scuba diver.

In Polpastrelli, players must have quick reflexes, finger speed, and a bit of luck as they try to catch the most rare and valuable fishes.

"Where's my beach umbrella? That's my towel! Come on, give me my swimming trunk". What a mess! The items in the beach bag are all mixed up!

Tutti al Mare is a fast and funny card game for sea-loving players who must take the right items from a messy pile, picking the one that belongs to them. Players will pick and trade items with their "beach umbrella neighbors" in order to be the first to be ready for the first swim of the season.

Tutti al Mare is a fast and funny game for 2-6 slobby bathers.

Körkortsspelet is an educational Swedish trivia game that teaches traffic rules.

From the box cover:

"Så - ska du ta B-körkort? Eller behålla det du redan har! Körkortsspelet är ett roligt och lärorikt kortspel med hundratals frågor som du i så fall behöver kunna svara på. Vägmärken, trafikregler, miljö, bilens funktion och mycket annat avhandlas i 360 frågor. 
Du lär dig teorin - lekande lätt!"

Card game for children.
6 cards are dealt to each player.
The first player draws a card, and discards another one face up on the discard pile. The next player can draw a card from the draw pile or the discard pile and then discard a card from their hand. And so on until a player has the 5 cards of a kind (5 cards of different background colour but with the same "beast") and shouts "Bestium!".
There are 6 suits (6 different "beasts"). For each "beast" there are 5 different coloured cards, numbered 1-5, per duplicate (so 10 cards with the same beast, 2 for each of the 5 colours).

There's a "Memory"-like variant of the game.

Letterbingo is a simple word game. Players roll 6 dice at once with 1 letter on every side. During a certain time span each player writes down as many words as possible which can be formed by the letters of the dice results. The Hoppe logo can be used as any letter. The player who has formed most words wins the game. Alternatively players can agree to grant 1 point to the player who calls the first correct word after the dice roll. Then play until the first player has reached 10 points. That player wins the game then.

Dubbel-Dobbel is a dice rolling game with a memory aspect. On their turn players throw 2 dice: 1 with a colour on each side, 1 with a Renault vehicle type on each side. Then they may take one of the closed spread cards on the table. If the card chosen matches both die results the player keeps it otherwise it will be put back on the table closed. A player turn only ends if a card is put back on the table. After all cards are taken from the table the player with most cards wins the game.

There are 36 cards in the game, a colour die (6 colours) and a number die (the 6 car types). Cars used are the Renault 4, 5, 9, 11, 18 and 25. Colours of the cards: Red, Green, Blue, Yellow, Purple and Black.

The Captain signals... "RED ALERT"
Moments later you and your crew will gather together and defend yourself from the alien menace. The only option is to escape the ISS Powhatan and destroy the ship before it enters Celeritas orbit. Continue the story. Save the crew. Win the game.

Escape Pod is the first in a trilogy of co-operative games about leading a doomed crew to their rescue. Each player assumes the role of a member of the ISS Powhatan, a supply ship in the Junxti system. When the ship is boarded by hostile aliens, the crew must work together to gather supplies and prepare the escape pod. Finally, you must activate the self-destruct system and escape to the planet below!

Gameplay consists of players taking turns. At the beginning of their turn each player rolls dice to place aliens on the board and then navigates the ship using their action points. Players can search rooms to reveal item cards that will assist them in their efforts. When a player comes into a room with aliens, combat ensues utilizing an 8 sided die with the outcome influenced by weapons the player may have. Certain rooms such as the fuel tanks and the transporter rooms can be "activated" to accomplish the goal and allow the players to escape!

Player characters include; Captain, Security Officer, Transporter Chief, Damage Controlman, Quartermaster, Chief Engineer, and Sensor Operator. Each character has a unique ability to assist in the players winning the game.

boardgame: Five Men in Normandy .30 Cal edition
description:
A skirmish war game for WW2 battles. Playable with any miniatures already in your colletion though better suited for 15mm and up.

If you have been on the look out for a skirmish game that gives you plenty to work with, 
which gives you a fully featured world war 2 campaign experience from the perspective of a small "band of brothers", 
which gives you tools with which to create interesting missions and captivating scenarios
which presents unpredictable situations and characters, keeping each campaign and each battle fresh and interesting
then Five Men in Normandy is the game for you.

                    
                
            
        
    


The rules provide the following content:

    Comprehensive skirmish rules, including rules for searching, patrol and stealth missions, reaction fire, mortar support, a task system for unforeseen events and persuasion rules. 



    Advice and rules to assist solo gamers included.



    Weapon and equipment lists



    Force tables to randomly generate a quick group of soldiers. Nationalities covered are British and Commonwealth, Americans, Soviets, Germans, Finland, Free French, Free Polish and Italians.



    Character creation system to add that extra flavour to your campaign. Includes motivations, backgrounds and prior experience as well as a skill system.



    Full featured campaign rules that link your battles together, include random events, injuries and court martials, non player characters and unforeseen circumstances. 



    Squad based morale system in the campaign that is influenced by your progress and success, the personality of your men and events that happen. 


The rules are quick to play, use a creative dice based system to quickly resolve fire and moves through turns very quickly. The turn sequence has unexpected moments permitting players to take advantage of a situation as it develops or forcing them to react on their feet when something goes awry.
While larger forces are possible, the typical game involves only a handful of soldiers on each side, allowing you to focus on the personalities you have generated in your campaign. 
Pick up games can be played quickly and efficiently as well. Simply roll up two forces, pick a mission and have a go at it.

This small expansion contains 12 red dice (with 2 traps, 2 Fleas and 2 Zoo Keepers on each die), which are mixed in with the base game dice.

Fleas can be played alone or together with other animals. When played alone, Fleas are smaller than Earthworms in size; when played together with other animals, Fleas are counted as the animals they are played with.

If there are already Fleas in play, a player who then puts out other animals with a larger body size on the playing surface will get all of those Fleas in play.

At the end of the game, each Flea side that faces up counts as 8 deduction points.

Administrators cannot be put out to play.
Immediately after throwing your own red dice, for each Administrator side that faces up, you get to choose one target white die (which can either be from your own collection or from any other player’s collection) and turn it to any side you desire to have face up. At the end of the game, each Administrator side that faces up counts as 9 deduction points.

Love Hotel is a party game with teenagers, mainly girls, being the target audience. In the game Love Hotel you choose a (fictive) partner. Your goal is to get to the love suite before any of the other couples for some private time. You are only allowed to enter the love suite after you've collected four types of cushions for your bed. Let the race begin!

You and your opponents move around the board, answering questions and acting out tasks for each of the four categories. Doing so correctly will earn you a cushion of the corresponding color. Helping your opponent with a task will earn you a heart. Hearts can be used to buy cushions or to mend broken hearts. If you land on a broken heart tile and don't mend it (by choice or because you don't have any heart tokens) you risk losing one of your cushions. The first person to collect all four cushions and make it to the love suite wins the game!

description:
Each player has 12 runners (infantry) and 4 flyers (planes). The goal is to occupy the enemy camp with 6 runners and all 4 flyers. Enemy pieces are eliminated from the game by enclosing them with ones own pieces. The game is won by occupying the enemy camp or by eliminating 6 enemy runners or 1 flyer.

The runners move any number of spaces in any straight direction (not diagonal) but may not move over friendly or enemy pieces.

The flyers move any number of spaces in any straight or diagonal direction and may fly over any other pieces. They may not land on a space, that is enclosed by enemy pieces.

Dozen Doubloons is a solitaire card game for anyone aged 8 and up.  It takes one minute to learn, just minutes to play, and keeps challenging your wits for hours. Kids won't know they're learning how to think strategically, and adults will wish they remembered how! Four levels of difficulty provide a challenge for anyone who sits down with Dozen Doubloons in front of them.

The deck consists of 54 cards.  There are four sets of numbers 1 through 12, plus 4 BLOCK cards, and 2 WILD cards. The object of the game is to deplete all cards in play.

Shuffle. Deal 6 stacks of four cards face down, and a fifth card on each stack face up.  The remaining cards are your draw stack. To play: combine up to three cards that equal 12, and place them in a discard pile.  Turn over the next card in each of your stacks.  If a BLOCK card shows up, do not remove the card but move it and all cards underneath it to a position above any other unblocked stack.  You may not touch the BLOCK stack until the stack beneath it is depleted.  Once the stack is depleted, slide the whole BLOCK stack down into position, discard the BLOCK card, turn over the next card, and continue playing.

When there are no cards that equal 12 (or if you choose not to use a combination that is available), turn over the first DRAW card. If it's usable, use it. If not, place it face down in front of you.  Continue to DRAW cards as needed, and keep placing them to the right of the original card. If a BLOCK card comes up from the DRAW pile, discard it and ALL the other cards accumulated from previous draws--they are now out of play.

Once you have gone through your DRAW pile one time, the game is over.  If you have not depleted all six original stacks, you have lost.

This is a summary of the rules and not meant to cover all possible scenarios.

description: A small expansion for Alley Thieves. At the start of the game, everyone gets a single guild card. This can be used once per game for a specific benefit (winning a die roll tie, bonus to die roll, etc.).

The Amega Weapon is all about defeating your opponents in a deep space war while surviving the dreaded Black Star, which is a totally random object that wreaks destruction wherever it goes.

While conventional weapons cause damage, The Amega Weapon quickly destroys ships, therefore, players want to obtain the parts to the weapon, build the weapon, then use the weapon to obliterate their opponents. The last star warrior standing rules the Parax System.

The Amega Weapon is played on a game board that is created by placing 4 different boards together in any way, shape, or fashion that you choose with over 30,000 possible game board variations.  The boards are the beautiful artwork of Greg Winters, illustrator of Star Wars featuring 12 planets, 6 star bases, novae, and asteroids.  Greg also created our fantastic box cover.

The Amega Weapon showcases 7 custom designed pewter playing pieces that we spin cast ourselves. Additional components include: top notch quality Mission Profile cards, Deep Space cards, Asteroid cards, and 6 denominations of money along with two 6-sided dice, a 10-sided die, and a 20-sided die.

Players can elect to play one-on-one, team play, or a regular game.  The game is player tested for ages 12 and up with a special kid's game for ages 6 and up that does not interfere with the normal game.
The questions you have to ask yourself are: Can you obtain The Amega Weapon?  Can you obliterate your opponents?  Will you survive the Black Star?  Do you even think you stand a chance?  Got control???  Think again, lol!!!

Planetaire is a astronomy-themed educational rummy-type card game for one to four players. This description will focus on the 2 to 4 player game, but there are also variants for one player or for 4 players on 2 teams. When playing with 2 or more people, the object of the game is to be the first player to make The Grand Tour by collecting all 9 planetary suites, including Pluto as a planet since the game was published before 2006. In case of a tie (2 or more players completing The Grand Tour in the same round), the player who completed the Grand Tour who has the most points wins. A planetary suite consists of a planet and all of its moons. For the sake of not making the sets impossible, the gas giants Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune each have 4 moons in this game. The moonless Mercury and Venus count as complete sets by themselves. The planets are categorized as inner or outer based on their position relative to the main Asteroid Belt located between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.

The game has 92 playing cards, a pad of scoring sheets, a rules booklet, and 4 reference cards, which list all the cards needed for each of the 9 suites. The set of cards are color-coded by type, and each planet is also in a different color to make it easier to see what goes together. There are 2 of each planet and the 20 moons used in this game, and 1 each of 8 asteroids and 8 comets. There are also 10 wild cards which can serve in place of other cards, 2 each of 5 different types. The two Planet X cards will substitute for planets. The other 8 wild cards can all substitute for moons, with 4 of them also being usable for an asteroid, the other 4 for a comet. There are also 8 action cards, 4 which reverse the player order and give the person playing the card an extra turn, and 4 which discard a moon card (you can either discard one yourself, or make the next player discard one before beginning their turn, which can result in some complications if they don't have any moons or moon wild cards in their hand). Action cards are played by discarding them at the end of your turn (if you choose to use the discard moon card on yourself, you get to discard that card and one of your moons, with the moon card on top in the discard pile).

During each turn, you start by drawing until you have 10 cards in your hand (at game start, you have 9). So the smaller your hand is at the beginning of your turn, the better are your chances to draw cards you need. You can then lay down any number of comets and asteroids, up to the maximum of 2 of each, but can only lay down the planet and moon cards when you have a complete set of the same color - a planetary suite, or enough wild cards to substitute for the cards you don't have. You can use any number of wild cards in making a set but cannot play 2 of the same moon in a suite - but must have 1 of each or a suitable wild card. You can also pick up wild cards or complete planetary suites if you can replace them from your hand. You don't need to replace a Wild Card in order to pick it up if it was acting as a comet or asteroid. You can use a planet you scored in a previous round, which is called Retro-Touring. Laying down a previously scored planetary suite does not earn you more points but can serve the player doing this in several ways. You end each turn by discarding a card from your hand, whether or not you laid down any cards on the table. You can not have more than 2 asteroids, 2 comets, 1 Inner Planetary Suite, and 1 Outer Planetary Suite laid down on the table at the same time. If you have all of these in front of you, you are said to "Go Out" and the round ends immediately.  When one player completes all the cards to Go Out, ending the round, all the other players are then said to be Set. "Set" players are penalized for the point value of the cards in their hand. You count up the points of all the cards you laid down, with the planets and Planet X on the table being multiplied by 10, and then everyone except the player Going Out have to subtract the point value of the cards in their hand (planets are at face value for this portion of the scoring). This can result in a negative score. Most of the wild cards have a value of 0, with the exception of the 2 Planet X cards, which are worth 10. The moons, asteroids, and comets are worth 1. Action cards in the hand of a "set" player cost them two points. The planets increase in point value from 1 to 9 based on distance from the Sun (so Mercury is 1, Pluto is 9, etc). Usually one player is designated as the scorer and writes down everyone's points at the end of each round. You only score for a completed planetary suite the first time you use that particular planet.

The rulebook suggests that a different player should be dealer in each round. The Dealer rotates counter-clockwise in each round, and the players also go in this order, with the player to the right of the dealer going first. This direction is used because it corresponds with the orbits of the planets around the sun. This brings up some of the other educational aspects of this game. Each of the cards has a photograph or art of the object, and a few interesting facts about the object pictured. The Wild Cards are named after and depict other objects in the Solar System that are not in the normal cards. There is also more information and many website links of other educational resources in the rulebook.

There is a Solitaire version of the game, in which there is no point scoring or shuffling of cards within a round, the planets have to be obtained in a specific order, you deal cards and draw to a Phantom player (which are not available until you shuffle for the next round), and there are also 9 cards permanently discarded at the beginning of the game.

boardgame: Hunters over Korea: A Solitaire Game of Air Combat over Korea (1950-1953)
description:
Hunters over Korea is a Game where you are a Commander of a Fighter-Bomber Squadron during the Korean War (1950-1953). Your Goal is to manage your aircraft and Complete a Campaign consisting of a series of Missions and score as many Victory Points (VP) as possible to assist the war efforts. Campaigns can be Short (8 Missions), Moderate (12 Missions), or Long (16 Missions). Squadron Aircraft include P-51 Mustangs, F-80 Shooting Stars and F-86 Sabres.

VPs are earned by destroying enemy aircraft and ground targets. You lose VPs by losing aircraft and failing to complete missions. The Player moves his/her Squadron Flight Counter on the Battlefield Map, however, combat is resolved easily off-map using an Inititiative Track. If you enjoy movies like "The Bridges of Toko-Ri" and "The Hunters", or Korean War Air Combat in general, this game is for you.

Missions include Bomber Escort, MIG CAP, Ground Support, and Tactical Air Strikes. Rules provide means for Pilot improvement and Squadron Aircraft Upgrades.

The Game, also, includes rules and counters for Naval Air Squadrons including F4-U Corsaires, A-1 Skyraiders and F9f Panthers.

Most Games usually take 2-4 Hours to print out and assemble, depending on the number of counters, Cards and Mapsheets.

description:
Memory game.  Cards are placed on the board face-down. These are 'Guests' at the party. A die is used for movement. You may go backwards or forwards. If a player lands on a guest card, they can guess its name. If correct, they take a chip in the appropriate colour. Whether guessed correctly or not, the card is revealed to all the players and players must remember which guest is where. Things get more difficult if the ghost Kasimir is revealed. He is a pest and all the guest cards are taken up, shuffled and then put on the board again.

First player to get five different colour chips, wins.

In Auge um Auge ("Eye for an Eye"), each player controls a six-member gang that wants to hand out eight black eyes before any other gang can do so.

In the dice brawls that take place, any gang can support any other — but of course each gang is interested only in its own victory, so don't rely too much on the support of others. In the event of a tie in terms of which gangs have delivered the most black eyes, the gang with the fewer black eyes of its own wins.

Eternity Wars: The Card Game is a 2 player strategic card game of epic warfare. Players construct buildings to establish economic superiority on an expanding battlefield.  Armies are deployed to clash in fantastic battles.  And in the end, the player that destroys the enemy Government Center first, is the winner.

The game draws elements from the Eternity Wars board game, a game inspired by Real Time Strategy video games. After running a Kickstarter campaign that failed to fund, only 20 units were ever produced.

Eternity Wars: The Card Game uses an "expanding battlefield" mechanic, where an invisible grid is created and anchored by the buildings that each player constructs.  The more buildings a player constructs, the more room he/she has to deploy and move units.

description:
•    Play as one of the 6 cool animal characters – Iscratchu the Enforcer Cat, Rings the Criminal Raccoon and many more.
•    Each team has a unique story and special trash affinity
•    Battle the other players for the 13 different types of trash -  BEWARE THE SLUGS!
•    91 Trash cards,  12 custom dice and various booster and blocker cards
•    This is a dynamic card game using dice based combat and YES, rummaging.

Game Summary
Dumpster Brawl is about rolling around in the alley against your opponents and trying to collect the sets of trash you need to claim victory from the other animals.  
Players meet in the alley of the city and take turns fighting for the control of the dumpster (where the trash is) and the right to rummage through it looking for what they need to complete their collections.

To earn the right to rummage the dumpster, and the other players' hands, you need to defeat the other players in dice based combat and win your rumbles.  The attacking is rolling for crowbars and the defender is trying to get enough trashcan lids to defend.
The winner gets to root through the other players hand, or even put nasty things into it.  If the winner is the person who started they fight they then get to rummage through the pile of trash.
Rummage through the trash, while collecting trash cards and trying to create matching sets of items. Match the image and names of the items on the face of your cards.

The goal of all versions of Dumpster Brawl is to collect enough matching trash items to make at least three (3) sets of three (3) or more matching items (such as three pizza boxes, three apple cores, and three soda cans) while not having any extra cards in your hand that are not part of the matched sets you are laying down. All extra cards, that do not match your victory sets, have to be discarded before you can lay your sets down and win.

Note: You can have more than three sets and the sets can be larger than three cards.
All play styles of Dumpster Brawl require the collection of the trash cards.

There are thirteen (13) types of trash items and seven (7) of each item in the game.
While rooting around in the trash you are, also, looking for booster cards that can help you win and defeating the blocker cards you might encounter as they can hurt your chances at winning but beware the slugs!

Key Features
•    Quick fun game play
•    Multiple play methods
•    Each play is unique
•    You have to make sure you keep and discard the right cards and play your boosters at the right time

Old Town Robbery is a communication-based memory game for families with easy rules.

In the first part of the game, the robber wanders through the city, and the players construct a story based on the places he visits. In the second part, players take turns playing the sheriff who is trying to retrace the steps of the robber, visiting the same places as the robber has before. The story element helps players do just that, and they earn points by correctly guessing the next building.

simple 'Roll&Move' game. Move forward according die roll; ending on an illustrated space follow instructions.

boardgame: The Reservation Road Planner: Tribal Board Game
description: The Reservation Road Planner: Tribal Board Game which was developed to educate its players about the environment in which transportation is planning, financed, and developed and to help facilitate increased public involvement within the tribal community in their transportation initiatives. This game has been effective in increasing the availability of user-friendly information regarding the transportation planning process. It has also enabled the tribal community to understand the challenges associated with highway planning and to become more engaged in transportation planning projects in their community. The game describes the various stages involved in reservation road planning including: Develop a Plan, Indian Reservation Road Inventory, Funding, Preconstruction, and Construction. An overview is also provided regarding the various public involvement options associated with each planning stage. The game utilizes scenarios to explain the challenges associated with each stage. Some of the lessons learned that will be featured include the utilization of user-friendly techniques for communicating the transportation planning process, the importance of tailoring information to meet specific needs of the tribal community, and the use of interactive techniques to engage the public in the transportation planning process. The board game was available by request at www.roadplanner.org, but the site is now defunct. You can still request a copy by contacting your local Bureau of Indian Affairs, or the public affairs department of the US Dept. of Transportation.

These new cards are used during the game the same way as any other card; the only difference is that the combinations are a little bit more complicated.

The King's Rings is a gambling and dexterity game. Players are thieves in a tavern, gambling with some of the treasure they captured from the King’s treasury. They first bid how many rings they think they can add to the Stack before it topples, then take turns placing one ring at a time. The last player to place the final ring of her bid before the Stack collapses gains points equal to her bid. The first thief to gain points equal to the goal will win The King’s Rings.

Created for the 2014 Nestorgames Rings contest.

Unicum is all about unique characteristics. Every object, every number, every form has special defining qualities that distinguish it from other concepts. You must identify these qualities and argue that its card deserves to be entered into the "Circle of UNICUMS". In addition to this common task, you also accumulate individual points by correctly estimating the cards and the other players.

Depending on the number of players a number of cards is placed on the table with the theme you want to use face up. Those cards are the candidates that might be selected to become members of the "Circle of UNICUMS". 
Players secretly write down 3 of the candidates they think will enter the circle (one as early as possible, two as late as possible).
Then by turn players may nominate one candidate and advance a reason, why this canditate is unique. Other players my support or object this argument.
A candidate can only enter the circle, if a majority of the players agrees.
After a certain number of rounds, players reveal their notes and score points for all cards on their note that made it into the "Circle of UNICUMS" (depending on their position). The player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Wok Star: Paige Turner Promo Character
description:
Paige Turner is from another game from designer Tim Fowers called Paperback.  Paige Turner still needs to earn some money while creating her hit novel, so she has been working at the Wok Star Restaurant.

Paige turner is an exclusive character with a new ability for backers of the Wok Star Kickstarter.

Rise of the Northmen is an action point allowance game in which 2-3 players expand their own viking tribes and conquer new lands in search of riches and glory.

The game is played on a map of Scandinavia and the northern European islands. (The map is based off of old viking maps, it represents what they had mapped out to be the northern European islands.  In actuality there were not as many scattered islands as they had thought.  Thematically, this was the way they pictured the world to the west.)

There are three main sectors on the map, from east to west they are A, B and C.  The greatest rewards can be found to the far west in the C sector, but they are also the hardest to reach as they require long journeys across the sea.

Each player starts the game with 3 vikings on the board, 2 steel and 2 food tokens, 5 ship tokens and 12 vikings in reserve, and 10 shield tokens.  During each turn players will each have 7 action points they can use for movement, harvesting resources, razing villages, building ships and sailing, constructing runes to the gods, and attacking each other, all of which offer varying victory point margins.  Many of the actions require resources and troops to complete, requiring players to plan ahead in order to carry out their turns.

The game is driven by an event deck of 32 cards.  At the beginning of each round one card is drawn from the event deck, some random events will help all players and some will hurt all players, most of the events are Crusader cards that correspond to the 3 sectors.  When a Crusader card is drawn a die is rolled  for its corresponding sector and a Crusader shield is placed in that village, players can destroy the Crusader shields for points and are encouraged to because if a 7th Crusader shield is ever placed on the map then all players lose the game.

The game ends when a player places their 10th player shield on a village, or when the event deck runs out.  Players then tally their points for player shields, Crusader shields, vikings and ships on the board, runes, money, attack tokens, and so forth, and whoever has the most points is the winner.

First Hug Me expansion. 
Contains 21 cards:
- 2 new Room Cards type (4 cards, 2 each room)
- 10 new Item Cards

Bloody Red Flag provides advanced & supplemental rules for the Close Action naval wargame. The supplemental rules are an integrated set designed to simulate tactical naval warfare in the late sail era in a more realistic manner than Close Action permits, but at the cost of some additional complexity.

The primary change in Bloody Red Flag is the implementation of a 12-point movement system to replace the regular 6-point hex system. Ships may now turn 30 degrees to point toward a hex corner instead of just hex sides.  Additional rules are included to address topics impacted by this modification in the movement system, including Collisions, Line of Sight, Firing Arcs & Range, Rakes, Marine Fire, Drifting, Anchoring, and Land terrain effects.

Additional rules include Impulse movement, Smoke effects, and Optional rules for Towing, Anchoring, Tacking, and Signaling.  There are also optional rules for Pre-1794 Signaling limitations and a Signal Book for Pre-1794 scenarios.

description:
In 3-Wide you decide how far to push your drivers abilities and the endurance of your race cars as you plot the strategy for a team of race cars.  Winning a race requires a combination of speed, tactics, planning and skill.  You will have to play the right tactics at the right time to improve your track position.  Each race car driver needs to decide if they should throttle back and save fuel, or scorch their tires making a charge to the front.  Will you risk a wreck, or play it safe?  Will you try a bold fuel strategy, or pit with the rest of the field?  3-Wide puts you in the driver's seat, and on the crew chief's pit box!

Each tire of each car has four levels of wear.  These tire wear levels in turn determine the custom colored dice that you will roll to determine the speed of your car.  As the team owner you have a hand of tactic cards which can be played on behalf of your team cars.  Successfully playing tactics is dependent upon the location of other cars around your car, and the timing of when the tactic cards are resolved.  Driver skills are precious resources that can be used to avoid a nasty situation on the track, or to exploit an opportunity on pit lane.  Pit stops become necessary as your tires wear away, and as your fuel gauge is nearing empty.  Planning a good fuel strategy can be vital.

Eighteen different race tracks are modeled in terms of configuration, braking zones, tactical card deck composition, tire wear, and fuel consumption.  Each track also features two different race lengths so you can play a short game or a long one.

Winning is everything, but if you don’t come across the line first maybe you can console yourself with how well your team did overall.  You can also string together a series of races and have a championship season!

MashUp Monsters is a party card game about combining two monsters and turning them into a new born monster.

The players get several monster cards with different symbols in their head and body, then place it face up in front of them. One player rolls the number dice and the symbol dice. The player need to combine the monsters they have simultaneously, so the number of symbol on its head or body is matched with the dice. The first player who can combine all of her monsters wins.

Panic Mode: Match the monsters until the monster deck is runs out and the player who had most succeeded in combining the monsters wins the game. Ah, in Panic Mode the player can also steal monsters from the other players field, so don't be panic if that's happen.

Put your hand, eye, and brain to the test with this fast paced app-enabled card game! It's Great for groups too!

The object of Kubeni is to take what you see on the three dimensional image - which is displayed on either a card or app screen - and recreate it in "real life" before the timer runs out (30 seconds)-  easy right?  But there's a bit more to it... Different modes add new elements to the game and a connected app introduces the possibility of new game modes and kards via update.

With Slicks: Tracks & Tricks – Expansion #2, up to ten different tracks may be created for your Slicks races.

On the front side, you get a very short curve right back to the finish line, enabling you to take a race over the full distance of six rounds. On the back side, you find a piece of track to enlarge your circuit to three sheets, connecting one track part with the pit lane and one with the curve of the basic game.

New options are saving fuel and tires by driving over matching symbols on the track. This allows you to stay out of the pit lane longer.

Tracks & Tricks can be played with the basic game alone or can be combined with Slicks: Tuning-Expansion #1.

In Marchia Orientalis, players get the order to develop the eastern territories (latin "Marchia Orientalis") of King Otto's Empire in the 10th Century.

Starting with one single city tile, they buy farms, fund monasteries, and in the end erect the castles to defend themselves against enemies from inside and outside their territory (mark). To put a new tile to your mark, you buy it from the market. If it is from one of your spaces, you pay the money to the bank; if you buy it out of the track of another player, you pay the price to him. The tile may be put to your mark only if you have matching trading symbols on both tiles (supply chain monastery/farm-village-city-castle). If a tile has neighbors all around, you get the according victory points.

As an alternative, you can choose to draw two new tiles from the pile and offer them in your track of the market. Since you are not allowed to buy tiles in such turn, you must decide whether to make them expensive and hope that you can afford them on your next turn — or to make them cheap and take the risk that others buy them before you have the chance to do so.

Final scoring includes bonus points for most tiles of each type of building and money as well as non-finished buildings (half the value).

Amphipolis (424/422 BC) covers the land battles and naval operations of Amphipolis campaign during the Peloponnesian War.

It is a two players game: Sparta and allies (Brasidas) vs. Athens (Thucydide, Nicias and Cleon) and allies.

One game turn covers three months.

Players will be able to act at grand-tactical and operational level.

Note: The rules are based on Sphacteria and Syracuse ones, which appeared in Vae Victis magazine issue #95 and #103. These rules include specifics at operational level and several additional developments.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Pictionary Disney is the all new way to play in the world of pictures. With all play game modes, the entire family will be engaged during every moment. Players draw, use charades or make sound effects to get opponents to use the slap hands (with suction cups) to grab the correct card the fastest and win the game. With more than 400 clues and three ways to communicate, the entire family will have hours of fun with their favorite Disney icons. Includes 200 picture cards, 4 slap hands, 2 Mickey-styled dry erase boards, 2 dry erase markers, 1 die, 1 wipe cloth and instructions.

Stack, pattern and match the multicolor shapes. Don’t be fooled by appearances, these endlessly enjoyable blocks mean brain-boosting business. Mental Blox help children practice following verbal instructions, asking questions and build critical thinking skills. Activity cards provide children with examples of what to build. Children can recreate what is on the card or can give verbal instructions to a partner or group.

Ways of playing:

     Stack, pattern, and match the multicolor shapes, but don't be fooled by appearances: these endlessly enjoyable blocks mean brain-boosting business
     Twenty activity cards keep strategic thinking skills in tip-top shape, while ensuring all players use strategy and visual literacy in a variety of clever cognitive activities.
     Re-create a hidden arrangement or listen to verbal instructions


How to play

Game #1: Look & Build
2 teams receive 10 identical blocks each. A player will be the counter.
A card is placed away. 
A player of each team will go to see the card, return to his place an take a block to start building the structure, This must be done before the counter gets to 10.  Then a another player from each team will do the same and so on until a team agree that they correctly built the structure calls out  “Mental Blox!”
Check the team’s structure against the card.  If he team built the structure correctly, they can  keep the card. If they built the structure incorrectly, see if the other team came closer to re-creating the structure; if so, that team keeps the card. Continue playing for three rounds.

Game 2: Listen & Build;;
In this game, you’ll have one caller and two builders.  Divide blocks so that each player has an identical  set of 10 blocks.  The caller should take a card and, with his or her back to the builders, describe the  structure to the builders.  After the caller finishes describing the structure and the builders complete their re-creations, compare the card to the structures. Which builder came closest to re-creating the picture with blocks? The most accurate builder can keep the card. Continue playing for three rounds.

Both games have a variant where players should answer the bonus question on the bottom of the card.

In WWI: The Tug of War, two players compete in a very light, fast-playing game of dice-rolling and battle, as all the major countries involved in “The Great War” are slowly and inexorably tugged into the conflict. Game turns quickly become combat events, with the outcome in doubt and usually dependent on the entrance of the United States into the fray, ending a long and bitter back-and-forth stalemate.

The active player always rolls two red dice. He/she will roll one or more four white dice along with the two red dice after he has cards with at least three dice icons, rounded down, in front of him. At the start of the game each player has a card with a half-die in front of him. He or she will therefore need to get cards into the war with two-and-a-half more dice icons to be able to roll a total of three dice (two red and one white).

If the red dice total matches the entry number of one of your nation’s cards it enters the war and its dice icon(s) will be available to you on your next turn. To score a hit (i.e., win a battle), you must roll a 5 or 6 on a white die. If you do, move the token one space toward you on the Tug of War track. Move the token to your "Win" space and you win the game.

Sometimes you'll have to choose between the red and white dice result, and when one player can roll all six dice, the end of game sequence will trigger.

Not my best work. I wish I could get BGG to remove it.

"The Ultimate Game of Chance". A roll and move game for up to 6 players with elements of blackjack, roulette and mock-poker.

Each player starts with $3300. The game is played by rolling a die and moving around the linear board. Squares you land on may tell you to draw a 'Blind Bid' card, or if you land on a Casino space you will be instructed as to which of the three casino games you must play, and what the minimum bid is.

From the rule book: "The object of the game is for all players to start at the airport and travel around the Financial Gaming Minefield, along the strip to Downtown and then back again as directed by the arrows on the board. Players to involve other players when playing Blackjack and Roulette. The player who reaches the airport at the finish with the most money is the winner."

Promo cards for Limes.

Special edition for "Stadt-Land-Spielt" 2014. Printed on cardboard.

2 cards with Lighthouses. Each player receives the Lighthouse card of his color and places it face-up near his other cards. If card #24 is drawn during the game, the players will have to place the Lighthouse card in the following turn. A worker placed on the lighthouse is a lighthouse keeper and will score 1 victory point for each water zone of any water territory adjacent to the lighthouse.

Operation D-Day is a solitaire game of Combat in Europe during World War II. The player assumes the role of a commander of an Infantry Rifle Company during an Allied Amphibious Landing Operation. As the Company Commander, you are responsible for securing your Sector of Advance by successfully taking and holding key objectives with the least amount of casualties. Although there are many support units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of Operation D-Day is to successfully take and secure an number of key objectives and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the Allied Forces. Once all Objectives have been secured or the Campaign Deck is exhausted, the Campaign is considered over. The Player must then total his VP and compare this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

GAME FEATURES: Operation D-Day is a fun and fast solitaire game filled with lots of action and replay value. If you enjoyed movies like "Saving Private Ryan", "Band of Brothers", or D-Day, you will enjoy this game.

This mini expansion adds two new character tiles to Helios.

The giant (three mana) destroys, when activated, one temple of each opponent. At the end of the game, it gives 6 points for each pair of identical landscape tiles.

The balloon driver (3 mana) places, when activated, one piece of thunderstorm on each opponents table on a free spot. The thunderstorm may not be built on a landscape, the sun may not be placed on it, and no temple may be built on it. It doesn't count as a landscape when placing new landscapes (that is, new landscapes may not be put next to only the thunderstorm and nothing else). However, if the sun shines on it, it vanishes. At the end of the game, the balloon driver gives 1 point for each free spot next to the player's landscapes.

boardgame: Guns at Gettysburg Scenarios 4: Stonewall – The Battles of General T.J. Jackson, CSA

description: Socken Pfeffern, a.k.a. Flying Socks, is a new indoor and outdoor trend activity. Players of all ages send socks with custom-made designs flying in a fun competition to hit the washer in as few attempts as possible. There is virtually no limit to where and how the game can be played. The washer is made from durable metal, the socks are washable, and the bean bags inside the sox are filled with high-quality pellets. Basic rules are provided but individual rules can and should be made up on the fly.

Bite Night is a chaotic game for those who like action. First, players play cards from their hand face down onto a common pile, then they simultaneously flip over tiles and try to grab those which are best for them. At the end of the game, the played cards are executed in order and determine who can score how many points for which combination of tiles.

The players can choose between a car, a taxi, an omnibus, a tram and a truck. The goal is to reach space 80, while observing the traffic rules. Movement is determined by a die. On colored spaces, the players have to follow the rules printed on the game board.

The game is playable by as many players as vehicles are available. The board is big enough (102 cm x 70 cm) to use H0 model cars.

The game was produced for the Berliner Verkehrs-Betriebe BVG in the 1930s.

boardgame: Video Game High School: Freddie Wong
description: Promotional item included with the purchase of Video Game High School from Plaid Hat Games web store. The promo introduces the Freddie Wong character, with a cardboard standee and character cards for use in the game.

Each Anno Domini game consists of 340 cards, with a description of a historical event on one side of the card and the year (and sometimes specific date) in which it happened on the other. Anno Domini: Bern focuses on the capital city of Switzerland. Like all Anno Domini sets, this game can be played as a standalone or mixed with some or even all editions.

In Anno Domini: Bern, each player receives nine cards (or fewer, if you want the game to be shorter) and may look only at the descriptions. In turn, players place a card on the table, trying to place their card in chronological order to those already present. Instead of adding a card, a player may claim that the order in which the cards have been placed is incorrect. In this case all cards are turned over and the correct years revealed.

If the order is correct, then the doubting player receives two cards and skips a turn. If the order is incorrect, then the previous player – who accepted the order as correct or made it incorrect through her own placement – receives three cards. The first player with no cards remaining in hand wins.

A Native-American-themed rhythmic reaction game.

Recognize the rhythms another player is producing on the tambourine, be first to hit the corresponding motif card with the swatting hand, and win the game by collecting the most motif cards.

The game also includes some variants for more challenging games, introducing memory elements and cards that reverse the meaning of the symbols on the motif cards.

boardgame: BattleLore: Second Edition – Hernfar Guardians Army Pack
description:
From the website:

Bring unprecedented military might to the battlefield with the Hernfar Guardians Army Pack expansion for BattleLore Second Edition. In Hernfar Guardians, you will find four new Daqan unit types: Greyhaven Battlemages shield your forces with spells, Citadel Lancers charge and trample enemy troops, while armored Ironbound automatons and giant Siege Golems withstand against enemy onslaughts while delivering brutal attacks.

Hernfar Guardians includes twenty-five figures representing these new units and nine additional Citadel Guard figures are included, along with everything necessary to use these units in battle. Army cards suggest deployments composed entirely of the units included in Hernfar Guardians, and a full lore deck grants incredible abilities to all Daqan units. New terrain tiles and scenario cards create never-before-seen battlefields and offer players fresh tactical challenges.

Hernfar Guardians contains:

     The first expansion for the Daqan army in BattleLore Second Edition
     Contains four new unit types with over thirty figures and everything necessary for deployment
         9 Citadel Guards
         9 Greyhaven Battlemage
         9 Citadel Lancers
         6 Ironbound
         1 Siege Golem
    
     Army cards offer ways to integrate the new units into powerful armies
     New terrain tiles and scenario cards introduce new battlefields and tactical challenges
     A full deck of Lore cards enhancing the abilities of all Daqan forces.

Spread chaos and destruction throughout Terrinoth with the Warband of Scorn Army Pack expansion for BattleLore Second Edition.
