description:
A jobs-themed collection of games for very young children.

The game contains 7 picture cards (each consisting of two jigsaw parts) showing a profession, 7 wooden pieces (something to to with these jobs, like a fire for the fire rescuer) and a draw bag.

In the basic game, the players take turns turning over two jigsaw pieces. If they match, the player gets the picture card and the wooden piece that goes with it. The player with the most wooden pieces wins the game.

Variants include one in which the players not only have to find the matching jigsaw pieces but also the corresponding wooden piece in the draw bag (by touch only, without looking), and another one in which one player secretly draws one of the wooden pieces and the other players have to guess his/her job by asking yes/no questions.

boardgame: Bibi & Tina: Das Spiel zum Film
description:
A memory and racing game themed on the German movie "Bibi & Tina", which, in turn, is based on the children's audiobook series of the same name.

The aim of the game is to reach the finish space on the board first or, in case Maik-and-Maik (adversaries from the movies) get there first, to have collected the most heart tokens.

Players take turns rolling the dice. The red die is for moving the Maik-and-Maik token, from the white dice the active player selects one result for him/herself and moves his/her token accordingly, then the other players chose the remaining dice results.
The racing track contains shortcuts and spaces on which an effect card has to be drawn. Most of these have negative effects (having to move back, changing place with the last player etc.). Other spaces get you a heart token and allow all other players to look at one of the movie cards. Remembering which movie card is where allows players to take shortcuts.
Heart tokens can be used to allow players to move fewer spaces than the die roll dictates, to turn over an additional movie card when trying to take a shortcut, or to ask other players for help with finding the right movie card.

boardgame: Race! Formula 90: Expansion #1 – RF90 Series Championship
description:
RF90 Series Championship is the first expansion module for Race! Formula 90, with two new tracks on a double-sided game board. The module includes:


    Spa Francorchamps track
    Monaco track
    35 fully illustrated Spa cards
    36 fully illustrated Monaco cards
    English rulebook


Spa Francorchamps features "Extreme Trajectories", a special set of rules introduced to convey the feeling (and risk) of taking fast corners flat out and to reward "driving" over other elements of the race.

The module also includes dedicated rules for racing in teams with other players or in pair with robots as well as Championship rules.

You must own the original Race! Formula 90 board game in order to use this expansion.

Comes with 5 blocks (dice) which are rolled to get the result of the play call. The player playing offense chooses whether to call a running, passing or punt play. The result of that roll determines what happens on the play. The result of the die roll can be either NO COMBINATION, SINGLE PAIR, DOUBLE PAIR, THREE OF A KIND, SEQUENCE , PAIR AND 3 OF A KIND , FOUR OF A KIND, or FIVE OF A KIND and then depending on what play you choose to run a chart shows the result in yards of that play. The results chart also shows results with either a black or white dot which accounts for fumbles or interceptions and break away touchdown runs or passes. Some black dot results increase the length of the play, or give the unusual results that a game might have including penalties. 30 plays make up a quarter.

It's not simple being Herakles. Everybody seems entitled to tell you what great heroic thing to do next, this Hera goddess keeps harassing you most annoyingly and in case you are too heroic, the gods might just kill you off for being hubristic. And in that case, no historian will publish your great deeds. Not even your good friend Appolodor.

In the Microgame of Herakles, the two cards you have available to choose from each round are handed to you by your neighbours. From those cards, you have to play one. You want to place heroic deeds in your play area, but only 2. The moment you place a third, the gods deem you too heroic and you are out of the game. Other cards allow you to manipulate the cards in front of you and others.

As soon as only one player is left or the stack runs out, the game ends. The player with the highest score in his play area and hand still participating in the game wins and is declared the new Herakles. After  a set number of rounds, whoever was Herakles the most, wins.

In Reserva Ambiental, players must explore the diverse ecosystems this reserve for 15 rounds. Each player will control one animal species that can be trapped or predator, depending on the round and space where the Board. Researcher who also have their walk by the various ecosystems. The location of this researcher is very important in the game as it can solve problems in the ecosystem where it is, build the Research, Environmental Education Projects, Government Actions and perform mainly perform Ecosystem Research. All actions give the player points, and conduct research is what gives more points. All actions and movements of species and Researcher run through the Cards the player has in his Deck. Knowing when and where to use the Cards as best as possible is very important, as they give many options of what to do. At the end of the game, the player who get the most points is the winner.

Em Reserva Ambiental, os Jogadores terão que explorar os diversos  Ecossistemas existentes nesta Reserva durante 15 rodadas. Cada jogador vai controlar 1 espécie animal, que poderá ser presa ou predador, dependendo da rodada e do espaço onde se encontra no Tabuleiro. Também terá seu Pesquisador que caminhará pelos diversos Ecossistemas existentes. A localização deste Pesquisador é muito importante no jogo, pois ele poderá resolver os problemas no Ecossistema onde se encontra, construir Centros de Pesquisa, Projetos de Educação Ambiental, realizar Ações do Governo e principalmente realizar Pesquisa no Ecossistema. Todas as ações dão Pontos ao jogador, sendo que realizar Pesquisa é o que mais dá pontos. Todas as ações e movimentos das espécies e Pesquisador são executadas através das Cartas que o jogador possui em seu Deck. Saber quando e onde usar as Cartas da melhor forma possível é muito importante, já que elas dão muitas opções do que fazer. No final do jogo, aquele jogador que mais pontos conseguir, será o vencedor.

Reindeer Races: The Lone Reindeer is an expansion that adds special abilities to each of the teams of reindeer. It also makes the game playable for 2-5 players by adding a fifth "team" composed of a single pawn that is played differently from the other teams.

Sword Girls (Korean: 소드 걸스) is a Korean collectible card game developed by Zeonix (제오닉스), released in 2012, available in an online and physical (printed) versions. The online version was available in Korean, Japanese and English; the physical cards are available only in Korean.

The four factions in the game are Vita, Academy, Crux and Darklore, all in the background of a war against the empire of Lazion.

The game art was influenced by manga art style.

boardgame: Chez Geek: Slack to the Future
description:
Technology hasn't made us any more productive. In fact, it's made slacking off easier than it's ever been! Chez Geek: Slack to the Future celebrates the bleeding edge of carefree, codependent laziness with 56 new cards of activities, people, jobs, food, and other stuff from the digital revolution.

Just remember, when your roommate and his S.O. keep you up all night: You can't throw them out. They live here.

In a silent night, a huge magic circle appears at the central plaza of Latsyrc village. The magic circle generates dark smog to the sky and forms a Magical Planet over it. The elves in the village need to find the Spirit Mediums, who can control the Crystal Spirits to access to the planet, draw the darkness magical power, and refine a new form of magical power like serum to encounter it.

In the game of Latsyrc, you are 1 of the 4 Spirit Mediums enlisted. You need to assign your Crystal Spirits on the 3D Magical Planet which placed over the Game Board, those Crystal Spirits can help you to gain different resources and also the magical power. Arrange the forms and positions of them in the best way, and compete with the others to gain enough and most magical power to settle the incident

Remember that only 1 of the Spirit Medium can be rewarded by the elves, show the elves that you are the best Spirit Medium to settle the incident and gain the treasure offered by them!

Two galactic forces, the Imperium and the Union, meet at New Mars for a discussion of peace.  The Union sends their most brilliant leaders, ready to hammer out a contract that will mean tranquility and prosperity for years to come.  But as the meetings are about to commence, the Imperium fleet arrives and bombards New Mars to a fine powder, evacuating Imperium leaders just before the destruction.  War immediately erupts in star systems far and near, and the Imperium circles the chaos like a vulture, snatching up planets and enticing new allies to join them.  The Union quickly gathers its forces as princes and vice-presidents take the helm in the sudden power vacuum left from the attack at New Mars. Will they be able to stand against the growing storm that the Imperium commands?

War of the Void pits two teams of players against each other in a struggle to control the galaxy through conquest or diplomacy.  Each player controls his own alien empire, building up a deck of cards unique to his alien faction to perform actions.  Players on the same team complete their turns simultaneously, resulting in action-packed, quick gameplay.

Players must work together to dominate systems by taking control of planets.  Planets can either be conquered, which will increase a player’s income, or befriended, which will give the player a special ability.  Players must build up a fleet of ships to defend and attack, choosing from many ship types with unique abilities.

War of the Void is a thematic sequel to Empires of the Void.  It in an entirely new game set in the same universe. The game is for 2-4 players and takes about 90-120 minutes to play. A planned expansion will add components for a 5th and 6th player.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.R.W. Avatar of Tomed Romulan/Borg Expansion Pack
description:
The I.R.W. Avatar of Tomed was a D'deridex-class Romulan warbird commanded by Salatrel in the late 24th century. Part of a Romulan-Borg alliance in 2371, the Avatar of Tomed was destroyed by the USS Monitor in combat over the Borg homeworld in the Delta Quadrant.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Assimilated Vessel 80279 Klingon/Borg Expansion Pack
description:
Klingons tell their children tales of the Bird-of-Prey taken by the Borg following the Battle of Wolf 359, the Klingon warriors spirits sapped and their ship assimilated by the Collective as they were taken away to the Delta Quadrant. Though their ship was never seen again, the personalities of its crew surfaced when Seven of Nine made contact with a Borg Viniculum aboard the U.S.S. Voyager.

This ship is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, Resistance is Futile.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Assimilated Vessel 64758 Dominion/Borg Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 3 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, Resistance is Futile.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Tactical Cube 001 Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the grand prize for the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, Resistance is Futile.
This expansion adds 10 tiles with special abilities that players receive at the beginning of the game. Each player received 1 tile and it is either random or picked in reverse turn order.

This Promo Expansion adds 5 tokens(1 per player) that allow a player to, once per game, renew diagonally instead of orthogonally.

It also has alternate rules for building renewing.

The Karms is a new finger flicking game with just a dash of dicey luck.

Played on a 13X13 grid players are trying to be the first to flick each of the eight pieces (think backgammon or crokinole discs) across the board.

Pieces are introduced, or moved based on the roll of two D6 dice. A  piece can move the value of a single die.

Each player also has a steel guard piece he places at the end of a turn.

The flicking part comes at the start of the 2nd players turn. His first action is to place a wall piece and then user the striker disc.

The striker is 'flicked' and can rebound off the wall. The idea is to push an opponent's piece farther from its goal, or advance one of his own pieces closer.

Carcassonne: Halb so wild takes the standard Carcassonne tiles and breaks them in half. More specifically, the sheet of tiles consists of twelve triangular landscape tiles that are half the size of a normal tile.

This version was released by Spielbox. It includes 12 tiles with different designs from Carcassonne: Halb so wild II, released by Hans im Glück at the same time. These two different versions are combined in one set as Carcassonne: Halb so Wild. Part of the Carcassonne series.

boardgame: Crossfire Hurricane: The Battle of Long Tan – August 18, 1966
description:
The High Flying Dice Games edition has multiple changes from the previous Firefight Games edition. For example, it has changes to line of sight rules, some OOB revisions (making the entry of the Australian reinforcements a bit more "worrisome"), and the map is completely redone.


Revised edition of the largest battle between the Australians and NVA in 1966 Vietnam.

From the publisher's website:

Undead have always plagued the land, but in recent years they have grown more prevalent. Shamblers are slow moving, mindless foes that react to enemies on an instinctual basis. Killing blows on these abominations are sometimes ignored if their masters are present on the battlefield.

From the publisher's webpage:
Soulless are the dreaded captains of the undead. Once powerful necromancers, these practitioners of the dark arts are able to bring the dead back to life. The Soulless have a deep hatred for the living, and channel that hate through their staff. This inflicts unbearable pain and curses those who succumb to the Darkness.
Contains the Bones expansion boss, two monster darkness cards, and 10 darkness event cards. Bones is the undead skeleton boss.

Bonus card for Meteor. Players could get the card by reviewing the game online.

Card text: Reveal two additional meteors when you launch this rocket.

Burger Dice is a casual press-your-luck dice game for 2 or more players ages 8 and up. A game lasts between 15 and 20 minutes. Players take turns rolling dice and making burgers with their ingredients based on a set of simple rules. Score points for dice you play but be careful: waste too many ingredients and you’ll get kicked out of the kitchen! Be the player with the most points at the end of the game to be declared the Chef de Burger!

Whoever ate a burger most recently goes first. Players take turn rolling 3 dice at a time and placing them in burgers based on a set of simple rules (must have buns, no duplicate ingredients, etc.). You get to score points for completed burgers when you end your turn. Any die you cannot play is considered waste and moved to your waste pile. If at any time you have 4 or more waste dice your turn is immediately over and you score no points.

The game is driven by the custom dice, each one featuring 2 buns and 4 ingredients (burger, pickles, ketchup, and cheese). Using these with the burger building rules the players make their rolls and place the resulting ingredients to create burgers. Players can stop any time they want (before rolling) and score points for their completed burgers (1 point per die) or they can keep rolling and risk losing it all!

In a wicked twist of inspiration, the Elemental Kings have decided to choose their champions by restricting all magic to a single source of spells in the form of an oracle.

This game requires the following components from TKG ARENA: The Elemental Kings & TKG ARENA: Rise of the Magi.

FULL SPELL DECK:    1
STANDARD DECK (DECK):    1 for each player.
OINKS:    5 from each element for each player (Default)
LIFE:    20 for each player (Default)

The objective of this game scenario is to achieve the highest number of KILLs in the rumble. A KILL refers to the elimination of another enemy player by reducing his/her LIFE to 0 during a player’s turn. If a player is self-eliminated due to an unfulfilled deck out cost, this will not be considered a KILL. The eliminating player will get all remaining Oinks of the eliminated player. The game ends when it is evident that it is not possible to score more KILLs than any leading player who will become the winner. If all players are self-eliminated or there are players tied with equal number of KILLs, the last player to deck out will be the winner of the game.

Players start the game by each drawing a card from the top of a common shuffled DECK. The player with the highest number starts first and the players get their turns in a clockwise manner. The drawn cards go back to the bottom of the DECK. In the case of ties, the tied players draw again till the turns are resolved. Each player may attack each other according to the rules as described in TKG ARENA: Rise of the Magi.

The FULL SPELL DECK is shuffled and the top 5 cards are drawn and placed face up. During a player’s turn, he/she may choose to expend 1 Oink in acquiring a special card of the same element among these 5 cards. The special card replaces its counterpart card (of the same ELEMENT and FACE VALUE) and the counterpart card is removed from play in the game. After replacement, the DECK is reshuffled. The acquired special card(s) goes to the bottom of his/her DECK and the player draws sufficiently from his/her DECK to recover his/her hand to 5 cards.

If a counterpart card is in a player’s CURRENT HAND during his/her turn to acquire spells, the player may acquire it without cost. A player may make as many acquisitions as long as he/she has the necessary counterparts in his/her CURRENT HAND. If there are no counterparts cards in his/her CURRENT HAND, the player may still acquire the special card(s) by expending the appropriate cost as above. In addition, the player must end his/her turn immediately after the transaction. If the player does not acquire any special card(s) during his/her turn, he/she can choose to engage in battle with any other player in the group and the same rules of engagement (for the usual 2-player mode) apply including the use of the CHARGE BAR.

Every time a special card is acquired by a player, another is drawn from the FULL SPELL DECK pile to replace it. If a cycle of turns (from player back to the same player) passes with no players acquiring any special cards, all the available 5 special cards will be placed at the bottom of the FULL SPELL DECK pile and another 5 new ones are drawn.

Players may wish to use their own customised DECK in tournament mode for this scenario and omit the oracle (spell source) altogether. It is recommended that all players should then be fairly matched to warrant a fair game.

boardgame: On the Seven Seas: Wargaming Rules for the Age of Piracy and Adventures c.1500-1730
description:
(From the rule book)

On the Seven Seas is a set of wargames rules covering the high adventure and low morals of the world of the pirate.

From Drake and his sea-rovers to Blackbeard, the Barbary Corsairs and the Wo-k'ou of the Far East, pirates have haunted seas across the globe, preying on port and vessel alike. Now you too can recreate the exploits of pirate captains or the naval commanders that hunted them.

Whether you want skirmish between crews on uncharted islands and in the alleyways of Caribbean ports or ship-to-ship duels that culminate in bloodthirsty boarding actions, the rules offer a quick-to-learn basic game. These small forces of buccaneers, commanded by captains and kept in line by trusted lieutenants, can also be sealed up with ease for larger engagements.

Gameplay centers on two driving motivations that epitomize the pirate life - Fear and Greed. Cunning captains will have to balance these two elements, instilling fear in their opponents with bloodthirsty reputations, while keeping their own crews in line with the promise of loot and wealth

The Collector's Edition of Thrash-Car includes 8 extra action cards, 4 extra race car tokens, and 2 extra team placards (4 teams) that have had foil applied to them.  Action cards and team placards have unique actions and abilities.

The Collector's Edition box also has an Embossed foil stamp on the cover and is signed and numbered.  There will only be 300 units of this version ever manufactured.


In Thrash-Car, you build your racing team from the ground up. As the team owner, you choose your crew, car, and strategy, then help them drive their way to victory in some of the wildest and toughest race conditions you have ever seen.

Each driver is trying to get around the track and survive it. The track is made of random cards drawn from the track deck, and each card has a target attribute, a target number, and a damage value. The attribute determines that base attribute from your car you are using to build your base roll modifier. The target number is the number you have to roll on a ten-sided die or greater to succeed and move to the next track space. Damage is how much damage your car takes if you fail your roll.

When you go to move, the other drivers can play up to one card on your action stack (this is the screw your neighbor part). The draw deck plays one card, then you can play up to two on yourself. These cards will result in a modifier for your roll to move. You add or subtract that number from your car's base and roll the die. If you make the target you move ahead and keep racing; if you fail you take the listed damage and try to keep racing. Fail too many rolls, and your car will be destroyed. You are always trying to get ahead and your friends are always trying to drag you down.

At times, the drivers can beat on each other with their cars and roll head-to-head to see who is better in these exchanges, but if you get outrolled you can take some serious damage. If you do the outrolling, though, you can hammer the other guy and leap ahead in the race.

You win Thrash-Car by being the first to complete the designated laps or being the last car running on the track.

You are a Dream Collector, a broker for one of the Dream Lords, powerful beings that feast on the energy of our unconscious imagination.  Your Dream Lord has tasked you to collect its favorite Dream Energies with the promise of anything you desire as a reward.

But be careful: there are other Dream Collectors hunting Dream Energies for their own Dream Lords and no Dream Lord likes to be outdone!

How to play:

Each player takes a Dream Lord that they will represent at the start of play.  That Dream Lord has a favored Dream Energy and two complimentary Dream Energies listed on its card.

Once you and the opposing player have selected your Dream Lords, you each take turns placing the remaining cards orthogonally to build the Dreamscape.  The objective is to build Dreampaths along the Dreamscape, your Dream Lord's favored Dream Energies that connect in a continuous unbroken path up, down, left, or right (but not diagonal).

Once the Dreamscape has been completely assembled, players score their Dreampaths.  The more preferred Dream Energies in the path, the higher points value it is worth.
This Expansion Set for UNITY allows 2–3 players to play on a 6×6×6 hex-hex grid, a 49% bigger playfield than in the base game.

boardgame: Monsters of London: Familiars and Masters Expansion
description:
Familiars and Masters is the first (mini-)expansion for the dice-cardgame Monsters of London. It expands the game by 8 cards, introducing new powerful PERMANENT abilities.

The four Masters, who created the monsters released into the streets of Victorian London, join the battle: Worth four Pounds when captured their permanent abilities may soon become quite a nuisance.
It's quite the other way around with the Familiars: Their helpful abilities support the player but cost one precious Pound Sterling at the end of the game.

This is a anti-set collection game in honor of the first 13 US colonies.

GAME OBJECTIVE:
Have the lowest point score when someone goes over the target score.

GAME PLAY:
Each player has cards from different cities in the first 13 US colonies.  On your turn, you may draw cards and play down sets on opposing players.  Those sets score points for the enemy players.

Special cards are used as wild cards, or to alter game play.

Additionally, there are two different modes, depending upon if you want to play more ruthless.

boardgame: The Game of Get to the Finish Line
description:
Cowbo, Astwonot, and pals are stranded on one side of a piece of paper with the burning urge to reach the other side. Can they make it without being shot?!

The Game of Get to the Finish Line is a game of stealth, deduction, and trickery for 2-6 players. Your goal is to cross the game board without being caught by other players.

Each game starts with every player secretly choosing a character. Remaining player slots are filled with Non-Player characters until 6 player slots are full.

Per turn, a player may move any character(s) up to 3 times. For example, a player may move a single character 3 spaces or 3 characters one space. After movement, a player may use their special "once per game elimination" ability to wipe out any suspicious challengers.

The game can end in two ways:

    A character (even non-player) crosses the finish line.
    All player characters have been eliminated.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Cartoona: Expansions – includes Heads on Tails and Long Necks
description:
Inside this box are two expansions for the creature-building, tile-laying game Cartoona.

The Heads On Tails expansion allows Cartoona players to build wacky two-headed creatures. Included are 3 new tiles and 3 new cards.

The Long Necks expansion includes neck tiles that can be placed in-between a head tile and a body tile. These neck tiles can be placed even when the head and body have already been connected, and multiples can be played for some seriously long necks! Neck tiles can also be placed on the creatures of your opponents to disrupt their strategy, or even take away points! Included are 6 new tiles and 4 new cards.

Both of these expansions require the Cartoona base game to play.

A game of inner city crime, drug dealing, and gang violence.

Hoodrats is a hybrid Euro-Trash game containing 44 meeples, 15 dice, 88 tokens, 36 cards, a fat wad of paper money, and a bag of oregano (fake weed) which is the first player marker.

Players begin with 5 "Hustlers" and a special gang ability available only to their gang, and place them on the 4 locations to buy drugs of 4 different kinds, 14 spaces to sell drugs on, the Funeral Home, where they can recruit the family members of killed gang members, the bail bondsman where they can purchase a bond to release incarcerated gang members, the gun store where they buy guns, or their 'Hood, where they can initiate drive-by shootings against enemy Hustlers who are on corners to sell drugs.

Hustlers are placed in turn, where players may place up to three Hustlers at once on most locations. Once all of the players' Hustlers are placed, each player, in turn, then activates one space and performs the actions which that space allows. Each corner allows players to sell one drug based on the icon shown, and may roll the Dope Dice (3 custom dice) in which depict a different drug on 4 faces (Weed, Meth, Coke, Heroin), a wild card on the fifth face, and finally, an arrest warrant on the sixth. For each face rolled players may sell one matching drug token.

If 2 gangs occupy a corner, the person who activates the corner initiates a robbery. No matter who wins, someone's going to the Emergency room, with one Beat Down token for each opposing Hustler that robbed him.

At the beginning of each round, a card is drawn and has either a persistent effect which lasts the entire round or a one time effect, as well as that night's prices to buy and sell drugs.

At the end of a set amount of rounds, the richest gang wins.

OVERVIEW
Apples & Oranges (A&O) cards is a game designed by Ray Lauzzana.

Apples & Oranges contains a deck of 36 playing cards with various numbers of apples and oranges on each card, plus three instruction cards.

In addition, there ore two indicator cards - one for the orange team and another for the apple team.

This game may be played by two to four players. When playing with four players, they play in two teams - the orange team vs. the apple team.

When playing with two players, one payer plays the apple side, the other plays the orange side.

OBJECT OF THE GAME
The goal is to build a group of fruit that is larger than your opponents,

For example, if at the end of the game, the orange team has a cluster of 26 oranges, and the apples are dispersed into smaller groups, then orange gets 26 points, and wins. If both players’ largest groups are the same size, they are tied.

Vuelta Rápida (Best Lap) is a party game in which you play as a motorcycle racer in a MotoGP race.

Each lap (turn) a player, using mimic and sounds, will reenact this lap so the other players have to guess how it was organized. The more moves they guess, the fastest their racers will move on the board. But be careful, each mistake will slow your racer down, making him fall behind.

Each race will have as many rounds as players plus one extra lap.The racer in first position at the end of the game wins.

Spanish description:
Vuelta rápida es un party game en el que los jugadores representan a pilotos de moto GP durante una carrera.

Cada vuelta, un jugador por medio de mímica y sonidos representará la vuelta para que el resto de jugadores adivinen cual es el trazado perfecto. Cuantas más maniobras acierten más rápido se moverá en la pista su piloto. Pero cuidado, cada fallo frenará al piloto haciéndole perder puestos.

Cada carrera constará de un número de vueltas (turnos) dependiendo del número de jugadores. Al finalizar esas vueltas, el piloto que esté en primer lugar habrá ganado la carrera.

These are 6 bonus cards that add to the game play options and create a whole new aspect to the game.  They are an interesting way to spice up your interactions and help in developing strategies.  These extra effects allow you to discover new ways of influencing the outcome of the game.

Before beginning the first round of the game, the player with the season/priority token takes 3 random cards from the pool of extra effects cards that have been obtained for Dice Brewing.  Then these 3 cards are placed face up above the recipe cards, side by side, and the effects themselves can be used in every round by any of the players for the rest of the game.

boardgame: Trench War: A Solitaire Game of Tactical Combat in the Trenches of the First World War.
description:
INTRODUCTION: Trench War is a solitaire game of Combat in the muddy trenches in France or Belgium during World War I. The player assumes the role of a commander of an Infantry Platoon during its grueling and miserable turn in the Trenches before they are relieved. As the Platoon Leader, you are responsible for securing your Sector of Front by successfully taking enemy positions and defending yours with the least amount of casualties. Although there are many support units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE: The objective of Trench War is to successfully survive a Tour in the trenches along the Western Front during World War I. A Tour consists of a number of Rotations in the Trenches. The Player can play a Short Tour (1 Rotation), an Average Tour (2 Rotations) or a Long Tour (3 Rotations). Each Rotation consists of making a Rotation Deck of 20 “Trench War” Cards and once all Trench War Cards have been drawn and resolved, the Rotation is over and the Player totals his Rotation VP and adds it to his Tour VP. Once all Rotations have been completed, the Player totals his Tour VP and then compares it to the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

GAME FEATURES: Trench War features plenty of colorful Game Counters, Map and Battle Mat, used for resolving Combat. The Game provides plenty of grim themes associated with the War in the Trenches. Counters include Infantry Soldiers, HMGs, Flamethrowers, MK-IV Tanks, A7 Tanks, FT-17 Tanks, Pillboxes, Aircraft, Sub-Machineguns and Light Machineguns.
If you enjoy movies like "All Quiet on the Western Front", "The Lost Batallion", "Sergeant York", "Westfront 1917" or World War One in general, you will enjoy this game.

boardgame: Babylon 5 Component Game System: 2258 Edition Vorlon Empire Starter Kit
description:
In the Babylon 5 Component Game System, you take control of a certain faction which is trying to gain power in the galaxy. You play the role of an ambassador on board Babylon 5. You explore the vast stretches of space, lead huge fleets into battle, and control the economy of your faction. There are several ways to achieve victory ranging from economic growth to all out militaristic control of the universe. Diplomacy is not to be neglected either, as the more powerful game changes are brought up in Babylon 5 council vote to be decided.

You construct your playing 'board' using hexagonal shaped pieces that come with the game. The various pieces are different and can represent a multitude of things so that the game is different every time you play. In addition to the board and the ships that move on it, there are cards. Players may buy cards allowing special actions above and beyond the normal game, like an extra move or more EPs.

Using your faction's wealth and resources you play the game and explore the map. You search for planets to colonize and possibly conquer. Use your fleets to engage in battles and use characters to assist you in reaching your faction's goals. The choices are yours and will decide if your faction is successful.

There are several ways to achieve victory ranging from economic growth to all out militaristic control of the universe. Diplomacy is not to be neglected either, as the more powerful game changes are brought up in Babylon 5 council vote to be decided.

You construct your playing 'board' using hexagonal shaped pieces that come with the game. The various pieces are different and can represent a multitude of things so that the game is different every time you play. In addition to the board and the ships that move on it, there are cards. Players may buy cards allowing special actions above and beyond the normal game, like an extra move or more EPs.

Using your faction's wealth and resources you play the game and explore the map. You search for planets to colonize and possibly conquer. Use your fleets to engage in battles and use characters to assist you in reaching your faction's goals. The choices are yours and will decide if your faction is successful.
